title: Toward Aquaculture Automation: Image Enhancement and Containment Net Removal for Robust Fish Detection for Behavior Recognition
authors: Abdullah Alraee, Mohammad Albaroudi, Inniyaka R. Irmiya, Hussam Alraie, Dominic B. Solpico, Raji Alahmad, Kazuo Ishii
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Aquaculture plays a vital role in global food security by providing controlled environments for raising aquatic animals. Effective and precise feeding management is essential for sustainability; however, traditional practices rely heavily on farmers’ judgment. This judgment varies among veterans and novices, leading to food wastage, poor fish health, and inadequate monitoring of fish health. To address these issues, automation offers a promising solution for accurate control of feeding operations. A vision system is essential. However, a significant challenge in underwater environments arises owing to variable lighting, light backscattering, and physical obstructions such as containment nets. These factors hinder the accurate observation of fish behavior, which is crucial for feeding strategy development. This study presents an image-processing algorithm designed to eliminate visual obstructions caused by the net structures in underwater environments. Our approach achieved a significantly high precision for fish $(98.8 \%)$. This enabled a better understanding of fish behavior and led to more reliable automated feeding systems. These findings highlight the potential of computer vision techniques in aquaculture monitoring, promising to improve operational efficiency and reduce reliance on manual labor.

title: Spectral Based Fatigue Analysis of Roro Ship Structure: Case Study in the Madura Strait
authors: Lukman Firmansyah, Rizky Chandra Ariesta, Dony Setyawan, Mohammad Nurul Misbah
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Structural fatigue is a critical factor that influences a ship’s operational lifespan and safety. As the vessel ages, its structural strength gradually deteriorates due to cyclic loads from cargo and ocean waves. If not properly addressed, these repeated loads can cause material cracking and eventually lead to structural failure. The ship analyzed in this study is over $\mathbf{2 0}$ years old, making a structural strength evaluation necessary to determine its remaining fatigue life. To estimate the ship’s fatigue life, a spectral-based fatigue method is employed using wave data from the past 10 years. The analysis is carried out under two loading conditions: full load and empty load, by considering eight wave heading directions. The frequency range used spans from 0.1 to $1.8 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$, in accordance with the recommendations of the American Bureau of Shipping (ABS). The wave heading angles range from -180° to 180°, with 45° intervals. The calculation results show that the total fatigue life of the ship in its current condition, under combined loading scenarios, is estimated to be 6 years.

title: Topology-Aware Hierarchical Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Virtual Power Plants
authors: Karthika M, R. Ravi, Abhishek Pandey, Ajit Kumar Singh, K.Muthukannan, Ganesh Kumar.G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rapid integration of distributed energy resources (DERs), including photovoltaic systems, wind turbines, battery energy storage systems, and flexible loads, has transformed Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) into complex and dynamic cyber—physical energy systems. However, the stochastic nature of renewable generation and the heterogeneity of DERs create significant challenges for traditional centralized or flat multi-agent control approaches, particularly in achieving scalable, real-time, and grid-aware coordination. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (HMADRL) framework for efficient coordination and control of VPP resources. The proposed framework adopts a two-level hierarchical architecture. A high-level supervisory agent performs global scheduling and dispatch decisions based on grid constraints and market signals, while multiple low-level DER agents execute localized control actions such as power dispatch, battery charging/discharging, and demand response management. Agents are trained using Deep Q-Learning with cooperative learning, combining partially centralized training with decentralized execution to reduce communication overhead while maintaining coordinated decision-making. To improve scalability and learning efficiency, the framework incorporates state abstraction, reward decomposition, and cooperative value sharing mechanisms. The approach is evaluated through simulations of a VPP consisting of 50 DER units over a 24-hour operational horizon with 15-minute intervals. Results show that the HMADRL framework reduces operational costs by 15—22%, increases renewable energy utilization by about 12%, and decreases power imbalance by approximately 30% without causing voltage instability. The model converges within 1500 training episodes, outperforming flat multi-agent reinforcement learning methods that require over 2500 episodes. Sensitivity analysis under ±20% renewable variability and ±15% load fluctuations, along with scalability tests up to 200 DER units, demonstrates stable convergence and robust economic performance. These results confirm that HMADRL provides a scalable and efficient control strategy for next-generation smart grid VPP operations.

title: Autonomous Multimodal Grievance Redressal System using Generative AI
authors: Arpita Paria, Yash Kumar, Rohit Raj, Sagnik Das, Shaikh Aaman, Nandini K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Efficient grievance resolution remains a critical challenge in large-scale public transportation networks such as Indian Railways, where existing complaint management platforms continue to suffer from manual processing delays, language barriers faced by non-English speaking passengers, and inefficient routing of complaints to the appropriate departments. This paper presents an Autonomous Multimodal Grievance Redressal System powered by Generative AI to fully automate the end-to-end complaint handling process from submission through to dispatch. The proposed system leverages GPT-4o, a Large Multimodal Model, to simultaneously process diverse unstructured inputs, including multilingual voice recordings and photographic evidence, automatically transcribing and translating regional language audio into English while applying computer vision techniques to assess the severity and authenticity of visual complaints. Built on a Next.js frontend and Firebase serverless backend architecture, the platform autonomously categorises and routes grievances to relevant departments, including Medical, Maintenance, and Security, based on real-time sentiment and priority analysis across four levels — Low, Medium, High, and Emergency. The proposed approach achieves zero-touch complaint dispatch, significantly reduces response time, improves accessibility for linguistically diverse passengers, and enables real-time prioritization of safety-critical issues, while a publicly accessible analytics dashboard ensures transparency and operational accountability across railway services.

title: Handwritten Answer Script Evaluation using Multi-OCR Model
authors: K R Sowmia, Yuvashree A, Subashini B, Vijayalakshmi K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Evaluating a student’s handwritten answer script responses is often time consuming and challenging manually, especially with students' various handwritten styles and with demand for faster grading on established time. Existing grading systems struggle with diverse handwritten inputs. To address these challenges, our study proposes an automated answer script evaluation system that combines multiple OCR models such as TrOCR and Tesseract for improved handwritten text extraction that can enhance consistency in grading and reduce the amount of work. The system enables users to upload a student's answer script and the reference answer key for automated evaluation. The Fine-tuned TrOCR model along with tesseract is used to extract text from the regions of the answer script, while the ensemble strategy enhances recognition across varied writing styles. The similarity metrics are classified using Logistic Regression, a machine learning model which automatically detects correctness and assigns marks. The accuracy achieved around 95% shows how precisely it has predicted, evaluated the answers and has graded marks which offers a transparent, and scalable solution for evaluating and grading the handwritten answers, reducing human effort while maintaining reliability and interpretability in academic assessment.

title: Class-Aware Feature Selection with Core and Auxiliary Subsets for Multi-Class Intrusion Detection
authors: Mohammad Hossein Aminian, Arash Khosravi, Ahmad Azarnik
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) must operate under high-dimensional traffic data, where redundant and irrelevant features inflate computational costs and obscure class-specific attack patterns. This paper explores a modular perspective on feature selection, distinguishing between global core features, which generalize across classes, and auxiliary features, which provide additional discrimination for specific attack categories. The study builds on an optimization-based framework (described in an extended journal version, under review) and focuses on empirical evaluation. Using the CIC-IDS2017 dataset, three classifiers—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and LightGBM—were trained under two scenarios: core-only features and core + auxiliary features. Results show that advanced learners such as Random Forest and LightGBM achieve near-optimal accuracy with the compact core subset, while auxiliary features yield substantial improvements for simpler models such as Logistic Regression, particularly for minority classes like Bot and Web Attack. These findings suggest that auxiliary features are most beneficial in contexts where linear models or underrepresented classes dominate, whereas core features provide a stable and efficient baseline. By presenting per-class performance analyses, this paper highlights the potential of modular feature decomposition for designing IDS that balance efficiency, adaptability, and interpretability.

title: Flying-Stack Modular Multilevel Converter for Wide Range High-AC/Low-DC Medium Voltage Applications
authors: Saeed Sharifi, Levi Bieber Levi, Liwei Wang, Juri Jatskevich
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a new overlapping modulation strategy for a recently introduced hybrid converter called the flying-stack modular multilevel converter (FS-MMC) in this paper. The new strategy extends the FS-MMC's capabilities to operate at high modulation indices, enabling high-AC/low-DC voltage conversion for modern medium voltage charging infrastructure. The approach maintains zero-voltage switching (ZVS) of the director switches (DSs) while introducing a parallel current path that reduces DS conduction losses by bypassing the flying-stack of each converter leg. By incorporating an overlapping interval for the outer stacks, the proposed modulation method eliminates the need for precise DS switching at AC voltage zero crossings. Unlike conventional topologies, the flying and DC split capacitors are not needed for the FS-MMC. This ensures robust performance under unbalanced and faulty grid conditions. Comparison results show that the proposed strategy reduces number of SM and DS IGBTs of the FS-MMC by ֺֺ% and ׇׇ% compared to the conventional modulation strategy. It also enables a ׇ׃.ַ% loss reduction at unity power factor while maintaining the energy storage size. In reactive power support scenarios, the FS-MMC achieves up to ֧ו% lower losses than recently reported converters. The performance of FS-MMC under the new modulation method is validated through detailed simulations and experimental tests on a lab-scale prototype, confirming its strong potential for medium voltage DC applications.

title: Anti-Swing Trajectory Tracking of Slung-Payload UAVs: An Attention-Infused Neural Observer Approach With Output Constraints
authors: Junjie Shi, Tong Wang, Mingzhe Hou, Liguo Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Slung-payload quadrotor uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) are pivotal in aerial logistics but suffer from performance degradation due to payload swing, strong coupling dynamics, and external disturbances. To address these challenges, this article proposes a constrained adaptive control scheme integrated with an attention-based neural observer. First, an enhanced cascade framework explicitly captures the UAV-payload coupling to generate active anti-swing commands. To ensure operational safety, barrier Lyapunov functions (BLFs) are employed to strictly confine position errors within prescribed safety constraints, preventing constraint violation even during complex trajectory tracking. Furthermore, a novel disturbance observer leveraging an attention mechanism is developed. Unlike conventional estimators, this mechanism selectively weighs historical error features to accurately reconstruct time-varying aerodynamic disturbances. Rigorous stability analysis proves that all closed-loop signals are uniformly ultimately bounded. Finally, comparative simulations and real-world flight experiments demonstrate the superior tracking accuracy and swing suppression of the proposed strategy.

title: Context-Conditioned Spatio-Temporal Predictive Learning for Reliable V2V Channel Prediction
authors: Lei Chu, Daoud Burghal, Rui Wang, Michael E. Neuman, Andreas F. Molisch
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Achieving reliable multidimensional Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) channel state information (CSI) prediction is both challenging and crucial for optimizing downstream tasks that depend on instantaneous CSI. This work extends traditional prediction approaches by focusing on four-dimensional (4D) CSI, which includes predictions over time, bandwidth, and antenna (TX and RX) space. Such a comprehensive framework is essential for addressing the dynamic nature of mobility environments within intelligent transportation systems, necessitating the capture of both temporal and spatial dependencies across diverse domains. To address this complexity, we propose a novel context-conditioned spatiotemporal predictive learning method. This method leverages causal convolutional long short-term memory (CA-ConvLSTM) to effectively capture dependencies within 4D CSI data, and incorporates context-conditioned attention mechanisms to enhance the efficiency of spatiotemporal memory updates. Additionally, we introduce an adaptive meta-learning scheme tailored for recurrent networks to mitigate the issue of accumulative prediction errors. We validate the proposed method through empirical studies conducted across three different geometric configurations and mobility scenarios. Our results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms existing state-of-the-art predictive models, achieving superior performance across various geometries. Moreover, we show that the meta-learning framework significantly enhances the performance of recurrent-based predictive models in highly challenging cross-geometry settings, thus highlighting its robustness and adaptability.

title: Representation-Aligned Atmospheric Light Decoupling for Stable Single-Image Dehazing
authors: Zhi Dou, Luyao Wang, Shuai Yuan, Wentao Mao, Hu Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Single-image dehazing remains a challenging inverse imaging problem, particularly under colored haze caused by industrial pollution and complex outdoor environments. Many existing methods rely on fixed color representations or data-driven learning, which often leads to unstable reconstruction, color distortion, or limited generalization. This paper presents a model-based dehazing framework that reformulates the atmospheric scattering inversion through a haze-aware coordinate system aligned with the physical image formation model. We introduce the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$FL\theta$</tex-math></inline-formula> representation, in which one axis is explicitly aligned with the atmospheric light vector, while the orthogonal subspace captures object-dependent optical information. This construction enables an explicit decoupling of air light and scene radiance components, simplifying the structure of the inverse problem. Within this representation, atmospheric light estimation, depth estimation, and transmission recovery become mutually consistent, leading to stable reconstruction without reliance on large-scale training data or image-specific parameter tuning. Extensive experiments on synthetic datasets and real-world hazy images demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves robust dehazing performance across diverse haze colors and scene types, with improved color consistency and structural preservation compared to representative prior-based and learning-based methods. Additional evaluations on downstream segmentation tasks further show that the recovered images enhance perceptual reliability, highlighting the practical relevance of the proposed approach for real-time outdoor vision systems.

title: Pedestrian Activity Classification with Hybrid Deep Learning Models Using Elevated LiDAR 3D Point Clouds
authors: Nawfal Guefrachi, Hakim Ghazzai, Ahmad Alsharoa, Gianluca Setti
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Recent advances in Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) and artificial intelligence have enabled new opportunities for traffic surveillance and pedestrian monitoring in urban environments. However, most existing systems focus on object detection and tracking, while reliable pedestrian activity recognition under realistic degradation remains insufficiently addressed. This paper presents an elevated LiDAR–based framework that captures overhead 3D point clouds for object recognition and multiclass pedestrian activity classification. Despite progress in 3D perception, three major challenges persist: (i) the scarcity of activity-labeled real-world LiDAR datasets suitable for supervised learning, (ii) limited robustness of deep learning models when LiDAR signals are affected by noise, sparsity, and occlusion, and (iii) the lack of unified pipelines that jointly integrate 3D detection with spatial and temporal behavior analysis. To address these challenges, we develop a Blender-based elevated LiDAR dataset simulating diverse urban scenarios and extend the KITTI tracking dataset with activity annotations for real-world validation. The proposed framework combines PV-RCNN for 3D pedestrian detection with a hybrid PointNet–Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architecture for spatial–temporal activity recognition, guided by a dynamic weighting fusion mechanism. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms PointNet, LSTM, and transformer-based baseline across all the activity classes, achieving 84.7% accuracy on synthetic data. Under severe LiDAR corruption, performance degrades by only 3.4 percentage points. On the KITTI-based dataset, the framework achieves 73.5% accuracy, surpassing single-stream baselines by 6.5–23.0 percentage points. These results demonstrate that joint spatial-temporal reasoning yields improved comparative robustness under controlled LiDAR degradations, while maintaining consistent cross-domain generalization under real-world conditions for pedestrian activity recognition in smart-city environments.

title: A Neuroadaptive Digital Twin System for Emotion Recognition and Socio-Emotional Training in Autistic Children
authors: Claire Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) affects 1 in 36 children in the U.S., often manifesting as impaired emotion recognition, limited eye engagement, and reduced attention to socially salient stimuli. These challenges are linked to neurological factors such as amygdala hypersensitivity, mirror neuron dysfunction, and atypical relevance detection. Current therapies are costly, difficult to scale, and lack precision tracking. Additionally, existing AI models rely on general facial datasets that underrepresent neurodivergent traits such as asymmetry and side-glancing behaviors. This paper presents HarmonyVisage, a neuroadaptive computing system that employs personalized digital twins and computer vision to deliver scalable, real-time socio-emotional training for autistic children. The system integrates facial emotion recognition, 3D facemesh-based modeling, and gaze analysis to identify and explain 13 key emotion-engaging facial regions. It uses temporal-spatial precision to measure and reinforce reciprocal eye engagement and affective expression. In an eight-week, four-phase study with 54 enrolled children (46 meeting > = 80% training sessions compliance for analysis) (aged 6-13), the system delivered over 3,500 training sessions. Statistically significant improvements in eye engagement and emotion imitation were observed in three of four participant subgroups (p-values = 0.027, 0.001, and 0.001), and digital twin engagement yielded a significant effect (p = 0.014). The system demonstrates potential as an accessible, affective AI-based intervention for neurodivergent populations, supporting both at home use and clinician-guided therapy.

title: FOPTD Based Reduced Order Modeling Assisted Real Time Robust Frequency Control of Islanded Microgrid with Inverter-Based Resources
authors: Sugandh Pratap Singh, Vinay Pratap Singh, Francesco Benedetto, Josep M. Guerrero
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The high penetration of inverter-based resources in islanded microgrids significantly reduces system inertia and damping, threatening frequency stability during load and generation mismatches. To address these challenges, this article presents a robust frequency control framework assisted by reduced-order modeling. First, the complex higher-order microgrid dynamics are approximated into a computationally efficient First-Order Plus Time-Delay (FOPTD) model. This reduced model is then utilized to synthesize robust controllers using five distinct deterministic rule-based tuning strategies, avoiding the computational burden of iterative optimization. The performance of the proposed approach is validated through real-time Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) simulations on an OPAL-RT platform, incorporating realistic scenarios such as stochastic load changes and renewable energy fluctuations. Furthermore, extensive sensitivity analysis demonstrates the controllers' robustness against parameter uncertainties, specifically under varying system inertia and damping conditions.

title: A Multi-Stage Cascade Framework for Face Anti-Spoofing using Lightweight CNNs, Depth-Aware ResNet18, and Implicit 3D Facial Geometry
authors: Navaneetha Krishnan M, C.N. Vanitha, Gokulkrishnan R, Gokul Kumar L, Ramkumar M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Presenting of faces is still a major problem to the biometric security system; this is due to the numerous types of spoofing media as well as variability that is faced in the real world environment. We have introduced in this work a multi-stage cascaded multi-face anti-spoofing scheme, whereby the any spoofing is filtered by the combination of complementary visual and geometric information. The initial step uses a small convolutional neural network that is effective in discarding low-quality attacks due to the detection of texture discrepancies. The second step takes advantage of a depth-sensitive ResNet18 that obtains discriminative depth and spatial data that can differentiate real faces and planar spoof images. Lastly, shape consistency is enforced by an implicit three-dimensional facial geometry modeling classifier and thus, high-resolution prints and replay videos are detected. The cascaded architecture increases robustness without taking too much computational overhead thus supporting real time deployment. Massive empirical analyses indicate that the presented framework delivers better generalization performance as compared to single-stage methods to various face anti-spoofing datasets.

title: Secure Embedded Hardware Architecture for Embodied Generative Intelligence in Robotic Perception–Action Systems
authors: Yanpei Su
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Embodied generative intelligence has emerged as a powerful paradigm for robotic perception–action systems, enabling long-horizon reasoning and adaptive behavior through generative world models and policies. However, deploying such systems on real robots introduces critical security and safety challenges, as adversarial perturbations can propagate across sensing, generative inference, and actuation, leading to unsafe physical behaviors. Existing defenses mainly rely on software-level or algorithmic mechanisms and fail to provide deterministic guarantees under real-time and resource constraints. This paper proposes a secure embedded hardware architecture tailored for embodied generative robotics. We first formalize a cross-layer threat model that captures sensor spoofing, model tampering, latent-state leakage, and action manipulation. Guided by this model, we design a hardware–software co-designed architecture integrating a Secure Sensor Interface, Trusted Generative Processing Unit, Isolated World-Model Memory, Action Verification Unit, and Real-Time Security Monitor. Experimental results on a representative embedded robotic platform demonstrate that the proposed architecture significantly reduces attack success rate and safety violation rate under increasing attack intensity, while introducing only modest and predictable latency and power overhead. The results highlight hardware-native security as a key enabler for safe embodied generative intelligence.

title: Robust Image Denoising: A Method Based on Multi-Scale Local Adaptive Hybrid Iterative Bilateral Filtering
authors: Qiang Fu, Yuhuan Zhou, Yongqiang Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Image denoising is pivotal in enhancing the quality of digital images corrupted by noise from acquisition or transmission processes, impacting fields like medical imaging, remote sensing, and computer vision. Conventional approaches such as bilateral filtering and non-local means often compromise between noise removal and detail preservation, especially under mixed noise conditions including Gaussian and impulse types. This paper presents the Multi-Scale Local Adaptive Hybrid Iterative Bilateral Filtering integrated with Non-Local Means (MS-LAHIABF-NLM), a robust algorithm that adaptively handles diverse noise without prior assumptions. It features selective impulse correction, LAB space processing with NLM for chroma, and variance-guided fusion of iterative bilateral layers for luminance. Noise estimation enables dynamic parameter adjustment. Evaluations on benchmark images under varied noise levels show superior PSNR (up to 6.92 dB gain), SSIM (0.7176), and edge PSNR over baselines. Ablation validates key components, and visuals confirm enhanced texture retention. This method advances efficient, high-fidelity denoising for practical applications.

title: Hierarchical Uncertainty-Driven Adaptive Ensemble for Cost-Sensitive Stream Fraud Detection
authors: Fatemeh Moradi, Mehran Tarif, Mohammadhossein Homaei, Oscar Mogollon-Gutierrez, Andres Caro
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Financial fraud detection faces a critical challenge: fraudulent patterns evolve continuously while traditional models remain static after training. This paper presents an adaptive ensemble framework combining incremental learning with hierarchical uncertainty quantification for streaming fraud detection under concept drift. The approach integrates Adaptive Random Forest, incremental SVM, online neural networks, and Bayesian networks with ADWIN-based drift detection and conformal prediction. Evaluated on IEEE-CIS dataset (590,540 transactions) with temporal validation, the framework achieves 92.3% accuracy and F1-score of 0.843 while adapting to distribution shifts within 1,053 samples. Calibrated uncertainty (ECE=0.074) is maintained through dynamic recalibration. The system processes transactions at 8.7ms median latency with 47.5% ROI compared to weekly retraining. Results demonstrate that temporal evaluation reveals 5-8% performance gaps masked by random splits, and incremental adaptation outperforms periodic retraining in cost-performance trade-offs.

title: Zero-Knowledge Access Control for Digital Twin Operations: A Privacy-Preserving Framework
authors: Mohammadhossein Homaei, Fatemeh Moradi, Mehran Tarif, Agustin Di Bartolo, Mar Avila
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Collaborative digital twin (DT) systems have important privacy problems because many stakeholders need to check access without showing credentials or operational data. Normal access control methods can leak user identity and attributes during login, which breaks privacy-by-design rules like GDPR and HIPAA. In this work, we propose a zero-knowledge access control method using zk-SNARKs with circuits specially made for DT tasks. Our system makes 53,000-constraint circuits and achieves 47 ms average end-to-end access control latency on cloud infrastructure and 161 ms on IoT devices, with verification itself completing in under 12 ms and 47 ms respectively. We use a hybrid blockchain-edge setup with Hyperledger Fabric to manage permissions for 10,000 users at the same time, keeping verification time same even when rules are more complex. Speed improvements reduce computing load by 73%. Real use in manufacturing, healthcare, and smart buildings shows 99.99% uptime and no privacy problems for 180 days, proving it works for privacy-sensitive cyber-physical systems.

title: Enforcing Zero Trust at the Endpoint: A Deterministic, Measurable, and Reversible Hardening Model for Microsoft Defender
authors: Ashish Bhatti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Applying One of the main issues in today's cybersecurity environment is the difficulty of applying and implementing Zero Trust principles at the endpoint level. To be successful, such systems require solutions that not only can be deterministic and measurable but also reversible so that they can manage the ever-changing threats effectively. In this work, the authors presented an entirely new Microsoft Defender hardening method that utilizes Hybrid Reinforcement Learning (HRL) along with policy enforcement mechanisms to attain highly detailed security controls that are adaptive as well. The proposed framework employs HRL capabilities to continuously evolve defensive strategies without compromising the strict Zero Trust requirements, which state that every access request must be checked and re-verified endlessly. The idea is to make the hardening component capable of reverse so that the model can safely reverse the enforcement steps, thus lowering the risk of failure and therefore, the system's trustworthiness is increased. The model was put through the test, and the outcome clearly showed that it is capable of identifying and eliminating endpoint threats with a precision of 95.2%, which is a substantial improvement over traditional static hardening methods. The experiments demonstrated the great potential of a combination of reinforcement learning and deterministic enforcement, thus presenting a scalable and transparent Zero Trust solution.

title: Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer for Battery-Less Automated Guided Vehicles using Segmented Magneto-Inductive Transmitter Coils
authors: J. Priyadharshini, S. Mohana Sundaram, M.S. Nandha Kumar, P. Regan Rossario
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The researchers developed and tested a dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) system which provides energy to batteryless automated guided vehicles (AGVs) through segmented rectangular transmitter coils with resonant inductive coupling. The system operates at a frequency of 100 kHz through its transmitter segments that create overlapping magnetic fields which cover the complete motion path. The zero-voltage switching (ZVS) inverter powers multiple 20 cm × 10 cm transmitter coils which use series resonant capacitors for tuning, while the compact 10 cm × 10 cm receiver coil operates without onboard energy storage through its parallel tuning system. The experimental results show that power delivery operates between 10 to 12 voltage and 0.7 to 1 ampere at an air gap distance range of 5 to 7 centimeters, which can power a DC traction motor throughout its operation. The segmented architecture improves current stability and reduces coupling dropouts more effectively than single-coil wireless charging systems, which makes it appropriate for use in industrial AGV applications that require low power.

title: Generative Deep Learning-Based Object Detection for Secure Image Processing Applications
authors: Xin Huang, Hongyu Shi, Ruixue Yan, Ping Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Object detection plays a crucial role in secure image processing applications such as intelligent surveillance, traffic monitoring, and safety inspection. However, traditional deep learning-based detection models often suffer from limited training data, complex environmental conditions, and insufficient robustness against noise or perturbations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a generative deep learning-based object detection framework for secure image processing applications. The proposed approach integrates a generative adversarial network for data augmentation, an enhanced feature detection network with attention mechanisms, and a robust training strategy to improve detection accuracy and stability. The generative module produces synthetic samples to expand the training dataset and enhance data diversity, while the detection network extracts discriminative features for more accurate object localization and classification. Extensive experiments conducted on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several representative detection models in terms of precision, recall, and inference efficiency, showing strong potential for reliable and secure object detection systems.

title: Large Language Models for Software Vulnerability Detection: A Survey of Methods, Evaluation, and Future Trends
authors: Canpeng Li, Yanghui Tan, Jie Zheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently achieved rapid progress and are being increasingly adopted across a wide range of software engineering tasks. Owing to their strong capabilities in code understanding, natural-language alignment, and generative reasoning, they have emerged as a promising development for software vulnerability detection. Based on the current research landscape and 32 representative studies, this article mainly elaborates from four perspectives: research evolution, methodological design, evaluation practice, and future directions, aiming to advance technological innovation and deliver forward-looking insights. Existing evidence suggests that direct prompting enables fast transfer to vulnerability detection, but its effectiveness remains constrained by benchmark quality, insufficient context, prompt sensitivity, and limited robustness. In contrast, context enhancement, program-analysis guidance, retrieval augmentation, specification-guided reasoning, and collaborative decision-making generally yield more stable improvements in recall, F1 score, and explanatory utility. Overall, the development of software vulnerability detection in the next stage is unlikely to be driven by model scaling alone. Instead, it will depend on the integration of high-quality datasets, structured program evidence, explicit security constraints, and verifiable reasoning pipelines.

title: RF Embedded System for Detecting Hidden Wireless Cameras using Signal Persistence and Active Probing
authors: Manoja R, Karthi V, Sudha K, Ashraf Ali I, Dillshaad Singh R, Sivavishnu A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Hidden wireless cameras built using low-cost modules such as ESP32-CAM have become an increasing threat to personal privacy in indoor environments. Existing camera detection techniques, including optical reflection methods and basic radio frequency (RF) monitoring, often produce high false alarm rates and perform poorly in environments with heavy wireless interference. This study proposes a low-cost and lightweight embedded device to locate hidden cameras via hybrid RF detection techniques. The proposed system combines continuous RF scanning, signal persistence analysis, active probing, and GSM-based alert notification to improve detection reliability. The system's performance was evaluated by performing thirty experiments in a variety of indoor environments including small rooms, medium-sized spaces, large halls, residential areas, and RF-rich offices. Results indicated that the method was successful with an overall accuracy of 97.2%, a false positive rate of 2.3% and an ROC-AUC value of 0.94. In addition, the system provides real-time GSM alerts with an average response time of 4.2 seconds. These results demonstrate that the hybrid RF-based approach significantly improves hidden camera detection reliability and can serve as a practical solution for real-time indoor privacy protection.

title: Radar-Communication Physical Layer Security via Fast and Accurate Array Response Control
authors: Jingtai Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Addressing the physical layer security challenges in Dual-Functional Radar-Communication (DFRC) networks, this paper proposes a low-complexity beamforming algorithm based on Complex-Domain Weight Vector Orthogonal Decomposition (CD-WORD). By injecting artificial noise strictly into the null-space of the radar mainlobe and innovatively utilizing the natural sidelobe amplitude as the baseline for communication energy constraints, a precise closed-form solution for the dynamic weight vector is mathematically derived. Simulation results demonstrate that the CD-WORD algorithm can achieve highly precise phase focusing in the communication direction. Compared with conventional Orthogonal Vector Amplification (C-OVA) and convex optimization methods, the CD-WORD algorithm thoroughly eliminates the anomalous sidelobe bulges in the radar beampattern, realizing strict waveform stealthiness and uncompromised sensing capability. Simultaneously, the CD-WORD algorithm successfully eradicates the communication Bit Error Rate (BER) error floor while exponentially reducing the symbol-level computational complexity to a quadratic level, exhibiting tremendous potential for fast and real-time engineering applications in massive antenna arrays.

title: High-Performing Quantum-Safe Password-Authenticated Key Exchange for Mobile Devices
authors: Peixin Ren, Xiaozhuo Gu, Kaibo Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The Password-Authenticated Key Exchange (PAKE) protocol is a useful tool for establishing secure remote communication between users, especially given the prevalence of amplifying a human-memorable password into a strong session key in the age of hand-held devices. Recent advances in quantum computing have stimulated interest in quantum-safe cryptographic schemes. Therefore, we propose QSPAK and QSPPK, two post-quantum and efficient PAKE protocols which are parallel extensions of PAK and PPK, but based on the hardness assumption over module lattices. Specifically, we make a comprehensive trade-off among multiple significant performance indicators such as the post-quantum security, the failure rate, the communication cost and the running time to guide optimal parameter choices. This rigorous instantiation has never been seen in previous PAKE papers. Additionally, utilizing the flexibility of module lattices, we provide three parameter sets (#1, #2, and #3) matching different security scenarios. We implement and benchmark QSPAK and QSPPK against other competitive proposals. The performance results indicate that our QSPAK and QSPPK, besides being more efficient, exhibit the flexibility and security advantages over the latter schemes. Furthermore, we construct a post-quantum password-authenticated TLS 1.3 framework, aimed towards provide a further suggestion for IETF standardization of quantum-safe PAKE.

title: Enabling Frictionless and Continuous Authentication for Edge Computing via Privacy-Preserving Behavioral Modeling_supp1-3688247.pdf
authors: Cheng Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Edge computing has emerged as an important paradigm capable of executing complex tasks at the edge of networks. User authentication, as the common gate of any edge service that attaches importance to security, has become increasingly critical. However, the existing authentication methods encounter challenges in meeting the demands of low-latency services within edge computing due to their intrusive access control. Additionally, the vulnerabilities in edge network security necessitate continuous protection. In this paper, we propose a novel edge behavioral authentication (EBA) scheme, which facilitates a frictionless and continuous authentication process. During user enrollment, diverse smart devices collaboratively establish identity extractors, generating measurable behavioral embedding vectors while prioritizing privacy preservation and cost efficiency. For user verification, a two-step method incorporating individual-level verification and swarm-level verification with a collaborative certification committee (C3) mechanism is devised to enhance authentication performance. Extensive experiments have been conducted, focusing on identity theft issues using two representative behavioral modalities: online transaction behavior and user driving behavior. The experimental results validate the efficiency and security of our proposed scheme.

title: Approximate Distributed Coded Computing: Polynomial Codes and Randomized Sketching
authors: Neophytos Charalambides, Arya Mazumdar
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine
abstract: Coded computing is a distributed paradigm that uses coding theory to introduce redundancy and overcome bottlenecks in large-scale systems. In the same vein, randomized numerical linear algebra employs probabilistic methods to compress and accelerate linear algebraic operations, addressing challenges in high-dimensional data analysis. This article reviews the foundations of both fields and presents distributed schemes that combine techniques from both to speed up optimization and machine learning algorithms, in the presence of slow or non-responsive servers. Along the way, we touch on various related topics and mathematical concepts.

title: Nano-Bio Systems for Intra-body Sensing, Actuation, and Communication in the Terahertz Band and Beyond
authors: Krzysztof Skos, Hadeel Elayan, Samar Elmaadawy, Pawel Kulakowski, Josep Miquel Jornet
publish: IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials
abstract: The terahertz (THz) band and the optical frequency window are emerging as powerful enablers of next generation biomedical technologies, supporting high resolution sensing of biological processes, targeted intra-body actuation, and intra-body communication for coordinated functional operation. Although each modality has demonstrated significant promise individually, research efforts have largely progressed in isolation, limiting the exploration of their potential for integrated deployment. This comprehensive survey bridges these domains by presenting a unified analysis of THz and optical modalities for intra-body sensing, actuation, and communication. Building on this foundation, we articulate a vision for intra-body systems in which multiple functions are seamlessly coordinated at cellular and subcellular scales. Applications such as continuous health monitoring and closed loop therapeutic interventions illustrate the transformative potential of these integrated approaches. We outline a three-stage roadmap covering foundational modeling, in-vitro integration, and in-vivo translation, identifying critical open challenges including compact source generation, power delivery, biosensing miniaturization, and biocompatibility.

title: A Wideband Directional Coupler With Stable Coupling Factor Based on Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Jiawei Zheng, Yuhui Ren, Cheng Xu, Yiqing Li, Yingxi Liu, Handong Wu, Ke Li, Fuwei Wang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a broadband miniaturized directional coupler based on two parallel spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) transmission lines (TLs). The design leverages the intrinsic dispersion and slow wave characteristics of SSPPs to significantly compress the guided wavelength, thereby achieving substantial miniaturization of the device. To balance the phase velocities of the odd and even modes in the coupled microstrip lines, the capacitive parasitic patches are introduced into the coupling region, resulting in a flatter coupling response. Furthermore, the lumped resistors are incorporated between the parasitic patches and the TL to enable flexible control of the coupling factor while simultaneously enhancing the directivity across a wide operating bandwidth. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed coupler achieves a directivity of at least 14dB over a 64% fractional bandwidth (2.64–5.1 GHz), with a coupling coefficient maintained at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10~\pm ~0.9$</tex-math> </inline-formula> dB. Owing to its wide bandwidth, high directivity, compact size, and stable coupling performance, the coupler meets the stringent requirements of modern wireless systems for high-performance components.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)

title: BoxCall AI: An Intelligent Predictive Inventory Management System for Automotive Spare Parts Using XGBoost Regression and Advanced Time-Series Feature Engineering
authors: P. Vasuki, S Vishvavasanth, A Rahul Chandrasekar, S Dhurga, M Tharun Balaji, Y Sowmiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Automotive spare parts have a great deal of variability and are irregularly demanded. Therefore, usual statistical forecasting methods are not very suitable, so businesses often run out of stock, and also have many unneeded spare parts sitting in their warehouses. To help businesses by using artificial intelligence to create a full predictive inventory management system for spare parts, one that uses Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) regression as well as a fixed 17 engineered time-series features as input variables to produce accurate demand forecasts and classified binary (high/low) inventory risk classifications. The system has been developed using the IEEE Indian spare parts dataset, which included over $\mathbf{1 5, 0 0 0}$ product transactions from May 2023 to June 2024, and which was evaluated separately using a different set of product transactions covering the period of July 2024 through January 2025; therefore there is a temporal separation (in time) between the development and evaluation portions of the system. On the independent hold-out test set, BoxCall AI achieved an $R^{2}=$ 0.92, with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.15 units, a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 3.42 units, and an accuracy of 89.3 percent in terms of high or low demand classification. BoxCall AI was found to have relatively superior prediction performance in comparative evaluations against Linear Regression, ARIMA and Random Forest, with a RMSE reduction of $58 \%, 42 \%$ and $30 \%$ for those systems respectively. BoxCall AI’s Flask-based micro-service deployment provides real-time decision support and planning assistance to procurement planners by enabling sub-15 millisecond inference latency, resulting in a projected 67 percent reduction in stockout frequency and a 42 percent reduction in overall aggregate inventory carrying costs for the business.

title: Deep-learning Architecture for Malaria Detection from Blood Cell Images: An Analysis
authors: C Neal Stephen Reon, Ali Hussein Ali Abu Zaid, K. Vijayakumar, K Kanishka, Sorabh Lakhanpal, P. Deepthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Recently, the disease occurrence rate in human community is gradually rising globally and early detection and treatment is crucial. Malaria is an infectious disease caused by plasmodium and needs treatment with appropriate antimalarial drugs. The diagnosis of malaria needs clinical examination using blood samples and blood-cell (BC) examination is necessary.. Malaria can only be analyzed through a comprehensive study of the BC, and to automate this process, a deep-learning (DL) architecture is proposed in this research. Necessary phases of this work includes, image collection from the data repository, implementing DL-models for extracting the features, executing softmax-based classification, identification of best features and generating fused-features (FF) using best two-model features, and implementing the classification with 3-fold cross validation to confirm the merit of the developed system. The experimental outcome confirms that the FF-based approach help to provide $\gt98 \%$ accuracy.

title: Research on Multi-dimensional CIM Dynamic Modeling and Lightweight Fusion Algorithm for Facility Operation and Maintenance
authors: Xuedong Xu, Haibo Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the challenges of massive multi-source heterogeneous data integration and low dynamic iteration efficiency in urban information modeling (CIM) facility operations, this study proposes a lightweight multi-modal fusion algorithm (SSG-LMF) based on spatial semantic graphs (SSG). The approach first formally defines a scenario-process-interaction (SPI) dynamic iteration model, then constructs a unified SSG to integrate BIM, GIS, and IoT data, and designs a lightweight update mechanism incorporating time-decay weights and change significance detection. Urban traffic simulation experiments demonstrate that SSG-LMF significantly outperforms traditional methods and cutting-edge semantic mapping approaches in event detection latency, state estimation accuracy, data throughput, inference time, and resource consumption, effectively supporting the closed-loop operation and maintenance of a digital twin.

title: Eagle-Eye Based Pain and Myocardial Infarction Monitoring and Control System Using Hierarchical Neuro-Physiological Intelligence
authors: K Mohammed Nadeem, U Deepak, K Raghul, S Srihari, W. Nancy, T. Sripriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: The resolution limits of contact-enabled monitoring tools, combined with delicate details in physiology, impede early-stage detection of rising acute pain and myocardial infarction. The anatomical, vascular, and morphological consistency principles are maintained using graph-constrained spectral attention modules for non-contact remote data input that captures features of more complex cardiological diagnostics, while a Vision Transformer-Convolutional model combination enables efficient modeling of longer-range dependencies in macroscopic facial expressions for non-contact micro-expressions of pain and heart attack exclusively associated with ischemia transitions, which are captured using causal dilated temporal convolution modules that eliminate recurring instabilities associated with continuous monitoring. The proposed anatomically and vascularly consistent noncontact cardiac risk assessment approach based on a Vision Transformer-Convolutional encoder combination enables calculation of cardiac risk indicators by combining distant photoplethysmography, face anatomy, and heart temperature data based on multi-modality feature representations, enabling early-stage controlled control activation and infarct prediction in a robust, continuous, real-time monitoring framework that, following simulation testing, outperforms other models to ensure a high level of sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy at over 98%.

title: Research on Railway Track Foreign Object Detection of Lightweight Model Based on YOLOv11 with Fusion Attention
authors: Yafan Shi, Tao Hou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To improve detection accuracy and speed for railway foreign object intrusion under complex environments, this paper proposes SC-YOLO, a lightweight detection algorithm based on YOLOv11 with fusion attention. The model integrates a ShuffleNet V2 backbone, SC-C2PSA attention, a multi-scale fusion upsampling operator (EUCB), and C3k2-OD convolution. Experiments on the RIDD dataset show that SC-YOLO improves mAP by 5.5%, reduces parameters by 0.3 M, decreases computation by 2.4 GFLOPs, and achieves 428 FPS, meeting real-time and accuracy requirements in complex railway scenes. This study supports intelligent manufacturing and automation by enabling efficient and real-time visual inspection in complex industrial environments.

title: An IoT-Enabled Smart Electricity Meter Integrating Advanced Analytics and Voice-Based Mobile Interaction
authors: M. Latha, Mohammed Salman M, Mohammed Zaheer Hussain M
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Modern smart grid networks require precise energy forecasts and monitoring. Real-time analytics, predictive intelligence, and user-centered engagement methods are absent from traditional power meters. The design and development of an Internet of Things-enabled smart power meter combined with sophisticated machine learning analytics and a voice-activated mobile application is presented in this study. To assure measurement accuracy and safety, the suggested system uses an ESP32 microcontroller with a multi-sensor configuration that includes a non-invasive current transformer (SCT-013), an invasive current sensor (ACS712), and an isolated voltage sensor (ZMPT101B). REST APIs are used to send energy data over Wi-Fi to a cloud backend built on Firebase and MongoDB. Several prediction and anomaly detection models, such as Multivariate Linear Regression, Bagging, Stacking, Histogram Gradient Boosting, ARIMA, SARIMAX, and Isolation Forest. Real-time visualization, billing insights, and voice-based interaction using text-to-speech and speech-to-text technologies are all offered by a Flutter-based mobile application. The suggested ensemble-based models beat conventional statistical methods in forecasting accuracy while successfully identifying aberrant usage patterns, according to experimental evaluation using real-world smart meter information. The outcomes validate the efficiency, scalability, and feasibility of the suggested intelligent energy management system.

title: Optimizing Retinal Vessel Segmentation Performance Through a Systematic Comparison of Modified U-Net Variants
authors: Fatima Ali, Bilal Gillani, Waqas Ahmed, Muhammad Anwaar, Shaista Iqbal, Haseeb Iqbal
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy, a serious complication of diabetes, is the primary cause of vision loss in adults. Diagnosing it usually requires examining fundus images, but manually screening large numbers of retinal images isn’t feasible. This challenge highlights the need for automated diagnostic systems to improve efficiency and accessibility. This study introduces a novel UNETbased deep learning framework enhanced by state-of-the-art encoder architectures—EfficientNetB0, VGG19, DenseNet169, and ResNet152—to optimize segmentation performance. A hybrid dataset of FIVES and CHASE DB1 fundus images was utilized, with data augmentation applied to mitigate overfitting during training. The DenseNet169-UNET model achieved exceptional performance, with a segmentation accuracy of $98.6 \%$, a Dice Score of 0.94, and an IOU value of 0.95, demonstrating superior capability in detecting both macro- and microvascular structures compared to traditional methods. A detailed comparison highlighted the balance between processing speed and segmentation accuracy, showing that EfficientNetB0-UNET stands out as a fast, lightweight option for real-time use, providing a scalable tool to enhance diagnostic accuracy and support clinical decisionmaking in ophthalmology and cardiovascular care.

title: High-Precision Positioning Control of Piezoelectric Inertial Driver Based on High-Voltage Sawtooth Wave and Segmented PID
authors: Jinqiang Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In this paper, a high-voltage nanoscale motion platform based on high-voltage sawtooth wave driving force and hierarchical PID control has been realized. A closed-loop driving-detection-control link is composed of a high-voltage power amplifier module(150VDC), a high-resolution grating ruler(with an accuracy of up to 2 nm), and a high-speed ARM processor. At the same time, a multi-level self-tuning PID parameter adjustment algorithm and a multi-level high-voltage sawtooth wave attenuation method are adopted, which can automatically identify the positioning process and adjust the control parameters to reduce the oscillation degree, significantly improving the positioning accuracy and stability. Experimental verification shows that this system can achieve precise positioning at 10 nm, and the stable error it measures is less than or equal to ± one nanometer. This level has reached the international advanced level, providing strong scientific and technological support for the development of high-end precision instruments in China.

title: Quantum-Enhanced AI Framework for Classification of Encryption Techniques in Medical Image ROIs for Secure Transmission
authors: Anindita Das Badhan, Diganth Vijayan R, K Ashwini
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Secure transmission of encrypted medical images demands trustworthy identification of the used encryption method, especially in settings where the availability of metadata is uncertain or unreliable. In this paper, a quantum-enhanced AI framework for the classification of encryption methods used in medical image Regions of Interest (ROIs) is introduced. After the extraction of the ROI and chaotic map-based encryption, a hybrid quantum-classical learning strategy is used to classify the encryption method without depending on any metadata. The proposed quantum AI framework combines variational quantum circuits as efficient latent feature encoders in CNN architectures. The high-dimensional CNN features are then compressed into low-dimensional latent spaces before the quantum embedding process, enabling the classification task to be conducted on the highly compressed feature representation. Quantum-enhanced ResNet18, VGG11, and EfficientNet-B0 models were trained and tested on encrypted ROIs extracted from dermoscopic medical images. The experimental results show that the proposed framework can successfully distinguish between different encryption methods, confirming the feasibility of quantum-assisted cipher classification for secure transmission of medical images. This work shows the potential of quantum-enhanced feature representation in privacy-preserving healthcare applications and automatic decryption routing in medical systems.

title: Graph Contrastive Learning for Function-Level Code Vulnerability Detection
authors: Kunfeng Du
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Source code vulnerability detection is critical for software security. While Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) capture code semantics, existing methods often fail to represent deep semantic and complex structural dependencies. We propose GCL-FVD, a novel function-level detection model based on Graph Contrastive Learning. GCL-FVD first constructs an extended Abstract Syntax Trees to capture program logic. It then employs a multi-branch contrastive framework with graph augmentation and a momentum update mechanism to learn robust representations across views. Finally, a joint optimization loss enhances feature discriminability. Experimental results on public datasets demonstrate that GCL-FVD significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in detecting complex vulnerabilities.

title: Quantum-Ready Education: Preparing Students for Post-Quantum Computing Societies
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid emergence of quantum computing presents unprecedented opportunities and disruptions across cybersecurity, optimisation, artificial intelligence and scientific simulation. As post-quantum technologies transition from experimental research to real-world deployment, education systems must urgently prepare learners with the conceptual foundations, technical competencies and ethical awareness required for a quantum-enabled society. However, most current STEM and computing curricula remain anchored in classical computational models, lacking structured pathways for quantum literacy, quantum-safe cryptography, and future-oriented workforce readiness. This paper proposes a Quantum-Ready Education Framework (QREF) to guide the design of curricula, pedagogical strategies and assessment models that equip students for post-quantum computing environments. Integrating quantum information theory, post-quantum cryptography (PQC), quantum algorithms, and hybrid quantum-classical programming, the framework emphasises competency-driven learning, multi-modal simulation environments, and industry-aligned skill progression. A mixed-methods evaluation across pilot institutions demonstrates measurable gains in conceptual understanding, algorithmic reasoning and PQC awareness, while highlighting challenges in instructor preparedness and resource-intensive simulation workloads. The findings offer scalable pathways for national education systems seeking to build resilient, future-proof talent pipelines capable of thriving in post-quantum computing societies.

title: A Framework on Automatic Speech Recognition Systems Using Natural Language Processing
authors: P. Nagaraj, P. S. Uma, S. Ummugulthum Natchiar, R. Pandi Prabakaran, B. Muralidharan, V. Virumapandi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Since antiquity, speech has been, and will continue to be, the primary form of human communication. Over the past fifty years, research into automated speech recognition by computer systems (Robots) has consistently attracted significant attention for various reasons, from language families to technical curiosity about mechanisms, to the desire to optimise simple tasks through interpersonal contact. It is well recognized that the voice signal carries not only the message's linguistic meaning but also substantial information about the speaker's identity, including gender, age, social background, and place of origin. The inclination to automate routine tasks is not a recent development; it has existed for more than a decade. Alexander Graham Bell, his cousin Chichester Bell, and Charles Sumner Tainter created a recording device in 1881 that used a rotating wax-coated cylinder, allowing a stylus to create up-and-down grooves in response to the pressure of incoming sound.

title: Generative AI-Driven Multi-Vector Phishing, Vishing, and Smishing Emulation with Adaptive Defense Training Framework
authors: S. Ramamoorthi, C. Venkata Jeshwanth, P. Lakshmi Chaitanya Sai, D. Pavan Kumar, M. Mohamed Sithik, S. Priya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Phishing, smishing, and vishing attacks continue to be among the most significant threats to organizational cybersecurity, primarily exploiting human behavior rather than technical vulnerabilities. With the rapid advancement of generative artifi- cial intelligence, attackers can now produce highly realistic and scalable social engineering content, making traditional security awareness training increasingly insufficient. This paper proposes a Generative AI-Driven Multi-Vector Phishing Emulation and Adaptive Defense Training Framework designed to simulate realistic social engineering attacks across email, SMS, and voice communication channels. The framework integrates large lan- guage models for dynamic attack content generation, multilingual scenario customization, and context-aware threat simulation. The system incorporates a closed-loop training model that monitors user interactions and provides adaptive feedback through an AI- driven security coaching mechanism. Experimental evaluation using simulated enterprise scenarios demonstrates significant improvements in user defensive behavior, including increased detection accuracy, reduced click-through rates, and faster response times to suspicious communications. The results indicate that realistic attack simulation combined with adaptive feedback can significantly enhance user awareness and resilience against modern AI-assisted social engineering threats. The proposed framework provides a scalable and cost-effective approach to continuous human-centered cybersecurity training using open- source technologies.

title: The Application of the Improved AES Algorithm in Information Security of Embedded Systems
authors: Xiaoyan Gao, Zhiling Tang, Xingzhuo Meng, Jianfa Ma, Jiayin Liang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved dynamic AES encryption algorithm to enhance security in embedded systems. The fixed S-box in the original AES algorithm exhibits deterministic characteristics, which can be exploited by attackers for feature analysis, thereby weakening the security of data encryption. The proposed algorithm adopts an S-box with 7-bit input and 7-bit output, and introduces a dual-key mechanism: the master key is used to generate round keys, while the auxiliary key is employed to construct a dynamic S-box. The S-box is generated based on the SHA-256 hash function and the Fisher–Yates shuffle algorithm, with dynamically varying reserved bits incorporated into the construction process. By modifying the auxiliary key, the S-box changes accordingly, enabling key-dependent dynamic generation.

title: Real-Time Signal Quality Monitoring and Interference Detection Using RTL-SDR: A Hybrid PSD Thresholding and PSO-Adaptive Line Enhancement Approach
authors: Aejaz Ahmad Ansari, Divyesh Shanmugarajah, Manik Shrivastav, Mutyala Jahnavi Sai, Sirisha Tadepalli
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Real-time spectrum monitoring is a critical requirement in modern wireless environments due to increasing spectral congestion, unintentional emissions, and interference originating from both communication systems and low-cost receivers. Software-Defined Radio (SDR) platforms, particularly RTL-SDR devices, provide an inexpensive and flexible solution for wideband signal monitoring, but they suffer from severe hardware-induced artifacts such as DC offsets, image frequencies, impulsive spectral spikes, IQ imbalance, and non-stationary noise. These impairments significantly degrade the reliability of conventional interference detection techniques based on fixed thresholding or gradient-based adaptive filters.This paper presents a real-time signal quality monitoring and interference detection framework using RTL-SDR that employs a hybrid two-stage denoising architecture. In the first stage, a smart percentile-based Power Spectral Density (PSD) thresholding method dynamically isolates statistically significant spectral components while suppressing broadband noise and impulsive distortions. Hardware-aware artifact mitigation is explicitly integrated to eliminate DC spikes and characteristic ±420 kHz image frequencies inherent to RTL-SDR receivers, which arise due to local oscillator (LO) leakage in the direct-conversion front-end. In the second stage, an Adaptive Line Enhancer optimized using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is applied to enhance correlated and structured signal components while minimizing residual noise. The PSO-based optimization strictly follows the canonical Kennedy and Eberhart update rules, enabling global convergence in non-linear and non-stationary noise environments where traditional LMS-based methods fail.

title: A Multi-Fusion Multi Staged Transfer Learning in Classification of Lung Disease Images with Applied CLAHE Algorithm
authors: Benchie L. Maribao
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This study presents a Multi-Fusion Multi-Staged Transfer Learning framework integrated with Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) for the classification of lung disease using chest X-ray images. A total of 4,862 images obtained from Kaggle were utilized and divided into training (3,403), validation (729), and testing (730) subsets. CLAHE preprocessing was applied to enhance image contrast and improve feature visibility prior to model training. The proposed framework combines MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB4 through feature fusion and employs a three-stage fine-tuning strategy to progressively adapt the model to domain-specific features. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieved a test accuracy of 93.42% with a corresponding loss of 0.1694. Class-level evaluation indicates a precision of 0.9909 for tuberculosis detection and a recall of 0.9983 for normal classification, demonstrating strong reliability and sensitivity. The integration of CLAHE, feature fusion, and multi-stage training significantly improved model performance and convergence stability. The findings suggest that the proposed framework is effective for lung disease classification and has potential applications in computer-aided diagnostic systems, particularly in supporting rapid and accurate screening. The novelty of this work lies in the unified combination of image enhancement, dual-backbone fusion, and progressive transfer learning within a single framework.

title: Ageing Monitoring for Commercial Microcontrollers Based on Timing Windows
authors: Leandro Lanzieri, Jiri Kral, Goerschwin Fey, Holger Schlarb, Thomas C. Schmidt
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Microcontrollers are increasingly present in embedded deployments and dependable systems, for which malfunctions due to hardware ageing can have severe impact. The lack of deployable techniques for ageing monitoring on these devices has spread the application of guard bands to prevent timing errors due to degradation. Applying this static technique can limit performance and lead to sudden failures as devices age. In this paper, we follow a software-based self-testing approach to design monitoring of hardware degradation for microcontrollers. Deployable in the field, our technique leverages timing windows of variable lengths to determine the maximum operational frequency of the devices. We empirically validate the method on real hardware and find that it consistently detects temperatureinduced degradations in maximum operating frequency of up to 13.79% across devices for 60°C temperature increase.

title: A Boundary-Aware Attention Mechanism For Accurate Skin Lesion Segmentation And Classification Using ABR-Net
authors: J.Manoranjini, Rohini C
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Skin diseases can be effectively diagnosed and treated by making an early and accurate diagnosis. Nevertheless, traditional diagnostic techniques typically base their approach on manual dermoscopic examination, which can lead to subjective interpretation and unstable results. To overcome such drawbacks, this paper suggests a two-phase deep learning model that will improve the speed of lesion segmentation and classification. During the initial phase, the attention U-Net, which is aware of boundaries, is used to produce fine lesion masks. The segmentation network combines residual connectivity and attention to precisely reproduce the fine-grained lesion boundary and minimize artifact effects such as hair, noise, and changes in illumination. These generated masks are then added to the initial dermoscopic images to create lesion-oriented inputs highlighting the clinically important areas. The second stage involves using an Efficient Net based convolutional classifier to categorize various types of skin diseases on the segmented region of interest (ROI). Attention modules are also added to the classifier to enable it to learn fine texture and color features in the lesion. Experiments on the HAM10000 dataset show great performance, as it gives a Dice coefficient of 0.935 with segmentation and classification metrics such as 89.2 accuracy, F1-score of 0.872, and macro-AUC of 0.943, which are better than traditional single-stage architecture.

title: AR for Cultural Heritage: Insights from a Moroccan Educational Study
authors: Safaa Bendhiba, Houria Kelkoul, Fatima-Zohra El Aahed, Mehdi Zhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: This article examines how the development of an augmented reality (AR) heritage platform, E-Kasbah, served as a catalyst for the creation of a new university program in heritage and visual arts technologies. Created by Moroccan students pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Audiovisual, Gaming, Design, and Interactive Multimedia, with the aim of documenting and visualizing Morocco’s traditional kasbahs, the project demonstrated technical mastery but also revealed significant gaps in the students’ training, particularly in heritage theory, research methodology, and interdisciplinary design practices. Using a mixed-method approach that included an analysis of the platform’s use, qualitative interviews with the development team, and institutional discussions, this study highlights the pedagogical and structural limitations of existing curricula. The results underscore the need for specialized academic frameworks that integrate immersive technologies into heritage studies. Based on these findings, the article outlines a new academic pathway and proposes an evolutionary model for implementing AR-based heritage education in higher education institutions.

title: Smart Shoe for Gaming: An Integrated Wearable Shoe for Motion-Based Interaction and Physical Activity Enhancement
authors: Satheesh Kumar, Sara Ann Mohan, Muhammed Faheem A K, Hiba S, Muhammed Sadiq K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital entertainment has significantly increased screen time, especially through video gaming. This shift often leads to less physical activity and sedentary lifestyles, negatively affecting both physical and mental health. Too much inactivity can contribute to serious issues like obesity, heart disease, diabetes, and stress. This is especially true for younger generations who depend heavily on digital platforms for fun. To tackle this issue, this project introduces the Smart Shoe. This device is designed to combine physical exercise with interactive gaming, turning passive gameplay into an active experience. The system merges wearable hardware with gaming interfaces to motivate movement while keeping users engaged and entertained. Motion-tracking tools like accelerometers, gyroscopes, microcontrollers, and power modules gather real-time movement data. They convert this data into gameplay input and track steps, distance, and estimated calories burned. By including immersive interaction through motion-based gaming or virtual reality, this solution connects boring exercise routines and enjoyable digital experiences. It promotes the idea of exergaming, where physical activity is part of the gameplay. The design focuses on being affordable, portable, and easy to use, making it suitable for students, gamers, and fitness lovers. Ultimately, the Smart Shoe shows how wearable technology can improve gaming experiences while encouraging healthier habits, lowering the risks of being sedentary, and supporting long-term wellness by merging fitness with entertainment. Index Terms: Smart Shoes, Wearable Technology, Motion Sensors, Gaming Interaction, Health Monitoring, Rehabilitation System.

title: Exploration of Optimizing Graduate Curriculum System Based on the Concept of Personalized Learning Using Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Tingyu Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study aims to address the inadequacy of traditional postgraduate curriculum systems in meeting personalized learning needs. A multi-objective optimization model was constructed, taking individual demand adaptability, curriculum relevance, and resource utilization as core objectives, with constraints on total credits and curriculum type ratio. An improved Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm was proposed, incorporating dynamic inertia weight and adaptive learning factors, and compared with basic PSO and Genetic Algorithm (GA) via MATLAB simulations.
