title: Gen-PBL in Vocational Big Data Education: Exploring Cognitive Offloading Behaviors via Structured GenAI Scaffolding
authors: Shu Chen, Fenghua Liu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The integration of Generative AI (GenAI) into programming education accelerates code production but introduces pedagogical challenges regarding novice schema construction. This exploratory study investigates the behavioral dynamics of human-GenAI collaboration within a Project-Based Learning (PBL) environment, specifically examining how variations in instructional constraints influence learners’ cognitive offloading behaviors. Through a 16-week quasi-experimental design involving 82 vocational college students, we longitudinally tracked two cohorts with divergent prior knowledge baselines as they interacted with GenAI under structured scaffolding (mandatory prompt templates) versus free exploration conditions. Our quantitative analysis suggests that the quality of cognitive engagement outweighs interaction volume. Hierarchical regression shows that prompt engineering quality is a significant predictor of project success (β = 0.42, p < 0.001), while raw GenAI querying frequency is not statistically significant. Furthermore, behavioral log data demonstrates that when structural scaffolds are abruptly removed, novice learners exhibit a distinct regression toward a "trial-and-error" regulation mode—marked by a high-frequency, low-quality querying loop and diminished project performance. These findings suggest that in GenAI-assisted environments, mandatory prompt structuring may serve as an external regulatory script. By intentionally introducing "cognitive friction," this approach compels learners to articulate problem decomposition before automated generation, thereby mitigating dysfunctional cognitive offloading. Based on these empirical observations, the study proposes design principles for preserving germane cognitive load through gradual scaffold fading and the integration of process-oriented assessments in future human-GenAI collaborative programming interfaces.

title: Universal Approach to Measuring Focused Beam Size for Pulsed-Laser SEE Testing
authors: Trevor Crane, Joel M. Hales, Adrian Ildefonso, Dale McMorrow
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: The repeatability and traceability of pulsed-laser single-event effects (PL SEE) testing depends on accurate characterization of the focused beam size. This study introduces a universal approach for accurate beam characterization called image profiling and details the associated acquisition process and analysis pipeline. The approach is capable of measuring sub-micron-sized beams, is compatible with standard PL SEE testing hardware, and requires minimal implementation time compared to other beam characterization techniques. The accuracy of the approach is validated by comparing the results against those from standard characterization techniques. The approach’s universality is assessed by testing on three PL SEE testing systems, two custom-built systems and one commercial system. The ramifications of inaccurate beam size measurements are discussed in the context of correlating PL and heavy-ion data and when interrogating devices with small sensitive volumes.

title: Multi-Point Simultaneous Tactile Force and Location Sensing through Soft Acoustic Waveguides
authors: Yufan Ye, Yuan Lin, Huiming Pan, Xinjun Sheng, Jianjun Meng, Shuo Jiang, Peter B. Shull
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Multi-point tactile sensing is fundamental for dexterous manipulation and wearable human-machine interactions. However, existing paradigms rely on complex and expensive array designs prone to significant crosstalk interference and typically fail to decouple force and location measurements across multiple contact points. Thus, we propose a soft acoustic waveguide with dual-end piezoelectric transducers for multi-point tactile sensing through Waveform-feature Integrated Tactile Sensing that extracts time-frequency features from echo signals and decouples multi-point tactile information. Multi-point contact experiment was performed by indenting at various locations with different depths, covering a working range of 60 mm and an applied force range of 0–1.6 N. The waveguide achieved simultaneous force and location measurements with mean absolute errors of 0.067 N and 1.35 mm, achieving 68% and 42% reductions in measurement errors compared to the latest acoustic waveguide based on time domain echo peak detection. The waveguide enables high-precision multi-point simultaneous tactile force and location sensing, providing fundamental capabilities for dexterous manipulation systems.

title: A Parallel Meshless Voronoi Method for Generalized SurfaceNets
authors: Will Schroeder, David Thompson, Spiros Tsalikis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: SurfaceNets is a powerful visualization technique typically used to contour non-continuous, discrete, volumetric scalar fields such as segmentation label maps. Label maps are ubiquitous to medical computing, biological studies, and materials characterization, used in applications ranging from anatomical atlas creation to nanotechnology analysis. Due to the uniform spacing of volume data, however, representing data with highly variable resolution is challenging. Consequently we have developed a generalized high-performance, parallel SurfaceNets algorithm that processes unorganized, labeled point clouds. Based on a scalable, meshless Voronoi approach, the algorithm independently processes each Voronoi hull in parallel using a hierarchical neighborhood point search metric. By employing novel topological constructs, the resulting meshless tessellation can be readily transformed into a connected conformal mesh, from which multiple, valid contour surfaces can be simultaneously extracted and smoothed. Additional contributions include a general API for locating points proximal to Voronoi hulls; the definition of topological coordinates used to detect and eliminate numerical degeneracies, merge coincident points, rapidly produce the dual Delaunay triangulation, and build smoothing stencils; and the construction of a Voronoi adjacency graph along with associated necessary conditions to ensure the generation of valid tessellations. Characterization of parallel performance is also quantified, including producing Voronoi and Delaunay tessellations of 128 million hulls and more than 750 million tetrahedra. A software implementation is available from the open source the Visualization Toolkit (VTK) system at vtk.org.

title: LSE-FRGCN: A Logical Schema-Enhanced Fuzzy Relational Graph Convolutional Network for Stance Detection
authors: Hu Huang, Li Dong, Jun Ma, Yi Yang, Genan Dai, Bowen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Understanding public attitudes expressed on social media platforms forms the foundation of opinion-mining applications, especially within the consumer electronics domain. Stance detection, as a fundamental subtask, aims to identify the viewpoint or position conveyed in subjective texts with regard to specific targets, such as products, features, or brands. Although there have been notable improvements in traditional stance detection methodologies, accurately interpreting texts with inherently ambiguous positions remains a challenging. Fuzzy logic offers a theoretical framework for representing uncertainty in knowledge. However, existing fuzzy-based and LLM-fuzzy approaches typically assume static and homogeneous uncertainty patterns, and their practical application is further hindered by the need for extensive labeled datasets to extract logical rules, which is unrealistic given the diversity of stance detection targets. Drawing on the strong zero-shot reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs), we introduce a novel architecture termed the LLM-enhanced Fuzzy Relational Graph Convolutional Network with Logical Schema Enhancement (LSE-FRGCN). Unlike prior frameworks that rely on fixed fuzzification schemes, LSE-FRGCN generalizes fuzzy reasoning from static fuzzification to an adaptive and learnable uncertainty modeling paradigm that is conditioned on relational graph structure. This design combines the representational flexibility of large language models with uncertainty-aware relational reasoning for stance detection. Specifically, LSE-FRGCN is structured around two key modules. The first module generates a logical schema graph that abstracts first-order logical rationales into transferable conceptual structures. The second module employs a fuzzy relational graph convolutional network to integrate logical information under adaptive uncertainty modeling. This generalization enables robust stance inference in zero-shot, cross-target, and low-resource scenarios. Experiments show that our method consistently achieves superior results across these settings. In particular, on the challenging zero-shot benchmarks SemEval-2016 and VAST, our approach surpasses the strongest baselines by margins of 5.1% and 5.5%, respectively.

title: IEEE Women in Engineering
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications

title: Rethinking Load Balancing for Inter-Datacenter Networks
authors: Yuan Li, Jiao Zhang, Huimin Luo, Junlong Chen, Yuxiang Wang, Jiafeng Jiang, Tao Huang
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) has become ubiquitous in modern datacenter networks. To accommodate the exponential growth of application demands, the scale of datacenters is continuously expanding, evolving from single- site infrastructures to geo-distributed inter-datacenter environments. This topological evolution imposes significant challenges on load balancing design by introducing novel Multipath Decision Points (MDPs). In this article, we critically review existing load balancing schemes and reveal that current works predominantly focus on intra-DC traffic optimization, largely overlooking the impact of long-haul links on the equilibrium of inter-DC traffic. To address this gap, we propose Strata, a novel architecture that achieves holistic load balancing by decoupling the path selection policies for intra-DC and inter-DC traffic. Strata comprises three core components: the Traffic Classification Module, the Intra-DC Load Balancing Module, and the DCI Load Balancing Module. By decoupling the scheduling logic for these two distinct traffic types, Strata enables fine-grained path selection and high-performance transmission. Evaluation results demonstrate that Strata significantly reduces the Flow Completion Time (FCT) for both intra- and inter-DC flows compared to state-of-theart solutions, effectively mitigating path conflict issues in inter-datacenter topologies. Finally, we provide an analysis and outlook on future trends in inter-datacenter load balancing.

title: Opportunistic Transmission Empowered Rate-Splitting Multiple Access for Military Communications: Approaches and Challenges
authors: Xi-Ran Zhang, Wen-Bin Sun, Zhaolin Zhang, Haochen Liu, Ling Wang, Ang Gao, Wei-Xiao Meng
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) is a promising multiple access (MA) technology that can be used to enhance the performance of military multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. By virtue of its rate-splitting method, this technology can flexibly regulate the interference management strategy, achieving superior spectrum efficiency (SE) compared to traditional MA schemes. It is well-suited to meet the requirements of the modern battlefield, such as secure tactical data transmission, enhanced energy efficiency (EE)-enabled edge computing offloading, and satellite-terrestrial tactical communication. However, due to the complexity of the actual combat electromagnetic environment, it is extremely difficult to obtain perfect channel state information (CSI), which results in performance limitations. The opportunistic beamforming (OBF) technique can only feed back signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) values and utilize a multi-tactical-node selection mechanism to free the system from the constraints of perfect CSI, thereby improving the robustness of the military networks. This article analyzes the advantages brought by the combination of RSMA and OBF technologies, and proposes an OBF-aided RSMA (ORSMA) scheme. The article describes three types of performance improvements that ORSMA can achieve and analyzes current applicable tactical scenarios. Subsequently, we analyze the challenges faced by ORSMA in future combat scenarios.

title: Toward Energy Efficiency in O-RAN: A Digital Twin for Carbon-Aware 6G Networks
authors: Elif Ak, Berk Canberk, Hyundong Shin, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive analysis of energy efficiency and carbon awareness in emerging sixth generation of cellular networks (6G) open radio access network (O-RAN) systems. Guided by the proposed 6S lifecycle model (sense, shrink, sleep, shift, steer, and show), the article identifies key energy efficiency drivers such as artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled control, computational optimization, digital twin, and carbon- aware integration. A central contribution is the in-depth examination of metering methodologies and the standardization gaps stemming from O-RAN's disaggregated, virtualized design, along with a reassessment of its main energy consumption sources. This article also consolidates industrial and standardization efforts by presenting essential key performance indicators and measurement approaches for transparent, accurate, and trustworthy energy and carbon accounting.

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp7-3682403.mp4
authors: Yongchang Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

title: A Novel Causal Robust Soft Sensor for Industrial Processes With Data Distribution Drift
authors: Liang Cao, Yan Qin, Dong Zhao, Bhushan Gopaluni, Youqing Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Modern industrial systems require real-time prediction for critical variables that are expensive or impossible to measure online. Existing soft sensing approaches suffer from catastrophic performance degradation under distribution shifts and sensor perturbations. This study develops a novel framework that bridges causal inference with robust optimization for reliable industrial soft sensing. We propose a causal robust soft sensor (CRSS) framework that employs spatiotemporal filtering to extract Granger-causal features that capture predictive temporal relationships reflecting fundamental process mechanisms. The framework incorporates explicit robustness constraints via regularization that penalizes sensitivity to bounded perturbations. Extensive experimental verification on real industrial refinery control system data demonstrates that CRSS achieves the best predictive accuracy (RMSE <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$=0.42$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}=0.90$</tex-math></inline-formula>). It maintains stable performance under 20% sensor noise with small degradation, and remains robust under complete sensor outages, nonlinear distortions, and heavy-tailed perturbations beyond the Gaussian model. CRSS provides theoretically grounded prediction error bounds, offering a practical solution for industrial soft sensing applications with interpretable Granger-causal insights.

title: DNA-Inspired Helical Microstrip Antenna for Compact Low-GHz Wireless Applications
authors: Silva Deena, Priyanka R, Keerthana S, Mohan C
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This paper presents a planar DNA-inspired helical microstrip antenna that can operate in millimeter-wave frequencies for compact, high-performance wireless applications. The proposed configuration uses a 2D helical strip patterned over a single-layer substrate to form a twisted current path that can effectively increase the electrical length while maintaining a reduced physical footprint. The antenna structure integrates a periodic zig-zag helix that further improves the inductive-capacitive loading and can provide better current distribution along the radiating surface. Electromagnetic modeling and analysis were performed using CST Studio Suite 2025, where the antenna yielded a strong resonance at 25.7 GHz with a measured return loss of approximately -26 dB at the design frequency, reflecting excellent impedance matching. Far-field radiation analysis revealed a peak gain of 9.63 dBi in the φ = 0° plane, as well as stable main lobe formation with acceptable sidelobe levels. Visualization of surface currents further confirms the efficient excitation of the helical strip and uniform flow of power along the meandered DNA-inspired pathway. In view of its planar profile, structural simplicity, and strong radiative performance, the proposed antenna is a good candidate for compact millimeter-wave modules, short-range high-density wireless links, biomedical telemetry nodes, and next-generation integrated communication platforms.

title: Modified Swastik Shaped Terahertz Patch Antenna
authors: Deepa Thangarasu, N. Saranya, G.P Sabtha Sree, D.Riduvarshini D. Riduvarshini, B.Rathimeena B. Rathimeena, P.Poojasri P. Poojasri
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Wireless communication systems require high data rates, low latency and improved reliability to support emerging technologies such as extended reality, autonomous vehicles, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Sixth-generation (6G) networks are expected to utilize the terahertz (THz) frequency spectrum (0.1–10 THz) to meet these demands. In this work, a modified Swastik-shaped graphene-based microstrip patch antenna operating in the THz band is designed and analyzed. The antenna is implemented on a polyimide substrate and simulated using CST Microwave Studio over a frequency range of 0.1–10 THz. Multiple resonant frequencies are observed at 2 THz, 3.9 THz, and 5.05 THz with corresponding return loss values of −11.2 dB, −25.6 dB, and −18.7 dB, respectively. The antenna maintains a VSWR below 2 across the 2–10 THz range, indicating good impedance matching. Furthermore, the antenna demonstrates stable radiation characteristics with a gain ranging from 4 to 7 dBi and high radiation efficiency at higher frequencies. These characteristics make the proposed design suitable for compact THz communication systems in future 6G, security scanning, and spectroscopy applications.

title: HERMES: An FPGA-Based Multi-Cipher HSM for Resource-Constrained IoT Systems
authors: Adithi Ambatipudi, Samarth Nadig, Sravanthi Nallajonna, Areeb Khan, Vadiraja Acharya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments has increased the demand for security mechanisms that provide low latency, scalability, and flexible deployment of cryptographic algorithms at the network edge. Software-only cryptographic solutions on devices with limited resources increase performance and energy costs. In contrast, fixed-function hardware options, such as ASIC-based hardware security modules (HSMs), lack the flexibility to adapt to diverse and evolving IoT environments. This paper introduces HERMES (Hardware-Enabled Reconfigurable Modular Edge Security), a centralized multi-cipher FPGA-based Hardware Security Module built on the PYNQ-Z2 platform for on-site cryptographic offloading in resource-constrained heterogeneous IoT networks. The system integrates AES-128, SIMON, and PRESENT as AXI4-compliant cryptographic IP cores that support parallel execution and batch processing. This design allows for efficient offloading of cryptographic tasks from low-power micro-controller-based IoT nodes while maintaining centralized key management. The experimental evaluation shows clear performance and resource trade-offs among the supported ciphers in a centralized HSM architecture. Lightweight ciphers reduce the complexity of the datapath, but their system-level overhead depends on the session management frequency and batching behavior. Under continuous batch-processing workloads, the proposed system runs steadily, with average CPU usage staying below 15% and memory usage below 50%. These results confirm that HERMES offers a scalable and flexible hardware-assisted security solution that can support diverse, resource-limited IoT edge deployments.

title: Advancing Crop Yield Forecasting with Random Forest: Enhancing Accuracy and Sustainability in Agriculture
authors: V Rajesh, M. Mathankumar, N Nagendra Rakesh, M Sreevarsan, Ganeash Vasanth, S Saravanan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Crop yield prediction has a potential to become an essential component of agricultural systems, which will enable optimization of food security, maximum production and sustain- able resource management. There are high degrees of complexity and variability in the agricultural environment and, thus, crop yield prediction by conventional methods is complex and leads to suboptimal results. Lately, the trend has been growing for the use of ML algorithm for crop yield simplification. Random forests are shown as one of the most effective methods for this task. Random Forest method is used for overcoming the overfitting problem which is typically present in some machine learning methods and to improve prediction accuracy on large datasets. In this paper, we discuss the random forests based on decision trees for crop yield prediction. Random Forest models combine also predictions from many decision trees in order to overcome the overfitting problem. This research investigates how Random Forest could transform the way we predict agricultural production through its strength and robustness as a framework for agricultural research. Random Forest has already been shown to be extremely effective across many different fields, making it an ideal candidate for addressing the challenges associated with predicting crop yields to promote and improve sustainability and efficiency in crop production. The results presented in this paper represent significant progress in predicting the productivity of crops, as they provide useful information to scientists, policymakers, and others working in the area of agriculture.

title: Agentic Artificial Intelligence Framework for Automated Adverse Drug Reaction Detection and Analysis
authors: Venkata Sivaranjani Vedanabhatla, N.Krishnaraj
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs) remain one of the leading causes of patient morbidity, mortality, as well as growing healthcare cost across the world. The existing pharmacovigilance systems rely mostly on spontaneous reporting systems and manual and expert-based analytical processes, thus being limited in their scalability, timeliness, and breadth. The rapid growth of real-world data stores such as electronic health records, adverse event databases, and patient-generated drug reviews necessitates deployment of intelligent automation in order to develop an effective drug-safety monitoring. In this paper, we present a proposal of a framework by means of Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) that aims at supporting the automated identification, validation, and description of ADRs. The architecture is designed in terms of a modular, multi-agent system that includes Data Collection, ADR Extraction, Signal Detection, Validation, and Comparative Analysis agents. All the agents perform autonomous and specialized duties, but as a centralized body of ADR knowledge. The models of natural language processing are used to identify drug-ADR relationships in textual data that lacks structure, and statistical signal-detection methods are used to find nascent safety signals in structured databases, such as FAERS. The validation processes uses maintained repository of knowledge repositories, especially, SIDER, to increase reliability and reduce false-positive identifications. The framework allows the continuous monitoring, provides explainable outputs and it can be deployed at scale. According to the results of empirical studies, agentic AI is capable of significantly increasing the accuracy of ADR detection, minimizing the cost of operations, and enhancing patient safety. This study highlights the suitability of agentic AI systems as a new generation paradigm of intelligent pharmacovigilance.

title: Contrastive Regularization for Spectral Graph Networks in Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Chaima Khemiri, Wissem Inoubli, Mohamed Farah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) remains challenging due to the intrinsic nature of hyperspectral imagery (HSI): high spectral dimensionality, strong within-class variation, and limited annotated pixels. Recent Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)-based studies show that making better use of spectral structure can significantly improve representation quality, and many existing models introduce contrastive objectives to strengthen this behavior.In this work, we integrate a lightweight Centroid Contrastive Learning Loss (CCL) module into a spectral-graph GCN framework providing a simpler and more stable alternative. The graph is built at the pixel level using a Gaussian spectral affinity, a common strategy for modeling relationships in hyperspectral data. The GCN then propagates spectral information across this graph, while the CCL term encourages embeddings to move closer to their class centroid and remain better separated from other classes.Experiments on the Indian Pines and Pavia University datasets show that this GCN+CCL formulation reduces overfitting and produces better-structured latent representations compared to a standard spectral GCN, confirming the benefit of a centroid-driven metric constraint in HSIC.

title: Predictive Maintenance Framework for Utility-Scale Solar Farms Using Wireless Networks, UAV Inspection, and Robotic Cleaning
authors: Kaovinath Appalasamy, Ajith Gopi, R Mamat, Irina Kirpichnikova, S. Shanmuga Priya, Sudhakar Kumarasamy
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of utility scale solar photovoltaic installations has rendered traditional manual operations and maintenance practices increasingly inadequate. Vast geographical footprints, remote locations, and the need for rapid fault detection demand a fundamental shift toward data driven, autonomous management strategies. This paper provides a systematic review of three core technologies enabling this transformation which are LoRaWAN based wireless connectivity, unmanned aerial vehicle inspection, and autonomous robotic cleaning systems. The wireless communication layer employs LoRaWAN to support long range, low power data transmission from distributed sensors that track module temperature, electrical output, and environmental conditions. This enables near real time visibility of system performance across extensive solar farms. The aerial inspection layer utilizes unmanned aircraft vehicles (UAVs) equipped with thermal and optical sensors to identify defects such as hotspots, interconnection failures, and diode faults. These inspections significantly reduce survey duration while improving fault localization accuracy. Robotic cleaning systems address soiling losses through automated, water efficient mechanical cleaning that preserves energy yield and reduces labor costs. Together, these components form a coordinated maintenance system that shifts operations from reactive intervention to planned and condition-based management. The study synthesizes current capabilities and identifies deployment challenges. The proposed approach improves reliability, reduces manual effort, and supports stable long term energy generation in utility scale solar farms.

title: Learning Adaptive Rules and Confidence-Based Engine for Finding Security Incidents by Pattern Correlation
authors: Khushi Bhargava, Metilda Florence
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The Security Operation Centers (SOCs) handle a broad range of security logs, which can increase alert fatigue for the staff. Traditional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems use static correlation rules, which tend to be outdated and often produce many false positives, while machine learning methods can act as black boxes and be computationally expensive.This paper presents an adaptive and transparent correlation system. We present a technique that learns probabilistic correlation rules from enterprise logs and assigns risk scores to individual sessions using a model that groups entities into confidence levels. The system designs a log format standardization process for various log sources, combines events into user-host sessions with a fixed time duration, and uses association rule mining with sliding time windows. The proposed scoring metric combines rule confidence, lift, severity, and temporal aging to measure correlation strength in a limited and transparent manner.Transparency is one of the key features of the proposed system. The technique can be implemented efficiently on a CPU-only system and does not depend on black-box predictive models. The system is tested on the CERT Insider Threat Dataset, Threat Dataset v6.2, and the results show a dramatic reduction in alerts while maintaining a conservative detection level in the scenarios used for validating proxies. The results obtained show that adaptive learning of rules with confidence correlation can be a viable alternative to traditional SIEM correlation engines and opaque systems for the detection of anomalous activity in an enterprise SOC.

title: MST-DeepLabV3+: An Integrated Deep Learning System for Real-Time Satellite-Based Flood Extent Mapping With Production-Grade Deployment
authors: A. Princy Arul, M. Keerthika, S. Kowshikan, Shahbaaz Ahmed, R. Mohammed Asif Fawaz, S. Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Rapid and accurate flood extent mapping is critical for disaster response and resource allocation, yet traditional manual interpretation requires 7-10 days post-event. This paper presents MSTDeepLabV3+, a production-ready deep learning system combining Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical imagery for automated flood detection with uncertainty quantification. The proposed architecture integrates DeepLabV3+ encoder-decoder structure with Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) and Squeeze-and-Excitation (SENet) channel attention mechanisms, enhanced by ImageNet transfer learning. Evaluated on 2,310 satellite tiles across five major flood events (Pakistan 2022, Kerala 2019, Germany 2021, Thailand 2011, Indonesia 2020), the model achieves 95.4% Intersection-over-Union (IoU), 96.1% precision, and 92.5% recall significantly outperforming U-Net (82.2% loU) and baseline DeepLabV3+ (89.1% IoU). Novel contributions include: (1) systematic integration of SENet attention into decoder pathways for multi-spectral flood segmentation, (2) transfer learning framework achieving 12.2 % improvement over training from scratch, (3) Bayesian ensemble predictions providing pixel-level confidence intervals for risk-aware decision-making, and (4) complete production system with REST API, interactive dashboards, automated damage as sessment, and continuous learning infrastructure. Real-time inference achieves 185 ms per 256x256 tile on GPU, enabling processing of continent-scale satellite imagery. Morphological post-processing combined with Conditional Random Fields reduces false positives by 34 % while maintaining 95.1 % IoU. Deployment across three platforms (Google Cloud Run, AWS Lambda, Docker containers) demonstrates production-grade robustness. This work demonstrates operational viability of deep learning for disaster response, reducing assessment time from 7-10 days to under 5 minutes per event with quantified uncertainty for critical infrastructure decision-making.

title: Inverting the Cross-Modal Experience of a Soft Haptic Display for Virtual Touch Interactions at a Science Festival
authors: Femke E. van Beek, Irene A. Kuling
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Visual displays present XR users with a virtual world, but haptic displays make this virtual world tangible and thus more interactable. The first generation of haptic displays were based on rigid feedback technology, but soft (pneumatic) haptic displays are now becoming more ubiquitous. We previously designed such a display and take it to science festivals to test the public’s opinion about haptics. In our first experiment at a science festival, participants showed quite consistent patterns when tuning the actuation parameters of our soft device to match visual images. In the current experiment, we tested the inverse paradigm at another science festival, by presenting 87 participants with the 13 preferred haptic stimuli from our previous experiment. For each haptic stimulus, we asked participants to choose the matching image from a set of three. Participants performed significantly above the chance level, with a score of 51±14%. Nonetheless, a direct comparison of our previous and current results shows no significant linear relationship between the two experiments. This indicates that the current experiment was inherently different from the previous one. Our results show that it is possible to find a matching image, when presented with a haptic stimulus on our device, but that the paradigm matters. Secondly, they indicate that large-scale experiments at science festivals can be used for basic perceptual research, such as comparing perceptual paradigms and testing cross-modal asymmetries. Finally, they underline that the multi-sensory experimental design is crucial for targeting the intended experience.

title: Research on Multi-Dimensional Learning Behavior Modeling and Grade Prediction Based on Transformer
authors: Yan Zhou, Jun Zhou, Xin Gao
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Traditional teaching evaluation methods, which primarily rely on final examination scores, struggle to comprehensively capture students' multi-dimensional competency development in blended learning environments. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a teaching evaluation prediction system based on the Transformer model. The system integrates student data across five dimensions: online learning behavior, classroom participation, assignment scores, project collaboration, and final examination grades. By employing a multi-head self-attention mechanism to model the nonlinear dependencies among these features, the system achieves accurate prediction of students' academic performance. Experiments were conducted using real learning data from 100 students, partitioned into training, validation, and test sets at a ratio of 7:1.5:1.5. Results demonstrate that the model achieves a prediction accuracy of 88% on the test set, with a mean squared error of 0.022. The correlation coefficients for both training and test sets approach 1, validating the model's strong fitting capability and generalization performance. Furthermore, the system provides teachers with real-time feedback on student learning progress and supports personalized teaching decision-making, facilitating a shift from outcome-oriented to process-oriented teaching evaluation. This research demonstrates that the Transformer-based evaluation prediction model can effectively enhance the precision of teaching assessment and provide data-driven technical support for personalized learning interventions in blended teaching environments.

title: Semantic Intention-Driven Service Composition in SOA: A Context-Aware Case Study in Smart Buildings
authors: Emna Fki, Ismael Bouassida Rodriguez
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Smart building environments require service-composition mechanisms that remain aligned with user intentions while adapting to rapidly changing contextual conditions. Classical SOA-based compositions, typically driven by static I/O matching or predefined schemas, are unable to guarantee stability, comfort, and responsiveness when daylight, occupancy, device availability, or sensor reliability fluctuate. This paper introduces a semantic, intention-driven, and context-aware dynamic composition approach tailored for smart buildings. The method combines (i) enriched intention graphs embedding comfort bands, equipment capabilities, and uncertainty profiles, (ii) weighted semantic matching between intentions and abstract services, (iii) multi-source context-aware semantic affinity for coherent chaining, and (iv) a hysteresis-enabled conditional refinement mechanism ensuring stable decisions under fluctuating environmental conditions. We define a comprehensive evaluation protocol based on realistic HVAC–lighting scenarios, including daylight transitions, glare episodes, thermal load variations, and sensor imperfections. Our results demonstrate that localized recomposition—guided by context validity predicates and fragment-level QoS propagation—significantly improves responsiveness, stability, and comfort compared to static or partially contextual baselines. The proposed approach provides a scalable and dependable foundation for adaptive cyber-physical orchestration in smart building settings.

title: Research on the Investigation and Improvement Path of College English Teachers' Digital Intelligence Literacy Based on the Use of Digital Intelligence Platform
authors: Weirong Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the context of the in-depth promotion of educational digital intelligence transformation, digital intelligence platform has become the core carrier driving the transformation of college English teaching paradigm, and the improvement of teachers' digital intelligence literacy is the key premise to release the teaching efficiency of the platform. Taking 32 college English teachers as the research object, this paper uses the questionnaire survey method to explore the use characteristics of teachers' digital intelligence platform, the status of digital intelligence literacy and the promotion dilemma, constructs a four-dimensional digital intelligence literacy evaluation model of "technology application, teaching integration, data governance, and safety ethics", and puts forward targeted improvement paths. The study finds that teachers' utilization rate of basic digital intelligence platform is high (such as Chaoxing Xuexitong 90.63%), but the application of high-order functions was insufficient; digital intelligence literacy is characterized by an imbalance of "up to standard basic ability and weak high-level ability", and data governance ability (3.59 points) is the core weakness; the main constraints are the imperfect training system, insufficient platform adaptability and imperfect guarantee mechanism. Through empirical data support and path optimization, this study provides theoretical reference and practical paradigm for promoting the transformation of digital intelligence in college English teaching and improving the quality of teachers' professional development, and enriches the empirical results of teachers' digital intelligence literacy research in the disciplinary context.

title: Digital Era Insights into Online Technical Readiness and Teaching Effectiveness: The Mediating Role of Teaching Presence
authors: Yuxin Jiang, Yuxia Du, Yuli Li, Xuemin Gao, Daoming Fu, Mei Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Under the dual imperatives of "digital transformation in education" and the "normalization of blended learning," online teaching has developed into an integral component of elementary education. However, effectively organizing online classrooms, sustaining student attention, and ensuring the quality of instructional interaction have emerged as pressing practical challenges for online teachers. To identify factors affecting the effectiveness of online teaching for primary school teachers and improve teaching quality, we developed a model that reveals the relationship between online teaching technical readiness, teaching presence, and teaching efficacy based on literature and survey research. We analyzed data collected from 1,647 primary school teachers in China using SPSS. The data showed that online teaching technical readiness positively predicts online instructional efficacy and presence, with the latter partially mediating the effect of the former on online teaching efficacy. These findings have practical implications for improving teacher professional development and enhancing the abilities necessary for successful online teaching among primary school teachers, trainers, principals, and researchers.

title: ICEIT 2026 TOC
authors: N/A

title: AI-Empowered Heterogeneity Response in Remote Classrooms: A Qualitative Study on the Mechanism of Human-AI Collaborative Assessment and the Construction of a Dynamic Ecological Model
authors: Bin Chen, Hui Sun
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As remote classrooms have demonstrated a pivotal role in promoting educational equity and supporting lifelong learning, how to effectively respond to learners’ heterogeneity in large-scale teaching has become a core challenge in improving educational quality. Addressing this issue, this study adopted the grounded theory method to explore the dynamic process of how teachers and AI collaboratively respond to student heterogeneity in remote classrooms. We conducted semi-structured interviews with 33 frontline teachers experienced in AI-assisted remote teaching. Through the hierarchical coding of raw data, the study constructed an AI-assisted heterogeneity response strategy model for remote classrooms. The model reveals that effective heterogeneity response is a dynamic circular system with "Human-AI Collaborative Assessment and Judgment" as the value conversion hub: Intelligent technology perceives and diagnoses students’ differences through multimodal data; on this basis, teachers integrate professional experience and situational insights to complete the translation from data to educational understanding; furthermore, through multi-dimensional and dynamic intervention strategies covering content, interaction, process, and structure, the assessment and judgment are put into teaching practice. The entire process relies on a systematic digital ecology as the supporting environment, with the fundamental goal of promoting students’ subjective development such as thinking autonomy, sense of identity meaning, and comprehensive agency. This study not only provides a systematic theoretical framework for understanding how teachers, students, and technology collaboratively respond to heterogeneity in remote classrooms in the intelligent era but also offers practical paths for educational practitioners to improve teaching quality in terms of heterogeneity diagnosis, strategy application, ecological cultivation, and evaluation reform.

title: AIGC-Driven Intelligent Teaching Transformation of Urban and Rural Planning Design Courses
authors: Song Huifang, Pemg Yuling, Wang Xiaopeng, Bai Shiyi, Li Jun, He Tiantian
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Against the backdrop of rapid advancements in artificial intelligence, this study focuses on the core computer technologies of AIGC (including large language models, generative adversarial networks, knowledge graph construction, and multi-modal data fusion algorithms) to drive the intelligent transformation of urban and rural planning design course teaching. Based on the technical characteristics of AIGC such as efficient content generation, multi-modal output, and data-driven iteration, this paper first optimizes three key technical modules: 1) the planning case knowledge graph construction technology (improving entity recognition accuracy to 92.3% through BERT pre-trained model fine-tuning); 2) the "drawing-model-data" parametric linkage system (reducing data interaction latency by 40% via Python-based real-time communication protocol optimization); 3) the AIGC contextualized task generation algorithm (enhancing task relevance by 65% through multi-round dialogue fine-tuning of GPT-4). This study not only enriches the technical application system of AIGC in the field of planning education but also provides a feasible technical path for the intelligent transformation of similar design courses, ultimately improving teaching quality and students' technical application capabilities.

title: IEEE Standard for Low-Rate Wireless Networks--Amendment 1: Ascon Cryptographic Algorithms
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: This amendment specifies modifications to the IEEE Std 802.15.4 medium access control (MAC) specification to add Ascon cryptographic algorithms.

title: Real-Time Diagnosis and Intervention of Teaching Behaviors Driven by Machine Learning in New-Engineering Classrooms
authors: Wei Guo, Zeyu Yan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: China’s New Engineering programming courses lose one fifth of first year students because instructors cannot spot the seven-minute silent confusion cycle that unfolds while they face the IDE. We trained an edge compute system on thirty thousand labelled frames extracted from one hundred twenty hours of video recorded in ten courses with two hundred eighty four students. A pruned YOLOv8 Transformer ensemble running on a four gigabyte Jetson Nano detects eight micro behaviors such as keyboard silence head pose drift and IDE error bursts at twenty five frames per second within forty milliseconds. When the posterior probability of confusion or distraction exceeds nought point seven for three consecutive frames the board flashes a three level cue on the instructor s secondary monitor and pushes a four line AST matched code hint into each student s IDE. An A B A experiment with one hundred eight novices showed immediate gains. Eye movement entropy fell from three point nine zero to three point one eight bits and quiz accuracy rose from seventy two point seven per cent to eighty four point six per cent. Four weeks after withdrawal retention remained seven point three percentage points above baseline. The bill of materials is two hundred seventeen euros per classroom. The dataset and Jetson image are released under MIT License for three hundred room scale up.

title: Intensity Discrimination of Electrocutaneous Haptic Feedback Is Enhanced at Low Frequencies and with Repeated Exposure
authors: A.R. Citterman, R.J. Urban, L.M. Nygard, A.E. Stewart, M. Teramoto, M.R. Brinton, J.A. George
publish: IEEE Transactions on Haptics
abstract: Electrocutaneous (EC) stimulation is a promising modality for noninvasive tactile feedback. Increasing the pulse frequency of EC stimulation has been shown to increase the perceived intensity in a nonlinear way. However, it is unclear why the relationship is nonlinear and how the nonlinearity impacts the theoretical number of discriminable sensory gradations that can be evoked by EC stimulation, as would be necessary to effectively convey the magnitude of tactile interactions. To address this, we systematically quantify just-noticeable differences (JNDs) and corresponding Weber fractions (WFs) through three psychophysical experiments in healthy adults to elucidate the perceptual resolution and mechanisms for frequency-based sensory encoding. First, we quantify the JNDs and WFs at 25-, 50-, 75-, and 100-Hz reference points and provide quantitative data supporting prior work that suggested intensity discrimination is worse at higher pulse frequencies. Second, we demonstrate that perceptual learning is frequency dependent; intensity discrimination at 50 Hz improved with greater exposure through additional JND experiments, whereas no improvement was observed with repeated exposure at 100 Hz. Third, by isolating frequency discrimination from intensity cues using pseudo-randomized pulse amplitudes, we show that temporal discrimination is impossible at higher frequencies. This suggests that temporal cues present at lower EC pulse frequencies contribute to enhanced perceptual sensitivity at lower frequencies. These findings serve as a quantitative benchmark for intensity discrimination across the working range of EC pulse frequencies, and provide new evidence that enhanced perceptual sensitivity at lower frequencies is likely due to the learnability of supplemental temporal cues. These findings highlight the importance of documenting the experience level of participants when reporting discriminability and may be used to optimize EC haptic feedback systems.

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp2-3682403.pdf
authors: Yongchang Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

title: Bidirectional Prototype-Reward co-Evolution for Test-Time Adaptation of Vision-Language Models
authors: Xiaozhen Qiao, Peng Huang, Jiakang Yuan, Xianda Guo, Bowen Ye, Chaocan Xue, Ye Zheng, Zhe Sun, Xuelong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Test-time adaptation (TTA) is crucial in maintaining performance of Vision Language Models (VLMs) when facing distribution shifts, particularly when the source data or target labels are inaccessible. Existing TTA methods predominantly leverage the output probability distribution of CLIP for feature evaluation, resulting in biases under domain shifts, which cause misclassified features due to text priors or incorrect textual associations. To address these issues, we propose <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</u>idirectional Prototype-Reward co-Evolution (BPRE), a novel VLMs framework with TTA that integrates feature quality assessment with prototype evolution via a synergistic feedback loop. First, the Multi-dimensional Quality-aware Reward Module (MQRM) is designed to evaluate feature quality and guide prototype refinement precisely. The continuous refinement of prototype quality via Prototype-Reward Interactive Evolution (PRIE) enhances the computation more robust. Through this bidirectional interaction, the precision of rewards and prototype evolution mutually reinforce each other, forming a self-evolving feedback cycle. Extensive experiments conducted on 15 diverse recognition datasets demonstrate that our model consistently achieves superior performance compared to other SOTA methods, and advances VLM generalization capabilities through emphasizing comprehensive feature evaluation.

title: Hybrid Modeling and Simulation for Urban Mobility: A Systematic Review and Future Research Agenda
authors: José Da Silva Ferreira Junior, Josiane Palma Lima, Alexandre Ferreira De Pinho
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates the relationship between Hybrid Modeling and Simulation (HMS) and urban mobility research through a systematic literature review conducted in accordance with the PRISMA protocol. This review synthesizes applied studies from the last five years, indexed in the Scopus database, to to map the current state of research on HMS in urban mobility. Based on the identification, screening, and eligibility criteria, 55 studies were selected and analyzed using a combined bibliometric approach and in-depth qualitative analysis, considering six urban mobility domains and four central research questions. The findings indicate that while convergence between these fields remains nascent, signs of maturation are emerging, particularly in studies employing empirical data and practical model applications. Recurrent challenges were identified, including the lack of terminological standardization for HMS, the inherent complexity of hybrid models, and limitations related to computational validation, issues that are also highlighted in the existing literature. Conversely, the analysis revealed a consistent set of research opportunities grouped into ten thematic axes: data enhancement, methodological integration, paradigm expansion, and emerging applications in complex urban environments. This study contributes by providing a consolidated overview of the state-of-the-art and outlining promising directions for future research at the intersection of these fields.

title: Unlinkable and Decentralized Credential Management for Agentic AI
authors: Qi Liu, Peng Jiang, Jun Zhu, Shaolong Tang, Wei Wang, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The evolution toward agentic AI is transforming edge devices from passive sensors into autonomous agents capable of collaborative decision-making. However, the open interaction among these agents exposes the system to malicious intrusions, making robust authentication essential to validate legitimate agents. Traditional authentication mechanisms still suffer from sensitive information leakage due to the potential exposure of agents' real-time locations and session linkability. Furthermore, previous privacy-preserving techniques often employ a centralized manager to prevent information leakage, whose compromise breaks the security of the whole system. In this paper, we propose B-CM, a blockchain-based credential management mechanism for agentic AI. B-CM augments traditional authentication by integrating anonymous credentials with blockchain technology to enable unlinkable and auditable agent authentication in a decentralized manner. To optimize efficiency, we leverage off-chain computation performed by edge servers and fast on-chain verification, accompanied by credential aggregation. We implement B-CM over Hyperledger Fabric to evaluate its performance. Experimental results show that the off-chain computation remains highly efficient, requiring only 8.35 s for distributed key generation and 9.7 ms for credential issuance, while the on-chain computational overhead is nearly negligible.

title: Fine-Grained Fashion Feature Embedding for Global, Local, and Contextual Analysis in Fashion Social Media Popularity Prediction_supp1-3694039.pdf
authors: SHUN OKAMOTO
publish: N/A

title: Tensor Product-Based Fractional Order Sliding Mode Control for Biological Pest Regulation in Agroecosystems
authors: Arsit Boonyaprapasorn, Parinya Sa Ngiamsunthorn, Suwat Kuntanapreeda, Kraisak Phothongkum, Thunyaseth Sethaput
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Sugarcane is a key economic crop in subtropical and tropical regions, serving as a major raw material for the food and biofuel industries. However, its cultivation is severely affected by the sugarcane borer, a destructive pest. To mitigate this issue, biological control strategies—using natural enemies to regulate pest populations—offer an environmentally friendly alternative to chemical pesticides. This study proposes a tensor product–based fractional-order sliding mode control (TP-FOSMC) approach to formulate an effective biological control policy for a nonlinear pest–parasitoid agroecosystem. The controller design utilizes tensor product model transformation (TPMT) to facilitate the synthesis of the fractional-order sliding mode controller, which enhances system robustness and significantly reduces chattering in the control inputs. System stability is verified using the Lyapunov stability theorem. Numerical simulations under bounded disturbances demonstrate that the proposed TP-FOSMC effectively regulates both pest and parasitoid populations to their desired levels. Compared with the state-dependent Riccati equation (SDRE) and tensor product–based conventional sliding mode control (TP-SMC) strategies, the proposed method achieves faster convergence and substantial chattering reduction, confirming its robustness and practical feasibility for sustainable pest management.

title: A Multi-Faceted Performance Evaluation of Metaheuristic Optimization Techniques in Radial and Meshed Distribution Networks
authors: Anu Kuriakose, S. Balamurugan, R. R. Lekshmi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Directional overcurrent relay (DOCR) coordination, a nonlinear constraint optimization problem, is highly important to the reliability of the power distribution system. Five metaheuristic algorithms namely, Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Adaptive Differential Evolution (JADE), Moth-Flame Optimization (MFO) and Walrus Optimization Algorithm (WaOA) are compared on three benchmark networks of increasing complexity namely, a 5-bus radial feeder, a WSCC 9-bus mesh system, and the IEEE 33-bus distribution system. A multi-faceted assessment model is used with five objective functions, three standard IEC time-current characteristic (TCC) curves and two user-defined (UD1 and UD2) formulations. UD1 deploys a single set of non-standard optimized constants across the relays, while UD2 uses independently optimized shape constants for each relay. Each algorithm is compared under an identical computational budget, based on 30 independent runs, and performance measures are reported using descriptive statistics, Friedman and Wilcoxon signed-rank tests, convergence analysis, and verification of the coordination margin. The findings imply topology-related behavior in the algorithm's performance: JADE is observed to be statistically consistent in the radial system, whereas GA is most dominant in the mesh and large-scale systems in terms of total operating time across all topologies. It is also observed that the full range of standard IEC curves with realistic TMS limits are not suitable for 33-bus system. This indicates that user-specified TCC curves are not merely beneficial but a requirement for the coordination of medium-scale distribution systems. The UD2 feature yields the shortest operating time for any system topology, and the optimized coefficients give physically interpretable trends that flatten as the network depth increases. These findings offer statistically confirmed principles for the protection engineer in choosing algorithms, the types of curves they need, and relay hardware to achieve effective coordination in current distribution networks.

title: Vector-Guided Embezzler: A Reconstruction Attack Against Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Agentic AI Communication
authors: Lishuang Hu, Hao Wang, Tao Xiang, Zhuo Chen, Jialing He, Shangwei Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Agentic AI systems increasingly rely on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to ground autonomous decision-making in external knowledge. They become critically dependent on distributed, often third-party, infrastructure — introducing new vulnerabilities at the intersection of embodied intelligence and networked communication. While prior work has examined privacy risks from end-users, we identify and formalize a more systemic threat: the LLM API provider itself acting as a malicious adversary within the agentic ecosystem. In this paper, we introduce a new threat model where the LLM API provider is the malicious adversary and demonstrate this vulnerability. Specifically, we propose a novel three-stage attack, Embezzler, to reconstruct the private knowledge base of a RAG system. The proposed Embezzler is to first infer the embedding model of the RAG system and use this surrogate model to prob new, non-redundant text chunks within the embedding space, rather than the semantic space. Then, we systematically explores and refines the embedding space to maximize the amount of unique chunks reconstructed with minimal adversarial queries. We theoretically analyze the effectiveness of our Embezzler and conduct extensive experiments under various system settings. Our results show that our Embezzler consistently and significantly outperforms four baseline methods and reconstructs nearly 90% of the RAG knowledge base. A component-wise ablation study further validates the critical role of each stage. Our findings highlight the severe privacy risks of deploying RAG systems with third-party LLM APIs in Agentic AI communication.

title: Heat Transfer Modeling of MHPA-Cooled Electrical Machines Using Modular 3D LPTN with Parameter Calibration
authors: Kun Wang, Bo Ma, Sheng Huang, Ji Zhang, Gang Lei, Shuo Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The application of advanced cooling technologies has promoted the development of high-performance motors, but also imposes challenges on thermal analysis. In response to this demand, this paper proposes a method aimed at improving the accuracy and efficiency of motor thermal analysis. The research focuses on a micro heat pipe array (MHPA)-integrated efficient cooling motor with an asymmetric structure. First, a three-dimensional lumped parameter thermal network (3D LPTN) is established based on the modular cell segmentation method, and the key thermal parameters in the model have been derived. After evaluating the correlation between different parameters and motor thermal performance, a calibration approach is employed to adjust the parameters’ initial values by using the optimization algorithm. Finally, the prototype is fabricated and tested, both numerical simulations and experiments validate the method’s accuracy for temperature prediction under different working conditions, which comprehensive confirms the effectiveness of this method.

title: BurnDC: A Progressive Propagation Framework for Low Coverage Depth Completion_supp2-3693984.mp4
authors: Bin Fan
publish: N/A
abstract: The growing adoption of compact and cost-effective solid-state LiDARs has greatly advanced robotics. However, their inherently limited Field-of-View (FOV) hinders their application in tasks requiring wide-range depth perception. To overcome this limitation, we introduce the Low Coverage Depth Completion (LCDC) task, which aims to generate a full-scene depth map from a low coverage sparse depth map and a corresponding RGB image, and propose a tailored framework named BurnDC. BurnDC progressively expands the propagation frontier around reliable depth anchors via Progressive Depth Burn (PDB) and utilizes Weighted Ring Attention (WRA) to inject stable geometric context into boundary regions, achieving a controlled refinement and completion of the entire depth map. To evaluate LCDC, we construct a real-world solid-state LiDAR based benchmark, LC-TIERS, and simulate low coverage settings in NYUv2 and KITTI. Experimental results demonstrate that BurnDC significantly outperforms existing methods in low coverage scenarios, with an RMSE reduction of 10-20% over top competitors. Our work provides a promising solution for unlocking the full potential of solid-state LiDARs in various applications. Code available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yudmoe/burnDC/</uri>.

title: Adaptive Image Batching and Slicing in Edge Networks for Delay-Critical Small Object Detection
authors: Haodong Zou, Jianxiong Guo, Suping Sun, Yuzhu Liang, Changfu Xu, Yong Zhao, Shu Zhao, Tian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Delay-critical small object detection is crucial for real-time applications, such as autonomous driving and aerial surveillance. While cloud-based approaches incur substantial transmission latency, edge-based detection can avoid such delays; however, it faces its own challenges: limited computational capacity at the edge often leads to unsatisfactory inference delays and low accuracy when employing lightweight models. To tackle these issues, this paper presents an adaptive image batching and slicing framework for delay-sensitive small object detection in distributed edge environments. We first formulate the problem as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming model, which is NP-hard. The proposed scheme then aggregates tasks with tight deadlines into batches processed locally or cooperatively across the edge network, thus meeting stringent timing constraints. For tasks with relatively relaxed deadlines, we introduce an adaptive image slicing strategy that preserves fine-grained pixel information to boost detection accuracy without violating deadline requirements. The framework is implemented on NVIDIA Jetson edge devices and evaluated using real-world datasets. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by an average accuracy gain of 0.5%, while improving the task success rate by 19%.

title: Millimeter Wave Inverse Pinhole Imaging
authors: Akarsh Prabhakara, Yawen Liu, Aswin C. Sankaranarayanan, Anthony Rowe, Swarun Kumar
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: High-resolution radio frequency (RF) imaging relies on large antenna arrays or Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR). It is challenging to enhance aperture without significant hardware complexity and cost of arrays, or high resolution motor encoder needs of SAR. We introduce Umbra, a high-resolution imaging system built using just a single millimeter-wave (mmWave) and rotating “inverse pinholes” to enhance angular resolution. This paper presents the imaging system model, design, and evaluation of mmWave inverse pinholes. The inverse pinhole is attractive for its lightweight nature, which enables low-power rotation. As they rotate in the field of view, they implicitly leave signatures in RF measurements that can be used to track pinhole location to sub-wavelength precision even on cheap, low end DC motors without encoders. We finally also show how ordinary rotating objects, such as fans can act as natural mmWave inverse pinholes. One can opportunistically upgrade a single antenna system just by carefully positioning such objects in the antenna’s field of view. Our evaluation shows Umbra resolving up to 2.5° with just a single antenna, a 5× improvement compared to 14° from a multi-antenna baseline.

title: Explicit Spatial-Angular Pattern Embedding for Heterogeneous Imaging-Oriented Light Field Spatial Super-Resolution
authors: Yeyao Chen, Chongchong Jin, Haiyong Xu, Ting Luo, Gangyi Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Light field (LF) imaging often faces a severe trade-off between spatial and angular resolution due to limited sensor capacity. Although various LF spatial super-resolution (SR) approaches have emerged, they often struggle with blurred textures and degraded parallax structures at large upscaling factors (e.g., 8×). Heterogeneous imaging aims to addresses this by leveraging supplementary 2D high-resolution (HR) information; however, effectively bridging 2D and LF data for angular-coherent high-frequency transfer remains challenging. In this paper, we propose a novel learning-based framework using explicit spatial-angular pattern embedding for heterogeneous LF spatial SR. Specifically, the proposed method first develops a pseudo-LF generation and implicit alignment module to establish the correspondence between the 2D HR image and the low-resolution (LR) LF image. Subsequently, local spatial and global angular pattern estimation strategies are designed to exploit the intrinsic LF geometry. Based on this, a spatial-angular pattern-embedded LF feature enhancement module is constructed to generate detailed LF texture features, thereby enabling high-fidelity SR LF image reconstruction. To handle excessive resolution gaps, a progressive restoration scheme is introduced, incorporating weight-sharing across different upscaling scales to fully exploit the network’s learning potential. Experimental results on multiple benchmark datasets show that the proposed method generates super-resolved LF images with sharp spatial textures and accurate angular parallax, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in both objective evaluation and subjective quality. Our code is available at https://github.com/YeyaoChen/LFSSR-ESAPE.

title: Magnetoelectric Sensor with Sensitive Unit Based on Composite Structure of Magneto-sensitive Elastomer and Polyvinylidene Fluoride for Rotational Speed Detection
authors: Benxiang Ju, Song Li, Benyuan Fu, Qi Mai, Bing Lv
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Rotational speed sensors are critical for operational monitoring and fault diagnosis across various domains, including automotive engineering, industrial machinery, and aerospace. This letter presents a sensitive unit composed of a magneto-sensitive elastomer (MSE) and polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), hereafter referred to as the MPSU. Owing to the excellent magneto-sensitive properties of MSE, the PVDF component within the designed MPSU generates a piezoelectric effect when the MSE was exposed to a magnetic field. By processing this output signal, the rotational speed can be accurately determined. Dynamic magnetoelectric performance tests and speed detection experiments were conducted to thoroughly evaluate its sensing capability. Experimental results indicate that, in contrast to conventional Hall sensors, the proposed MPSU delivers highly accurate rotational speed detection while operating independent of external power supply. This design offers a promising pathway for deploying rotational speed sensors in energy-constrained environments.

title: Trust-Aware Intrusion Detection Framework for AI-Augmented Vehicular Digital Twin
authors: Khyati Raiththa, Mohammad Shabaz, Gyanendra Kumar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: The driving experience has been enhanced through intelligent content processing, adaptive streaming and personalized interactions, as the increasing usage of artificial intelligence (AI) in vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication has advanced. Nonetheless, the more intricate intrusions, aggressive manipulation, and data exfiltration can be witnessed using these devices because of the augmented attack surfaces created by this growing intelligence. The traditional intrusion detection systems often lack the dynamism to adapt dynamically to the complex, and volatile threat environment that is linked with the V2V systems augmented with the artificial intelligence. This research paper integrates the adaptive learning methods and the trust evaluation models to introduce a Trust-Aware Intrusion Detection System (TA-IDS) designed for Vehicular Digital Twin (VDT) environments. The proposed system generates dynamic trust scores based on the constant monitoring of user interaction patterns, usage of the application, and communication patterns of devices. These ratings are subsequently used to commence the adequate security actions. TA-IDS helps ensure the violation of fraud is known early without affecting the performance or user experience of the device through a lightweight engine of anomaly detection and a trust management layer. As per the findings, TA-IDS maintains real-time operating efficiency and enhances accuracy of the intrusion detection by 39.0 percent and false positives by 27.0 percent as compared to traditional methods. This paper provides a defense architecture on the AI-enhanced vehicular communication that can be scaled and effectively defended by explaining the importance of incorporating the trust-awareness vehicular digital twin ecosystems today.

title: VOLE-PDRAA: An Efficient Privacy-Preserving Data Retrieval Protocol with Anonymous Authorization Based on Vector-OLE
authors: Zuodong Wu, Dawei Zhang, Mianxiong Dong, Kaoru Ota
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) aims to enable the free flow of personal data while enhancing individual control. Integrating privacy-preserving data retrieval methods can provide stronger protection for personal privacy. However, existing approaches lack compliance mechanisms aligned with the GDPR, making it difficult in practice to simultaneously satisfy the principles of lawfulness and data minimization, while also exhibiting clear limitations in both security and efficiency. To address these problems, we propose VOLE-PDRAA, an efficient privacy-preserving data retrieval protocol with anonymous authorization based on the Vector-OLE (VOLE). Specifically, VOLE-PDRAA constructs a VOLE-blinded identifier by integrating pseudorandom linear encoding with VOLE-derived correlation vectors, enabling rigorous anonymity guarantees during authorization. Building on this, the protocol incorporates a non-interactive zero-knowledge proof (NIZK) to achieve anonymous authorization for the data subject and to generate verifiable informed consent proofs, thereby meeting the principle of lawfulness. Meanwhile, the data controller can verify whether each retrieval request falls within the scope authorized by the data subject without learning any identifiable information, thus maintaining adherence to the data-minimization principle in a post-quantum environment. Furthermore, VOLE-PDRAA utilizes labeled private set intersection (labeled-PSI) to safeguard the confidentiality of identifiers and their associated records under post-quantum security conditions, while enabling large-scale batch retrieval. Our protocol takes a comprehensive security analysis within the Universal Composability (UC) framework. Experimental evaluation validates its superiority through comparison with state-of-the-art work.

title: A Robust and Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Early Lung Cancer Risk Prediction using Synthetic Data Augmentation and Cross-Dataset Validation
authors: B Rajani, K Varsha, K Srinivas, N Sivamani, K Tanveer Ahmed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Early detection of lung cancer significantly improves patient survival rates; however, machine learning models developed for early lung cancer risk prediction often suffer from limited generalization and lack of interpretability due to small, imbalanced, and institution-specific datasets. This study proposes a robust and explainable machine learning framework that integrates synthetic data augmentation and cross-dataset validation to enhance predictive performance and transparency. To address class imbalance and data scarcity, a generative adversarial network-based tabular data synthesizer (CTGAN) is employed to generate high-quality synthetic samples while preserving statistical properties of the original dataset. Multiple machine learning classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Logistic Regression, are trained on both original and augmented datasets. Model robustness is evaluated using cross-dataset validation to assess generalization capability across heterogeneous data distributions. To ensure clinical transparency, explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques such as SHAP are applied to quantify feature importance and provide case-level explanations. Performance is assessed using accuracy, AUC-ROC, precision-recall metrics, calibration curves, and generalization gap analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that synthetic augmentation improves minority class detection and reduces overfitting, while explainability analysis enhances trustworthiness and interpretability of predictions. The proposed framework contributes a systematic approach to improving reliability and transparency in early lung cancer risk prediction, making it applicable to clinical decision support systems in reality.
