abstract: With the large-scale development of renewable energy, liquid metal batteries hold broad application prospects in grid-scale energy storage, but the inevitable inconsistency among individual cells in series battery packs severely limits their overall performance. To address the prevalent issue of slow equalization speed in traditional equalization technologies for liquid metal batteries, this paper proposes a novel hierarchical equalization topology and designs a corresponding rapid equalization strategy. This strategy employs a hierarchical approach for equalization current distribution, which effectively enhances the objectivity and rationality of current allocation within the topology. To verify the feasibility of the proposed topology and its control strategy, a twelve-cell simulation model was established in MATLAB and Simulink, utilizing the state of charge as the equalization variable to conduct experiments under static conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional equalization strategies, the proposed hierarchical strategy increases the equalization speed by 43.75 %. Simultaneously, compared to traditional single-inductor topologies, the novel topology improves the equalization rate by 36.52 %. This study fully verifies the feasibility of the improved topology and its equalization strategy, providing significant reference value for subsequent practical design and engineering applications of liquid metal batteries.

title: Influence of PCB Structural Layout on Solder Joint Stress of Components
authors: Cun Zhao, Xiaolei Xu, Ming Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To investigate the influence of PCB structural layout on the solder joint stress of components, three PCB layout examples were provided. The mechanical response of the PCB under vibration loading was simulated using finite element analysis, focusing on key parameters such as modal characteristics, deformation, and stress on chip solder balls. The simulation results indicate that uneven screw distribution in conventional layouts can lead to stress concentration in the chip solder balls. Compared to the conventional layout, the optimized PCB scheme designed with uniformly distributed screws and centrally arranged chips achieved a 36% increase in the first natural frequency, a 46.5% reduction in maximum deformation, an approximately 81.8% decrease in maximum stress on chip solder balls, and about an 87.3% reduction in the standard deviation of solder ball stress. This work demonstrates that optimizing PCB layout significantly reduces maximum stress on chip solder balls, improves force uniformity on the chips, and indirectly enhances the reliability and lifespan of PCB components.

title: A Self-Supervised and Explainable Framework for Zero-Day Attack Detection and Mitigation in Windows Firewall
authors: S. Vasudevan, P. Gowtham Sai Saketh, U. Suraj, K. Boopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Signature-based firewall mechanisms remain a primary defense in many networks; however, their reliance on predefined signatures makes them ineffective at detecting previously unseen threats. This paper presents an intelligent security framework that augments the native Windows Firewall with a self-supervised learning approach for detecting and mitigating zero-day attacks. The proposed system learns normal traffic behavior directly from Windows pfirewall.log records without requiring labelled attack data. Masked feature prediction and contrastive learning are used to generate traffic embeddings, while a temporal representation module captures sequential communication patterns across connections to reveal stealthy attack behavior. These representations are evaluated using a Local Outlier Factor-based anomaly detector to identify suspicious activities. When anomalous traffic is detected, the framework automatically generates mitigation rules that can be applied by the firewall in real time. SHAP-based feature attribution provides interpretable explanations for blocking decisions, while a rule-conflict management component ensures compatibility with administratordefined policies. Firewall log integrity and forensic traceability are preserved through AES-256-GCM encrypted archives. Experimental evaluation on sixty days of firewall logs containing approximately 1.9 million raw entries processed into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 6, 6 0 0}$</tex> temporal windows demonstrates strong detection capability, achieving 91.5% precision, 93.1 % recall, and an AUC-ROC of 0.967 while maintaining low computational overhead which is suitable for real-time deployment.

title: Triple band Split Ring Resonator Integrated Yagi Uda Antenna for LTE, ISM/WLAN and S Band Application with Multiparameter AI based Performance Prediction
authors: Niketh K A, Suraj Panayanchira Sunilkumar, Abhinav K, Prasad Jones Christydass Samuel
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This work presents the design and AI assisted performance prediction of triple band Yagi Uda antenna. The antenna is integrated with split ring resonator for achieving multiband. The antenna operates for LTE, ISM/WLAN, and S-band applications, and is fabricated on a FR4 substrate. The antenna operates in the frequency range of 1.95–1.99 GHz, 2.34–2.61 GHz, and 2.94–3.00 GHz. The VSWR is maintained below 2, the gain is maintained above 1 dBi and directivity is above 4 dB in the operating region. The driven element, reflector and director is having a optimized dimension of 0.46λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, 0.54λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, and 0.08–0.45λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, respectively. To reduce the computational cost, random forest regression model is used with 11 geometrical parameters to predict the S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and frequency range. Feature important analysis indicates the substrate width (SW) as the dominant influencing parameter for both frequency and impedance characteristics. The proposed methodology integrates the metamaterial antenna design with high accuracy AI based predication for efficient antenna optimization.

title: Dynamic permission extension control and lightweight data lineage tracking for enterprise R&D departments
authors: Jiale Fan, Minyong Yin, Dewen Kong, Yubing Duan, Guoxin Zhong, Yulong Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the rapid digitalization and cloud migration of enterprise R&D collaboration, the sharing and circulation of sensitive documents across departments and domains have become increasingly frequent. Traditional access control models, such as Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), often exhibit response latency and audit blind spots in real-time authorization and behavior tracing. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an event-driven dynamic authorization extension and lightweight data lineage tracking framework specifically designed for enterprise R&D environments. The proposed framework leverages the Drools rule engine to automate permission propagation and revocation, while integrating metadata-based watermarking and Neo4j graph structures to construct replica-level traceability chains. This approach enables bidirectional synchronization between access decisions and lineage updates, ensuring consistent coordination between access control and provenance management. Experimental results from a Nextcloud-based prototype using a workload derived from the CERT v4.2 dataset show that the proposed framework reduces average authorization latency from 8.5 ms (ABAC baseline) to 3.8 ms, improves lineage reconstruction accuracy from 81.2% (log-based baseline) to 95.7%, and decreases CPU utilization and storage overhead compared with metadata-lineage and blockchain-based provenance schemes, thereby validating its efficiency and practicality for secure R&D data management in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

title: Comparative Analysis of Model Interpretability in Diabetes Prediction: A Shap-Based Study of Ensemble and Distance-Based Algorithms
authors: R Ramya, K. Kavitha
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a severe health concern in public health, and thus, the predictive models that can be used should be precise and interpretable to enable clinical use. This paper compares four ML models, which are Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, XGBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbors on the Pima Indians Diabetes data. Through SHAP (SHapley Additive explanations) we can assess the importance of clinical features across all models like Glucose, BMI, Insulin, and Age. It has been found that SVM was more accurate (76.62%), with KNN followed closely by (66.23 percent). In all models, Glucose was always the most significant predictor, but the prioritization of the secondary features differed: tree-based models displayed BMI, and distance-based models were more concerned with Insulin. The presented results emphasize the need to address the gaps by incorporating a set of explainable AI frameworks in the context of healthcare diagnostics so that predictive performance could be accompanied by clear reasoning. The research shows the applicability of SHAP in the validation of model behavior and leading clinicians to the reliable AI-based support of decisions.

title: An Uncertainty-Aware Attention-Based Residual U-Net for Cochineal Insect Segmentation
authors: Adel Benali, Rahma Fourati, Imen Jdey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The quantification of uncertainty in image segmentation tasks has become essential to improve the reliability and applicability of computer vision systems. Two main approaches emerge: the standard training of the model and the integration of uncertainty directly into the loss function during training. This comparative study evaluates these two methodologies on the ResAtt-SE-UNet architecture for scale segmentation (Dactylopius opuntiae), a destructive pest of the prickly pear cactus. The experiment uses our original Tunisian database, comprising 451 annotated cactus images. The results show that the approach integrating uncertainty during training significantly improves accuracy by 4.19% (from 69.73% to 72.66%), while maintaining comparable performance in terms of Dice (66.78% against 66.77%). In the agricultural context, this approach offers better calibration of predictions and enhanced explainability, thanks to XAI analysis. The measured epistemic uncertainty (0.0099, 0.0505) indicates a high confidence of the model, while the random uncertainty (0.0490) mainly reveals ambiguities inherent to agricultural data. These results highlight the importance of integrating uncertainty quantification from the training phase for applications in precision agriculture, particularly in automated pest detection, where the reliability of predictions is paramount.

title: Solar Photovoltaic Glare: Impacts and Mitigation Across Aviation, Transportation, Agriculture, and the Built Environment
authors: Nisha Kaur, M.R. Mohamed, Cristina Efremov, Tahta Amrillah, Mohd Fairusham Ghazali, K. Sudhakar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) systems are rapidly expanding to meet renewable energy demands, but specular reflections from panel surfaces can produce glint (momentary flashes) and glare (sustained bright light). These glare phenomena pose safety and operational challenges across multiple sectors. This paper reviews the mechanisms of PV glare, quantifies its impact on aviation (pilots and air traffic control), transportation (road and rail users), agriculture (crop and livestock effects), and the built environment (residential and commercial areas), and evaluates mitigation strategies. Drawing on regulatory guidelines, glare simulation tools, solar geometry analysis and case study evaluations, we demonstrate that while glare risks are real, they are predictable and manageable through site-specific design, anti-reflective coatings, orientation adjustments, and screening. The results indicate that PV glare can cause visual discomfort and potential safety concerns in aviation and road transportation, while in agricultural settings it may influence crop microclimates and worker visibility. In the urban environment, glare could impact nearby buildings. The importance of glare in the planning of PV installations with sensitive receptors is emphasized in the study. Recommendations for best practices, mitigation options and future research are provided.

title: Addressing Distribution Shifts in Strength Behavior Prediction of Coarse-Grained Soils Through Model-Agnostic Sample Reweighting
authors: Ying Zhang, Ziying An, Xiaoxin Gao, Yuangeng Zhu, Yao Lu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Coarse-grained soils are widely employed in geotechnical engineering due to their favorable compaction and load-bearing properties. However, accurately modeling their strength behavior, typically characterized by the deviatoric stress–axial strain (q−ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf>) relationship, remains challenging under real-world conditions, particularly when training and testing data exhibit distribution shifts. Existing deep learning approaches often rely on the independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) assumption, limiting their robustness and generalization to out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios. To address this limitation, we propose Model-Agnostic Sample Reweighting (MASR), a novel framework that integrates sample reweighting and invariant learning via bilevel optimization. In this approach, the inner loop optimizes the model on weighted samples, while the outer loop adjusts these weights to enhance generalization under OOD conditions. This iterative mechanism enables the model to progressively identify invariant and causally relevant features while suppressing spurious correlations. Empirical evaluations on real-world coarse-grained soil datasets demonstrate that MASR outperforms baseline models in predictive accuracy under significant distribution shifts.

title: Enhancing Human-Robot Connection in Space Teleoperation through Shared Mental Model Creation
authors: Luisa Mayershofer, Nesrine Batti, Ajithkumar N. Manaparampil, Anne Köpken, Florian S. Lay, Xiaozhou Luo, Adrian S. Bauer, Rute Luz, Daniel Leidner, Peter Schmaus, Thomas Krüger, Neal Y. Lii
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Previous research in human-robot-interaction in teleoperation suggests that human-robot teams with a Shared Mental Model (SMM) are able to achieve better task performances and lower the mental workload of the user. To create a SMM, the robot must be able to estimate the task model of the user and incorporate knowledge about the user into its user model. Most existing concepts use probabilistic methods or symbolic reasoning frameworks, with predefined features for estimating the user’s teleoperation goal. They are therefore highly dependent on a-priori knowledge about the task, thus limiting their flexibility. Only very few approaches mention or explicitly take into account knowledge about the individual user and lack adaptability to human behavior and user preferences. Machine Learning (ML) based concepts, on the other hand, are more flexible to adapt their SMM during the user interaction, but require a lot of data points for precise estimation. In this paper, we propose a new approach to address these limitations by combining the advantages of symbolic and subsymbolic methods, and dividing our estimation of the user’s teleoperation goal into subgoals next to the overall task goal. Furthermore, by integrating the knowledge about the individual user and human decision-making into the prediction of the user’s task goal, we can improve SMM creation of human-robot teams for teleoperation. Our approach is applied to an example space exploration task in a planetary surface habitat by a telerobotic team. The SMM implementation is described and analyzed, followed by an outlook on future development and validation.

title: Bridging the Academia-Industry Gap: A Competency-Based Pedagogical Framework for Computer Science Talent Cultivation
authors: Rui Zhou, Tianyi Wang, Jiayi Nie, Zhiye Wang, Hao Yan, Anping He, Wenbo Chen, Qingguo Zhou
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: A significant competency gap persists between traditional computer science education and the rapidly evolving demands of the tech industry. This paper introduces a competency-based pedagogical framework, grounded in the CC2020 competency model, designed to bridge this gap. Our approach began with a diagnostic survey that confirmed computer science student deficiencies across the core competencies of Knowledge, Skills, and Dispositions. In response, we implemented an integrated, project/problem-based curriculum featuring three targeted interventions: a restructured teaching plan, embedded academia/industry-oriented open-source projects, and student-led collaborative sessions. The framework's efficacy is supported by a powerful dual validation. Internally, end-of-course evaluations revealed high student satisfaction and self-reported competency growth. Externally, students achieved tangible successes, including winning prestigious industry competitions. This study provides robust evidence for our approach, offering a replicable model for academic institutions to cultivate computer science talents. By systematically embedding competencies into the learning experience, this framework equips students with the robust capabilities needed to excel in complex, real-world challenges.

title: Personalized Learning Pathways for Students with Special Needs Using Adaptive Learning Analytics
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Students with special educational needs (SEN) require individualized instructional approaches due to diverse cognitive, behavioural, and sensory profiles. Conventional digital learning systems often lack the responsiveness needed to adapt content, pacing, and scaffolding to each learner’s unique needs. This study introduces an adaptive learning analytics framework designed to generate personalized learning pathways for SEN learners, including those with autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, and mixed learning disabilities. The framework integrates multimodal data sources—interaction logs, eye-movement patterns, speech and affect indicators, and task-performance metrics—to construct dynamic learner profiles that evolve in real time. Using knowledge-tracing algorithms and reinforcement-learning models, the system tailors instructional sequences to optimize cognitive load, engagement, and skill mastery. An evaluation with 326 SEN students across inclusive and special-education environments demonstrates significant gains in learning efficiency, task completion, and sustained attention when compared with traditional learning platforms. Qualitative insights further reveal improved motivation, reduced anxiety triggers, and stronger learner autonomy. The proposed framework advances personalized education by combining explainable AI, universal design for learning principles, and ethical data-governance practices. Findings highlight the potential of adaptive learning analytics to deliver more equitable, responsive, and effective learning pathways for students with special needs, and outline directions for future large-scale deployment.

title: Barrier-Engineered Drain-Adaptive Thyristor 1T-DRAM for Disturb Suppression
authors: Hyeon-Seo Do, Chae-Ryang Heo, Sung-Woong Chung
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Capacitorless 1T-DRAM is attractive for high-density memory, but its implementation is limited by the write/disturb trade-off, sneak current, and array-level IR-drop/RC delay. Here, we propose a barrier-engineered drain-adaptive thyristor 1T-DRAM in a 4F² cell. An array configuration utilizing a ground source plate is proposed to suppress IR drop. More importantly, the adaptive barrier gate dynamically modulates the internal barrier, lowering it for write/read operations while maintaining a high barrier in disturb/sneak states. The proposed operation is explained from a device-physics perspective in terms of capacitive coupling, barrier-pinning, and depinning. TCAD simulations demonstrated that the disturb current was reduced by several orders of magnitude with the adaptive barrier gate at Vd = 1 V. These results provide a route toward low-voltage, disturb-immune, and array-scalable capacitorless DRAM.

title: Centrally Built-in Power-Terminal Matrix Transformer for Vertical Power Delivery in LLC DCX
authors: Tongrui Sun, Wenjie Chen, Panming Li, Wei Zhou, Jiarun Xie, Xingwei Huang, Kangping Wang, Xu Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: In low-voltage, high-current LLC-DCX converters, vertical power delivery (VPD) tightly couples power terminal placement with transformer geometry, constraining achievable current density and scalability. This paper proposes a centrally built-in power-terminal (CBiPT) matrix transformer, in which the power terminals are embedded within the transformer to reduce external PCB power routing. The proposed structure supports both horizontal and vertical matrix extension, enabling high turns ratio and high current density while maintaining a compact footprint. The proposed structure is designed, optimized, and thermally analyzed, based on which a 48 V–0.75 V LLC-DCX prototype is built. The prototype achieves a peak efficiency of 94.2%. Under immersion liquid cooling, it delivers up to 800 A at full load, corresponding to a current density of 1.12 A/mm².

title: Modeling Coincident Peak Pricing in Electricity Markets: Challenges and Peak Shaving Effectiveness
authors: Qian Zhang, Sadie Zhao, Lucy Diao, Conleigh Byers, Yiling Chen, Derya Cansever, Le Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Markets, Policy and Regulation
abstract: Coincident Peak (CP) pricing is widely used in U.S. electricity markets to allocate capacity and transmission costs. This paper develops a behavioral game-theoretic framework for CP-driven load shifting that couples a nonlinear cost-allocation model with day-ahead (one-shot) and real-time (sequential-learning) decision processes. We examine two update rules, namely best-response dynamics (BRD) and fictitious-play dynamics (FPD), across continuous and finite action spaces to quantify how flexibility, action resolution, and participation influence peak outcomes. Using ERCOT peak-day data, we find that FPD reliably reduces system peaks, whereas BRD is more variable and can increase peaks under tight-capacity conditions. Finer action resolution improves peak shaving, while the number of participants is largely neutral when aggregate flexibility is fixed. Meanwhile, information-provider signals can induce herding, whereas response-aware or diverse signals improve peak shaving. These results highlight both the potential and limits of CP pricing: smoothing information and enabling granular control are as important as the amount of available flexibility. The framework offers practical guidance for system operators and consumers: For ISOs, broadcasting smoothed CP signals and setting minimum controllable-capacity thresholds enhance coordination. For consumers, greater flexibility and finer control resolution improve both cost savings and peak-shaving performance.

title: DriveAware: Multimodal Wearable Sensing–Based Machine Learning Approaches to Detect Driver Distraction
authors: Heba Montaser, Ghazal Bargshady, Hakki Gokalp Ustun, Girija Chetty, Houshyar Asadi, Jeroen Van Boxtel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Driver distraction is a major public health and road safety concern, linked to delayed reactions, impaired attention, and increased crash risk. This study introduces a simulation-based framework to detect distraction by systematically collecting multimodal physiological signals, electrodermal activity (EDA) and photoplethysmography (PPG), across three driver states (baseline, undistracted, and distracted) under diverse road conditions. Sessions and secondary-task orders were randomised, and performance was assessed using nine-fold cross-validation by leaving five subjects out each fold for evaluation. Features were extracted from both modalities and refined using Shapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to identify the most significant features, enhancing model transparency and offering new insights into physiological markers of distraction. This design frames the method as an enhanced AI decision-support pipeline suitable for driver distraction alerting. We conducted a comprehensive evaluation of multiple machine learning models, comparing unimodal (PPG-only, EDA-only) and multimodal fusion approaches. In particular, middle fusion with Random Forest and XGBoost, and late fusion through soft voting and stacking, were systematically explored. The results show that late fusion through soft voting and stacking, combined with XGBoost and SHAP-based feature selection, significantly outperforms single-modality approaches, achieving accuracy of (73.37 ± 3.52)% for three level of baseline, undistracted, and distracted driving. Multimodal gains were consistent across folds, underscoring the reliability benefits of decision-level fusion over single-modality models. Whereas, we obtained the best performance in XGBoost by (71.19 ± 2.85)% for single modality of EDA only and by (69.63 ± 4.07)% for single modality of PPG only. These findings highlight the potential of interpretable multimodal physiological monitoring, combined with wearable sensing, as a promising direction for real-time driver-state monitoring, subject to further validation.

title: Spatiotemporal Satellite User Resource Forecasting in MEO–LEO Networks via a CNN–LSTM Model
authors: Yi Jing, Chunxiao Jiang, Jiawei Wang, Jiachen Sun, Sicong Zhou, Yafeng Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Large-scale low Earth orbit (LEO) constellations are driving satellite communications toward increasingly dynamic and user-centric operation, making short-horizon demand forecasting important for proactive resource reservation and scheduling in cooperative MEO–LEO networks. To support this task, this paper constructs a satellite user request-map dataset from desensitized operational business records by transforming raw logs into grid-structured spatiotemporal demand maps through temporal slicing and spatial aggregation. Based on a cross-layer MEO–LEO workflow, we further develop a CNN–LSTM hybrid predictor that captures spatial patterns within each demand snapshot and temporal dependencies across historical windows, so as to support short-horizon forecasting of future demand maps. Experiments on 5-minute demand maps with a 30-minute forecasting horizon show that the proposed method achieves 90.59% mean total-traffic accuracy, outperforming Heuristic, ConvLSTM, and Transformer-Latent by 3.02, 3.43, and 3.66 percentage points, respectively. These results indicate that the proposed scheme can effectively characterize the spatiotemporal regularities of satellite service traffic and provide practical support for proactive reservation and scheduling in cooperative MEO–LEO systems.

title: Near-Optimal Reinforcement Learning With Shuffle Differential Privacy
authors: Shaojie Bai, Mohammad Sadegh Talebi, Chengcheng Zhao, Peng Cheng, Jiming Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a powerful tool for sequential decision-making, but its application is often hindered by privacy concerns arising from its sensitive interaction data. This challenge is particularly acute in advanced networked systems, where learning from operational and user data can expose systems to privacy inference attacks. Existing differential privacy (DP) models for RL are often inadequate: the centralized model requires a fully trusted server, creating a single point of failure risk, while the local model incurs significant performance degradation that renders it unsuitable for many networked applications. This paper addresses this gap by leveraging the emerging shuffle model of privacy, an intermediate trust model that provides strong privacy guarantees without a centralized trust assumption. We present Shuffle Differentially Private Policy Elimination (SDP-PE), the first generic policy elimination-based algorithm for episodic RL under the shuffle model. Our method introduces a novel exponential batching schedule and a “forgetting” mechanism to balance the competing demands of privacy and learning performance. Our analysis shows that SDP-PE achieves a near-optimal regret bound, demonstrating a superior privacy-regret trade-off with utility comparable to the centralized model while significantly outperforming the local model. The numerical experiments also corroborate our theoretical results and demonstrate the effectiveness of SDP-PE. This work establishes the viability of the shuffle model for secure data-driven decision-making in networked systems.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Regional Analysis of EEG During Imagined Swallowing: Identifying Cortical Contributions
authors: Sevgi Gökçe Aslan, Bülent Ylmaz
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Imagined swallowing (MI-SW) is a promising paradigm for brain–computer interface (BCI)-assisted dysphagia rehabilitation; however, reliable EEG-based decoding remains challenging because of the low signal-to-noise ratio and high inter-subject variability. In addition, prior EEG-based MI-SW studies have not explicitly modeled interregional brain interactions and have rarely employed subject-independent validation frameworks, limiting their generalizability. To address these limitations, this study proposes a regional graph-based EEG decoding framework that integrates (i) region-wise graph modeling of EEG channels, (ii) leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) subject-independent validation, and (iii) occlusion-based interpretability analysis. EEG data were collected from 30 healthy participants under two paradigms: volitional imagined swallowing and sensory-induced imagined swallowing, which was achieved by holding water in the mouth before imagery. Spectral, event-related desynchronization, entropy-based, and nonlinear features were extracted and classified using Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN). The classification task was formulated as a binary problem distinguishing Rest versus Imagined Swallowing conditions. All experiments were evaluated using leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) cross-validation to assess subject-independent decoding performance. The proposed framework achieved robust subject-independent performance across regions, with the sensory-induced paradigm consistently outperforming volitional conditions. Peak classification accuracy of >87% was obtained in the premotor region under sensory-induced conditions, whereas the volitional paradigm showed optimal performance using whole-head electrode integration. No direct comparison with conventional CNN or CSP-based baselines was performed in this study; therefore, performance gains are reported within the proposed framework under a consistent evaluation setting. Occlusion-based interpretability analysis further confirmed physiologically meaningful patterns, with low-frequency (δ–θ) desynchronization and entropy/complexity features emerging as the dominant discriminative markers of imagined swallowing. These findings demonstrate that integrating spatial graph representations with the peripheral somatosensory context enhances the EEG-based decoding of swallowing motor imagery and supports the development of reliable and interpretable BCI systems for dysphagia rehabilitation.

title: Energy-efficient train control for metro with onboard energy storage-based external power regulation
authors: Yu Rao, Qiangqiang Liu, Qingyuan Wang, Yujie Lu, Jianping Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: For a DC traction power supply system (TPSS) comprising multiple trains, the coordinated optimization of onboard energy storage (OBES) operations and train control for reducing substation energy consumption poses a significant challenge due to the inherent complexity of the problem’s nonlinear characteristics. In this paper, we adopt the indirect solution methodology outlined in [1]. The power of other trains in the TPSS is represented as the external power for the selected train via power flow calculation. The OBES is employed to achieve active regulation of the external power, and consequently, the optimal control problem, i.e., the energy-efficient train control (EETC) in spatial-temporal area (STA) [1] with OBES-based external power regulation is proposed. The Pontryagin’s Maximum Principle (PMP) is used to analyze the optimal control conditions, and a bi-level iterative solution framework is designed to solve the optimal control problem. Case studies are conducted to show that the proposed method achieves an energy saving of more than 20%.

title: IEEE Connects You to a Universe of Information!
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine

title: IEEE CAS Society Meets Industry in Europe [CASS Industry Outreach]
authors: Francois Rivet, Patricia Desgreys, Sonal Shreya, Mustak Erhan Yalcin, Andreas Demosthenous, Laura Bégon-Lours, Clément Dufossée, Roberto La Rosa, Dick Carrillo Melgarejo, Maide Bucolo, Costas N Psychalinos, Tomasz Marciniak, Florence Podevin
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: “IEEE CAS Society Meets Industry in Europe” coordinated series of more than ten technical and professional events across Europe, comprising over fifty invited talks, including more than thirty-five contributions from industry representatives spanning thirty industrial companies. The primary objective was to strengthen industry-academia partnerships while showcasing the central role of circuits and systems technologies in application domains such as wireless networks, biomedical systems, artificial intelligence, Internet-of-Things (IoT) integration, energy harvesting, neuromorphic and integrated systems, automotive, AgriFood electronics, and the role of AI in next-generation communication technologies. Through this large-scale and geographically distributed initiative, IEEE Circuits and Systems Society (CASS) aims to increase its visibility within the European industrial ecosystem and to facilitate deeper, sustained engagement between the Society, industry partners, and the academic community.

title: High-sensitivity PDMS film-based Fabry–Pérot interferometer for magnetic field sensing via magnetic fluid volume effect
authors: Youlian Wang, Yinping Miao, Xiaolan Li, Shouchuan Guo, Zhi Zhang, Jianquan Yao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: reflective fiber-optic magnetic field sensor based on the magnetic fluid (MF) magnetic volume effect is proposed. The sensor incorporates a Fabry-Pérot (F-P) interference structure formed by a single-mode fiber (SMF) and a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) film. By detecting the volume changes of MF under varying magnetic field strengths, the sensor achieves highly sensitive magnetic field measurement. When exposed to an external magnetic field, the nanoparticles in the MF undergo oriented alignment, resulting in anisotropic volume changes. These changes are transmitted to the F-P air cavity via the PDMS film, enabling precise measurement of magnetic field intensity. Experimental results demonstrate that with a 48.4 μm-thick PDMS film, the sensor achieves axial and radial magnetic field sensitivities of -2.3 nm/mT and -2.73 nm/mT, respectively, within the 0-12 mT range. Furthermore, we observed that reducing the PDMS film thickness significantly enhances magnetic field sensitivity. After optimization to 46.92 μm, the sensor achieved a radial sensitivity of -9.40 nm/mT, a detection limit of 0.0021 mT, and excellent linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9988). The sensor combines the advantages of simple structure, high sensitivity and low fabrication cost, which provides a new solution for magnetic field measurement in the fields of power equipment monitoring and aerospace.

title: A 3-D Beam-Reconfigurable Simple Liquid Dielectric Resonant Antenna by Distilled Water with High Transparency
authors: Fengyu Tang, Tao Wang, Wenbo Liu, Nan Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, a 3-D beam-reconfigurable simple liquid dielectric resonant antenna by distilled water with high transparency is proposed. The proposed antenna consists of a hexagonal star-shaped radiator and a liquid ground. Moreover, the entire liquid dielectric resonator antenna (LDRA) is transparent except for the feeding structure, its high optical transparency addresses the critical demand for visually unobtrusive integration in emerging 5G/6G and IoT applications. This design combines innovative cavity design with asymmetric dielectric loading to drive the field distribution to achieve beam reconfigurability. By utilizing the fluidity of the liquid and injecting water into different radiators, different 3-D beam can be generated, achieving five 3-D beam radiation directions of ppppppppppppppppppppp15dddddddddddddd, pppppppppppppppppppp30ddddddddddddddddddd, and 0ddddddddddddddddddddd. With a total size of 1.04ttttttttttttttttt1.04tttttttttttttttttt0.26 lllllllllllllllllll0 3 (llllllllllllllllllllllllll0 is the free space wavelength at the center frequency). By measuring, the proposed antenna can achieve five 3-D beam radiation directions in the frequency range of 2.22 to 2.64 GHz, includes 2.4 GHz band-a frequency band widely employed in Wi-Fi and smart home devices due to its superior penetration capability.

title: Spatial Channel Characterization Based on Scalar Frequency Response Measurements in UAV-Assisted Systems
authors: Zhixin Huang, Fengchun Zhang, Wei Fan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-based channel sounding and antenna measurement have been attracting significant interest. However, acquiring complex channel frequency responses (CFRs) with accurate phase is often challenging due to strict UAV payload constraints and synchronization complexities. This letter proposes a novel array signal processing framework to retrieve the power-angle-delay profile (PADP) using only scalar frequency responses (SFRs) in line-of-sight (LOS) environments. By exploiting the strong LOS path typically present in air-to-ground links, which naturally favors the minimum-phase condition, the proposed method first reconstructs the phase response via the Hilbert transform. Furthermore, with a-priori knowledge of the LOS path distance, angle of arrival (AoA), and array geometry, a LOS path calibration and relative phase compensation procedure is introduced to restore the original complex CFRs of all array elements. Experimental validation in a controlled LOS scenario demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, offering a low-cost phaseless solution for constrained UAV-assisted measurement applications.

title: A Hybrid Approach Combining Quantum and Neuromorphic Computing for Live City Traffic Management
authors: Shramit Sarupya Swain, Ramesh Sekaran, Girisha G S, Jeeva S, K.Vengatesan, Bharathiraja Nagu
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Even when traffic shifts, signals change just quick enough to keep cars rolling through downtown lanes. Things change fast. Old methods lag behind. Slow reactions make them weak. Speed leaves tradition behind. Fast moves break old habits. Quick changes ignore past rules. Outdated approaches fail when pace increases dealing with constant shifts in schedule. QNF fixes it – fast putting pieces together while merging separate actions simultaneously. Movement stirs awake brain-like networks-switched on solely during engines hum to life, sitting still but awake. Voltage slips downward when they wait resting, it uses less than a single watt. Near silence, the chips run just above idle creations like Intel Loihi [1], running light on need. Today yet after that, how things link shifts. Still, picks must match the moment-nothing more a faint replay fades within slow-moving delays. Roads start to when things jam up, attention fades into a shift-guided suddenly by quantum ideas. A different path appears without warning. This change moves quietly beneath the surface faster light patterns found by Grover’s method [2] - just √N tries needed. Connecting neural-style detectors through quantum cores is a pathway where pulses reshape qubit states, every few seconds, chances shift close to crowded areas. During tests, keep falling nearly a third, ups stumbled as electricity needs slipped. Almost all of the area showed changes when tested on a city layout compared to usual methods. Around 320 watts get used by DRL systems when running on edge devices [3]. This framework is still just an idea for now - bringing it into real use would take more steps than expected. What stands in the way isn’t effort, but timing. Testing comes next, though only under strict conditions. Progress waits on results, not plans. So far, nothing has moved past early checks. So far, actual hardware based on quantum principles remains unrealized..

title: Application and Research of Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content in Routing and Switching Instruction
authors: Jun Tao, Yuting Lin, Lei Yue, Hao Xu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) into technical education represents a transformative approach to addressing persistent challenges in networking instruction. This comprehensive study investigates the implementation of AIGC technologies in undergraduate Routing and Switching courses, examining their impact on learning outcomes, instructional efficiency, and student engagement. Through a mixed-methods research design involving 127 computer engineering students over two academic semesters, we demonstrate significant improvements in conceptual understanding, practical configuration skills, and problem-solving abilities. The research framework incorporates AIGC across five pedagogical dimensions: personalized learning paths, dynamic lab generation, intelligent assessment, conversational tutoring, and curriculum adaptation. Quantitative analysis reveals a 31.2% enhancement in practical examination scores, a 44% reduction in configuration errors, and a 2.3-fold increase in out-of-class engagement among intervention group participants. Qualitative data from instructor interviews and student feedback further substantiate the value of AIGC in creating adaptive, scalable learning environments. This paper contributes to the growing literature on AI-enhanced technical education by providing empirical evidence, implementation frameworks, and ethical considerations for effectively leveraging generative AI in networking instruction.

title: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: VRhythmSpace: Designing a Musical Game for Play, Learning, and Performance
authors: Jiawen Li, Zheyuan Jiang, Yifei Chen, Yuwei Ren, Jingji Li, Kai Yao, Xu Sun, Bin Jia, Ningning Xu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) is an emerging technique that alters the way users interact with music content. In this paper, we introduce VRhythmSpace, a VR musical game prototype that enables users to engage, learn, and play with music. We first conducted a preliminary investigation with seven volunteers in three different VR musical games to examine how users engage in these games and identify key differences in game features and preferable in specific contexts. Drawing from our findings, we formulate five user requirements, followed by design and user test with eight participants. The results show that users have high expectations for rich, diverse and immersive game experience with different motivations and purposes. This work provides valuable insights for researchers and practitioners, laying the foundation for more engaging and creative music experiences.

title: Overcoming AI Dependency: Practical Pathways and Effectiveness of Educational Agents in Python Instruction
authors: Fang Xia, Ren Ying
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The proliferation of generative artificial intelligence (AI) has given rise to the phenomenon of "AI dependency" in programming learning, where students tend to directly obtain code solutions, thereby undermining the cultivation of problem-solving abilities and computational thinking. This study developed an instructional intervention system centered on "Role-based Pedagogical Agents" for the Python Programming course, transforming AI from an "answer provider" into a "thinking support platform." Based on a one-semester quasi-experimental study, students in the experimental group significantly reduced their behavior of directly requesting complete code. They demonstrated more prominent performance in problem decomposition, algorithm design, and debugging capabilities, along with a noticeable enhancement in reflection depth. The research confirms that role-based pedagogical agents, through structured process design and agent intervention, effectively reduce AI dependency, promote the improvement of students' computational thinking and programming quality, and provide a practical pathway for programming instruction in the era of artificial intelligence.

title: TIME: A Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model for IDH Prediction_supp5-3693918.pdf
authors: Pengwei Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Background: Intradialytic hypotension (IDH) is a critical complication in hemodialysis that increases morbidity and treatment risks, yet existing machine learning methods inadequately address session-level physiological state heterogeneity and fail to balance linear and nonlinear feature interactions, limiting predictive accuracy and interpretability. Methods: We developed TIME (Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model), which explicitly separates linear interactions, higher-order nonlinear dependencies, and residual terms inspired by Taylor series expansion, while incorporating a physiological state embedding layer to capture session-level physiological state heterogeneity revealed through hierarchical clustering on autoencoder-derived latent representations of hematologic indicators selected using SHAP analysis and clinical knowledge. Using data from 532 hemodialysis patients (18,309 records), we compared TIME against 19 state-of-the-art baseline models, including blood pressure-stratified subcohort and out-of-distribution analyses. Results: Hierarchical clustering identified two distinct hematological states with different IDH risks (57.93% vs. 55.75%, $p=6.60\times 10^{-7}$). TIME achieved 0.6929 accuracy, 0.7435 F1-score, and 0.3663 MCC on the full cohort, the highest AUC across all three subcohorts (0.7010, 0.6795, and 0.7201), and strong out-of-distribution performance (accuracy 0.7053, F1 0.7377, MCC 0.4009). Integrating TIME's architecture into baseline models improved MCC by 2.67 percentage points on average. Conclusions: TIME effectively predicts IDH by modeling session-level physiological state heterogeneity and balancing feature interactions, with robust generalizability across diverse populations. Significance: TIME advances precision medicine in dialysis care by enabling early risk identification and personalized treatment strategies, while revealing broader immune, nutritional, and fluid-related markers of IDH risk.

title: Haptic-Enhanced XR Classrooms for Skill Transfer in STEM and TVET Education
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Haptic-enhanced extended reality (XR) classrooms are emerging as a transformative paradigm for advancing practice-based learning in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) and technical and vocational education and training (TVET). By integrating immersive visual–auditory simulations with force, tactile and kinesthetic feedback, haptic XR environments enable learners to directly experience tool–material interactions, fine-motor constraints and dynamic task conditions essential for high-stakes vocational competencies. Recent developments in haptic devices, physics-based rendering and XR learning analytics indicate strong potential for improving psychomotor accuracy, reducing training errors and accelerating time-to-mastery across welding, robotics programming, electronics assembly and other skill-intensive domains. Despite these advances, empirical findings on long-term retention and real-world transfer remain inconsistent, underscoring the need for structured frameworks and rigorous evaluation methodologies. This paper presents a technical framework for designing, implementing and assessing haptic-enhanced XR classrooms for future-oriented STEM and TVET education. It introduces a multi-layer architecture integrating XR engines, haptic interfaces and competency-aligned analytics, and proposes an experimental protocol comparing traditional, visual-only XR and haptic XR modalities. Illustrative results demonstrate the conditions under which haptic XR most effectively enhances performance, offering evidence-based guidelines for scalable, industry-aligned adoption.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Immersive Virtual Reality Simulation System for Classroom Questioning Training
authors: Yuanxi Yang, Yongbin Hu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study explores the application of Immersive Virtual Reality (IVR) in enhancing pre-service teachers' classroom questioning skills, comparing it with traditional video-based training. The Classroom Questioning VR System was developed using the Unity 3D engine and the Photon Fusion framework, employing a dual-client architecture consisting of a Teacher Client and a Pre-service Teacher Client. It aims to provide pre-service teachers with a realistic, interactive, and low-risk classroom simulation environment to cultivate their questioning abilities. The study adopted a quasi-experimental design, randomly assigning 56 pre-service teachers from a university in China to either an IVR group (n=27) or a Video group (n=29). The results highlight the advantages of IVR in facilitating embodied interactive learning, bridging the gap between theory and practice, and enhancing learning engagement. The study suggests that Virtual Reality (VR) technology should be integrated into teacher education to promote sustainable skill development in the digital age. Future research directions should focus on the convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and VR technologies to achieve personalized tutoring.

title: Magnetic Coupling Path Reconfiguration in Multi-Node Domino Wireless Power Transfer Systems for Insulator Monitoring
authors: Wei Wang, Penghui Li, Zheng Lu, Zihao Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: In domino wireless power transfer (WPT) systems, inefficient magnetic energy circulation arises from complex coupling networks and redundant mutual inductance among resonators, which limits power delivery to downstream nodes, particularly in high-voltage insulator strings with multiple sensors. A magnetic coupling path reconfiguration strategy based on dynamic relay switching is proposed in this paper. By controlling the connection and disconnection of key nodes in the linkage, the target load operates under optimal coupling conditions, enabling controllable energy flow. Results show that, disconnecting node 2 can significantly enhance the downstream coupling strength, increasing load input power by 1.5–2 times, as verified in four- and five-coil structures. A feasible solution for long-term stable power supply to multiple sensors on high-voltage insulator strings is provided.

title: Algorithmic Exploration of Matching Network Topologies for Power Amplifiers
authors: Sergio López de Pablo, Joel Mesas, Pedro de Paco, Jordi Verdú
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this article, a novel exploration algorithm for the design and optimization of matching networks (MNs) in power amplifiers (PAs) is presented. The methodology is based on the automatic generation and optimization of both input and output MNs (IMNs/OMNs) according to a set of design rules and constraints that ensure technological feasibility. All candidate MN topologies are optimized and ranked using a unified cost function, enabling the automatic selection of the most suitable solution while facilitating a comprehensive tradeoff analysis among key performance metrics such as power-added efficiency (PAE), gain, bandwidth, physical size, and expected yield. As a result, the designer obtains a global view of the solution space and can objectively compare different MN configurations within a consistent optimization framework. To validate the proposed algorithm, a PA covering 1.1–1.9 GHz—fully encompassing the Galileo navigation bands E5, E6, and L1—and targeting high efficiency with harmonic control, was designed, fabricated, and experimentally characterized.

title: Multi-component Echo Signal Estimation in MIMO Systems Based on the GESR-Atan-Log Nonconvex Regularization
authors: Xiaoting Tang, Yuhang Liang, Zhen Liu, Yuhua Cheng, Jingyuan Wang, Hang Geng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Distinguishing and extracting weak, transient signal components in noisy environments remains a key challenge in radar and communication signal processing. This paper addresses this problem by proposing an advanced generalized enhanced sparse regularization based on Arctangent-Logarithm (GESR-Atan-Log) penalty. The penalty is integrated within a coherent processing framework that includes improved complex variational mode decomposition (ICVMD) and adaptive dictionary learning via K-SVD. Theoretical analysis guarantees the convexity of the cost function under certain conditions, ensuring the global minimum and its stability. Besides, a multi-objective optimization scheme is used to determine hyperparameters, and an efficient approximate solution is provided. Evaluations through simulations and practical RF echo experiments show that GESR-Atan-Log significantly outperforms L1-norm and GMC methods. Quantitative gains include accuracy improvements of 5.389% and 9.998% for the simple point target, and 4.898% and 7.381% for the complex target. In robustness tests across 200 signals with varying noise levels, our method achieved the lowest and most consistent RMSE. These findings conclusively demonstrate the method’s effectiveness for blind source separation, high-fidelity reconstruction, and robust noise suppression.

title: Blockchain-driven Freshness-keeping Efforts in Community Group-buying Platforms_supp1-3694210.docx
authors: Yuqian Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: The loss of freshness-keeping efforts in fresh product supply chains not only diminishes consumers' purchasing experiences but also leads to significant economic losses. This challenge is particularly pronounced in community group-buying (CGB) supply chains. This study investigates how blockchain adoption can influence CGB supply chains, which involve a platform that sources products from the supplier and delegates order fulfillment to the group leader. By leveraging blockchain-enabled real-time traceability, a platform can precisely attribute freshness-keeping efforts to the respective parties, thereby facilitating an incentive mechanism tailored to the effort level. The analysis yields three key findings. First, counterintuitively, blockchain adoption under platform-determined pricing induces a free-riding effect: freshness-keeping efforts are intensified by the group leader and reduced by the supplier. However, the supplier's efforts rebound when pricing power is granted with a higher incentive coefficient. The acquisition of pricing power facilitates blockchain adoption in enhancing supplier's freshness-keeping efforts, yet proves more challenging in improving group leader's freshness-keeping efforts. Second, the platform adopts blockchain only when the traceability cost is sufficiently low and the incentive coefficient remains moderate. Third, lower traceability costs result in a win–win–win (platform-supplier-group leader) outcome, expanding with consumer sensitivity to freshness-keeping efforts. The robustness of these findings is verified through extensive numerical simulations and sensitivity analyses. Furthermore, the optimal incentive coefficient decreases monotonically with the commission rate and exhibits an inverted U-shaped relationship with consumer freshness-keeping effort sensitivity. Our study contributes to existing theoretical frameworks regarding the value of blockchain in fresh product supply chains and provides novel managerial insights for CGB platforms.

title: Get Published in the New IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications

title: Sustainable Federated Learning in 6G Networks: The Case for Cybersecurity
authors: Gokcan Cantali, Wissem Soussi, Gurkan Gur, Burkhard Stiller
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: 6G networks are expected to integrate distributed artificial intelligence methods, such as federated learning (FL), across layers from core infrastructure to end-user applications. While FL offers significant advantages by enabling collaborative model training without sharing local and sensitive data, its deployment in large-scale, complex 6G environments raises important sustainability and security concerns. In particular, FL's communication overhead, including carbon footprint, requires careful optimization to ensure sustainable operation. Simultaneously, the cybersecurity implications of sustainable FL remain underexplored. In this work, we examine the sustainability challenges associated with FL and analyze them from a cybersecurity perspective through a use case, highlighting relevant trade-offs and key research directions for future 6G networks.

title: Beamforming Reflective Metasurfaces Supporting Polarization Control
authors: Tianke Qiu, George V. Eleftheriades
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Reflective electromagnetic metasurfaces (MTSs) provide a passive platform for manipulating the reflection of incident electromagnetic waves, offering opportunities for beamforming and polarization control in wireless communication systems. However, systematic 3D design methods that account for anisotropic unit cells and full-wave interactions remain limited. In this paper, a 3D full-wave design procedure for reflective MTSs based on the integral equation method and tensor impedance boundary conditions is presented. The MTS consists of impedance sheets placed on top of a grounded dielectric slab. Based on a method of moments formulation that explicitly incorporates coupling between unit cells, the impedance values are optimized via gradient descent to achieve beamforming and polarization control of the reflected field. A numerical example demonstrates the beamforming capability of the proposed method, where precise amplitude and phase control through auxiliary surface waves allows the synthesis of a target sector pattern. For practical implementation, a regression neural network is employed to establish an inverse mapping between the optimized impedance tensors and the geometric parameters of anisotropic unit cells featuring three degrees of freedom, enabling efficient realization of spatially varying anisotropic impedance distributions. Two MTSs are designed and experimentally validated at 10 GHz based on an introduced novel physical unit cell. The first, with 576 unit cells spanning four wavelengths by four wavelengths, performs anomalous reflection and converts linear to right-hand circular polarization (RHCP), achieving 13.0 dB cross-polar discrimination and 21.8 dB directivity, with an instantaneous 3-dB fractional gain bandwidth of 6.2%. The second, with 1296 unit cells, splits the reflected wave into RHCP and LHCP beams in different directions, with measured directivities of 23.9 dB and 23.7 dB, respectively, and a bandwidth of 7.6%. These high directivities are achieved by accounting for mutual coupling and by exploiting auxiliary surface waves that redistribute incident power across the MTS.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for Antenna Devices through Interval Arithmetic - A Generalized Approach
authors: Nicola Anselmi, Paolo Rocca, Andrea Massa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a novel method for the sensitivity analysis of electromagnetic (EM) systems whose transfer function (TF), that is the input-output (I/O) relationship between the input parameters affected by tolerance and the system response (i.e., the arising EM performance of interest), is not available in closed (or explicit) form. The method is a generalized analytic technique based on the Interval Analysis (IA). First, an analytic surrogate model (SM) of the TF is defined by means of a learning-by-example (LBE) approach starting from a set of available I/O examples. Then, the LBE-derived SM is extended to intervals through IA to yield inclusive, yet finite, performance bounds of the output system response when the control parameters are affected by unknown, but bounded, tolerances. A set of representative numerical examples is reported to validate the proposed IA-LBE method as well as to assess its effectiveness and reliability when dealing with realistic EM systems (e.g., antennas) for which the TF is not explicitly known.

title: Measuring Linear Array Mutual Coupling Terms and Element Positions from Embedded Element Patterns
authors: Jacki Gilmore, Michele Pugno, Annie Cuyt
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a method to derive the antenna element positions and S-parameters of linear antenna arrays of identical elements from measurements of the embedded element patterns. The problem is formulated as an exponential data modelling problem, and validated exponential analysis is used to extract the element positions and the S-parameters. Results in the calculation of the element positions indicate combined absolute-relative errors (i.e. absolute error divided by 1+ the exact element position) of less that 0.1% for a synthetic example of an 11-element array, and less than 0.2% for a 4-element experimental example. The combined absolute-relative error in the calculation of the coefficients from which the S-parameters are derived is < 0.002 for the synthetic example. There is excellent agreement between S-parameters directly measured with a VNA and S-parameters determined using the proposed method.

title: Manufacturing and testing of electrospun nanofiber-enhanced mask textiles
authors: Réka Alexandra Pózmán, László Székely, Dr. Richárd Cselkó, Mohd Yasir Al Saif, Balázs Illés, Attila Géczy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Electrospun nanofibers offer a promising route for enhancing the filtration performance of textile-based masks, particularly in situations where certified respirators are scarce. This study presents a simplified and economical electrospinning device capable of producing uniform mask-sized nanofiber layers without the need for a syringe pump. Electric-field simulations were used to optimize key design parameters, including the number and spacing of needles, revealing that a two-needle configuration provides the most uniform deposition while minimizing mutual field distortion. Experimental tests further identified the optimal solution properties, electrode distance, and voltage required to generate defect-free nanofibers. The resulting electrospun PA-6 layers were characterized by scanning electron microscopy and demonstrated significantly improved filtration performance when applied to cotton substrates. These findings establish a practical, low-resource approach for producing nanofiber-enhanced mask materials suitable for emergency deployment.

title: RUBCA: Detecting Anxiety Levels in Arabic Tweets Using a Rule Based Classification Approach
authors: Eithar I. Almujalli, Muna M. Alhammad
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Anxiety detection within social media platforms has become an essential focus in computational mental health due to the prevalence of dialectal ambiguity and orthographic inconsistencies in user-generated Arabic content. Existing models often exhibit limited interpretability and reduced sensitivity when applied to linguistically diverse Gulf regions. This study aims to develop a robust, explainable system for identifying anxiety-related patterns in Arabic tweets.We present RUBCA, a hybrid symbolic–distributional framework integrating three core modules: a manually constructed Arabic anxiety lexicon aligned with eight clinically validated symptom dimensions; a symbolic classification engine employing nested conditional rules for dialectal variance; and an iterative human-in-the-loop refinement strategy for lexicon and rule tuning. While the inference stage is fully deterministic and explainable, the lexicon refinement stage incorporates distributional embeddings to capture semantic proximity across dialectal variants. The architecture incorporates co-occurrence verification, binary encoding of symptom presence, and multi-label assignment to capture complex psychological constructs. Semantic perturbation functions and context-aware filters further enhance resilience against orthographic noise. Simulation results demonstrate RUBCA’s superiority over eleven state-of-the-art models, achieving 93.7% accuracy, 91.4% precision, and 95.2% category-level coverage across Gulf datasets.

title: Enhancing Multimodal Recommender System Performance Using Large Language Models
authors: Soohyun Woo, Seonu Park, Xinzhe Li, Seongkook Lee, Jaekyeong Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the rapid advancement of e-commerce platforms and the overwhelming amount of information available online, recommender systems have emerged as essential tools for helping users efficiently discover items aligned with their preferences. Online reviews, in particular, offer rich information for understanding both user preferences and item features, and have been widely utilized in existing multimodal recommender systems. However, most prior approaches have not sufficiently addressed modality-specific noise in multimodal reviews, thereby constraining their capacity to precisely represent users’ experiences. Furthermore, by merely extracting and fusing features from texts and images without considering the structural heterogeneity between modalities, these approaches often fall short in accurately predicting user preferences. To address these limitations, this study proposes multimodal aspect summarization for recommender systems (MAS-Rec), a novel model that leverages large language models (LLMs) to summarize multimodal reviews by aspect, thereby enhancing preference prediction. The proposed model is structured as follows: First, LLMs are employed to independently summarize user preferences and item features into aspects from textual and visual data, respectively. Second, these aspect-based summarizations are then encoded using a bidirectional encoder representations from transformers (BERT) to extract semantic features. Finally, a co-attention mechanism is applied to model the semantic interactions between user preferences and item features. Experimental results on two review datasets provided by Amazon.com demonstrate that MAS-Rec outperforms existing unimodal and multimodal models. These findings demonstrate that summarizing images by aspect using the LLM effectively captures the semantic connections between texts and images, thereby enabling more accurate prediction of user preferences compared to traditional approaches.

title: Simulation-Based Evaluation of Underwater Sensor Network Performance Using DBR and Broadcast MAC in Shallow and Deep Sea Scenarios
authors: Koko Oktavianus Asmara, Wirawan, Endang Widjiati
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Reliable and adaptive underwater monitoring systems are essential due to the diverse oceanographic conditions in the Southeast Asian maritime region, encompassing both shallow coastal areas and deep-sea zones. Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) enable realtime data acquisition in such environments. This study employs the Aqua-Sim-NG simulator on the NS-3 platform to evaluate the performance of Depth-Based Routing (DBR) combined with Broadcast MAC under shallow- and deep-sea scenarios. Simulations are conducted at two acoustic frequencies (3 kHz and $\mathbf{2 5 ~ k H z}$) across four network topologies-combinations of one or two sink nodes with 5 or 10 sensor nodes. Underwater acoustic properties, including the Sound Speed Profile (SSP) and SOFAR channel, are considered alongside key performance metrics such as end-to-end delay, throughput, and packet loss ratio. Results show that $\mathbf{2 5 ~ k H z}$ yields lower latency and higher throughput in shallow marine environments, whereas 3 kHz enables more reliable long-distance communication in deep-sea conditions with significantly reduced packet loss. The simulation results align well with theoretical predictions from the Thorp attenuation model, indicating minimal deviation within numerical precision. These findings highlight the importance of network topology and frequency selection in UWSN deployment. The study provides practical insights for sensor placement strategies in Indonesian waters, where bathymetric diversity significantly affects network performance. Future work will consider the impact of various underwater noise sources, including ambient, shipping, and thermal noise, on communication performance. In addition, the Bit Error Rate (BER) will be evaluated alongside existing performance metrics-delay, throughput, and packet loss-to provide a more comprehensive assessment of acoustic channel quality.

title: Probabilistic Modeling of Human Driver’s Natural Behavior Distribution: An Ensemble Learning and GMM Framework for Car-Following Model
authors: Huihua Gao, Hong Chen, Ting Qu, Yongfu Li, Yunfeng Hu, Bingzhao Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Probabilistic modeling of the natural distribution of human driving behavior is crucial for constructing realistic and dynamic traffic simulation environments. To capture the complex, high-dimensional, and multi-modal characteristics of driving behavior, this paper proposes an ensemble-based probabilistic modeling framework. A Bayesian Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) is employed as a base predictor to model the multi-modal distribution, while ensemble learning is utilized to reduce model bias and improve robustness. To address challenges associated with high-dimensional features and limited sample sizes, dimensionality reduction and weighted bootstrap sampling are introduced to construct diverse meta models. An error evaluation network is developed to assess prediction quality in terms of mean, variance, and reliability. To handle distributional shifts across scenarios, an adaptive meta model with online learning capability is proposed for dynamic parameter updating and improved transferability. Furthermore, a reliability–uncertainty fusion strategy inspired by mean–variance theory is designed to dynamically assign optimal weights to individual meta models. Experiments on three datasets—HighD, SPMD, and MTBD—demonstrate superior performance in accuracy, robustness, and generalization.

title: Hybrid Adversarial Retraining Approach For A Proactive Network Intrusion Detection System
authors: Noora Al Roken, Hakim Hacid, Ahmed Bouridane, Abir Hussian
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Artificial intelligence provides benefits across many areas, including network security. It also enables novel attacks and new security risks. However, AI-generated attacks now outpace the capabilities of current defense systems. Therefore, it is crucial to implement proactive security measures to identify unknown threats and protect digital systems. Existing research is limited by small or outdated datasets, data from virtual machines, inefficient or complex behavior-based methods, and reliance on a single adversarial condition. To address this, our study developed a new attack-detection framework. We used a proactive approach with a hybrid retraining technique that integrates both black- and white-box attacks. The framework combines two different attack concealment methods with a two-stage classification method to enhance detection accuracy and reduce false negatives. We joined two large network attack datasets, CICIDS2017 and CSE-CIC-IDS2018, to train and test the framework. Experiments show that our intrusion detection system achieved high detection rates. The two-layer convolutional neural network, using our all-attacks adversarial retraining method, demonstrated superior generalization across 12 adversarial attack algorithms, achieving an overall accuracy of 89%. This finding shows that simpler models can still provide robust protection with our hybrid retraining approach. Our method improved accuracy by up to 7.02% on CICIDS2017 and 3.7% on CSE-CIC-IDS2018. These results demonstrate that our method provides a practical and reliable defense against real-world network security threats.

title: Automated Binary Code Analysis Technology Enhanced by Variable Knowledge Graph
authors: Shunda Meng, Bing Xia, Yi Lin, Jiabin Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the escalating threats to software supply chain security, binary code analysis plays a critical role in malicious software detection, vulnerability mining, and software composition analysis. However, traditional binary analysis techniques face challenges such as symbol removal, contextual semantic fragmentation, and obfuscation interference. Although emerging large language models (LLMs) possess strong semantic understanding capabilities, they often suffer from hallucinations due to a lack of domain-specific knowledge. This paper proposes an automated binary code analysis technique enhanced by a Variable Knowledge Graph (VKG). The approach first extracts variable-level entities and relationships through decompilation and static analysis, constructing a structured VKG stored in Neo4j. Second, it designs a graph-text joint reasoning engine based on ToG-2 (Think-on-Graph 2.0), which leverages multi-hop retrieval to obtain deep dataflow information from the VKG and integrates external heterogeneous textual knowledge to guide LLMs in interpretable semantic recovery. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with traditional static analysis tools and pure LLM baselines, this technique improves function identification accuracy by 18.5% and achieves an F1 score of 0.83 in variable semantic recovery, significantly reducing model hallucination rates.

title: A Unified Semantic Space-Based Backend Frame-work for Speech Spoofing Detection
authors: Yuxiao Qiu, Gang Liu, Zexuan Liu, Yuxi Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Graph-attention-based spectro-temporal modeling, as used in AASIST, relies on explicitly defined heterogeneous graph topologies whose structural necessity remains unclear and computationally costly. This work reformulates relational modeling from a unified attention semantic space by replacing customized graph attention with standard multi-head self-attention (MHSA), while preserving the original two-stream topology and feature space. Type-aware projections maintain modality heterogeneity, and a learnable attention fusion module replaces heuristic max fusion. By learning node dependencies through Query–Key–Value similarity in a fully connected attention space, the proposed model implicitly captures spectro-temporal topology without predefined edges. Experiments on ASVspoof 2019, 2021, and 2024 demonstrate competitive or superior EER compared to AASIST2 and AASIST3, improved cross-dataset robustness, and reduced inference latency and training memory. These results suggest that unified MHSA provides an efficient and expressive alternative to customized graph attention for speech spoofing detection.

title: Random Forest Driven Dietary Habit Analysis and Preventive Health Prediction for Intelligent Nutrition Management
authors: D. Shanthi, P. Shreya, R. Sneka, S. Swetha, V. Swetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Dietary habits play a critical role in determining overall health and the likelihood of developing chronic diseases. Increasing prevalence of lifestyle-related health disorders has created a strong need for effective dietary monitoring and preventive healthcare strategies. Many individuals follow unhealthy eating patterns that contribute to obesity, cardiovascular complications, metabolic disorders, and other nutrition-related conditions. Early identification of risky dietary behaviors can support preventive health management and promote healthier lifestyles. However, systematic dietary analysis and health risk identification remain challenging due to the complexity of nutritional patterns and variability among individuals. NutriGuard AI presents a structured framework for dietary habit analysis and preventive health prediction aimed at supporting personalized nutrition management. The proposed system analyzes dietary intake patterns, lifestyle attributes, and health indicators to determine preventive health risk levels. Data related to nutritional consumption, body mass index, physical activity levels, and metabolic indicators are collected and processed for predictive analysis.
