The essay shows that a combination of EfficientNet-B4 encoder and compound scaling with skip connections of concurrent spatial and channel Squeeze and Excitation (scSE) and multi-scale loss yields the best boundary fidelity. This enables the model to effectively indicate anomalies and not yield false positives in locations with water puddles but still the viable edge latency is 29.8 FPS.

title: Multi-Source Collaborative Identification of AS Border Routers via Cyberspace Mapping Engines
authors: WenHang Lou, JinLong Fei, Di Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Accurate identification of Autonomous System (AS) border routers—including vendor, model, and firmware version— is critical for routing security assessment and inter-domain traffic engineering. Existing fingerprinting techniques rely primarily on initial TTL (iTTL) signatures or SNMPv3 probing, yet both suffer from structural blind spots: iTTL signatures cannot distinguish Cisco IOS from Huawei VRP , while SNMPv3 achieves only 15–18% response rates due to widespread port filtering. This paper proposes a multi-source collaborative identification framework that systematically introduces cyberspace mapping engines (FOFA, Shodan, Censys, ZoomEye) as a third fingerprint source, complementing LFP and SNMPv3 through a unified three-level model matching pipeline and a Weighted Confidence Fusion Algorithm (WCFA) with context-aware dynamic weight adjustment. Experiments on 1,500 border routers across three geographic regions demonstrate that the tri-source fusion raises vendor identification coverage from approximately 65% to 79.4%, and WCFA achieves 91.3% resolution accuracy on 188 conflicting cases—a 7.6 percentage-point improvement over simple priority-based arbitration. Nearly half of covered routers receive model-level identification, and over one quarter obtain complete vendor-model-firmware triplets.

title: The Impact of BYOD and THSD Program Participation on Parental Perspectives for Students’ Digital Learning: A Case Study of Two Elementary Schools in Hsinchu, Taiwan
authors: Hsieh I-Ling, Wang Wei-Yu, Chang Pin-Shan, Chiu Fu-Yuan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of the "Bring Your Own Device" (BYOD) and "Take-Home Student Device" (THSD) programs on parental support for digital learning in two elementary schools in Hsinchu City, Taiwan. A modified version of the Parental Digital Learning Perspectives Questionnaire for BYOD & THSD Programs (PDLPQ for BYOD & THSD), originally developed by Chang and Kao [1], was used to examine five key dimensions: Pa-rental Beliefs about Digital Learning (PB), Parental Digital Competence (PC), Support systems provided by Schools and Teachers (SS), Student learning behaviors at home (SL), and Parental Involvement in Student learning (PI). A total of 338 valid responses were collected and analyzed using t-tests, ANOVA, and Pearson correlation. Results showed that School 1 significantly outperformed School 2 in SS. Parents with Fully familiar Self-Perceived Understanding Levels (SPUL) scored significantly higher across all dimensions and overall PDLPQ for BYOD & THSD scores. Grade-level differences were found in both PI and SS. In PI, parents of younger students showed greater involvement. In contrast, SS scores varied significantly across grade levels but did not follow a linear trend of higher support for lower grades. Strong correlations were observed among all five dimensions, especially between SL and SS. The findings highlight the importance of pa-rental understanding and school support in enhancing students’ digital learning at home and provide empirical evidence to inform digital learning policy refinement.

title: A Secure and Transparent Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Financial Decision Systems
authors: Uthayan K.R, Shanthanu G, Santhosh V
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Traditional credit decision systems rely on opaque machine learning models that lack transparency, auditability, and secure coordination between system components. This paper proposes a secure multi-agent artificial intelligence pipeline for automated credit decisioning using a centralized orchestration layer. The architecture integrates specialized agents for digital identity verification, fraud risk assessment using interpretable fuzzy risk scoring, credit risk prediction, regulatory policy retrieval, and tamper-proof audit logging.To ensure secure and trustworthy operation, the system incorporates zero-trust communication between agents, role-based access control, authenticated message exchange, and cryptographically verifiable audit trails. The proposed pipeline enables transparent, auditable, and regulator-compliant automated credit decisioning while ensuring secure handling of sensitive financial data.This work demonstrates how secure multi-agent orchestration can enable trustworthy, explainable, and secure artificial intelligence deployment in financial web applications.

title: UAV-Assisted MEC: A Hybrid PSO-GA for Joint Task Offloading Optimization
authors: Cheru Haile, Zheng Xiang, Long Yang, Jabar Mahmood
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Enhancing the energy efficiency of ground users and UAVs or MECs sharing tasks in offloading mode. The main challenge to creating a UAV-assisted MEC task offloading mechanism is maintaining service quality and minimizing latency, as well as reducing energy consumption. The task offloading problem in UAV-assisted MEC systems is formulated as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem. We propose a joint optimization mechanism to process the tasks originated from end users, which is solved by the hybrid PSO-GA algorithm. The proposed mechanism aims to minimize energy consumption and reconstruct the service quality based on a task offloading mechanism that can accept some tolerance levels of latency in both task uplink and downlink. We utilized three network scenarios, each featuring varying numbers of UAVs, ground users, and MEC. The results show that the proposed hybrid PSO-GA outperforms, compared to benchmark studies, in terms of performance improvement mechanisms, increasing 4.7% and minimizing 7.14% of the energy efficiency of an entire network.

title: A Multi-Scale Micro-Structure Residual Enhancement Network for Liver Small Tumor Segmentation
authors: Jiaxuan Ran, Zhiyong Zhang, Yixiang Ji, Xiaoping Yi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper proposes a liver small tumor segmentation method based on a multi-scale micro-structure residual augmentation module. To address challenges such as small tumor size, blurred boundaries, and low contrast, improvements are made upon the BasicUNet architecture. The MSD Task03_Liver dataset is preprocessed using HU windowing and normalization, and three-dimensional CT images are converted into 512×512 slices for training. An encoder–decoder framework is adopted, where a spatial and channel Squeeze-and-Excitation (scSE) attention mechanism is embedded in skip connections to enhance feature selectivity. A multi-scale residual augmentation module with multi-branch depthwise separable convolutions is introduced in the decoder to strengthen fine-grained structural representation. Experimental results show that the proposed model significantly improves the Dice score and reduces the HD95 metric compared with the standard UNet baseline, achieving more accurate boundary segmentation and superior performance in small tumor delineation.

title: Attack-Resilient Trust Management Framework for SDN-Enabled IoT Networks Using Blockchain
authors: Wiem Bekri, Rihab Jmal, Lamia Chaari Fourati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Cyberattacks on IoT infrastructures are increasingly exploiting the lack of coordination between detection methods and network-level response strategies. Attacks like denial-of-service flooding and man-in-the-middle poisoning can quickly disrupt communication. At the same time, existing security solutions often struggle to turn detection results into immediate actions for mitigation. This paper presents ARTM-SBIoT, an attack-resilient trust management framework based on SDN/blockchain for IoT Networks. ARTM-SBIoT framework is designed to enable a coordinated response to attacks in IoT environments. A Proof-of-Authority blockchain is used to maintain unchangeable records of trusted and blocked devices through smart contracts. SDN controllers then dynamically enforce mitigation policies. The proposed framework was implemented in a realistic emulation environment and tested under various attack scenarios. These included external and internal DoS attacks, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, and man-in-the-middle attacks based on ARP and NDP poisoning. Traffic analysis and packet-level observations illustrate how the framework can identify malicious behavior, update device trust levels through blockchain records, and isolate compromised nodes via SDN-based policy enforcement. The results demonstrate the feasibility of coordinated trust management and network-level mitigation in IoT environments.

title: A method for creating visual cues to improve depth perception in remote machine operation. How much help is needed?
authors: Victor Zhidchenko, Egor Startcev, Heikki Handroos
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: The paper describes a method for creating visual aids that might help operators of remotely operated mobile working machines perceive depth in 2D video camera frames. The method uses RGB-D data to create a virtual shadow of the machine manipulator tip on a horizontal surface below the manipulator. The shadow is depicted as a semi-transparent line across the frame. To evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of the proposed approach, an evaluation study was performed using a remotely operated mobile crane. The results of the study are also presented.

title: Solving the Post-Quantum Control Plane Bottleneck: Energy-Aware Cryptographic Scheduling in Open RAN
authors: Neha Gupta, Hamed Alimohammadi, Mohammad Shojafar, De Mi, Muhammad N. M. Bhutta
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) offers flexibility and innovation but introduces unique security vulnerabilities, particularly from cryptographically relevant quantum computers. While Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) is the primary scalable defence, its computationally intensive handshakes create a significant bottleneck for the RAN control plane, posing sustainability challenges. This article proposes an energy-aware framework to solve this PQC bottleneck, ensuring quantum resilience without sacrificing operational energy efficiency. The system employs an O-RAN aligned split: a Crypto Policy rApp residing in the Non-Real- Time (Non-RT) RIC defines the strategic security envelope (including PQC suites), while a Security Operations Scheduling (SOS) xApp in the Near-RT RIC converts these into tactical timing and placement intents. Cryptographic enforcement remains at standards-compliant endpoints: the Open Fronthaul utilizes Media Access Control Security (MACsec) at the O-DU/O-RU, while the xhaul (midhaul and backhaul) utilizes IP Security (IPsec) at tunnel terminators. The SOS xApp reduces PQC overhead by batching non-urgent handshakes, prioritizing session resumption, and selecting parameters that meet slice SLAs while minimizing joules per secure connection. We evaluate the architecture via a Discrete-Event Simulation (DES) using 3GPP-aligned traffic profiles and verified hardware benchmarks from literature. Results show that intelligent scheduling can reduce per-handshake energy by approximately 60% without violating slice latency targets.

title: Context-Awareness for Dependable Low-Power IoT
authors: David E. Ruiz-Guirola, Prasoon Raghuwanshi, Gabriel M. de Jesus, Mateen Ashraf, Onel L. A. Lopez
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Dependability is the ability to consistently deliver trusted and uninterrupted service in the face of operational uncertainties. Ensuring dependable operation in large-scale, energy-constrained Internet of Things (IoT) deployments is as crucial as challenging, and calls for context-aware protocols, where context refers to situational or state information. In this article, we identify four critical context dimensions for IoT networks, namely energy status, information freshness, task relevance, and physical/medium conditions, and show how each one underpins core dependability attributes. Building on these insights, we propose a twostep protocol-design framework that incorporates operation-specific context fields. Through three representative use cases, we demonstrate how context awareness can enhance system dependability by leveraging context-awareness.

title: Hybrid Domain Joint Low-Rank and Sparse Constrained Framework for Simultaneous RFI Separation and SAR Imaging
authors: Jun’ao Li, Zhongyu Li, Jing Yang, Qing Yang, Junjie Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The inherent contradiction between limited spectrum resources and ever-growing spectrum demand makes it difficult for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) to avoid in-band radio frequency interference (RFI) during observation, leading to significant degradation in imaging quality. In this context, RFI suppression technology has become crucial for obtaining high-fidelity SAR images. Existing RFI suppression methods are primarily divided into pre-processing and post-processing categories, both of which have inherent limitations. Pre-processing methods often struggle to achieve sufficient suppression due to the high overlap between target echoes and RFI in the time, frequency, time-frequency and other domains. Post-processing methods, which operate directly on the imaging results, tend to cause loss of image details and weak targets. Motivated by these problems, this paper proposes a hybrid domain joint lowrank and sparse constrained framework for simultaneous RFI separation and SAR imaging. The innovation lies in leveraging the low-rank characteristic of RFI in the echo domain as well as the low-rank and sparse representation of scene targets, while coupling the echo and image domains through the SAR imaging operator to establish a unified inversion model with relational constraints. To solve this model, a deep learning network named SSI-net is constructed by integrating the alternating direction method of multipliers with deep unfolding techniques. Bedisdes, for scenarios with limited or no training samples, a gradient descent-based optimization algorithm is also proposed. Simulations and experiments with measured data demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework.

title: Adaptive Context-Aware Localization Network for Remote Sensing Object Detection Under Haze and Class Imbalance
authors: Yingjun Wang, Xiaopeng Yang, Jiali Wang, Zheng Liang, Wenyi Zhao, Weidong Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Object detection in optical remote sensing imagery is severely challenged by haze degradation and long-tailed category imbalance, which result in blurred appearances, noise interference, and frequent missed detections of minority classes. These challenges hinder robust feature representation, accurate cross-scale localization, and balanced optimisation. To address these issues, we propose ACLNet, a context-aware feature-enhancement and adaptive-localization-optimization framework for robust remote sensing object detection. Specifically, we first propose a context-aware poly-kernel (CAPK) module, which enhances mid- to high-level feature representations via parallel multi-kernel modelling and axial context modulation to compensate for haze-induced detail loss. Subsequently, we introduce a dual-stream dynamic feature fusion (D2F2) mechanism, which dynamically fuses local structural cues with global dependencies, to improve cross-scale alignment and discriminative consistency in complex scenes. Furthermore, we adopt an adaptive differential modulation localization (ADML) loss that suppresses noisy gradients while adaptively emphasising medium-quality and hard samples, thereby enhancing localization stability for small objects and degraded scenes without increasing inference overhead. Experimental results show that ACLNet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the DIOR, RSOD, and Hazy-Det datasets. Additionally, ACLNet is highly deployable with only 10.7 M parameters. The source code will be available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AmorFatio/ACLNet</uri>

title: Improved Endurance in HfZrOₓ-Based Junctionless FeTFTs Using an Asymmetric Dual-Gate Architecture
authors: William Cheng-Yu Ma, Chun-Jung Su, Kuo-Hsing Kao, Ta-Chun Cho, Jhao-Fong Deng, Yen-Lun Hsieh, Huan Hsu, Tzu-Chi Liu, An-De Xu, Che-Hsun Tsai
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: This paper presents an asymmetric dual-gate (ADG) junctionless ferroelectric thin-film transistor (JL-FeTFT) featuring a HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> ferroelectric layer and a heavily doped n⁺ poly-Si channel. By exploiting asymmetric gate stacks and dual-gate biasing, the proposed ADG architecture enables effective redistribution of the vertical electric field across the gate dielectric. The influence of single-gate (SG) and dual-gate (DG) operations on device characteristics and endurance behavior was systematically investigated. The DG configuration provides enhanced electrostatic control and achieves an endurance exceeding 2 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> program/erase cycles, four times that of SG operation, while maintaining stable threshold voltage and transconductance. Energy-band analysis reveals that the asymmetric dual-gate biasing mitigates oxide stress and suppresses trap generation during cycling. Post-cycling transconductance measurements further confirm reduced oxide degradation under DG operation. These results highlight the critical role of asymmetric dual-gate field engineering in improving reliability, demonstrating the ADG JL-FeTFT as a promising candidate for high-endurance, low-voltage nonvolatile memory applications.

title: IEEE VR 2026 International Program Super Committee
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

title: CARGO: Compliant Analytics for Resilient Governance and Orchestration of Maritime Emissions in an Explainable Serverless Framework
authors: Mahtab Shahin, Avleen Malhi, Mahsa Khorasani, Riina Palu, Austin Kim, Sanja Bauk, Ralf-Martin Soe
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Decarbonization of maritime transport requires analytics frameworks capable of processing large heterogeneous datasets while remaining compliant with regulatory standards and explainable. Although current distributed platforms, such as Apache Spark and HPC clusters, offer scalability, they are limited in their interpretability and have high orchestration overheads. In this paper, we present CARGO (Compliant Analytics for Resilient Governance and Orchestration), a serverless framework for monitoring maritime emissions that unifies adaptive orchestration, explainable analytics, and built-in governance. In order to manage maritimeworkloads in real-time,CARGOintegrates Association Rule Mining (ARM) and clustering to identify operational and meteorological drivers of CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> surges, and utilizes AES-256 encryption, TLS 1.3, and role-based access control for secure data governance. With EU MRV CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> reports (2018-2024) coupled with ERA5 meteorology and AIS trajectories, CARGO achieves 42% lower orchestration latency and 25% higher throughput than Apache Spark and cloud-native baselines on 500 GB workloads. Furthermore, the framework provides interpretable association rules linking speed, fuel type, and rainfall to emission anomalies, providing regulatory-readable evidence for compliance decisions. Based on these results, it can be concluded that CARGO provides a deployable foundation for digital twin enabled, climate-aware maritime governance by balancing scalability, cost, and transparency.

title: No-Reference Generic Image Quality Assessment via Adaptive Fusion of Naturalness-Enhanced Colour and Appearance-Driven Spatial Metrics
authors: Muhammad Usman Khan, Imran Mehmood, Ming Ronnier Luo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: No-reference image quality assessment (NR-IQA) remains a challenging problem due to the diverse nature of image distortions and the absence of a reference image. While most existing no-reference image quality metrics (NR-IQMs) mainly target spatial distortions, comparatively few methods address appearance-driven colour renderings, and generalization across heterogeneous datasets remains limited. To address this gap, this study proposes a unified NR-IQA framework that adaptively integrates colour and spatial quality predictions through a distortion-aware fusion strategy. The framework comprises three metrics: (1) CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub>, an enhanced colour-domain image quality metric (IQM) that extends our earlier CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Z</sub> metric by incorporating five naturalness-related statistical and texture descriptors; (2) SIQMZ, a spatial-domain IQM that derives natural scene statistics (NSS) features from the chroma and depth channels of the CIELAB colour space; and (3) GIQMZ, a classifier-guided generic metric that adaptively fuses CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub> and SIQMZ predictions based on rendering-type probabilities. Extensive evaluations were conducted on 17,387 images from seven benchmark datasets using both same-database and cross-database protocols. The results demonstrate that CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub> significantly improves predictive capability over CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Z</sub> for both colour renderings and spatial distortions, SIQMZ achieves competitive performance on spatially distorted datasets with a compact feature set, and GIQMZ provides robust and generalizable performance across mixed-distortion datasets through distortion-aware probabilistic fusion. Although deep learning-based models achieve higher prediction accuracy on large-scale datasets, the proposed framework offers a favourable trade-off between performance, interpretability, and computational efficiency.

title: LTE signal-aided GNSS/PDR System for Mix-pose Pedestrian Positioning in Urban Environments Using Smartphone
authors: Li Cong, Jingnan Tian, Jianbiao Wei
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Smartphone-based positioning is highly promising due to its widespread availability. However, its reliance on global navigation satellite systems (GNSS) leads to accuracy degradation in complex environments such as urban canyons. While integrating GNSS with inertial sensor-based pedestrian dead reckoning (PDR) can improve performance, challenges such as cumulative positioning errors as well as reduced accuracy in step length and heading estimation due to mixed phone poses remain unresolved during GNSS outages. To address these issues, a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) signal-aided GNSS/PDR system is proposed to improve the applicability to complex environments and mixed poses. Firstly, based on the analysis of propagation characteristics of LTE signal, we establish the relationship between the reference signal received power (RSRP) variations and positions to extract position, step length parameter and heading observations. Additionally, theoretical accuracy of the position observations is derived and its influencing factors are analyzed, providing a basis for the design of subsequent availability assessment strategy. Secondly, multiple observations from GNSS are extracted. Thirdly, after compensating for PDR heading offset caused by pose switching, we employ an unscented Kalman filter (UKF) model to fuse PDR with multi-observation from GNSS and LTE signals. An availability assessment strategy is designed to adaptively adjust filter parameters to exclude moments when GNSS or LTE signals are unavailable, which can mitigate the step length and heading errors induced by environments and poses effectively. Experiments conducted at three sites demonstrate that the introduction of LTE signal significantly reduces step length and heading errors, thereby improving positioning performance.

title: Benchmarking Open-Loop Central Pattern Generator Controllers for Lower-Limb Exoskeleton Walking Trajectories
authors: Hod Pery, Tal Ben Eli, Eldad Holdengreber
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Central Pattern Generators (CPGs) enable rhythmic, biologically inspired control of robotic locomotion. This study presents a comparative evaluation of three CPG models: Hopf, Matsuoka, and sinusoidal implemented on a simplified lower-limb exoskeleton with three actuated joints per leg (hip, knee, and ankle). Each controller was simulated in MATLAB and validated using IMU measurements from a physical prototype. All models reproduced the fundamental gait frequency (0.6 Hz), though accuracy varied: hip RMSEs were below 1°, while knee and ankle errors reached up to 19° for Hopf. PCA analysis revealed that Hopf generated clean and stable cyclic patterns, the sinusoidal method produced smooth but less adaptive motion, and Matsuoka introduced richer variability with reduced regularity. Power analysis under loaded and unloaded conditions showed similar average consumption (4.9 W) but distinct stability profiles. These results demonstrate the trade-offs between simplicity, adaptability, and energy robustness in selecting CPG controllers for exoskeleton locomotion.

title: End-To-End Imitation Learning for Socially Aware Navigation using Memory-based Trajectory and Action Planning
authors: Alejandro Olivas, Miguel Ángel Muñoz-Bañón, Fernando Torres
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The increasing deployment of service robots and autonomous vehicles operating in human-populated environments underscores the importance of planning trajectories to ensure pedestrian comfort while optimizing robot travel times. End-to-end models are promising for shortcutting the complex problem of pedestrian detection, trajectory prediction, and robot movement planning by using a single network. Imitation Learning (IL) has been widely adopted in self-driving because it enables implicit understanding of complex environments and expert driving behaviour, whereas socially aware navigation has usually relied on Reinforcement Learning (RL) approaches that learn from abstracted environment representations, prioritizing information relevant to human interaction. In this research, we present Memory-based Trajectory and Action Planning (MTAP), an IL approach extended for socially aware navigation to perform intelligent pedestrian avoidance while maintaining a deeper understanding of the environment and the expert driving behaviour. Our method predicts the trajectory and the control action commands directly from the information of the sensors and the global target, employing a recurrent layer to anticipate pedestrian movements based on past frames. The network is trained and evaluated in the CARLA simulator, where we introduce a benchmark for assessing pedestrian avoidance efficacy, presenting also new social navigation metrics to measure both trajectory efficiency and pedestrian comfort during avoidance maneuvers.

title: Practical Time-Synchronized Model Predictive Control for Energy-Efficient Dynamic Positioning of Fully Actuated Surface Vehicles
authors: Zhihao Yu, Jialu Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: For the robust dynamic positioning (DP) of the fully actuated electric vessel, this paper presents the first practical time-synchronized (TS) Lyapunov-based model predictive control (LMPC) scheme, guaranteeing the practical TS stability and the optimal energy consumption. To ensure TS convergence of vessel states, a slack variable vector of ratio persistence is introduced into the stage cost of LMPC. To guarantee the stability of the presented scheme, a practical TS auxiliary controller based on a TS disturbance observer and adaptive compensators is designed to construct the contractive constraint for LMPC. A chattering-reduction thrust allocation algorithm is designed to smooth the thrust force commands. Supported by theoretical analyses of the feasibility and stability, all states of the closed-loop DP control system under the proposed scheme converge to their desired values with a synchronized settling time. Consequently, the vessel under the proposed scheme can precisely maneuver with optimized trajectories and reduced thruster power consumption. Comparative hardware-in-the-loop simulations on a model-scale electric vessel demonstrate the precise DP performance and the energy-saving capability of the proposed scheme.

title: Attenuation Correction for Flexible MR Hardware in PET/MR Using MLAA-Guided Template Registration
authors: Nicole Jurjew, Simon Wan, Paul Schleyer, Mohammadreza Teimoorisichani, Cameron Anderson, Marilena Rega, Alexandra Mackenzie, David Atkinson, Kris Thielemans
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Flexible Magnetic Resonance (Imaging) (MR) hardware, such as body coils, can cause substantial attenuation of the Positron Emission Tomography (PET) signal in PET/MR imaging. Despite this, such hardware is typically not accounted for in clinical attenuation correction (AC), as its variable positioning and invisibility in standard MR sequences present significant challenges. In this study, we investigate the use of Maximum Likelihood Estimation of Attenuation and Activity (MLAA) guided registration of template hardware attenuation maps (μ-maps) to support AC for flexible hardware on PET data without Time Of Flight (TOF) information. Assuming the patient or phantom attenuation as known, attenuation maps beyond the patient outline were reconstructed from emission data and used as reference images for the non-rigid registration of pre-defined hardware template attenuation maps. The approach was evaluated and compared to the performance of MLAA-reconstructed attenuation alone on both phantom and patient data acquired on the Siemens Biograph mMR, including <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-Fluorodeoxyglucose (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG) and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">68</sup>Ga-DOTATATE tracers, and at various count levels. For the phantom data, both MLAA-only and MLAA-guided registration of template hardware attenuation maps improved quantification. Registration-based correction generally resulted in closer agreement with the ground truth, with residual errors of up to ≈5% that were largely localized, while MLAA-only reconstructions showed deviations of up to ≈10%, tending to be more spatially extended. The method also tolerates moderate errors in MR-based AC and non-physical deformations of the hardware template, with quantification most sensitive to gross misplacement of the hardware. These findings suggest that MLAA-guided registration offers a practical and effective solution to address the current gap in attenuation correction for flexible hardware in clinical PET/MR imaging, also in non-TOF settings.

title: Dual-Mode LSPR Biosensing of DNA and SARS-CoV-2 Spike Protein using Gold Nanorod-Coated Hetero-core Fiber
authors: Ai Hosoki
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: A gold nanorod (AuNR)–coated hetero-core optical-fiber LSPR biosensor was designed and experimentally demonstrated for label-free detection of complementary ssDNA and the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. The sensor consists of a hetero-core fiber segment onto which AuNRs were directly immobilized, exhibiting two plasmon resonance modes. DNA recognition was achieved by introducing complementary ssDNA to the AuNR-coated fiber, which resulted in clear absorbance responses in both modes across the examined concentration range. The longitudinal mode achieved a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.11 pM. For SARS-CoV-2 spike protein detection, the sensor functionalized with the S1-binding aptamer yielded a LOD of 19.1 pM in the transverse mode. Control tests with non-complementary ssDNA verified molecular specificity. Dissociation constant (KD) analysis further revealed mode-dependent binding characteristics: the longitudinal mode exhibited a smaller KD for DNA hybridization, whereas the transverse mode showed a smaller KD for S1 binding. These KD tendencies were in reasonable agreement with the mode-dependent LOD performance observed in each assay. These results demonstrate that the AuNR-coated hetero-core fiber serves as a simple and mechanically robust platform for implementing LSPR-based biosensing, enabling label-free detection of both nucleic-acid and protein targets within a compact fiber-optic format.

title: A Random PWM Technique to Reduce Switching Frequency Harmonics for Induction Motor Drives
authors: Shaya Abou Jawdeh, Pengwei Li Pengwei, Ali Bazzi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Conventional PWM techniques are widely used in electric drive systems. They generate substantial harmonic content in the output current waveform at the switching frequency, its harmonics, and sidebands. These harmonics significantly contribute to high frequency noise, mechanical vibrations, and electromagnetic interference (EMI), both conducted and radiated, potentially disrupting the performance of nearby systems and equipment. To mitigate the high-frequency harmonics in the output current, this paper proposes a novel random PWM technique for electric drives. The approach is based on generating different random frequencies based on a Weiner process for each phase leg of the inverter. The aim is to spread the output current harmonic spectrum and minimize the amplitude of high frequency harmonics, thereby simplifying the filtering process for enhancing power quality, reducing EMI, and addressing related problems. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is assessed through key metrics including the output current harmonic power density spectrum, total harmonic distortion and the common-mode voltage. Results are obtained using MATLAB/Simulink simulations, and experimental validation which are then analyzed and compared against other PWM techniques.

title: Multi-Port Segmented Event-Triggered Control for USV-UAV Cooperative System: A VO-driven Dynamic Cross-Bridge Strategy
authors: Guoqing Zhang, Guipeng Yao, Jiqiang Li, Shaocheng Tong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In the cruising mission of the unmanned surface vessel-unmanned aerial vehicle (USV-UAV) cooperative plant, although the UAV is capable of executing aerial maneuvers, it still faces inevitable obstacle avoidance and control challenges in narrow waterways and cross-bridge scenarios. As such, this study proposes a variable-speed multi-port segmented threshold event-triggered cooperative collision avoidance control algorithm based on the geometric velocity obstacle method (GVO). In the guidance module, first define the obstacle zone based on the detected bridge dimensions. Then, by setting safety thresholds and target points, implement a bridge collision avoidance guidance function with an automatic selection mechanism for the narrow waterway navigation mission. In the control module, in the presence of bridge obstacles, a variable-speed multi-port segmented threshold event triggering mechanism (MSETM) is designed for path tracking and collision avoidance missions to ensure the coordination of the USV-UAV cooperative plant at the beginning and end of collision avoidance. Furthermore, a multi-layer neural networks (MNNs) is adopted to approximate the system’s nonlinearities, achieving high approximation accuracy and computational efficiency. Stability of the algorithm is proven using Lyapunov stability theory, and its effectiveness is validated through simulation experiments.

title: An FPGA-Calibrated Modulo ADC for High-Dynamic-Range Signal Acquisition
authors: Zeyuan Li, Wenyi Yan, Lu Gan, Guoquan Li, Hongqing Liu
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Circuits and Systems
abstract: Conventional analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) suffer from irreversible clipping when input amplitudes exceed their fixed full-scale range. Modulo ADCs address this by folding the input signal prior to quantization; however, stable hardware operation under deep-folding conditions and experimental validation of algorithmic recoverability remain outstanding challenges. This paper presents a calibrated FPGA-based modulo ADC platform in which a finite state machine with multi-bit update control governs folding dynamics, replacing instantaneous comparator-triggered feedback to ensure deterministic and stable operation. A controlled under-compensation calibration strategy converts fold-dependent threshold mismatch and oscillatory instability into a bounded constant residual, enabling reliable folding at high folding depths. The platform achieves a dynamic range expansion exceeding two orders of magnitude while maintaining signal fidelity comparable to that of the standalone ADC. Experimental validation across diverse waveforms confirms robust signal acquisition and demonstrates the practical feasibility of dynamic-range extension using modulo sampling principles.

title: Discrete Fuzzy Approximation-Based Sliding Mode Current Control for Torque Ripple Suppression in Switched Reluctance Motors
authors: Xijian Lin, Shuo Zhang, Zifeng Chen, Huayu Ji, Dianxun Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Switched reluctance motors exhibit strong nonlinear magnetic characteristics, which make accurate current control challenging and lead to pronounced torque ripple. This article presents a discrete-time adaptive fuzzy sliding mode controller designed to achieve robust current tracking under model uncertainties and time-varying behavior. An integral sliding mode surface is then constructed, and a single-input fuzzy system with Gaussian membership functions is employed to approximate the ideal control law. Unlike conventional fuzzy controllers with fixed parameters, the proposed method updates the fuzzy vector parameters online using a Lyapunov-based adaptive law. In addition, an adaptive high-frequency switching gain is developed to compensate for residual approximation errors while alleviating chattering. The overall controller is derived and analyzed entirely in the discrete-time domain, enabling direct implementation on digital platforms. Experimental results demonstrate improved current tracking accuracy and significant torque ripple reduction.

title: Diverse Style Prompt Assistant for Generalized Remote Sensing Object Classification_supp1-3693680.pdf
authors: Wenda Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: Due to the existence of domain shift between different remote sensing datasets, remote sensing object classification models trained on the source domain often face the challenge of reduced accuracy when applied to unseen domains. In this paper, we propose a diverse style prompt assistant for generalized remote sensing object classification. We utilize the powerful knowledge reserve of the CLIP model to solve the domain generalization problem from the perspective of text prompts. CLIP’s text features simulate feature-space style diversity, and then diverse styles guide the classification model to generate general image features. Specifically, we first implement a style diversity loss and maximum entropy loss to train prompts to obtain multiple style prompts. Then, we introduce the style prompts to guide the optimization process of image features cross-modally within the joint feature space, enabling the classification model to generate domain-invariant features. We conduct extensive experiments on three remote sensing datasets, and the results show that our method achieves superior generalization performance compared with the state of the arts. Codes are available at https://github.com/Jia-dd/DSPA.

title: A Stochastic Game-Theoretic Framework for Robust Ventilator Safety: The Trust Gate Mechanism and Life Confidence Score (RLCS)
authors: Bedadipta Bain
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Mechanical ventilation requires precise synchronization between patient effort and machine actuation to prevent Ventilator-Induced Lung Injury (VILI). Conventional trigger mechanisms, typically reliant on single-sensor thresholds, differ in their ability to distinguish valid respiratory effort from sensor artifacts or complex asynchronous events such as Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA). This paper proposes a novel computational safety architecture, the Robust Life Confidence Score (RLCS), which reformulates physiological state estimation as a non-cooperative potential game. We introduce a hierarchical decision framework comprising two layers: (1) A Nash Equilibrium Controller that dynamically penalizes the global confidence score based on this variance, and (2) A Bayesian "Trust Gate" that utilizes the ensemble variance of normalized sensor beliefs to calculate a univariate Mahalanobis-type Z-score distance from consensus, effectively isolating non-cooperative (faulty) sensors. Crucially, by utilizing static reliability weights derived from hardware specifications, the system achieves a theoretical breakdown point of 50%, preventing dominance by high-variance outliers. Experimental validation on a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testbed using an ESP32 microcontroller confirms the computational feasibility of the algorithm, demonstrating sub-100ms execution latency and deterministic fault rejection under simulated conflict scenarios. These results offer a viable pathway for implementing intelligent, low-cost safety interlocks in resource-constrained medical cyber-physical systems.

title: Timing Yield-Driven Clock Skew Scheduling Based on Hybrid Distributions
authors: Kaixiang Zhu, Jiangnan Li, Lingli Wang, Wai-Shing Luk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Clock skew scheduling is a cost-effective technique for enhancing synchronous digital VLSI systems. The technique simply involves adjusting the clock skew to meet signal path timing constraints in order to increase the clock frequency or timing yield. Traditionally, Gaussian distributions have been assumed for path delays under process variations. However, this assumption may be inappropriate due to the notable asymmetry of actual path delay distributions. In this paper, we propose the generalized extreme value (GEV) distribution as a potential alternative, along with a hybrid distribution approach that utilizes different distributions for different path delays to improve accuracy. We also analyze three parameter estimation methods: Maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), L-moments, and the method of moments (MoM) to evaluate the accuracy of the fit. Our experimental results show that the GEV distribution more accurately approximates path delays in benchmark circuits, achieving an average improvement of 40% and 30% in the Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) statistic compared to the Gaussian and Lognormal distributions, respectively. In addition, Q-Q (Quantile-Quantile) and P-P (Probability-Probability) plots are employed to visually validate the goodness-of-fit for different distributions. For timing yield-driven CSS, the hybrid method achieves the highest performance, improving yield by an average of 6% over the Gaussian model and by 2% over the GEV-based model. These results demonstrate that the hybrid distribution approach effectively captures heterogeneous path behavior and provides robust yield optimization.

title: Robust Pitch Control for Floating Offshore Wind Turbines Using LLF-Based Aerodynamic Modeling and Nonsingular Terminal Sliding Mode With Prescribed Performance
authors: Hao Chen, Zijian Luo, Youming Cai, Jingang Han, Nadia Aït-Ahmed, Mohamed Benbouzid
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: Floating offshore wind turbines exhibit complex and highly nonlinear dynamics, making the development of accurate mathematical models particularly challenging. Under substantial external disturbances, conventional control strategies often fall short in delivering fast and precise responses. To address the difficulties associated with modeling aerodynamic characteristics, this study employs extensive simulation data from the standalone AeroDyn module to construct a representative aerodynamic response surface. A locally linear fitting technique is adopted to approximate this surface, resulting in an accurate aerodynamic model. To further mitigate the influence of modeling errors and improve system robustness, a novel pitch control scheme is developed by integrating prescribed performance control (PPC) with nonsingular terminal sliding mode (NTSM) control, referred to as the NTSM-PPC controller. This approach not only enhances tracking accuracy but also circumvents the singularity problems inherent in traditional sliding mode controllers. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through cosimulations on MATLAB/Simulink and OpenFAST platforms. Simulation results confirm that the NTSM-PPC controller significantly enhances output power stability and suppresses fluctuations. Specifically, the controller’s root mean square value of 5.00001236 MW is closest to the rated power, with a power variation coefficient of only 0.01%, outperforming the gain scheduled proportional integral (GSPI) (0.57%) and other controllers such as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{\bm{C}}_{\bm{p}}^{\rm{exp}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-SMC, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{{\bm{\hat{C}}}}_{\bm{p}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-SMC, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{{\bm{\hat{C}}}}_{\bm{p}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-PPC-SMC.

title: FPGA-Accelerated Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Networks for Dynamic Terahertz Beam Steering
authors: Maya B Dhone, Uday Kumar Gundagani, Pravallika Koppula, Muppu Prathyusha, Mallareddy Adudhodla, Pavan Kumar Reddy Manellore
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Advanced terahertz communication systems demand sophisticated beam steering capabilities to optimize signal quality and coverage in dynamic environments. This work presents an FPGA-accelerated spatiotemporal graph neural network framework for real-time terahertz beam steering optimization. The proposed system models antenna arrays and propagation channels as dynamic graphs, capturing spatial relationships between array elements and temporal evolution of channel conditions. A specialized graph neural network architecture processes spatiotemporal features to predict optimal beam directions and power allocations. FPGA implementation achieves ultra-low latency processing through parallelized matrix operations and optimized memory hierarchies. Experimental validation demonstrates beam steering accuracy exceeding 94% with processing latency below 100 microseconds. The framework adapts to mobile scenarios and environmental changes, maintaining optimal beam alignment under varying conditions. Results indicate 35% improvement in signal-to-noise ratio compared to conventional beam steering methods, while reducing computational overhead by 60% through hardware acceleration. The integrated approach enables real-time optimization for next-generation terahertz wireless systems.

title: Smart Indicator Turn Off System for Forgotten Appliances
authors: M Premalatha, PoovishRaja D, Vanaraj K, Vishnu A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Two-wheeler users are also very numerous; they rely on manual indicator switches when trying to convey their intentions of turning at the road. On the majority of traditional bikes, an indicator has to be turned off by the user after making a turn, and a rider may simply forget when it happens, giving wrong signals to the other cars, and posing the threat of a crash. As such, the main objective of this project is to create an automatic indicator OFF system in the two-wheelers that enhance road safety through minimization of human error and workload in the hands of the riders. The proposed system will control indicators using independent push buttons and automatically cancel them depending on the replace motion of the handle bar after turning. Handle stabilization is detected by mechanical clip switches to produce a cancellation signal when the handle is pushed in the anti-direction. A microcontroller receives the input signals and drives the indicator lamps by a relay driver circuit in order to provide safe electrical isolation. To ensure a stable operation of the control unit and switching circuitry, a controlled power supply is applied. The system designed is a reproduction of the auto-cancel indicator mechanism applied on cars but the system will be inexpensive, easy, and applicable in the practically applicable two-wheeler-based riders to enhance traffic safety and comfort to the rider.

title: A Microcontroller-based Intelligent Shopping Cart with Automated Navigation and Real-Time Billing
authors: Chava Sunil Kumar, Sujatha Banka, Regula Anjali, Nakka Harshitha, Jonnana Poojitha, Chinnavula Sushmitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The objective of this work is to design an intelligent, automated shopping cart that traces customers on its own and enhances the shopping experience. Traditional shopping requires customers to push carts manually and wait in billing queues, which is time-consuming and inconvenient. Since one need not push the cart by hand, time is conserved; physical energy is reduced, hence providing an easy and comfortable shopping experience. Sensors such as an ultrasonic sensor are installed in the cart, which detect the customer and follows, effectively avoiding obstacles. Power to the wheels is provided through two DC motors controlled by an Arduino Nano controller, thus ensuring a smooth and power-saving movement of the cart. In this project, an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module is used for managing wireless communication in RFID - based billing, which makes it easier to transfer data to the distant server or billing system to provide a modern and automated retail solution. The system demonstrates near 100% accuracy in RFID detection with a latency of 1 - 2 seconds for billing updates and reliable performance. Overall the proposed system reduces human effort, minimizes billing time and provides an user-friendly and efficient solution.

title: An Efficient and Verifiable Multi-Keyword Search Scheme with Forward Security
authors: Nianyi Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of cloud storage, privacy security issues arising from data outsourcing have become increasingly prominent. Searchable encryption technology allows users to perform keyword queries over encrypted data, yet most existing schemes suffer from limited functionality, low efficiency, or insufficient security. This paper proposes an efficient and verifiable multi-keyword searchable scheme supporting forward security. The scheme adopts a "filter-then-verify" query strategy, selecting the least frequent keyword as the s-term to achieve sublinear query efficiency; designs a hash accumulation-based verification mechanism to ensure the integrity and correctness of server-returned results; and implements forward security through counter-driven dynamic address updates to resist file injection attacks. Experimental results on a dataset of 10,000 documents demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves significant improvements in both single-keyword and multi-keyword query efficiency, validating its effectiveness and superiority.

title: EAG-SR: Entity Association-Guided Self-Reflection for Document-Level Enterprise Relation Extraction
authors: Xiangfeng Luo, Mengyu Han
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Document-level relation extraction (DocRE) aims to extract semantic relations between entities from an entire document. Recent large language model (LLM)-based approaches typically identify relations by filtering all potential entity pairs within a document and then performing triplet fact extraction. However, long documents often contain substantial redundant information that can obscure key relational evidence. Moreover, existing methods usually process all candidate entity pairs simultaneously, introducing noisy contextual representations and inefficient filtering of candidate relations. In addition, the lack of verification mechanisms makes LLMs prone to generating hallucinated relations. To address these challenges, we propose EAG-SR, a novel framework featuring Entity Association Guidance and Self-Reflection. Specifically, we design an entity-enhanced summarization module to emphasize entity-centric semantic information and reduce irrelevant context. We further introduce an entity-pair level retrieval module to efficiently filter candidate relations by retrieving relevant evidence for each entity pair. Finally, a self-reflection mechanism is incorporated to verify extracted relations and suppress hallucinations generated by LLMs. Experiments on two benchmark datasets, DocRED and DWIE, demonstrate that EAG-SR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an F1 score of 62.57% on DocRED.

title: DiffTKGR: Dual-Branch Conditional Diffusion for Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning
authors: Guancheng Tian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Temporal Knowledge Graphs (TKGs) serve as a pivotal framework for modeling dynamic relational data, unlocking immense research value by enabling precise event prediction and strategic forecasting in rapidly evolving real-world scenarios. However, current most temporal knowledge graph reasoning (TKGR) methods face two critical challenges: the multi-modal nature of future events which deterministic models fail to capture, and the complexity of multi-scale temporal dependencies ranging from sudden bursts to long-term cycles. Drawing upon recent advances in generative modeling, we propose DiffTKGR, a novel framework that reformulates TKGR as a conditional generation problem in a continuous embedding space. Unlike existing discriminative methods that output single point estimates, DiffTKGR employs a dual-branch conditional diffusion process to explicitly model the probability distribution of future entities. Specifically, we design a Local Evolution Branch utilizing Relational Graph Convolutional Network (R-GCN) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) to capture short-term dynamic shifts, and a Global Periodic Branch that mines historical repetition patterns via candidate set pooling. These branches serve as conditions to guide a reverse denoising process, reconstructing diverse plausible futures from Gaussian noise. An Adaptive Gated Fusion mechanism dynamically weights these branches based on query characteristics. Extensive experiments on five benchmarks, demonstrate that DiffTKGR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving an average MRR improvement of over 2.0%. Notably, case studies reveal that DiffTKGR successfully captures multi-modal outcomes where deterministic models exhibit over-confidence and miss valid alternatives, validating its superiority in reasoning under uncertainty.

title: Electricity Theft Detection using GA-Optimized Random Forest
authors: M. Reha Sai, P. V. Divya Sree, Y. B. Ganga Lohitha, N. Neelima, T. V. Smitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The ongoing global issue of electricity loss through theft continues to cause major financial losses for power utilities and instability to the electric grid. A hybrid detection framework is presented for identifying non-technical revenue losses in utility billing systems through smart meter data. The proposed detection framework incorporates class imbalance correction, evolutionary optimization, and ensemble learning techniques. To tackle the problem of class imbalance, Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) is applied to increase the number of minority class instances. Optimal feature selection and hyperparameter tuning were performed using a Genetic Algorithm. A Random Forest model was then used in the final classification of billing losses. The evaluation of this proposed framework was done by using benchmark datasets and resulted in improved performance metrics such as accuracy, balanced accuracy, Matthews correlation coefficient, ROC–AUC and fewer false positive alarms in comparison with current Machine Learning and Deep Learning approaches. The proposed framework is modular, scalable, resilient, and suitable for real-world deployment.

title: Empirical Study of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Detection based on Ultrasound Images using Deep Learning
authors: Pavithra B, Dr.L.Lakshmanan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is one of the most common endocrine disorders affecting women of reproductive age and is a major cause of anovulatory infertility. Ultrasound imaging plays an important role in identifying ovarian cysts and supporting clinical diagnosis. However, manual interpretation of ultrasound images may lead to variability due to operator dependence. Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have enabled the development of computer-aided diagnostic systems that can improve the reliability and efficiency of PCOS detection. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of existing methodologies for PCOS detection, focusing on traditional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches. A total of 25 research articles is analyzed and compared based on datasets, analytical metrics, tools, and reported performance. The survey also examines commonly used databases and evaluates the strengths and limitations of existing techniques. Furthermore, key challenges and potential future research directions in AI-based PCOS detection are discussed.

title: From Activation to Semantics: Explaining Neurons through Their Contributions to Model Outputs
authors: Yuan Sun, Fengsheng Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Despite the significant progress achieved by large generative language models across a wide range of tasks, their internal reasoning and decision-making mechanisms remain highly opaque. In recent years, automated interpretability methods based on neuron activation patterns have provided new avenues for understanding the internal semantics of such models; however, existing approaches still exhibit limitations in the precision of high-activation token selection and in the effective validation of neuronal contributions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a neuron analysis framework that combines a high-activation token selection strategy based on maximum neuron activation values with a RelP-based attribution verification mechanism, aiming to systematically analyze the relationship between neuron activation patterns and model output decisions. By performing activation-level filtering and attribution verification at the neuron level, the proposed method effectively enhances the correlation between selected tokens and model outputs, as well as the reliability of the resulting interpretations. Experimental results demonstrate that this framework can stably identify key neurons that exert a significant influence on model outputs, along with their corresponding semantic triggering patterns, thereby providing a feasible pathway for understanding the fine-grained decision structures within generative language models. Overall, this study validates the effectiveness and generality of neuron-level interpretability methods that integrate activation-based selection with attribution analysis for model behavior investigation.

title: Smart Agricultural Hub: Intelligent Decision Supporting AI Multilingual Farmer Interface
authors: Hemakumar L, Dhanush S, J. Cruz Antony
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Modern agriculture is facing several challenges like choosing the wrong crops, using fertilizers in the wrong way, not detecting plant diseases early enough, and not having access to expert advice. To help with these issues, this paper introduces Smart AgriHub, a new agricultural decision support system that uses artificial intelligence. It combines machine learning, computer vision, and generative AI to help farmers make better choices based on data. The system uses a Rotation Forest model to suggest which crops and fertilizers are best suited based on soil and environmental data like nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, temperature, humidity, and rainfall. A computer vision model trained using Roboflow helps detect plant diseases from leaf images and estimates how severe the infection is using HSV-based segmentation. The platform also includes a chatbot that gives real-time advice to farmers in their local languages. Smart AgriHub is built as a full-stack web app using FastAPI on the backend and React for the user interface. It also has a digital marketplace where farmers can connect with suppliers and buyers. The results show that the system works well, achieving 94.2% accuracy in crop recommendations and 93.7% accuracy in disease detection, along with high satisfaction from users in advisory interactions. The platform offers a scalable and practical way to bring modern precision agriculture technologies into traditional farming practices.

title: From Awareness to Creation: Shaping Nursing Students' Job-hunting skills through Blended AI Resume Approach
authors: Shaoxian Pan, Yongqiao Li, Wenbin Liang, Junjie Gavin Wu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Nursing students generally lack resume writing skills, and traditional teaching has limitations, such as delayed feedback and a lack of personalization. Generative artificial intelligence offers technological potential for resume instruction.To explore the effect of blended artificial intelligence resume instruction on improving nursing students' career perception and resume writing quality, and to examine the relationship between them.A pretest-posttest nonequivalent control group quasi-experimental design was adopted. Two parallel second-year classes from a vocational medical college were randomly assigned to an experimental group (n = 59) and a control group (n = 61). The experimental group received a blended AI Resume (AIR), while the control group received traditional lectures.1. The post-test total score and scores in all dimensions of career awareness in the experimental group were significantly higher than those in the control group (p<0.01). 2. The improvement in the post-test total score of the resume in the experimental group was significantly greater than in the control group. 3. Pearson correlation analysis showed that career awareness and resume scores had only a weak linear correlation (|r|<0.3), but the random forest model revealed a strong nonlinear predictive relationship between the two (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.746 for the experimental group, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.809 for the control group).The Blended AIR approach can effectively enhance nursing students' career awareness and resume quality. The impact of career awareness on resume quality is a complex, nonlinear, synergistic effect rather than a simple linear correlation. It is recommended that future teaching reforms integrate qualitative analysis to reveal the underlying mechanisms.

title: EMBC Special Issue: Advancing Electrolaryngeal Speech Enhancement Through Speech–Text Representation Learning
authors: Ding Ma, Jinyi Mi, Fengji Li, Lester Phillip Violeta, Jiajun He, Wenchin Huang, Kazuhiro Kobayashi, Tomoki Toda
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: laryngectomees depend on an electromechanical device to generate electrolaryngeal (EL) speech for verbal communication. Compared with normal speech, EL speech suffers from severe distortion, limited phonetic variation, unnatural prosody, and temporal shifts, degrading naturalness and intelligibility. Although sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) voice conversion (VC) based EL-speech-to-normal-speech conversion (EL2SP) is promising, substantial mismatches between EL and normal speech inevitably cause cumulative mapping errors that limit performance. To address this, we describe a novel representation learning framework integrating speech and text representations to improve mapping and reconstruction quality within a seq2seq VC model. Methods: our methodology comprises two main stages: 1) representation integration and learning, and 2) reconstruction training. A network capable of incorporating auxiliary text information is first constructed with pretrained modules to learn speech–text-based integrated representations. Then, an autoencoder-style reconstruction strategy finalizes EL2SP model to inherit these representations without increasing model complexity. Additional optimization designs are performed across these stages. We introduce three fusion strategies including middle-, input-, and hybrid-level fusion strategies that progressively enhance learning. Moreover, besides standard seq2seq VC objectives, an additional reconstruction loss on the integrated representation is introduced to refine representation transfer. Results: experiments under different EL2SP datasets consistently demonstrate that our methods, combined with data augmentations, outperform baselines relying solely on speech representations regarding both conversion quality and intelligibility. Furthermore, progressive improvements with system design depth validate the effectiveness of our methods. Significance: the proposed methods provide an extensible and practical methodology for EL speech enhancement and assistive communication technologies.

title: Digital Twin Architecture for Drone Logistics Operations
authors: Pachayappan Murugaiyan, Tanmoy Kundu, Stephan M. Wagner, Balan Sundarakani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: This study proposes a digital twin (DT) model for drone logistics systems. While DT models typically rely on real-time data from physical assets, identifying and structuring such parameters for drone logistics remains underdeveloped in current literature. Existing studies primarily focus on routing and fleet optimization, offering limited guidance on the systematic design of DT architectures for drone operations. To address this gap, this work conducts an evidence-based, structured review of 116 drone logistics models to extract and consolidate key operational parameters, resulting in the development of a Drone Logistics Parameter Framework (DLPF). Building on this foundation, the study introduces the Drone Logistics Digital Twin (DLDT) architecture and model. The DLDT is a five-dimensional system consisting of a physical entity, a virtual entity, a service interface, a data layer, and connection layers. The DLDT enables essential capabilities, including real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, route optimization, last-mile delivery management, and scheduling. To validate the DLDT architecture and model, a route-optimization case demonstration within the DT environment illustrates their internal coherence and practical utility. Hence, this study contributes to a structured approach for designing DT models in drone logistics, addressing a notable gap in the literature and offering actionable insights for advancing intelligent, data-driven logistics operations.

title: Rethinking AI Literacy in L2 Writing: Empirical Evidence from Chinese University Students
authors: Zhao FANG, Stephen E. Sandelius
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study examined the current status of AI literacy in L2 writing among Chinese university students and explored how existing AI writing literacy frameworks and measurement scales could be refined. Adopting a mixed-methods design, the study drew on quantitative data from a questionnaire survey for hierarchical regression analysis and qualitative data from reflective journals for thematic analysis. Results indicated that while students generally reported moderate to high levels of AI writing literacy, only the Understanding and Use dimensions significantly predicted their performance in AI-assisted writing tasks. Evaluation and Ethics, though rated positively, showed limited influence on actual writing outcomes. Thematic analysis further revealed students’ strong reliance on AI for language-level support but a lack of attention to discoursal or ethical aspects. More notably, it also disclosed the underestimation of the nuances of affective components and the overlooking of regulatory and regional constraints within the existing AI literacy frameworks. The study calls for a more student-centered approach to conceptualizing and assessing AI writing literacy, one that better reflects learners’ lived experiences and diverse needs.

title: STAR-RIS–Assisted UAV Communications for Dynamic Spectrum Sharing in NOMA Under Imperfect SIC: Enabling Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Ali Nazari, Ali Olfat
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The simultaneous transmission and reflection of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) enhances the spectrum efficiency (SE) of primary users (PUs) and secondary users (SUs) in cognitive radio (CR) networks by mitigating interference from incident signals. The STAR-RIS-assisted unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) can fully cover the environment due to its high mobility and fast deployment. According to the dynamic air-to-ground channels, the STAR-RIS-assisted UAV faces a challenge in determining the coefficient of the element's role during flight. To meet the requirements of dynamic channel determination with the SE approach, this paper proposes the sum rate maximization of both PUs and SUs through non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) in a CR network to jointly optimize the trajectory and transmission-reflection beamforming design of the STAR-RIS-assisted UAV, and power allocation under the imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). Since the non-convex joint optimization problem includes coupled variables, we develop an alternative optimization algorithm. Simulation results examine the impact of parameters on fixed and mobile users, evaluate the performance of intelligent surface modes and operating protocols, and highlight the advantages of adopted NOMA (under perfect/imperfect SIC) over OMA. The gap analysis confirms that our proposed dynamic system model numerically outperforms the benchmarks.

title: Research on the Pre-Electromagnetic Incremental Forming Process: Principle, Dynamic Deformation Behavior, and Experimental Validation
authors: Yang Hu, Zhipeng Lai, Changxin Li, Wei Xu, Liang Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Electromagnetic incremental forming (EMIF) accumulates localized small deformations into a large overall deformation, thereby reducing the required energy per discharge and the size of the forming device. However, the EMIF of largescale thin-walled shell components currently suffers from low forming controllability and a limited depth-to-diameter ratio, which makes it difficult to satisfy the forming requirements in aerospace and related fields. In this study, a pre-electromagnetic incremental forming process (PEMIF) is proposed, in which the workpiece is preformed into a conical geometry with a prescribed spatial curvature prior to EMIF. A finite element model was established to investigate the dynamic deformation behavior of the preformed conical workpiece in comparison with that of a flat workpiece. The results indicate that preforming significantly shortens the oscillatory deformation process and reduces the proportion of elastic deformation. Moreover, the localized deformation configuration and the associated work-hardening effect further suppress deformation in the non-loaded regions, leading to a pronounced improvement in forming controllability. A 300 mm -scale PEMIF experimental platform was developed to validate the proposed process. The experimental results show that a forming depth of 95 mm was achieved, increasing the depth-to-diameter ratio, while the maximum die-fitting deviation was less than 1.5 mm. High-precision, high-quality forming of 300 mm -scale thin-walled shell components was thus successfully realized. The present study provides a basis for regulating the forming behavior and designing forming strategies for large-scale thin-walled shell components.

title: Attention Level Classification Method Based on Uncertainty-Aware Pseudo-Label Optimization
authors: Yitong Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: We propose a pseudo-label optimization framework for semi-supervised EEG-based attention level classification, integrating entropy-aware adaptive thresholding with prototype similarity calibration. The method employs an entropy-driven dual-threshold mechanism to dynamically filter hard and soft pseudo-labels, and further refines them via class prototype similarity with entropy-weighted fusion. A hybrid loss combining KL divergence and mean squared error is used to balance alignment and precision. Experiments on a driving simulation EEG dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 90.61 percent accuracy with only 10 percent labeled data, outperforming existing methods and exhibiting strong robustness to individual variability and low signal-to-noise ratios. This work provides an effective solution for leveraging unlabeled data in cognitive state monitoring.

title: LEC-SFdeepnet: Multi-Scale and Detail-Enhanced DeepLabv3+ for Semantic Segmentation
authors: Rui Wang, Hongjun Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Across domains such as intelligent autonomous driving, intelligent road planning, intelligent urban traffic, and road environment detection, street-view semantic segmentation faces challenges regarding small targets, boundary details, and multi-scale adaptation-particularly with small and medium-sized urban street-view datasets. Existing lightweight segmentation networks often suffer from deficiencies in semantic expression or boundary refinement. Thus, a novel semantic segmentation model-LEC-SFdeepnet-is proposed based on the DeepLabv3+ architecture. The model innovatively integrates LMSAConv and EM supplemented by innovative dual-channel attention fusion of CRAM and ASPP. An SE block is inserted into the main channel, while a two-level gated fusion is incorporated in the decoder. Validated on the m -city dataset-comprising 4,000 images from diverse cities and derived from Cityscapes. the proposed method demonstrates remarkable effectiveness, significantly enhancing mIoU, boundary quality, and prediction accuracy. Serving as a general-purpose enhancement plugin for lightweight semantic segmentation, it is applicable to autonomous driving scenarios and small-to-medium-sized datasets.

title: Aggregate Modeling of Power System Frequency Response and Sensitivity Analysis of Deep Peak Regulation Under DC Bipolar Fault
authors: Teng Zhu, Chuan Qin, YuJie Xue, Lu Tang, WenLe Ding, JinZe Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Driven by the “carbon neutrality” target, the burgeoning power system increasingly exhibits “high proportion of renewable energy and power electronics” characteristics, leading to a continuous reduction in equivalent system inertia. Consequently, the demand for frequency security and rapid assessment under low-inertia conditions has become more prominent. To balance physical mechanisms with computational efficiency, this paper develops an aggregate simplified model of frequency response for the North China Power Grid. System equivalent inertia time constants and damping coefficients are obtained through parameter aggregation. Furthermore, the governor systems of steam and hydraulic turbine units with primary frequency control capabilities are aggregated separately, incorporating nonlinear elements such as governor limits and dead bands. The proposed model is validated against the detailed model of the North China Power Grid. Under scenarios where DC bipolar blocking results in power losses of $4,000 \mathrm{MW}$ and $\mathbf{9, 0 0 0 ~ M W}$, the aggregate model maintains consistency with the detailed model regarding both the frequency nadir and steady-state frequency. On this basis, this paper further establishes various deep peak regulation proportion scenarios within the aggregate model to conduct comparative frequency response experiments. These tests evaluate the impact of operational mode variations on the system’s frequency support capability and the trends of key indicators, providing model support for the safety checking and rapid assessment of deep peak regulation.
