publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) ecosystem has produced complicated security concerns that require a modern Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that can respond to emerging threats and maintain data protection. The dynamic behavior of clients and the heterogeneity of the system can affect the convergence and performance of models in federated learning for IoV security. Traditional federated learning may overlook data quality, computational resources, and client confidence. This issue is resolved by behavioral digital twin technology, which generates virtual replicas of real-world IoV clients that continuously monitor, analyze, and forecast client behavior. Heterogeneous client attributes become a robust IDS with adaptive client selection, customized optimization, and trust-based aggregation using digital twins. This paper proposes a novel approach, DT-ASFL, an adaptive Split Federated Learning (SFL) system that combines behavioral digital twins with multi-head temporal networks to improve IoV security. The lightweight multi-head Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) extracts temporal features, and SFL stores sensitive vehicle data on edge devices. These attributes are analyzed by a more advanced classifier on a distant server, so raw data never leaves the device. For each client, behavioral digital twins dynamically modify aggregation weights based on trust and health measures, evaluate data quality, and monitor performance. This adaptive method provides protection against malicious users and system heterogeneity. TCN architecture captures diverse temporal patterns for optimal feature fusion using parallel processing heads with different dilation rates and an attention mechanism. Evaluation on several security datasets demonstrates considerable increases in attack detection accuracy and robustness. The approach ensures privacy and system flexibility while outperforming conventional federated learning techniques.

title: Maximal Biclique Enumeration with Improved Worst-Case Time Complexity Guarantee: A Partition-Oriented Strategy
authors: Kaixin Wang, Kaiqiang Yu, Cheng Long
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: PACMMOD V4, N3 (SIGMOD), June 2026 Editorial
authors: Carsten Binnig, Sudeepa Roy, Divy Agrawal, Angela Bonifati
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: A Single-Tail CMOS Comparator for High-Speed and Low-Power Data Conversion
authors: K.Lokesh Krishna, M.Kalpana, K. Madhavi, Kanipakam Yaswanth, Kasim Kuppam Sushmitha, Lomada Bhanneswar Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Comparators are essential building blocks in high-speed data converters and mixed-signal integrated circuits, particularly in flash analog-to-digital converters, where strict requirements on power efficiency, conversion speed, and circuit area must be met. This work describes the design and realization of a single-tail CMOS comparator tailored for high-speed and energy-efficient operation. The proposed topology adopts a dynamically operated latch with a single tail device, which limits transistor stacking and reduces dynamic power consumption while preserving strong regenerative feedback for rapid decision making. The comparator is implemented and evaluated using Cadence Virtuoso tool in 90nm CMOS technology with a 1.8V power supply (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>). Transient analysis simulations are conducted using differential input signals biased around mid-supply and clocked in the nanosecond regime. The simulation results exhibit rail-to-rail output voltage transitions (from zero to 1.8 volts), reduced propagation delay on the order of nanoseconds, and markedly lower dynamic power dissipation relative to conventional designs, demonstrating the suitability of the proposed single-tail comparator for high-speed, low-power flash ADC applications.

title: Controllable Echocardiography Video Generation for Safe Medical Data Sharing
authors: Jinduo Wang, Fang Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Echocardiography is a key non-invasive imaging modality for cardiac assessment, yet its analysis is limited by noise, operator dependency, and the lack of large-scale annotated video data. Moreover, privacy concerns restrict data sharing across institutions. To address these challenges, we propose a generative-model-based framework for privacy-preserving echocardiographic data sharing. The model is trained offline to learn the distribution of real echocardiography videos and is released instead of raw data, enabling the synthesis of realistic and diverse cardiac sequences. By incorporating physiologically controllable signals, the proposed method generates semantically meaningful cardiac dynamics while protecting patient privacy. Experiments show that models trained on synthetic data achieve competitive performance on real echocardiographic data. This work offers a practical solution for scalable and secure echocardiographic analysis.

title: Reinforcement Learning based Energy Management Strategy for Hybrid Renewable Micro Grid with EV Integration
authors: Karanam Deepak, G Pandu Ranga Reddy, S Raj Kumar, Bondili Jithendra Singh, Jadala Aravind Kumar, Pasupala Jayanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rise of renewable energy and electric vehicles (EV) has led to new operational difficulties for today's power systems. Intelligent energy management strategies are imperative for integrating renewable microgrids powered by PV, wind, and batteries to ensure stability, economy, and efficiency in the use of electric energy resources. Traditional techniques used for optimization are not able to account for the variabilities associated with renewable sources of energy and EV charging requirements. An energy management approach is proposed based on the use of reinforcement learning (RL) for hybrid renewable microgrids with EVs. In this method, the operation of the microgrid is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in order to facilitate the learning of energy dispatch policies through an RL agent. Using the proposed model, renewable energy generation, battery storage systems, EV charging demand, and grid interaction can all be managed together. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can increase the efficiency of renewable energy utilization, reduce operational costs, and improve system reliability compared with traditional rule-based control techniques.

title: Enhanced Encrypted IoT Malicious Traffic Detection via Adaptive Fusion and Focal Loss: An Improved Heterogeneous Graph Approach
authors: Mianzhang Luo, Xiaoyi Yang, Yuqing Lan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) has significantly expanded the attack surface of network environments, making malicious traffic detection a critical security challenge. The widespread adoption of encryption technologies further exacerbates this challenge, as traditional plaintext feature-based detection methods are no longer effective. Although existing deep learning approaches have shown progress in encrypted traffic classification, they still face limitations in feature fusion strategies and the accurate identification of hard-to-classify samples. To address these shortcomings, this paper proposes an enhanced approach based on a dual-granularity heterogeneous graph neural network framework. Specifically, we introduce an adaptive feature fusion mechanism that dynamically adjusts feature weights and employs cross-view attention to fully exploit the complementarity of features across different granularities. Additionally, we replace the conventional cross-entropy loss with Focal Loss, enabling the model to focus more on challenging MitM (Man-in-the-Middle) samples during training. Extensive experiments conducted on the CIC-IIoT 2025 and IoT-23 datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves significant improvements in both weighted average accuracy and F1 score over baseline methods. Compared to the state-of-the-art encrypted traffic classification framework, MH-Net, the F1 score improves by 11.5% on the CIC-IIoT dataset, while also significantly reducing the misclassification of MitM samples as benign traffic. These results confirm the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method. This work provides a practical solution for encrypted traffic classification in IoT environments, enabling more accurate detection of malicious traffic and improving the security of IoT networks.

title: SPCL: Semantic Prototype Contrastive Learning for Incomplete Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Tiantian Zhang, JiaQi Zhao, Qianwen Han, Fuying Zhao, Zexi Zhang, Xueyuan Cai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In the field of artificial intelligence, data incompleteness represents a pervasive and highly challenging issue that severely constrains model performance, leading to information loss, statistical bias, and a significant decline in generalisation capabilities. Existing solutions, such as data deletion or various interpolation techniques, often suffer from inherent flaws including the loss of valuable information, the introduction of secondary noise, or the distortion of the original data distribution. To overcome these limitations, our core idea is to introduce a ‘prototype space’ constructed from vast, high-quality, and clean linguistic data. This space serves as a set of stable and robust semantic anchors. Building upon this, we devise a ‘prototype-guided contrastive learning’ objective. This objective imposes semantic regularisation during model training by aligning the projections of incomplete data in the representation space with corresponding semantic anchors within the prototype space. This work offers a novel perspective on addressing data incompleteness by starting from the representation space.

title: Proactive Traffic Surge Forecasting for Student Result Portals using LSTM Networks
authors: Dhyana SK, Dharshana Priya V, Alexander
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Student result portals face extreme and predictable traffic surges immediately following examination result declarations, placing sudden strain on server infrastructure that standard reactive management strategies cannot adequately address. This study proposes a proactive traffic forecasting framework using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to predict near-future user volumes on academic result portals before surges occur. The system ingests sequential traffic measurements collected at one-minute intervals, applies Min-Max normalization and temporal feature engineering, and generates multi-step-ahead forecasts covering a 10–30 minute horizon. Experimental evaluation conducted across multiple result-release cycles demonstrates that the LSTM model achieves a Mean Absolute Error of 118, an RMSE of 174, a MAPE of 4.92%, and a Peak Timing Error of just 2 minutes — substantially outperforming naïve persistence, moving average, and ARIMA baselines. These results confirm that sequential demand forecasting enables system administrators to initiate autoscaling and load-balancing operations well in advance of congestion, improving portal availability during the most critical academic events.

title: LightARM-Net: Accelerating Sparse-Attention Tabular Model for Android Malware Detection
authors: Nattapong Neadtip, Kian Ming Lim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Android malware continues to pose a major security risk as attackers develop increasingly sophisticated and evasive techniques. Although deep learning methods have shown strong potential for malware detection, existing models often suffer from high computational cost and inefficient attention mechanisms. This is the limitation of their use in real-time security systems. In particular, the Adaptive Relation Modeling Network (ARM-Net) relies on a bisection-based α-Entmax solver when α > 1, which introduces substantial computational overhead. To address this limitation, this paper presents LightARM-Net, a fast and efficient variant of the ARM-Net that leverage a Triton-optimized α-Entmax solver based on a hybrid Halley–bisection algorithm. Experiments on two benchmark datasets; TUANDROMD and KronoDroid (binary and multi-class), show that LightARM-Net consistently outperforms state-of-the-art deep tabular and classical machine learning models. The proposed model achieves up to a 2.4% improvement in F1-score while reducing the time per epoch by up to 4.3×, total training time (under early stopping) by up to 3.5×, and inference throughput by up to 8.5× compared with the original ARM-Net. These results indicate that LightARM-Net provides an effective and practical solution for efficient, real-time Android malware detection.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Approach for Wind Turbine Fault Detection
authors: N. Bhupesh Kumar, Rajesh Thangaraj, Kandukuri Kumari, K. Kiran Kumar, Imran Abdul, D. Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: As the world's need for clean electricity continues to rise, wind power is quickly becoming a viable and long-term option. On the other hand, wind turbines are susceptible to many problems since they are complicated electromechanical devices that function in very dynamic and unpredictable environments. Reduced energy efficiency, unplanned maintenance, catastrophic damage, and expensive downtime are all consequences of crucial component failures such the gearbox, generator's bearings, & blades. Failure to adapt to changing fault patterns or notice tiny changes in turbine performance data render’s traditional fault detection methods inadequate. These methods include threshold-based monitoring, vibration assessment with defined criteria, and periodic physical inspections. To guarantee the dependability and lifespan of wind turbines in massive amounts wind farms, more sophisticated, automated, and intelligent problem detection technologies are urgently required.

title: AI-Powered Multimodal Human Detection System Combining YOLOv11 and Terrain Data in Search and Rescue Operations
authors: Sangeetha Archana J, Mohanraj K, Monishkumar S, Sanjay S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Rapid identification and prioritization of victims can bring tremendous value to rescue operations. So far, the existing methods rely only on vision-based detection, often yielding broad results with no risk-based prioritization. In this paper, a deep learning-based rescue aid system is proposed using YOLOv11 for target detection, trained on diverse incident scene datasets, namely Incidents1M, and fine-tuned using human-focused benchmarks, C2A. This novelty in vision fusion merges the YOLOv11-based target detection with terrain intelligence categorization through Digital Elevation Model and Inertial Measurement Unit data to offer context-aware rescue decision-making. Based on such extensive training and sensor fusion, the system provides reliable identification of victims even in challenging situations and conveys precise timely information that enhances rescue missions. By applying the methodologies proposed in this work, we were able to obtain an accuracy of 95%. Experimental results shown that YOLOv11 provides highest result in terms of accuracy for human detection on the Incidents1M and C2A dataset.

title: Path Loss Prediction for Maritime Tropospheric Scatter Based on CNN-MLP
authors: Hengzhi Liu, Kunlei Du
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Tropospheric scatter is a key method for achieving maritime over-the-horizon communication, and accurate prediction of its path loss is crucial for communication link design. To address the issue that traditional empirical models rely on static climatic parameters and struggle to adapt to the complex and dynamic maritime surface environment, this paper proposes a dual-branch path loss prediction method based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), utilizing measured channel data. The proposed method is compared and analyzed against BP and LSTM networks. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN-MLP model effectively mines the deep coupling features among input parameters, achieving a prediction Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 6.1 dB, which is significantly superior to the comparison methods, thus validating its superiority in predicting maritime tropospheric scatter path loss.

title: Enhanced Iris Recognition Framework using Hybrid Cosine and Hamming Similarity Measures
authors: Abhinav R B, Dilshad Begum, Monish L, Manjula M, Siri A K, Thungashree I L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: A new way of recognizing irises that works with people with dark colored eyes is explained in this paper. This hybrid method uses two different ways of measuring similarity between iris features using our combined metric (the cosine and Hamming distances). The authors have developed a highly advanced preprocessing pipeline through which all images will be converted to their final form (via binarization, adaptive thresholding, and extracting the best features). These techniques will also allow for greater image quality and decrease the noise in the images that may distort matching accuracy. The authors are utilizing cosine distance to measure angular similarity between the two iris features as their first metric for measuring similarity; the second metric is the use of the Hamming distance that measures the number of bits that do not match between the two iris features. Together, these two metrics provide a dual-metric matching scheme which will allow for greater accuracy when determining an individual's identity based upon their iris features. The new hybrid method was evaluated against the CUHK Iris database, and additional tests were performed using a number of different noise models (Gaussian, Poisson, Speckle, and Impulse) for 50 sample cases. The results from these trials reached a maximum similarity rate of 99.32 %, which was 1.26 % and 0.29 % higher than that of the median and Wiener Filtered methods, respectively. Therefore, the results demonstrate that this new hybrid method is a reliable means of authenticating a person's biometric identity under the challenging conditions associated with darkly pigmented irises and degenerate iris quality characteristics.

title: FSM based I2C Communication Protocol for Reducing CPU Overhead
authors: P. Sivaranjani, S. Sasikala, S.K. Sobika, P. Kavin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: An efficient dual-role I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C communication protocol with independent Finite State Machines for the master and slave devices is proposed and verified on an FPGA platform. The proposed design allows for self-containment and dynamic role switching, eliminating the need for constant CPU polling in favor of interrupt-driven control and priority scheduling via a priority encoder. The design was synthesized on a Cyclone V (5CSEMA4U23C6) FPGA using the Intel Quartus Prime Lite Edition software and resulted in remarkably low hardware utilization of only 1 ALM (<1% of 15,880 available), 17 pins (5%), and zero DSP, PLL, or memory blocks, thus proving its compactness. Simulation results show that the proposed I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C protocol timing is correct with improved latency of approximately 7.8 μs and total power dissipation of 318.10 mW. The proposed design shows a substantial improvement over existing designs CPU utilization is reduced from 68% and 43% to only 12%, and data throughput is increased dramatically from 92 kbps and 355 kbps to 910 kbps. Hardware utilization is also minimized from <6% and <3% to less than 1% ALU usage, thus proving the proposed FSM-based interrupt-driven I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C design to be a high-performance and low-power solution for modern embedded and VLSI systems.

title: Theory-Guided Modeling for Network Performance Prediction
authors: Luyu Qi, Yulei Wu, Dimitra Simeonidou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The rapid evolution of wireless communication technologies has dramatically increased the complexity of network environments, making accurate performance prediction a critical task for network planning and optimization. Reliable prediction supports efficient resource management, quality of service assurance, and robust system operation. However, current approaches face three key challenges: (i) the wireless propagation environment is highly complex and difficult to model, (ii) measurement data are costly to collect and often imbalanced, and (iii) the lack of interpretability and credibility in data-driven models. To address these issues, we propose a Theory-Guided Modeling (TGM) framework that integrates communication theory with machine learning. TGM embeds classical propagation laws such as the log-distance path loss model and Shannon capacity into its architecture and loss function, introduces theoretical priors to enhance robustness under imperfect data, and outputs interpretable physical coefficients constrained by partial differential equations (PDEs). This hybrid paradigm effectively balances accuracy, robustness, and interpretability. Comprehensive experiments on path-loss and throughput prediction show that TGM achieves up to 62.6% reduction in mean absolute error (MAE) compared with state-of-the-art methods.

title: P-Channel Tri-gate Nanowire InGaN/GaN/AlN/InAlGaN MOS-HFET for Power Switching Applications
authors: Jian-Hong Ke, Ching-Sung Lee, Chung-Liang Liao, Wei-Chou Hsu
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: This work reports record-high three-terminal on-state drain-source breakdown voltage (BVDS) of -975 V and superior switching characteristics of p-channel InGaN/GaN/AlN/ InAlGaN metal-oxide-semiconductor heterostructure field-effect transistors (MOS-HFETs) with a tri-gate nanowire structure. Improved gate modulation capability and breakdown performance were achieved by the devised tri-gate nanowire and gate field-plate (GFP) structures. Non-vacuum ultrasonic spray pyrolysis deposition (USPD) technique was used to deposit the high-k wide-bandgap Al2O3 as the gate dielectric and surface passivation simultaneously. The present p-channel tri-gate nanowire MOS-HFET design has exhibited improved maximum drain-source current density (IDS, max) of -28.0 mA/mm, subthreshold swing (SS) of 75.6 mV/dec, on/off current ratio (Ion/Ioff) of 1.1 × 108, two-terminal off-state gate-drain breakdown voltage (BVGD) of 995 V, BVDS of -975 V, and Baliga’s figure of merit (BFOM) of 60.5 MW/cm2. The reliability of the present design was also evaluated through time-dependent electrical stress, demonstrating its advantageous potential for high-voltage complementary power-switching circuit applications.

title: Federated Learning with Enhanced Privacy via Model Splitting and Random Client Participation
authors: Yiwei Li, Shuai Wang, Zhuojun Tian, Xiuhua Wang, Shijian Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) often adopts differential privacy (DP) to protect client data, but the added noise required for privacy guarantees can substantially degrade model accuracy. To resolve this challenge, we propose model-splitting privacy-amplified federated learning (MS-PAFL), a novel framework that combines structural model splitting with statistical privacy amplification. In this framework, each client’s model is partitioned into a private submodel, retained locally, and a public submodel, released for global aggregation. The calibrated Gaussian noise is injected only into the public submodel, thereby confining its adverse impact while preserving the utility of the retained local model components. We further present a rigorous theoretical analysis that characterizes the joint privacy amplification achieved through random client participation and local data subsampling under this architecture. The analysis provides explicit bounds on both single-round and total privacy loss, demonstrating that MS-PAFL significantly reduces the noise necessary to satisfy a target privacy protection level. Extensive experiments validate our theoretical findings, showing that MS-PAFL consistently attains a superior privacy–utility trade-off compared with conventional DP-FL baselines and enables the training of highly accurate models under strong privacy guarantees.

title: End-to-End Vectorized HD Map Construction Based on Graph Structure Modeling and Graph Transformer Optimization
authors: Wenjing Bai, Yunzhou Zhang, Qi Guo, Wei Liu, Zuotao Ning, Shuai Cheng, Chaoran Yang, Li Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: High-definition (HD) maps play a crucial role in autonomous driving by providing a reliable foundation for behavior prediction and path planning. Recent methods model map elements as point sets and employ Transformer-based detection frameworks for end-to-end vectorized map construction. However, these approaches do not fully exploit geometric structural properties, thereby limiting the accuracy of predicting the shape and position of map elements. To address this limitation, we propose a novel method called GraphMapTR, which models map elements as graph structures. Each map element is represented as a subgraph where key points serve as nodes and edges capture structural continuity. To optimize the node query embeddings within subgraphs, we design a Graph Transformer-based decoder comprising three core components: Multi-head Self-Attention Between Subgraphs (MHSA-BSG), which facilitates efficient feature interaction across different subgraphs while enhancing node attention to their respective subgraph regions; Local Geometric-aware Self-Attention Within Subgraphs (LGSA-WSG), which dynamically aggregates global node relationships and local edge structures to enrich node representations; and Deformable Cross-Attention (DCA-BEV), which refines node embeddings through interaction with BEV features. In LGSA-WSG, priors from the rasterized map segmentation branch are incorporated to generate local geometric consistency scores (LGC-scores), which constitute edge structural bias. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GraphMapTR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods. In particular, it outperforms the state-of-the-art algorithm MapTRv2 by 5.0% mAP and 3.4% mAP on the nuScenes and Argoverse2 datasets, respectively.

title: Cooperative Edge Caching with Large Language Model in Wireless Networks_supp1-3687739.pdf
authors: Ning Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: Cooperative edge caching within spatially overlapping coverage zones introduces intricate coupling among Base Station (BS) caching decisions, rendering online content replacement highly sensitive to both spatial topology and temporal content reuse. Conventional heuristics often suffer from myopia, while Deep Reinforcement Learning agents typically rely on brittle numerical state/action representations and necessitate computationally prohibitive retraining under topological or traffic dynamics. This paper studies a centralized, cooperative multi-BS cache-replacement controller driven by a Large Language Model (LLM) within a deterministic text-to-action execution loop. At each time slot, the global cache state is deterministically rendered into a prompt encapsulating the cache inventory of each BS, the current set of deduplicated requests, and multi-scale frequency summaries. The LLM generates exactly one decision line per BS, after which a strict parser and feasibility checker either accept a feasible joint action or deterministically fall back to the all-BS no-operation (NoOp) action. We align the LLM through a two-stage training paradigm: initially performing completion-only Supervised Fine-Tuning on look-ahead expert trajectories to acquire action syntax and robust initialization, followed by Group Relative Policy Optimization. The latter employs an “opportunity-aware” reward mechanism, utilizing the multi-step cooperative hit rate gain relative to a NoOp baseline as the primary signal, together with an explicit invalid-output penalty and an annealed early-stage NoOp correction that prevents degenerate passivity. We focus on a reactive replacement setting with equal-sized files, at most one replacement per BS per slot, and insertion candidates restricted to current-slot requests. Under a frozen-trajectory evaluation protocol that reuses identical request traces and association graphs across methods, the proposed orchestrator approaches a single-step exhaustive-search reference (0.610 vs. 0.617 in a standard 5-BS scenario), surpasses classical baselines (e.g., +4.1% over least-frequently used), and exhibits robust zero-shot transfer across cache capacity, library size, popularity skewness, and user density. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/gracefulning/CoopLLM-Cache</uri>.

title: HFAVL: Hard-label Fusion Attack toward Vision-language Model
authors: Hongbo Cao, Yongqi Sun, Li Duan, Yifan Sui, Xisu Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: With the advancement of artificial intelligence, vision-language models (VLMs) that integrate text and image modalities have become central to multimodal learning and are increasingly deployed in Internet of Things (IoT) environments such as smart surveillance, autonomous driving and industrial monitoring. However, VLMs are also highly susceptible to adversarial attacks. Most previous black-box attack methods towards the VLMs rely on either the soft label or substitute models. However, most of them need detailed information on the target model, which is often unavailable for real-world threat models due to limitations on additional access and resource restrictions. In this study, we propose a surrogate-free hard-label attack towards the VLMs, which does not require a substitute model, called Hard-Label Fusion Attack towards Vision-Language models. HFAVL directly leverages the model’s final decision to jointly generate the perturbation for both text and image modalities instead of the confidence score necessary for the soft label attack. By transforming the text into a continuous embedding space that enables them to be aligned with the image in a unified manner, HFAVL applies cooperative Monte Carlo-based estimation on the multimodal gradient. Aiming to improve the efficiency of attacks, we introduce a Mutual Iterative Refinement mechanism (MIR), which gradually searches for a better adversarial example through small iterative steps that blend perturbations across both modalities. Additionally, to alleviate the imbalance between textual and visual noise during the attack, we propose a new multimodal edit distance evaluation metric to measure the quality of multimodal adversarial examples. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that HFAVL consistently outperforms existing multimodal adversarial attack methods across various vision-language pretraining (VLP) models. Notably, under the setting with recall@10, HFAVL achieves over 80% attack success rate on most benchmarks, demonstrating its efficiency and high precision in generating effective adversarial examples.

title: PCCA: A Hypothesis-Driven Prompt-Conditioned Causal Auditing Framework for Industrial Knowledge Graphs
authors: Hongwei Jiang, Jiapeng You, Jiayu Shi, Zhiyang Chen, Huaxing Gou, Xinguo Ming, Poly Z.H. Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Knowledge-driven industrial systems are brittle to dynamic conditions as their knowledge graphs (KGs) are static. This leads to costly decision failures (e.g., needless hardware replacement) and erodes operator trust. These KGs remain static because existing knowledge completion and causal discovery methods struggle with statistical correlations or complex industrial data, lacking a mechanism to proactively find their own knowledge deficiencies. To address this knowledge maintenance gap, this article proposes prompt-conditioned causal auditing (PCCA), a framework for the systematic, incremental auditing of existing KGs. PCCA translates operational hypotheses into prompts, guiding a deep graph model to efficiently evaluate their structural impact. This hypothesis-driven audit precisely locates overlooked or erroneous causal relationships. The framework is validated on both public benchmarks (Win95pts, DREAM4) and an industrial inkjet case study, demonstrating robust generalizability across diverse topologies. PCCA provides a practical process for evolving these knowledge assets, enhancing operational reliability and preventing unnecessary maintenance.

title: Distributed CPS-Based Predefined-Time Control of Large-Scale Plug-and-Play Inverter Air Conditioner Clusters for Demand Response
authors: Fan-Rong Qu, Zhi-Wei Liu, Yan-Wu Wang, Jia-Kai Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: Industrial cyber-physical systems (CPS) increasingly rely on coordinated flexible loads to enhance grid stability and renewable energy integration. Among these, inverter air conditioners (IACs), as networked CPS agents, offer significant potential for demand response (DR) applications. This paper studies the predefined-time coordination of large-scale IAC clusters and proposes a CPS-based distributed control framework. By coupling the power tracking objective with user comfort consensus, the proposed approach coordinately regulates the power variation rates of individual air-conditioning units. This integrated design enables fast and accurate tracking of power commands at the system level within a predefined time, while simultaneously ensuring fairness in user comfort across the cluster. Rigorous convergence is established via Lyapunov-based stability analysis. To accommodate the plug-and-play nature of IACs, where units may dynamically join or leave the DR process, an adaptive shared-variable mechanism is further proposed to preserve network connectivity and coordination performance under dynamic topology variations. Extensive simulation studies demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively mitigates demand fluctuations and achieves rapid response to DR signals within the predefined time.

title: Component-Reuse Hybrid Filter Design for Effective Leakage Current Suppression in Parallel Inverter Motor Drive Systems
authors: Qiao Li, Ziyao Zhang, Yun Huang, Jiale Li, Xiaokang Zhang, Chao Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Common-mode (CM) leakage current in parallel-inverter motor drive systems degrades electromagnetic compatibility and system reliability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a component-reuse hybrid filtering topology that integrates passive and active functions through a unified design framework, eliminating the need for dedicated CM filtering components. The key contribution lies in a dual-level functional reuse strategy. At the magnetic level, the leakage inductance of the coupled inductor, originally used for circulating-current suppression, is directly reused as the CM filtering inductance, forming a unified passive attenuation path without additional magnetic components. At the circuit level, a voltage-sensing current-compensation (VSCC) active stage is implemented using a dual-channel operational amplifier, enabling shared sensing and injection while reducing hardware complexity. A frequency-domain model is developed to describe the sensing, amplification, and compensation stages, and the impedance-based mechanism of CM attenuation is analytically established. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with the interleaved baseline, the proposed filter reduces CM leakage-current RMS and peak values by 64.2% and 71.9%, respectively, and suppresses the resonant peak by 29 dB. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed component-reuse hybrid filter for compact and wideband CM suppression in motor drive systems.

title: Bdes: Blockchain-Empowered Data and Energy Sharing in Internet of Electric Vehicles
authors: Xu Yang, Saiyu Qi, Wei Wei, Hongguang Zhao, Jingxian Cheng, Xinpei Dong, Qiuhao Wang, Ke Li, Wenjia Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The Internet of Electric Vehicles (IoEV) facilitates intelligent and sustainable transportation by enabling electric vehicles (EVs) to participate in traffic data sharing and energy trading. However, the highly dynamic and decentralized nature of IoEV introduces critical challenges in identity authentication, data privacy, and trading security. Traditional centralized solutions are vulnerable to single points of failure and privacy leakage, while existing blockchain-based approaches often lack fine-grained identity control and unified handling of both data and energy interactions. To address these challenges, we propose Bdes, a blockchain-empowered data and energy sharing system tailored for IoEV environments. Bdes adopts a modular architecture that integrates three core components: a Decentralized Identifiers (DID) management module for decentralized identity registration and Verifiable Credential (VC) binding; a requirement matching module that enables automated, privacy-preserving authentication and matching between data and energy providers and consumers via smart contracts; and a trading module that decouples key exchange from data transmission, ensuring secure, tamper-proof, and traceable trading. By leveraging Decentralized Identifiers, Verifiable Credentials, and blockchain smart contracts, Bdes supports flexible, secure, and transparent sharing of traffic data and energy information across multiple untrusted entities. Extensive security analysis shows that Bdes effectively resists credential theft, inference attacks, and man-in-the-middle threats. Experimental evaluations demonstrate the system’s scalability, efficiency, and suitability for large-scale deployment in IoEV ecosystems.

title: Digital Group Therapy: Interdisciplinary Challenges and Opportunities for Biomedical Circuits and Systems
authors: H. F. Jelinek, H. Al-Nashash, F. V. Kusmartsev, J. Barreiro-Gomez, T. Aminosharieh Najafi, I. M. Elfadel
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: Ahstract Digital Group Therapy (DGT) combines clinically-led group psychotherapy with continuous multimodal sensing, edge AI analytics, and online interaction platforms. This article synthesizes five perspectives presented at the IEEE CASS Workshop on Digital Group Therapy: neuro-dynamic modeling of group processes, cognitive workload management, next-generation edge—AI wearables, game-theoretic modeling of group behavior, and physics-inspired models of social interaction (sociophysics/social physics). We frame DGT as an open, multi-layer system of systems in which neural, physiological, cognitive, and social dynamics co-evolve under continuous feedback. We then connect this framework to recent evidence on online and mixed-reality group therapy and internet-delivered Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and to classical and modern work in sociophysics and opinion dynamics. The resulting architectural view reveals concrete circuits-and-systems challenges in scalable signal acquisition, robust edge intelligence, human-in-the-loop adaptation, incentive design, and validation of closed-loop DGT interventions.

title: High-Gain End-Fire Antenna Based on Slow-Wave Substrate-Integrated Waveguide Loading
authors: Hailu Jin, Xiaorong Jing, Ping Wang, Haiyan Jin, Guixue Liang, Feixiang Huang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In conventional series-fed substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) end-fire antenna arrays, the realized gain is often limited by the feed-to-radiation phase mismatch between the guided excitation and the free-space end-fire phase. To address this issue, polyline-loaded slow-wave SIW sections are inserted between adjacent radiating elements to tailor the inter-element guided phase progression, thereby enabling gain-enhanced end-fire radiation. For verification, a five-element deformed Yagi–Uda array based on an SIW traveling-wave line is designed, fabricated, and measured. The antenna features a compact transverse cross section of 0.15λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> × 0.06λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> Measured results show a usable end-fire band of 13.75–14.25 GHz, within which the reflection coefficient is below −10 dB, the beam remains end-fire, the average radiation efficiency is 75%, and the front-to-back ratio (FBR) exceeds 15 dB. At 14.25 GHz, the antenna achieves a peak gain of 15.2 dBi.

title: Geometry-Driven Performance Optimization of Dispense-Printed Interdigitated Electrodes for Potassium Biosensing_supp1-3694171.pdf
authors: Shamim Torkian
publish: N/A
abstract: Printed impedimetric ion-selective sensors based on interdigitated electrodes (IDEs) enable low-cost and flexible ion sensing, yet the role of electrode geometry under practical printing constraints remains poorly understood. Here, dispense-printed carbon IDEs were systematically optimized by varying finger length and inter-electrode gap to study their effect on interfacial capacitance and impedimetric response. Capacitance measurements and analytical IDE modeling reveal a trade-off between electrical performance and printability, yielding an optimized geometry with a 7 mm finger length and a 250 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m gap. Dispense-printed potassium sensors integrating a PEDOT:PSS solid-contact layer and a drop-cast ion-selective membrane (ISM) show stable, concentration-dependent responses from 0.1 to 100 mM. At 10 kHz, the normalized impedance change reaches up to 39% per decade. These results highlight geometry-aware IDE design for scalable printed impedimetric ion sensors.

title: 3D-Printed Folded Transmitarray with Gain-Filtering Response and Low Cross-Polarization
authors: Yuanxi Cao, Le Chang, Sifan Wu, Jianxing Li, Juan Chen, Sen Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: An air-filled cavity filter-based folded transmitarray antenna (FTA) is proposed in this communication. The transmitarray (TA) of FTA is realized by rectangular waveguide filters. The varied size of the coupling inductive iris between the resonators enables the filters to realize nearly the same filtering response and the 3-bit phase delay. The bottom plate of the FTA is periodically arranged with 45°-tilted parallel plate waveguides (PPWs), which can out-of-phase reflect the ±45° polarized incident waves, then achieve the polarization conversion. The main contribution of the proposed design is the utilization of transmission and reflection characteristics of rectangular waveguide-based TAs. The concept enables polarization selection in the frequency domain, achieving extremely high reflection coefficients. Moreover, the cutoff characteristic of the TA can effectively suppress the transmission of cross-polarized electromagnetic waves, resulting in low cross-polarization level. To verify the feasibility of the design, an FTA prototype is fabricated using 3D printing technology at the center frequency of 26.0 GHz. The measured results show that the antenna can realize the gain filtering beam with the rejection level around -20.0 dB, and the maximum aperture efficiency of 50.3%. In addition, several defocus sources are used to excite the FTA, demonstrating its multi-beam capability.

title: Bridging the Gap between Construction and Application: A Policy-Driven Model for Enhancing MOOC Effectiveness in Universities
authors: Chen Gu, Haiyan Wang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: significant gap persists between the scale of Massive Open Online Course (MOOC) construction and its practical integration into teaching, limiting the realization of its educational potential. To explore systematic strategies for enhancing MOOC effectiveness, this study proposes and examines a "Policy-Driven MOOC Ecosystem Model." Based on an in-depth, longitudinal case study of a Chinese research university, the research employs a mixed-methods approach combining policy text analysis, quantitative performance data, and qualitative interviews.The findings indicate that bridging the "construction-application" gap requires synergistic institutional design at the university level. The validated model comprises four core, interacting mechanisms: An Incentive and Recognition Mechanism formally links MOOC outcomes to faculty evaluation and reward systems; a Quality and Standard Mechanism regulates the course development lifecycle with clear technical and pedagogical specifications; a Support and Service Mechanism provides comprehensive, proactive assistance from training to technical implementation; and an Assessment and Feedback Mechanism utilizes learning analytics to drive evidence-based, continuous improvement of both courses and policies.Empirical evidence shows that under this framework, the university launched over 200 MOOCs, adopted by nearly 200 institutions nationwide with high enrollment, facilitating resource sharing and blended learning. The study concludes that transforming MOOCs into integrated teaching practices requires strong top-down policy architecture. This model provides a systematic approach for global higher education to turn digital course investments into tangible educational outcomes, supporting quality and equity.

title: TIME: A Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model for IDH Prediction_supp1-3693918.pdf
authors: Pengwei Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Background: Intradialytic hypotension (IDH) is a critical complication in hemodialysis that increases morbidity and treatment risks, yet existing machine learning methods inadequately address session-level physiological state heterogeneity and fail to balance linear and nonlinear feature interactions, limiting predictive accuracy and interpretability. Methods: We developed TIME (Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model), which explicitly separates linear interactions, higher-order nonlinear dependencies, and residual terms inspired by Taylor series expansion, while incorporating a physiological state embedding layer to capture session-level physiological state heterogeneity revealed through hierarchical clustering on autoencoder-derived latent representations of hematologic indicators selected using SHAP analysis and clinical knowledge. Using data from 532 hemodialysis patients (18,309 records), we compared TIME against 19 state-of-the-art baseline models, including blood pressure-stratified subcohort and out-of-distribution analyses. Results: Hierarchical clustering identified two distinct hematological states with different IDH risks (57.93% vs. 55.75%, $p=6.60\times 10^{-7}$). TIME achieved 0.6929 accuracy, 0.7435 F1-score, and 0.3663 MCC on the full cohort, the highest AUC across all three subcohorts (0.7010, 0.6795, and 0.7201), and strong out-of-distribution performance (accuracy 0.7053, F1 0.7377, MCC 0.4009). Integrating TIME's architecture into baseline models improved MCC by 2.67 percentage points on average. Conclusions: TIME effectively predicts IDH by modeling session-level physiological state heterogeneity and balancing feature interactions, with robust generalizability across diverse populations. Significance: TIME advances precision medicine in dialysis care by enabling early risk identification and personalized treatment strategies, while revealing broader immune, nutritional, and fluid-related markers of IDH risk.

title: BilimALL AI: An Intelligent Platform for Integrating Computer Science and Natural Sciences in Digital Education
authors: Meruert Yerekesheva, Adilet Darmenov, Ryskul Zhakhina, Anar Tashimova, Moldir Muratova
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the modern education system, the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies into the learning process has become one of the key priorities. This study focuses on the development of the BilimALL AI platform, which was created based on the concept of connecting informatics with natural science subjects and enhancing digital learning practices. BilimALL AI is an intelligent system that identifies interdisciplinary links between informatics and natural sciences, assisting teachers in automatically generating educational materials and visual slides. The platform introduces a novel approach by combining AI-driven content generation with automated detection of interdisciplinary connections, a feature that has not been extensively explored in current educational technologies. The platform aims to modernize the teaching process, automate lesson preparation, and reduce teachers’ workload. During the research, informatics teachers tested the BilimALL AI system in practice and evaluated its functional capabilities. The results revealed that the platform positively impacts lesson quality, increases students’ interest in science subjects, and improves teachers’ productivity through automation. The findings confirm that the BilimALL AI platform is an effective digital tool for promoting interdisciplinary integration, enhancing teacher efficiency, and advancing the use of AI in education, representing a significant contribution to the digital transformation of teaching practices.

title: Magnetic Confinement of Dust Particles: Novel Instrumentation for Potash Mining Applications
authors: A. Okusanya, R. Paranjape
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The control of dust particles in mining environments poses an occupational health and operational challenge. Traditional mitigation strategies, including water sprays, surfactants, and baghouse systems, face limitations due to high costs, maintenance requirements, and safety concerns. This paper introduces a novel instrumentation and measurement system designed to monitor triboelectric charging of moving dust particles and employ those measurements to evaluate magnetic confinement in potash mining. The setup combines mass, differential voltage, capacitive voltage, differential pressure and air quality monitoring measurements to monitor particle charging and the resulting transport behavior. The results from these experiments demonstrated that magnetic confinement improves dust capture efficiency and reduces the outlet particle concentration by up to 75%. These findings demonstrate that combining triboelectric charging with external magnetic fields provides an effective dry method for dust mitigation. It also showcases the depth of the instrumentation and its potential to support sustainable dust control in mining operations.

title: Improving Fairness in Airline Integrated Crew Scheduling - Colleague Familiarity and Team Experience Distribution Considerations
authors: Tianyu Wangs, Hoi-Lam Ma, Xin Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Psychological health of flight crew is critical to flight safety. Due to job nature (e.g., irregular work schedules, frequent jet lag), colleagues can sometimes outweigh the importance of family for flight crew. Thus, building colleague familiarity and workplace friendship is crucial. However, under the existing air crew scheduling (ACS) approach, crew members face frequent colleague changes and have to work with strangers, causing poor work environments. Besides, reasonable experience level distributions in crew teams are also crucial for crew satisfaction. However, the existing ACS approach frequently produces crew teams with unbalanced distributions of experience levels (e.g., all members from various ranks have lower-end experience levels within the qualified range). To overcome these drawbacks, this study develops a novel multi-objective ACS approach that can improve colleague familiarity (i.e., increase the chance for crew members to work together) and form crew teams with more reasonable and balanced experience level distributions. An evolutionary algorithm that incorporates the hyperplane separation and heuristic search strategy is proposed to derive Pareto solutions. The proposed ACS approach is examined on long-haul flight schedules which impose more complicated crew work/rest regulations. Extensive computational experiments with subsequent analysis validate the proposed method's effectiveness, showing an impressive improvement in colleague familiarity (1989.45%,

title: CFMDCTCodec: A Low-Bitrate Neural Speech Codec with Noise-Prior-aware Conditional Flow Matching for MDCT-Spectral Enhancement
authors: Xiao-Hang Jiang, Yang Ai, Hui-Peng Du, Zhen-Hua Ling, Ji Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: High-quality speech coding at low bitrates is crucial for bandwidth-constrained applications, yet remains challenging due to the severe loss of quality-critical information in highly compressed representations. To overcome this challenge, we propose CFMDCTCodec, a low-bitrate neural speech codec that operates entirely in the modified discrete cosine transform (MDCT) domain. CFMDCTCodec integrates a lightweight encoder-quantizer-decoder-style MDCT-spectral codec with a noise-prior-aware, conditional-flow-matching (CFM)-based MDCT-spectral enhancer. Within this framework, the codec serves as a base module that compactly discretizes the MDCT spectrum extracted from speech and produces an initial coarse reconstruction, while the enhancer further restores fine-grained spectral details. The enhancer improves the decoded MDCT spectrum by integrating a conditional MDCT velocity-field filter with an ordinary differential equation (ODE) solver, under the guidance of an MDCT-derived magnitude-adaptive noise prior, aiming to emphasize perceptually significant high-energy regions while stabilizing low-energy and silent regions. Finally, the enhanced MDCT spectrum is reconstructed into the decoded speech using the inverse MDCT. When optimizing CFMDCTCodec, we adopt a unified non-adversarial training strategy that jointly combines reconstruction, quantization and CFM objectives. Both objective and subjective evaluations show that CFMDCTCodec outperforms competitive baselines in low-bitrate regimes, e.g., 0.65 kbps, while approaching the perceptual quality of large-scale codecs with significantly fewer parameters and computations.

title: SkyDSafe: Federated Learning Driven Security for Drone Data
authors: Soniya Rohhila, Kedar Nath Singh, Amit Kumar Singh, Brij B. Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With the rapid development of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), such as drones, data can be gathered from multiple sources across various sectors. Compared with other media, images are the most vital carriers of information and are more lifelike. As illegal access to these images can result in serious privacy breaches, ensuring the authenticity of digital data remains a challenging task for drones. This paper proposes a method, called SkyDSafe, for securing image data based on federated learning and advanced encryption techniques. To address data poisoning attacks, this paper employs a cryptographic Merkle tree commitment over the training dataset, allowing auditors to detect data poisoning via Merkle proofs and each client’s training transcript. Using a multi-scale deep neural network, tamper-proof image features are effectively divided into abstract representations and secret hierarchical tensors. Subsequently, only the abstract representations of the image are encrypted through a cryptographic embedder to balance the efficiency and visual security. At the receiver end, the encrypted abstract image is decrypted and combined with the corresponding hierarchical tensors retrieved from the central server to reconstruct the original image. A comparative analysis with existing techniques highlights the advantages of SkyDSafe, including tamper-proof learning and reduced time and cost, as demonstrated by the experimental results. This aspect makes SkyDSafe a robust security solution for protecting drone data and other critical applications.

title: Robust Phase Unwrapping for Near-Field mmWave Phase Imaging: An Integrated TGV and NLSS Approach
authors: Yiming Ding, Xifeng Li, Dongjie Bi, Mingwu Tu, Libiao Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Near-field millimeter-wave (mmWave) phase imaging is a powerful non-destructive testing (NDT) technique. Phase unwrapping (PU), a critical step in this process, directly determines the accuracy of subsequent defect evaluation. Conventional two-stage denoising and PU algorithms often suffer from error propagation, while existing integrated methods are prone to generating erroneous edges when applied to near-field mmWave data. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework that integrates denoising and PU. The proposed method employs a weighted Poisson equation as the data fidelity term, regularized by a Total Generalized Variation (TGV) functional to effectively suppress noise while preserving sharp phase transitions. Furthermore, a novel quality map based on non-local self-similarity (NLSS) is introduced to adaptively weight the fidelity term, significantly enhancing performance in regions with a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through extensive experiments on real-world near-field mmWave data from diverse samples, including metallic targets, polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) blocks, and multi-layer composites. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high-precision phase unwrapping with superior structural fidelity, providing a highly reliable solution for complex NDT scenarios.

title: An Intelligent Distributed Base Station Deployment Strategy for Low-Altitude UAV Sensing
authors: Lichao Zhu, Bo An, Liang Wu, Zaichen Zhang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In the context of a developing low-altitude communication and sensing, the distribution of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) is characterised by dynamism and heterogeneity, posing a substantial challenge to conventional wireless network planning methodologies. Conventional metrics, such as mean squared error (MSE), which are predicated on point-to-point errors, are less efficacious in evaluating the overall congruence between the network service distribution and the highly concentrated, irregular patterns of UAV activity. This letter proposes an alternative paradigm for the distributed base station deployment, which is framed as an information-theoretic task. The fundamental premise is to minimize the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence between the target UAV activity distribution and the network service distribution. To investigate this concept, a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agent is proposed, and it autonomously adjusts the spatial positions of distributed base stations to minimize the KL divergence. This proposed strategy enables a more precise and efficient alignment of network resources with the real-time UAV demand, significantly enhancing the coverage efficiency and the service quality in dynamic environments.

title: SPARNet: A Framework for Airway Invasion Tracking from Fluoroscopic Videos of Dysphagia Patients
authors: Sanjeevi G, Uma Gopalakrishnan, Rahul Krishnan Pathinarupothi, Arya CJ, Kaustuv M. Talukdar, Subramania Iyer, Srisairam Achuthan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The videofluoroscopic swallowing study (VFSS) is the clinical gold standard for evaluating dysphagia and detecting airway invasion. However, manual interpretation is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to rater variability due to the rapid and complex nature of swallowing events. Existing deep learning approaches for airway invasion are limited by relying on manual frame selection, a lack of interpretability, and black-box decision-making. In this study, we propose SPARNet (Swallowing Phase Airway Risk Network), AI pipeline integrates swallowing-phase classification, bolus and airway delineation, and airway-invasion tracking to provide a clinically interpretable assessment. SPARNet introduces a novel Airway Invasion Tracker (AIT) that partitions the visible laryngo-tracheal air column in VFSS into quadrants, tracks the location of the bolus, and computes temporal invasion parameters (event duration, total exposure time, and number of invasion episodes) to characterize airway invasion during the pharyngeal phase. We curated a VFSS-MultiLabel dataset comprising 125 clips from 75 patients, annotated for multiple tasks. SPARNet achieved strong performance across all components, including the pharyngeal phase detection (F1-score = 88%), bolus segmentation (Dice score = 0.83), laryngo-tracheal air column detection (mAP = 0.95), and airway invasion detection (F1-score = 92%). Unlike prior black-box models, SPARNet provides transparent spatial and temporal cues that align with clinical VFSS reasoning. These results demonstrate the feasibility of an system-level AI pipeline for VFSS analysis, highlighting the potential to support clinical decision-making in the management of dysphagia.

title: Megahertz Soft-Switching Wireless Power Transfer for Capsule Robots Under Dynamic Position and Orientation Variations_supp2-3693630.avi
authors: Chi Kwan Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Capsule robots for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics have gained increasing attention in recent years owing to their miniature form factor and non-invasive, painless operation. However, their limited size restricts the integration of large batteries, which poses a major challenge when advanced functions such as high-frame-rate, high-resolution video recording are required. Wireless power transfer (WPT) offers a promising solution to this limitation. In this work, we propose an MHz-range WPT scheme that leverages the high-Q characteristics of coils at MHz frequencies, enabling a significant reduction in both transmitter and receiver coil sizes, which is essential for miniaturized capsule robots. Furthermore, a three-dimensional receiver coil structure is analyzed and compared, identifying configurations with improved efficiency. A key challenge in capsule WPT is that capsule motion through the digestive tract alters the reflected impedance seen by the Class-E driver, thereby disrupting its zero-voltage switching (ZVS) operation. To address this issue, two control methods are introduced to ensure consistent soft-switching under varying capsule orientations. In addition, a power regulation algorithm is developed to stabilize the output voltage and its performance is experimentally validated. Finally, the average received power along the entire GI trajectory is evaluated under a constant transmitter current. These results establish a foundation for the development of next-generation wirelessly powered capsule robots.

title: Responsible Integration of Generative AI in Software Engineering Education
authors: Miguel Matey-Sanz, Carlos Granell, Ramón A. Mollineda
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) is rapidly transforming higher education, requiring a consequent shift in teaching methodologies. This article describes a two-year teaching experience (2024/2025 and 2025/2026) integrating the free, responsible, and critical use of GenAI into a coordinated, complex software development project for fourth-year Computer Science students. The study aimed to explore GenAI’s potential as a learning support tool across the software lifecycle and analyze student interaction patterns. The methodology evolved over two academic years: an initial exploratory phase (2024/2025) was followed by a revised implementation (2025/2026) that incorporated active training seminars focused on prompt engineering and critical analysis. Results show that GenAI is highly effective as a “companion” for automating low-level tasks such as code implementation, debugging, and test scenario specification, allowing students to focus on higher-level design and decision making. Initial findings revealed student frustration when GenAI struggled to reason about advanced project contexts, often due to a lack of prompt expertise. However, the corrective training actions successfully mitigated this frustration, significantly improving the critical interpretation of GenAI-generated outputs and the control of “hallucinations”. Usage patterns were asymmetric, with general-purpose tools dominating early stages and specialized tools gaining relevance as project complexity increased. This experience confirms the value of GenAI in promoting critical and responsible use within software engineering education, supporting a shift from a basic skill focus to one centered on analysis, integration, and high-level problem-solving.

title: Reliability Class Testing Hypothesis for EBUCL*t 0 with Applications for Medical and Engineering Data Modeling
authors: Walid B. H. Etman, Rashad M. El-Sagheer, Mahmoud E. Bakr, Arwa M. Alshangiti, Farouq Mohammad A. Alam, Oluwafemi Samsonbalogun, Ali M. Sharawy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a new reliability class, the exponential better than used after age t0 in increasing convex Laplace transform order (EBUCL∗t0), and develops a corresponding statistical test for exponentiality. The test is constructed within the U-statistics framework and its theoretical properties, including asymptotic distribution and Pitman’s asymptotic efficiency, are established. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations are conducted to evaluate critical values and power performance under both complete and censored samples. Results demonstrate that the proposed procedure achieves higher efficiency and superior power compared to existing nonparametric tests. Applications to diverse engineering datasets (electrical insulation, glass fiber strength, polyester fibers, LED lifetimes, and windshields failure) and biomedical data (melanoma patients) confirm its practical utility in detecting deviations from exponentiality and modeling aging phenomena. Overall, the EBUCL∗t0 test provides a robust and versatile tool for reliability engineering and survival analysis, particularly in scenarios involving aging effects and censored data.

title: Efficient Index Layout and Search Strategy for Large-scale High-dimensional Vector Similarity Search
authors: Weijian Chen, Haotian Liu, Yangshen Deng, Long Xiang, Liang Huang, Bo Tang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Decoding Autonomic Stress Responses Using Multimodal Photoplethysmographic Representations and Boosted Learning Frameworks
authors: Kabilaesh M, Lingesh Karthikeyan M E, Akilandeswary G
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Stress is one of the significant determinants in physical and mental health where it must be detected properly and without the necessity of employing invasive techniques so that appropriate interventions can be implemented in good time. The paper deals with the problem of credible stress classification using the photoplethysmography (PPG) signals and the latest machine learning methods. A time-frequency-nonlinear scheme is suggested, which distorts time domain, frequency domain and nonlinear characteristics of PPG signals to reflect stress related physiological changes. The classification of these features with CatBoost and LightGBM algorithms is due to the capability of dealing with intricate feature interactions and helping to increase the accuracy of prediction. The suggested technique increases sensibility to noise and intersubject variation, which are typically found in wearable sensor data. The experimental analysis has shown a better classification performance than the conventional models with LightGBM having faster computation and CatBoost having better generalization. The results validate the usefulness of the PPG based stress detection and emphasize the appropriateness of the gradient boosting models in relation to the real-time monitoring of the state of physiology through wearable health care systems.
