abstract: Onboarding is an important activity to integrate new employees into an organization as soon as possible, in order to support them in becoming productive. Whereas onboarding has traditionally been done primarily onsite, it is now increasingly common for new software professionals to be onboarded either fully remotely or partially remotely in hybrid settings. We ask how software professionals develop a sense of belonging through the onboarding process. Using a conceptual framework from the general onboarding literature, we report on case studies of onboarding of software professionals in three different settings on the hybrid continuum, drawing on a dataset comprising interviews with 46 informants, Slack logs, observations, and company documents. In Case 1, we focus on the onboarding of consultant developers in two divisions: in one division, the consultants work internally with the consulting organization. In the other division, the consultants are placed with clients. In Case 2, we study the onboarding of software professionals in a hybrid setting, and in Case 3, we study the onboarding of a new team of remote developers based in Portugal, who joined a company in Norway. We extend the literature on onboarding of software professionals by shedding light on how software organizations conduct onboarding in different types of hybrid settings. We extend and refine the initial conceptual model, and formalize it through the development of a series of propositions. We then discuss a number of recommendations for software organizations who wish to onboard new software professionals in different settings on the hybrid continuum.

title: NeuralTouch: Neural Descriptors for Precise Sim-to-Real Tactile Robot Control
authors: Yijiong Lin, Bowen Deng, Keju Pu, Chenghua Lu, Max Yang, Efi Psomopoulou, Nathan F. Lepora
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Grasping accuracy is a critical prerequisite for precise object manipulation that may require careful alignment between the robot hand and an object. Neural descriptor fields (NDFs) offer a promising vision-based method to generate grasping poses that generalize across object categories. However, NDFs alone can produce inaccurate poses due to imperfect camera calibration, incomplete point clouds, and object variability. Alternatively, tactile sensing enables more precise contact, but existing approaches limit policies to simple predefined contact geometries. In this work, we introduce NeuralTouch, a multimodal framework that integrates NDFs and tactile sensing to enable accurate generalizable grasping through gentle physical interaction. Our approach leverages NDFs to implicitly represent the target contact geometry, from which a deep reinforcement learning policy is trained to refine the grasp using tactile feedback. This policy is conditioned on the neural descriptors and does not require explicit specification of contact types. We validate NeuralTouch through ablation studies in simulation and with zero-shot transfer to real-world manipulation tasks, such as peg-out/in-hole and bottle lid opening. Our results show that NeuralTouch significantly improves grasping accuracy and robustness over baseline methods, offering a general framework for precise, contact-rich robotic grasp-based manipulation.

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp8-3682403.mp4
authors: Yongchang Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

title: Privacy-preserving Decision-focused Learning for Multi-energy Systems
authors: Yangze Zhou, Ruiyang Yao, Dalin Qin, Yixiong Jia, Yi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Decision-making for multi-energy system (MES) dispatch depends on accurate load forecasting. Traditionally, load forecasting and decision-making for MES are implemented separately. Forecasting models are typically trained to minimize forecasting errors, overlooking their impact on downstream decision-making. To address this, decision-focused learning (DFL) has been studied to minimize decision-making costs instead. However, practical adoption of DFL in MES faces significant challenges: the process requires sharing sensitive load data and model parameters across multiple sectors, raising serious privacy issues. To this end, we propose a privacy-preserving DFL framework tailored for MES. Our approach introduces information masking to safeguard private data while enabling recovery of decision variables and gradients required for model training. To further enhance security for DFL, we design a safety protocol combining matrix decomposition and homomorphic encryption, effectively preventing collusion and unauthorized data access. Additionally, we develop a privacy-preserving load pattern recognition algorithm, enabling the training of specialized DFL models for heterogeneous load patterns. Theoretical analysis and case studies, including real-world MES data, demonstrate that our framework not only protects privacy but also consistently achieves lower average daily dispatch costs compared to existing methods.

title: Fourier-Net+: Band-Limited Spatial Representation for Efficient Medical Image Registration
authors: Xi Jia, Alexander Thorley, Alberto Gomez, Wenqi Lu, Dipak Kotecha, Alejandro F. Frangi, Jinming Duan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: U-Net style networks are commonly utilized in unsupervised image registration to predict dense displacement fields in the full-resolution spatial domain. For high-resolution volumetric image data, this process is, however, resource-intensive and time-consuming. To address this challenge, we propose Fourier-Net+, an image-domain deformable registration framework that operates on real-valued images for broad modality compatibility. Fourier-Net+ uses deterministic Fourier-domain band-limiting for efficient down- and up-sampling and employs a parameter-free, model-driven decoder to learn a band-limited, low-dimensional representation of the displacement field; all learnable network layers are real-valued. In addition, to enhance the registration performance and encourage diffeomorphism, we propose the cascaded and diffeomorphic versions of Fourier-Net+. We evaluate the proposed methods on five datasets, including two brain MRI, one 3-D cardiac MRI (3-D-CMR), one abdominal CT-MR dataset, and one noisy ultrasound cardiac dataset, comparing them against various state-of-the-art approaches. Our Fourier-Net+ and its variants achieve comparable results with these approaches while exhibiting faster training and inference speeds with a lower memory footprint and fewer multiply add operations (mult-adds). For example, on the 3-D-CMR dataset, our Diff-Fourier-Net+ significantly outperforms strong baselines such as TransMorph, TransMatch, and SACB-Net in Dice and HD, as well as in clinical metrics including end-diastolic (ED) volume and ejection fraction estimation, while using substantially less memory and computational cost. This efficiency enables large-scale 3-D registration training on low-VRAM GPUs.

title: A Fractional-Order Model-assisted Reduced-order ADRC for Photoelectric Stabilized Platforms
authors: Guoyu Lin, Jiangong Wang, Hongwei Di, Xu Chen
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: To enhance the dynamic performance and disturbance rejection capability of photoelectric stabilized platforms, this study proposes a Fractional-Order Model-assisted Reduced-order Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control (FO-MRLADRC) algorithm. First, a Model-assisted Reduced-order Linear Extended State Observer (MRLESO) was designed to improve observation efficiency and accuracy by leveraging the measurability and reliability of system output signals. Next, a fractional-order PD controller served as the control law to strengthen system stability and control performance. These improvements are closely interrelated and collectively contribute to the algorithm’s performance. Comparative experiments were conducted by simulation experiments and real platform experiments to evaluate dynamic response, input tracking and disturbance rejection, benchmarking against conventional linear ADRC and reduced-order linear ADRC algorithms reported in the literature. Results demonstrated that the proposed FO-MRLADRC outperformed the reference algorithms in terms of accuracy, dynamic response speed, and resistance to external disturbances.

title: Mobility-Aware Wireless Path Optimization with Physics-Guided Surrogates and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenyuan Zhu, Ramin Khosravi, Rashid Mirzavand
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Reliable, low-latency wireless links for mobile Internet of Things (IoT) in complex indoor environments are critical for digital twins and augmented reality, where motion and blockage rapidly change line-of-sight and reflected path quality. High-fidelity ray tracing accurately evaluates such paths but is too costly to run online, while reinforcement learning (RL) enables fast sequential path selection yet requires a physically meaningful, transfer-capable representation. In addition, practical mobility-aware operation should optimize not only received power but also propagation delay. To address these needs, this paper proposes a mobility-aware physics-guided surrogate framework for dual-objective wireless path optimization under mobility, together with controller benchmarking and hardware validation. We extend a static surrogate-guided path-optimization pipeline into a mobility-aware framework that combines deployment-aligned state design, controller benchmarking, and competence-based training under receiver motion. Crucially, we integrate a Zad-off–Chu (ZC) correlation-based Time-of-Arrival (ToA) tracker for deployment-time measurement and hardware validation of received power and propagation delay under mobility. Hardware validation confirms that the proposed framework preserves strong agreement between surrogate predictions and measured received power / propagation delay under static and mobile conditions. Experiments show strong agreement between surrogate predictions and measurements, with coefficient of determination for both predicted received power (R2 = 0.9907) and propagation delay (R2 = 0.9848). Combined with initial zero-shot transfer to a held-out environment, the results demonstrate a practical framework that substantially narrows the sim-to-real gap for mobility-aware digital-twin and IoT systems.

title: Autonomous Orchestration of IoMT Applications with Network Slicing in Distributed Edge Computing
authors: Hafiz Faheem Shahid, Muhammad Zeeshan, Erkki Harjula
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The extensive use of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) services requires deployment in distributed computing infrastructures that provide high reliability, low latency and efficient resource usage. However, current orchestration approaches for resource-intensive and dynamic IoMT services face challenges similar to those encountered in conventional networks, such as hardware constraints and limited computing resources at the edge. In this paper, we introduce an autonomous orchestration system framework for deploying IoMT applications by applying resource and network slicing methods in distributed edge computing. The proposed system leverages 5G network slicing to enable three primary categories: uRLLC, mMTC, and eMBB, thereby addressing the distinct Quality of Service (QoS) requirements of IoMT. The discrete-event experiments account for resource constraints at edge nodes, including CPU, GPU, memory, and storage capacity, unlike many prior approaches that primarily focus on latency and bandwidth. This architecture enables dynamic task offloading, supports user mobility, and NS-aware service deployment within the computing zones. The experiment with resource-aware orchestration using an isolated network slice significantly improves system performance compared to the baseline solution, including enhancements in service deployment, user handover count with a high acceptance ratio, minimizing blocking probability, and overall resource efficiency.

title: Energy-efficient Beamforming for STAR-RIS-aided ISAC with Hardware Impairments: A Generative AI-enabled DRL Method
authors: Yu Yao, Jinju Sun, Pu Miao, Gaojie Chen, Rahim Tafazolli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper investigates an energy-efficient beamforming design for a hardware-impaired simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-aided integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where the base station (BS) concurrently performs target sensing and multi-user communication. Accounting for hardware impairments (HWIs) at the BS, user equipments (UEs) and STAR-RIS, a joint optimization problem is posed to maximize the system energy efficiency, subject to constraints on required sensing and transmission capabilities, and the total power budget. To tackle the intractable conflicts among sensing and transmission metrics introduced by HWIs, we propose a novel learning-based method that integrates a denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) into a twin-delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (TD3) algorithm enhanced with prioritized experience replay (PER). By leveraging the DDPM and PER for beamforming policy determination, our approach accurately models the complex dynamics, achieving a better balance between sensing and communication performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PER-DDPM-TD3-based beamforming strategy achieves a 69.3% higher energy efficiency performance than the existing deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based method.

title: Energy Efficiency and Carbon Reduction Potential of HTTP Caching in ICT-enabled Critical Infrastructure
authors: Sebastián Soler Domínguez, Rajesh K. Ahir, Jagruti Thakur
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: The rapid growth of web services is increasing the energy consumption and carbon footprint of Information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure. While HTTP caching is widely deployed to improve performance, its quantitative energy and CO2 benefits remain insufficiently characterized. This paper presents a measurement-calibrated framework to estimate the energy use and associated carbon emissions of HTTP content delivery with and without caching. This study introduces a Projection-Calibrated Web Energy-Carbon Calculator (PC-WECC) that maps web workloads to energy and emissions by integrating data-center power usage effectiveness (PUE), network energy intensity, and country-level grid carbon intensity projections through 2030. To capture application-level effects, we develop an Empirically-Fitted Cache Efficiency Regression Model (ECERM) based on controlled server experiments that measure joules-per-gigabyte for cached and non-cached delivery. Results show that HTTP caching is 5−20% more energy efficient than non-cache delivery for typical web objects (< 5MB), with efficiency strongly dependent on file size and protocol overhead. When scaled to realistic traffic volumes, caching can yield substantial emissions reductions; for example, an average news website (∼ 300k daily visits) can avoid approximately 150tCO2 per year. This framework provides a transparent and reproducible pathway to translate HTTP architectural choices into energy and carbon impacts, supporting sustainable design and deployment of web infrastructure.

title: Counter-Based Scatter Matrix Estimation for One-Bit Centered Bivariate Cauchy Signals
authors: Yi-Heng Lin, Chun-Lin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Scatter matrix or covariance matrix estimation under data with outliers is crucial to signal processing applications. These data can be modeled as Cauchy or elliptically symmetric (ES) distributions, under which many estimators have been developed. However, these estimators primarily assume continuous-valued data. For implementation purposes, these data are quantized into discrete levels, which can be represented by finite bits. The complexity of the estimators is also restricted. These hardware constraints degrade the performance of existing scatter matrix estimators. This paper proposes the Cauchy onebit scatter matrix estimator (COSME). The COSME is founded on the proposed integral expressions of the marginal and joint probability for one-bit centered bivariate ES (CB-ES) data. These probabilities are simplified in closed forms for one-bit centered bivariate Cauchy (CB-Cauchy) data. For equal normalized quantization thresholds, these forms admit the COSME with lowcomplexity steps. In particular, these steps involve counting the occurrences of the events in the one-bit CB-Cauchy data and evaluating trigonometric functions. The minimum number of snapshots and the multivariate extension of the COSME are also investigated. Numerical examples demonstrate that the COSME exhibits shorter CPU time under the same number of snapshots and improved estimation performance under the same number of bits, compared to several existing estimators. Empirical results show the robustness of the COSME to Student’s t distributions and parameter variations.

title: Integrated System for Wireless Power Transfer and Auxiliary Charging of High-Voltage and Low-Voltage Batteries in Electric Vehicles
authors: Yiming Zhang, Wei Xu, Chao Liu, Ronghuan Xie, Xiaoying Chen, Xiangpeng Cheng, Xingkui Mao, Yuanchao Wu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology can be used to charge the high-voltage (HV) power battery of electric vehicles (EVs), while the low-voltage (LV) auxiliary battery is charged via a separate auxiliary power module (APM). Maintaining two distinct charging systems leads to increased costs, bulkier designs, and added weight for the EV. This study introduces a novel integrated architecture that combines the WPT and APM systems by sharing key components. A compact pickup coil is integrated into the receiving coil of the APM, enabling it to directly draw power from the WPT system. This innovative approach allows the DC/AC converter, compensation network, and receiving coil to serve dual purposes, supporting both WPT and APM functionalities. Moreover, it enables power transfer from the HV side to the LV side while the EV is in motion. The system achieves a significant reduction in weight and size while boosting the overall power density of the EV charging infrastructure. A prototype was developed and tested to verify the feasibility of the proposed design. This integrated solution offers a more compact, efficient, and cost-effective approach to EV charging.

title: Simulation of the Longwave Radiance Tendency on Sea Surface Temperature Observed from Moon-based Platform
authors: Yuhan Wei, Guoqiang Chen, Mingyang Lv, Wenhui Wan, Dan Song, Pei Tan, Huadong Guo
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Longwave radiance (LWR), a fundamental measure of Earth’s thermal radiation, serves as a key indicator of global climate change. A future Moon-based radiation observatory, characterized by stable orbital configuration, unique spatial environment, and etc., facilitates the acquisition of longwave radiance data and underpins associated climate research. This study has developed a model to simulate earth LWR observed from Moon and corresponding coefficient of determination (R²) with sea surface temperature (SST) in critical zone, utilizing outgoing longwave radiation based on CERES Edition 4 ADMs, and MCD12C1 data. Results show (1) relatively small monthly variations (standard deviation ~0.5 W/(m²·sr)) and stable, periodic interannual fluctuations ranging from approximately 70.5 to 80.5 W/(m²·sr); (2) the oceans contribute the largest share (~70%) to earth's total LWR; (3) long-term data from 2004–2022 has no significant trend in global LWR; (4) R² between SST and global oceanic LWR exhibits a distinct zonal distribution pattern. During El Niño events, R² in affected regions varied synchronously with event intensity, exhibiting a proportional relationship between event strength and the magnitude of R² enhancement. In general, this study lays a foundation and a novel approach for interpreting earth radiation features in future missions.

title: Overload Investigation of 3.3kV Si-IGBT and SiC-MOSFET Power Modules
authors: Bochen Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: The overload capability of two power modules, i.e. $3.3 \mathrm{kV} / 450 \mathrm{~A} \mathrm{Si}$-IGBT and $3.3 \mathrm{kV} / 750 \mathrm{~A}$ SiC-MOSFET, are investigated for STATCOM using delta-connected chain-link MMC. The critical indicator, i.e. overload duration limit, is utilized to compare the overload capability under various operational conditions, including grid-connection voltage, device working current, semiconductor case temperature and allowed maximum junction temperature. To achieve this, the power losses coupling with temperature are iteratively calculated and thus the junction temperature can be obtained with respective thermal models. The results indicate a comparable overload capability for the SiC module, with the expected advantage of a higher allowable temperature limit.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Faradaic Processes Simulation at Electrochemical Interfaces
authors: Haoyu Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The accurate atomistic modeling of pseudocapacitive materials is a grand challenge, hindered by the immense computational cost of quantum mechanical methods required to describe Faradaic charge transfer. While DFT-based molecular dynamics (DFT-MD) provides the necessary quantum accuracy, its application is limited to small systems and short timescales, precluding the study of realistic electrode interfaces. To bridge this critical gap, we introduce ReaQ-ML (Reactive Quantum / Machine Learning), a novel hybrid simulation framework. ReaQ-ML partitions the system into a quantum mechanical (QM) region, encompassing the active site of a Faradaic reaction, and a machine learning (ML) region for the remainder of the polarizable electrode. This approach extends recent developments in ML electrode models for non-Faradaic systems by explicitly incorporating electron transfer between the electrode and electrolyte. The ML component, trained on high-fidelity DFT data, learns to reproduce the complex, non-local electronic response of the entire electrode to a local reactive event at a fraction of the cost of a full QM calculation. This enables quantumaccurate simulations of charge storage mechanisms, such as proton adsorption on MXenes or redox reactions on transition metal oxides, at unprecedented scales.

title: ThermGNN: Physics-Informed Graph Neural Networks with Spatiotemporal Attention for Vehicle Thermal Management Prediction
authors: Bo Feng, Zichen Xu, Ya Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Vehicle electrification demands precise thermal management to ensure battery safety, extend component lifespan, and optimize energy efficiency under dynamic driving conditions. However, existing approaches face three critical limitations: (1) computational fluid dynamics simulations are prohibitively expensive for real-time deployment, requiring hours to model transient thermal events, (2) lumped-parameter models oversimplify multi-component thermal coupling, failing to capture complex heat transfer pathways across battery packs, motors, and cooling circuits, and (3) data-driven methods ignore fundamental thermodynamic principles, producing physically implausible predictions during extreme operating conditions. We propose ThermGNN, a physics-informed graph neural network framework that synergistically integrates domain knowledge with deep learning for spatiotemporal temperature prediction. Our approach introduces three key innovations: (1) a thermal graph construction module that encodes conduction, convection, and radiation mechanisms through learnable physics-guided adjacency matrices, (2) a spatiotemporal attention mechanism that explicitly models heat transfer pathways and thermal inertia effects with component-specific time constants, and (3) thermodynamic regularization constraints ensuring energy conservation and symmetric heat exchange throughout training. Extensive experiments on real-world electric vehicle datasets demonstrate that ThermGNN achieves 0.845°C RMSE, outperforming LSTM and Transformer baselines by 46.2% and 28.9% respectively, while maintaining physical consistency across diverse thermal scenarios including cold start, acceleration, and regenerative braking cycles.

title: Feature-Guided Stereoscopic Video Retargeting with Comfort-Oriented Depth Remapping
authors: Qi Wu, Zhenhua Tang, Linghui Cai, Yulin Li, Guangqing Li, Yongwen Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Effective screen resource allocation is crucial for maintaining the geometry of salient objects and depth perception in stereoscopic video retargeting. Existing methods, however, predominantly depend on global resizing or heuristic cropping, which often cannot separate perceptually important regions from complex backgrounds and may lead to distortion of salient structures. To overcome this limitation, we propose a stereoscopic video retargeting framework that incorporates two complementary feature metrics—Depth Structural Complexity and Significant Information Loss—to explicitly model background depth distribution and the importance of salient content. Together, these metrics steer a feature-aware intelligent cropping scheme, allowing adaptive allocation of limited screen space to perceptually meaningful areas. Furthermore, a visual-comfort-oriented depth constraint strategy is integrated to adaptively remap excessive disparities in videos that cause viewing fatigue. Experimental results show that the proposed approach successfully preserves the structure of salient objects while also enhancing stereoscopic visual comfort.

title: TCN-DA1: A Dual Attention Enhanced Temporal Convolutional Network for Inertial Sensor-driven Pet Behavior Recognition
authors: Chaoyuheng Yang, Zhiwei Li, Yuan Xi, Hongshan Liu, Juan Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In recent years, the pet smart device market has been expanding, and pet behavior recognition based on inertial sensors has become a research focus. Traditional machine learning methods have limitations in extracting temporal features and are unable to fully capture the long-term dependencies of pet behaviors, leading to poor differentiation of static behaviors. To address this, we propose an improved Temporal Convolutional Network model (TCN-DA1), which builds on the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) and incorporates a dual attention mechanism, consisting of channel attention and temporal attention. The channel attention module (SENet) optimizes the expression ability of six-axis inertial sensor signals (three-axis accelerometer and three-axis gyroscope) through adaptive channel re-weighting, while the temporal attention module dynamically weighs key behavioral segments to enhance the model’s ability to capture complex behavioral patterns. Experiments conducted on the publicly available dog behavior dataset (Movement Sensor Dataset for Dog Behavior Classification) demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms traditional machine learning methods, as well as baseline models such as LSTM and CNN-LSTM, with notable improvements in precision (79.05%), recall (82.88%), and F1-Score (83.11%), especially in the recognition of static behaviors like Sitting and Standing. This provides an efficient technical solution for behavior recognition in pet smart collars.

title: Neural-Network-Free Face Recognition on STM32 using a Lightweight Embedded Vision Framework
authors: Ved Khandeparkar, M. Sujith Khanna, M. Mathan Kumar, R. Vinoth Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: In response to the problems of high computational cost, large memory footprint, and high-power consumption of deep learning models in the era of Edge AI, this paper designs and implements a lightweight facial recognition system based on the STM32 microcontroller (NUCLEO-F334R8). The STM32 is used as the core control center, combined with the OV7670 camera module for image acquisition. To overcome the resource constraints of embedded systems, the system utilizes a pipeline of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction and a Linear Support Vector Machine (SVM) for classification, replacing traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The Labeled Faces in the Wild (LFW) dataset was optimized and balanced to train the model. Experimental tests show that the system achieves a projected accuracy of 80.29 % for limited person recognition, realizing the automatic identification of individuals with good real-time performance and low memory usage, which meets the design requirements of embedded security applications.

title: ResNet-YOLO-SLAM: An Integrated Deep Learning Framework for Autonomous Hospital Navigation
authors: Zhi Cheng, Xinyu Pang, Jiaxin Deng, Yuanyuan Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent medicine delivery robot system integrating advanced computer vision algorithms for autonomous hospital navigation. We propose a hierarchical ROS2-based framework combining ResNet18 for deep learning-driven line following, YOLOv5 for real-time obstacle detection, and depth-enhanced visual SLAM for robust localization. The system leverages the RDKX3 AI accelerator to achieve 28 FPS ResNet18 inference and 45 ms YOLOv5 detection, enabling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87 \pm 12ms$</tex> end-to-end control latency. Deployed on an Ackermann chassis with RGB-D sensors, our approach demonstrates superior performance in hospital corridor environments with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.8 \pm 2.1cm$</tex> path deviation, zero collision incidents across 20 autonomous trials, and 16.7% improved energy efficiency compared to LiDAR-based systems. Comprehensive benchmarking in laboratory-simulated hospital corridors against traditional CV, YOLOv3, MobileNetV2, and LiDAR-SLAM baselines validates the technical feasibility of our integrated deep learning pipeline as a proof-of-concept for medical logistics automation, pending clinical validation and medical device certification.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)

title: Lithium Battery Fault Detection Method Based on Feature Extraction Images
authors: Zekai Zhao, Wenjie Zhang, Zhihao Zhang, Nianfu Li, Chentao Wang, Mifeng Ren
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Aiming at the weak early signals, difficult identification and delayed early warning of sudden internal short circuits in power batteries for new energy vehicles, a power battery fault detection method integrating multi-dimensional cell feature extraction and Vision Transformer (ViT) is proposed. Firstly, battery operation data is classified into four typical working conditions (charging, dynamic driving, standby, vehicle-off) via adaptive working condition analysis technology, with more than 26 cell aging features highly correlated with internal short circuits extracted. Secondly, an internal short circuit feature evolution database is constructed by combining lithium battery failure mechanisms, which mitigates the scarcity of fault samples. Finally, a ViT-based internal short circuit recognition model is designed to accurately identify weak early fault features. Test results show that the model achieves a fault recognition accuracy of 97% and a precision of 85%, with the maximum F1-score difference across datasets <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 5 {\%}$</tex>. Its core performance is significantly superior to the traditional CNN+LSTM model, breaking through the weak anomaly identification bottleneck of conventional monitoring technologies and providing a feasible technical solution for the early warning of internal short circuits in power batteries.

title: Fake Job Post Detection Using NLP
authors: Muthuraj D, Veera Kumar M M, K. Mahalakshmi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Fake job postings are a growing global problem that target applicants via deceptive descriptions, stolen logos, and coordinated recruiter behavior. Systems that look only at text often miss tricks that combine images and account-level signals. We propose a practical multi-modal detection pipeline that combines transformer-based text embeddings, CNN-based image verification (with perceptual hashing), and recruiter-behavior analytics. The fusion model returns a calibrated fraud risk score and human-friendly explanations (SHAP contributions, highlighted phrases, Grad-CAM overlays). We report extensive experiments, ablation studies and a small human-in-the-loop evaluation to show the approach is both effective and usable. On our 18k-post dataset the fused model yields 95.8% accuracy and significantly reduces dangerous false negatives compared to text-only baselines. We also discuss deployment considerations, monitoring strategies for model drift, and practical steps for integration into moderation workflows.

title: Research and Application of Wiring Matrix Technology in Factory Testing of Hualong Unit Nuclear Power DCS Control Systems
authors: Deshan Kong, Yongxue Qie, You Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The nuclear power DCS (Distributed Control System) comprises numerous functional subsystems, each of which is designed with a large number of input/output (I/O) interfaces for interaction between field devices and systems in a nuclear power plant. If only traditional manual methods are used for I/O channel interface verification, it will not only result in low testing efficiency but also introduce human errors during the testing process.Through systematic analysis and scientific classification of interface types between field devices and systems in the nuclear power DCS, combined with an in-depth evaluation of existing manual testing methods, this study reveals that the current verification process involves nearly 30,000 variable points. Relying solely on manual or existing semi-automated single-point testing methods leads to insufficient efficiency, especially in the context of the current batch construction of nuclear power plants, where such inefficient testing approaches can no longer meet the required construction timelines. To address these challenges, this study innovatively proposes a new method for nuclear power DCS interface verification. By introducing a wiring matrix technology, this method enables integrated batch testing with a single wiring setup for interface variables. Practical application results demonstrate that the wiring matrix technology significantly improves testing efficiency (approximately 60 % faster than traditional methods) while reducing human error rates to below 0.5 %. Additionally, this method exhibits strong portability, allowing for technical reuse across different nuclear power projects, thereby effectively shortening the factory commissioning cycle of nuclear power DCS systems (by an average of 30 %). This technological breakthrough provides crucial support for the batch construction of nuclear power plants.

title: Temporal Consistency-Driven 3D Human Pose Estimation from RGB Videos
authors: G. Ms.Sakthi Priya, K. Vijayalakshmi, K. Vignesh Saravanan, KM. Hemapriyah, B. Kavya Srinidhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Estimating accurate 3D human pose from monocular RGB video remains a challenging task due to depth ambiguity, self-occlusion, and temporal instability across consecutive frames. While recent monocular pose estimation methods achieve reasonable per-frame accuracy, many approaches lack explicit structural constraints and robust temporal modeling, leading to anatomically inconsistent poses and motion jitter in dynamic sequences. To address these limitations, the paper proposes a temporal consistency-driven framework for monocular 3D human pose estimation that integrates structural skeleton representation with motion-aware temporal modeling. The proposed framework consists of three key components, skeleton topology construction to preserve joint connectivity and anatomical structure, body-aligned coordinate transformation to decouple global body orientation from local joint articulation, and temporal motion modeling to capture limb-wise dynamics and ensure smooth pose transitions across frames. The framework enables stable and structurally coherent 3D pose reconstruction from video sequences. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining structural constraints with temporal motion analysis for robust and reliable 3D human pose estimation from monocular video data.

title: Ambient Parametric Test Reduction in Post-Silicon Production Testing via Temperature-Dependent Modelling: Three Approaches and a Case Study
authors: Bianca Carbunescu-Stoenescu, Emilian David, Mihai Popovici, Valentina Davidoiu, Marina Topa, Andi Buzo, Georg Pelz
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The post-silicon integrated circuits (IC) testing is a very expensive process, especially in automotive industry where chip functionality has to be guaranteed by verifying multiple electrical parameters over a wide range of operating conditions. However, some costs can be avoided by reducing the amount of redundant tests, as certain parameters may exhibit a predictable behavior on the operating conditions variation. This paper presents a comparison between three approaches that use temperature parameters behavior for reducing the number of IC tests. The methods are tested on a production dataset consisting of 25 parameters and 300000 chips, results showing that the best method saves approximately 12 % of the measurements.

title: Explainable Emotion Recognition in Real-World Images via Caption-Aligned Dual-Branch Contextsubject Fusion Framework
authors: Rauki Yadav, Vineet Kumar Goel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Emotion recognition in real-world images remains a challenging frontier in affective computing, constrained by intricate interactions between scene context and human subjects. Existing approaches demonstrate limited interpretability and struggle with caption-visual misalignment, resulting in suboptimal performance on unconstrained image datasets. This paper introduces CADF-Net, a novel Caption-Aligned Dual- Branch Framework (CADF) that systematically disentangles contextual and subject-centric emotional cues through a dual- branch architecture with explicit caption-image alignment. Our framework leverages Vision Transformers (ViT) for subject- branch encoding and ResNet-50 with spatial attention for context- branch encoding, achieving hierarchical feature fusion via cross- modal attention mechanisms. A caption-guided alignment module ensures semantic coherence between visual representations and descriptive cues, enhancing explainability through attention visualization and feature attribution maps. Evaluated on AffectNet (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n =250 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> images, 8 emotions), RAF-DB (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=30 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> images), and a curated real-world dataset (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=15 K$</tex> images), CADF- Net achieves 89.4% accuracy on AffectNet and 87.6% on RAF- DB, representing 4.2 % and 3.8 % improvements over state-of- the-art dual-branch methods. Explainability metrics demonstrate superior interpretability through caption consistency analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\kappa=0.82$</tex> Fleiss' kappa agreement with human annotators) and saliency map coherence. Our framework advances the frontiers of trustworthy emotion recognition for real-world deployment in affective computing, human-computer interaction, and clinical psychology applications.

title: Explainable Distillation-Based Machine Unlearning for Sensitive Subgroup Removal in Diabetic Healthcare Data
authors: Ramalingam Murugan, Rajeswari Chengoden
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Healthcare is a domain that demands more privacy with respect to individual information, as it is more sensitive in nature. AI and Machine learnings now-a-days used widely in the healthcare domain to assist medical professionals. Due to this, models remember the data on which it got trained, and this leads to a violation of the privacy policy of individuals. After the launch of unlearning approaches, data are removed from the model, but it still retains knowledge of sensitive information. This work proposes a selective unlearning framework using a teacher-student architecture to eliminate the influence of a specific demographic group without retraining the model from scratch. A deep neural network is used as a teacher model to learn from the diabetic readmission dataset. The dataset is further subdivided into keep and forget set based on the race. The teacher model is getting trained on the full dataset, whereas the student model is trained on the keep set using knowledge distillation. The soft probability outputs generated from the teacher model help in guiding the learning process. No data from the forget set is used during Student training. Experimental results demonstrate that the Student model preserves performance on the retained data while showing a substantial performance reduction on the forgotten subgroup. Evaluation metrics and explainable AI analysis further confirm the effectiveness of the selective unlearning process, making the proposed approach suitable for privacy-sensitive healthcare applications.

title: Sentiment Analysis and LSTM-Based Hybrid Approach for Bitcoin Price Prediction
authors: Ranim Khedhiri, Dalila Amara, Latifa Rabai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile and tend to respond sharply to changes in public sentiment. While a few studies have explored the influence of investor emotions on price fluctuations, there remains a notable gap in integrating social media sentiment with advanced predictive models. This paper proposes a framework that combines sentiment analysis of Reddit discussions with deep learning techniques to improve Bitcoin price forecasting. Sentiment scores are obtained through the VADER model and subsequently integrated with past pricing information to serve as inputs for a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network. The findings show that integrating sentiment data substantially improves prediction accuracy compared to models that depend only on historical price information. This approach highlights the value of behavioral finance when paired with machine learning and offers practical benefits for investors and decision-makers in the crypto space.

title: Lung Cancer Prediction Using Multi Input Model
authors: M. Raghini, M. Satheesh Kumar, S. Sasikala, R. Preetha, J. J. Shivadharshini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Lung cancer is a serious disease which forms cancer cells that lines the air passages of the lungs. It has become a leading cause of death in both men and women due to lifestyles. Early screening of the individuals, vulnerable to lung cancer helps to increase the survival rate. In many research papers, researchers have performed several machine learning, deep learning, and image classification techniques to predict the lung cancer. Machine learning and image classification techniques produce more accurate predictions thereby aiding early treatment. The proposed paper involves a combination of two models each trained with different datasets. Multi-input model is used to make predictions accurate. CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) is used for the image data and traditional algorithms like Random Forest and Gradient boosting is used for structured data. The final performance of the multi-input model is measured using several metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall and ROC-AUC. The results demonstrate that the combination of image data and the clinical data improves the accuracy compared using the either dataset alone. This can aid the health care professionals in making more informed diagnostic decisions.

title: Blockchain Practical Teaching based on Dual-Platform
authors: Ke Xu, Deyou Tang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The industrial development of blockchain technology demands high-quality talents with both theoretical foundations and practical engineering capabilities. To address the prominent challenges in current blockchain talent cultivation, such as over-reliance on a single blockchain platform, fragmented practical teaching, and scarce high-quality teaching resources, this paper designs and implements a dual-platform practical teaching system underpinned by in-depth university-industry collaboration. Centered on two mainstream consortium blockchain platforms (Hyperledger Fabric and FISCO BCOS), the system develops two core courses: "Blockchain Technology and Applications" and "Blockchain Practical Training", both featuring layered and progressive practical content. Additionally, a "Blockchain Practical Teaching Case Repository" covering finance, supply chain, public services, and other related fields is constructed. Teaching practice demonstrates that this system significantly improves student learning outcomes, strengthens the demonstrative impact of teaching reform, and gains wide recognition from the industry. It thereby provides a replicable model for cultivating blockchain engineering talents in higher education.

title: Final Program
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)

title: TeleSim: A Network-Aware Testbed and Benchmark Dataset for Telerobotic Applications
authors: Zexin Deng, Zhenhui Yuan, Longhao Zou
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Telerobotic technologies are becoming increasingly essential in fields such as remote surgery, nuclear decommissioning, and space exploration. Reliable datasets and testbeds are essential for evaluating telerobotic system performance prior to real-world deployment. However, there is a notable lack of datasets that capture the impact of network delays, as well as testbeds that realistically model the communication link between the operator and the robot. This paper introduces TeleSim, a network-aware teleoperation dataset and testbed designed to assess the performance of telerobotic applications under diverse network conditions. TeleSim systematically collects performance data from fine manipulation tasks executed under three predefined network quality tiers: High, Medium, and Low. Each tier is characterized through controlled settings of bandwidth, latency, jitter, and packet loss. Using OMNeT++ for precise network simulation, we record a wide range of metrics, including completion time, success rates, video quality indicators (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM)), and quality of service (QoS) parameters. TeleSim comprises 300 experimental trials, providing a robust benchmark for evaluating teleoperation systems across heterogeneous network scenarios. In the worst network condition, completion time increases by 221.8% and success rate drops by 64%. Our findings reveal that network degradation leads to compounding negative impacts, notably reduced video quality and prolonged task execution, highlighting the need for adaptive, resilient teleoperation protocols. The full dataset and testbed software are publicly available on our GitHub repository: https://github.com/ConnectedRoboticsLab/TeleSim and YouTube channel: https://youtu.be/Fz_1iOYe104.

title: Broad Learning Control for Uncertain Strictfeedback Systems with Asymmetric Full-State Constraints
authors: Youzhi Dong, Donghao Zhang, Linghuan Kong, Wei He
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: In the paper, a novel adaptive control scheme based on broad learning system (BLS) is developed for uncertain strictfeedback systems with fixed-time convergence under the requirement of asymmetric full-state constraints. Compared with existing works, a great difference is that the boundary of virtual control in the paper is known a prior and furthermore can be adjusted by changing controller gains. Moreover, a barrier function is used to guarantee the asymmetric full-state constraints under the fixed-time convergence, and the considered constraint is time-varying. Then, a broad learning control scheme is proposed, which not only eliminates the effects of unknown dynamics, but also ensure that system states converge to a neighborhood of zero in a certain time interval. With Lyapunov stability theory, it is proved that all the state variables are semi-globally uniformly ultimately bounded (SGUUB) with a fixed-time convergence. A simulation example is used to validate the effectiveness of the proposed schemes.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on a Bayesian-Optimized CNN-Transformer Hybrid Network
authors: Boyuan Du, Yanyu Pan, Long Liu, Weiguo Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To overcome the insufficient integration of local and global features in rolling bearing fault diagnosis, as well as the reliance on empirical experience in setting activation function hyperparameters, this paper proposes a Bayesian-optimizationbased CNN-Transformer hybrid fault diagnosis method (BO-CT). The proposed approach develops a hybrid architecture that combines CNN-driven local feature extraction with Transformerbased global temporal modeling, while a Bayesian optimization strategy, using a Gaussian process surrogate model, is employed to automatically determine the negative slope parameter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> of the LeakyReLU activation function. Experimental results on the CWRU dataset indicate that, in a ten-class classification task, the BO-CT model achieves markedly superior diagnostic accuracy and feature clustering performance compared with existing baseline models. These findings confirm that adaptive hyperparameter optimization effectively enhances the performance of hybrid deep learning networks.

title: Applications of Intelligent Image Recognition Technology in Modern Security Inspection and the Evolution of Screening Equipment
authors: Xiang Shen, Guangming Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of public security risks, traditional security inspection systems that rely on manual image interpretation are gradually being replaced by proactive intelligent paradigms driven by deep learning, computer vision, and generative artificial intelligence. This paper provides a systematic review of the intelligent evolution of modern security screening equipment. It examines the technological foundations of intelligent image recognition, including convolutional neural networks, three-dimensional deep learning, generative models, and vision-language models, and analyzes their roles in baggage screening and human body inspection. In baggage screening, X-ray computed tomography systems, supported by volumetric reconstruction, three-dimensional convolutional neural networks, and automatic explosive detection algorithms, have significantly improved the capability to resolve object overlap and occlusion, thus promoting the transition from operator-assisted interpretation to machine-dominant decision support. In human body inspection, millimeter-wave imaging systems combined with automatic target recognition and privacy-preserving display mechanisms have enabled accurate detection of concealed nonmetallic threats while maintaining human-centered design. The paper further summarizes key technical challenges, including small object detection, unknown threat adaptation, limited edge computing resources, and data scarcity, and discusses future directions such as multimodal sensor fusion, non-intrusive screening, continual model evolution, and holistic intelligent security ecosystems. In particular, this review is organized along the technological evolution path of modern security inspection and highlights the applicability boundaries of representative technologies in terms of accuracy, speed, cost, and deployment role.

title: A Dual-Stage Convolutional Framework for Pancreatic Tumor Detection and Classification on Pancreas CT Images
authors: Dhishya E, Anandababu P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Examination of pancreatic tumors according to the computed tomography (CT) images has been a challenging task due to the low tissue contrast, high variability in anatomy, and small morphological difference between normal and pathological regions. This paper will present a new two-stage convolutional network capable of further improving the performance of detection and classification and have an economical cost to implement in the clinic. The first stage involves the segmentation of pancreatic specific regions by using a lightweight encoder decoder and features contrast sensitive to extraction with boundary preserving convolutional unit blocks. The second processing is the guided region-of-interest classifier stage that involves a small convolutional architecture to detect the normal tissue and tumor-affected region by utilizing the fine-tuned segmentation mask. The proposed model is evaluated using the conventional measures, which are more sensitive and specific than the traditional single-stage CNN baselines. It also has a modular design that enables it to be straightforwardly incorporated into downstream diagnostic pipelines so that it can be extended in the future to incorporate transformer-based fusion or generative augmentation frameworks. Overall, the system has provided a strong and justifiable approach of early detection of pancreatal tumors.

title: An IoT-Driven Intelligent Drainage Safety System using Federated Machine Learning for Real-Time Gas and Blockage Prediction
authors: Navaneetha Krishnan M, C.N.Vanitha, Gokul Nath S, Dhanush G, PrasanthT, Bharaniraj M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Urban drainage systems often experience hazardous gas accumulation and physical blockages that threaten maintenance workers and public safety. Conventional drainage monitoring methods rely on periodic inspections and centralized data processing, which suffer from delayed response, communication overhead, and limited scalability. To address these challenges, this study proposes an IoT-driven intelligent drainage safety system that integrates distributed sensing with federated machine learning for real-time gas leakage and blockage prediction. Multiple low-power IoT sensor nodes continuously monitor environmental parameters such as methane concentration, hydrogen sulfide levels, water flow rate, and obstruction indicators. Edge computing devices locally process sensor data and train predictive models, while federated learning enables collaborative model updates without sharing raw data, thereby preserving privacy and reducing network bandwidth usage. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed federated learning approach achieves improved prediction performance with an accuracy of 93.6%, outperforming both rule-based and centralized machine learning models. Furthermore, edge-based processing significantly reduces response latency and accelerates alert generation. The proposed framework provides a scalable, privacy-preserving, and efficient solution for intelligent drainage monitoring, contributing to safer urban infrastructure and smart city development.

title: A Two-Tier Stacking Ensemble Approach for Noise-Resilient Fitness Classification
authors: Arnav Pandey, Rahul Sheoran, Parth Singh Chandel, Yamini B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Achieving accurate fitness classification from wearable and self-reported data remains a significant challenge due to intrinsic noise and complex, non-linear relationships within physiological metrics. To address this, we propose a Two-Tier Stacked Ensemble Model (TTSEM) specifically architected to enhance predictive robustness in noisy environments. The study utilizes a synthetic dataset of 20,000 health records, intentionally injected with 15% label noise to mirror real-world data inconsistencies. The ensemble’s primary tier leverages Random Forest, XGBoost, and CatBoost algorithms to generate probabilistic outputs. These are subsequently synthesized by a Logistic Regression meta-learner in the secondary tier. Empirical evaluations reveal that the TTSEM framework yields an accuracy of 85.48%, alongside a precision of 0.8574, a recall of 0.9203, and an F1-score of 0.8877. This exceptionally high recall minimizes false negatives, ensuring the reliable identification of fit individuals. Furthermore, the system is integrated into a Flask-based deployment architecture, facilitating scalable, real-time inferencing. Ultimately, these findings underscore the efficacy of stacked ensemble techniques in mitigating health data noise, proving highly suitable for modern health informatics applications.

title: EnsembleShield-DM: Interpretable, Shift-Robust Ensemble Learning for Type 2 Diabetes Prediction Across Multi-Regional Cohorts
authors: Aylar Seyedi, Mohammadreza Pournameti, Mahdieh Mosavi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Early identification of diabetes is essential to prevent serious complications and reduce healthcare costs. Many existing predictive models perform well only on limited regional datasets and lose accuracy when applied to new populations. This study presents a scalable and interpretable ensemble learning framework for diabetes prediction using clinical and demographic variables collected from two regional datasets and their merged cohort under a unified structure. The proposed pipeline applies class-aware imputation, data standardization, and synthetic minority oversampling to correct missing information and class imbalance. Three base classifiers, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Logistic Regression, are combined through soft voting, while a confidence-based decision rule improves reliability when model uncertainty is high. The interpretability of the system is evaluated with SHAP values, which clarify the influence of individual features. Experimental results show an average area under the receiver operating characteristic curve above 0.95 with balanced precision and recall across the evaluated datasets. The proposed framework demonstrates stable cross-dataset performance, calibration-aware risk estimation, interpretability, and reproducibility within the studied cohorts, providing a solid foundation for future multi-center or federated investigations in diabetes prediction.

title: FocusED: An NLP-Driven Personalized and Affective-Aware Learning Framework for Neurodivergent Students
authors: C. Gayathri, Yedhu Krishnan. TV, S. Santhosh Kumar, K. Poovarasan, M. Sri Harish Vel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Neurodivergent students, including learners with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), autism spectrum disorder (ASD), dyslexia, and anxiety, often experience difficulties when interacting with conventional digital learning platforms designed for neurotypical cognitive patterns. Most existing systems emphasize content delivery and assessment while providing limited personalization and minimal support for emotional well-being. To address these challenges, this paper proposes FocusED, a personalized learning framework that integrates natural language processing, multimodal behavioral modeling, and affect-aware adaptation. The system continuously collects learner interaction data to construct latent cognitive profiles that guide adaptive content sequencing and presentation. A neuro-symbolic NLP module simplifies complex instructional text and converts key concepts into visual representations to improve comprehension. In addition, an affective inference component identifies emotional states and provides supportive interventions. Experimental evaluation using learner interaction logs shows that FocusED achieves 96.51% learning outcome prediction accuracy, outperforming existing adaptive and deep learning tutoring systems while improving engagement, comprehension, and emotional stability.

title: MA-STGNN: Monsoon-Aware Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Networks with Hybrid Attention for Groundwater Forecasting in India
authors: Makanyadevi K, Mounika B R, Elavarasi A, Ivanthika R, Monikashree P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: In India, groundwater depletion is a major agricultural sustainability and water security issue, compounded by the large seasonal variability in water usage associated with the country's monsoons. As such, traditional methods of estimating future groundwater levels fail to account for the interconnectedness of aquifers, and the nonlinear impact of climatic variability on these levels. Consequently, this study proposes a Monsoon-Aware Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Network (MA-STGNN) framework to predict groundwater levels with improved accuracy. By combining graph convolutional layers to capture the spatial relationship of monitoring wells and LSTM layers to model the temporal changes in groundwater levels, the MA-STGNN accurately predicts future groundwater levels. The MA-STGNN also includes an attention mechanism that is aware of seasonal rainfall recharge patterns and lag effects from rainfall. In addition, and in order to improve forecasting accuracy further, the MA-STGNN combines ARIMA-based linear forecasting with graph neural networks to predict the nonlinear residuals of the forecast. Fifteen years of groundwater level data from India-WRIS were utilized in the MA-STGNN experiments, which were conducted in multiple hydrogeological zones across India. The results of the study indicate that the MA-STGNN significantly improved forecasting accuracy over traditional statistical forecasting methods and deep learning methods such as LSTM and Transformer models, as demonstrated by lower RMSE and higher reliability of the predictions. Thus, the MA-STGNN provides a scalable, data-driven tool for assessing the risk of groundwater levels and for sustainably managing water resources.

title: Auditing Hallucination in Lightweight Vision-Language Models for Radiology Captioning
authors: Ziwei Wang, Haoyun Zhang, Chen Xie, Di Zhu, Zihan Wei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Lightweight vision-language models (VLMs) are attractive for medical image captioning because they run on commodity hardware, but their reliability on radiology images remains poorly characterized. We audited two widely used open-source lightweight captioning models, BLIP-Base and ViT-GPT2, on 991 real radiology images collected from the ROCO dataset. Each image was evaluated in four conditions (clean, Gaussian noise, center crop, and low contrast), yielding 7,928 captions. We assessed a stratified sample of 400 captions with an automated semantic judge and computed exact medical-term string metrics for the full set of 7,928 outputs. Both models hallucinated on every judged caption across every condition. At the model level, hallucination was observed in 200/200 judged outputs for BLIP-Base and 200/200 for ViT-GPT2 (95% exact confidence interval 98.2–100.0 for both). Omission was universal in both models, while fabrication was markedly higher for ViT-GPT2 than for BLIP-Base (97.0% versus 68.0%). BLIP-Base retained limited modality and anatomy recognition (29.5% and 23.5%, respectively), whereas ViT-GPT2 achieved 0% on both endpoints. Input perturbations did not materially worsen performance because failure was already saturated on clean images. In contrast, string matching produced misleadingly favorable scores for ViT-GPT2 through incidental lexical overlap with the reference captions. These results show that lightweight general-purpose VLMs can appear operationally convenient while remaining fundamentally unsuitable for radiology captioning without domain-specific adaptation and semantically grounded evaluation.

authors: Zixuan Li, Pengfei Shen, Hanxiao Sun, Zibo Zhang, Yu Guo, Ligang Liu, Lingqi Yan, Steve Marschner, Milos Hasan, Beibei Wang

title: A New Approach for Coupling Absorbers to Magnetic Microcalorimeters
authors: Nathan Hines, Luke Emmert, Inwook Kim, Chang Lee, Tashi Parsons-Davis, Josh Wimpenny, S. T. P. Boyd, Geon-Bo Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory is developing magnetic microcalorimeter-based decay energy spectrometry for nuclear materials analysis. Our standard technique uses gold wire bonds to thermally connect large, external absorbers to microcalorimeter sensors, which limits signal rise time and thus the maximum measurable activity. To address these limitations, we have developed a new absorber sensor direct coupling approach. We find that the direct-coupling approach achieves an order of magnitude reduction in signal rise time as compared to our standard method. We have also demonstrated that the direct-coupling technique enables accurate isotopic analysis, with results in agreement with those obtained by mass spectrometry. This report describes standard and direct coupling DES measurement techniques and presents measurement results, including a comparison of signal rise time, signal amplitude, and isotopic analysis between the two configurations.

title: DiCLIP: Diffusion Model Enhances CLIP&#x2019;s Dense Knowledge for Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation_supp1-3692055.pdf
authors: Zhiwei Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation (WSSS) with image-level labels typically leverages Class Activation Maps (CAMs) to achieve pixel-level predictions. Recently, Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) has been introduced to generate CAMs in WSSS. However, previous WSSS methods solely adopt CLIP’s vision-language paired property for dense localization, neglecting its inherently limited dense knowledge across both visual and text modalities, which renders CAM generation suboptimal. In this work, we propose DiCLIP, a novel WSSS framework that leverages the generative diffusion model to enhance CLIP’s dense knowledge across two modalities. Specifically, Visual Correlation Enhancement (VCE) and Text Semantic Augmentation (TSA) modules are proposed for dense prediction enhancement. To improve the spatial awareness of visual features, our VCE module utilizes diffusion’s reliable spatial consistency to mitigate the over-smoothing issue in CLIP’s attention. It designs the Attention Clustering Refinement (ACR) module to reliably extract diverse correlation maps from the diffusion model. The correlation maps act as a diversity bias for CLIP’s self-attention, recursively pushing its visual features towards a more discriminative dense distribution. To augment the semantics of text embeddings, our TSA module argues that a single text modality is insufficient to encompass the variability of visual categories. Thus, we leverage diffusion’s generative power to maintain a dynamic key-value cache model, shifting CAM generation from a patch-text matching mechanism to a novel visual knowledge retrieval paradigm.With these enhancements, DiCLIP not only outperforms state-of-the-art methods on PASCAL VOC and MS COCO but also significantly reduces training costs. Code will be publicly available at https://github.com/zwyang6/DiCLIP.

title: Transient Detection on EAF Current Signals Sampled at Low Rates via Statistical Analysis
authors: Erhan Sezgin, Ebrahim Balouji, Özgül Salor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Electric Arc Furnaces (EAFs) introduce stochastic current transients that degrade electrode life and power quality. Detecting such events is challenging when high-frequency components are attenuated by the anti-aliasing filters of standard monitoring devices. This paper proposes a fast, deterministic, and training-free transient detector operating on EAF currents sampled at low rates compared to transients. The method forms half-cycle RMS features, extracts a residual via a light Savitzky–Golay trend estimate, and applies numerical differentiation followed by a sliding-window Z-score test with hysteresis logic to robustly label transients. Performance is evaluated on both synthetic signals and field measurements, and is benchmarked against a robust LSTM autoencoder (LSTM-AE) reconstruction-based detector enhanced with robust normalization, training cleaning, and adaptive thresholding. In the evaluated cold-start setting, the proposed approach significantly outperforms the deep learning baseline. While the LSTM-AE suffers from severe event fragmentation and degrades to an F1-score of 0.13 on actual EAF measurements, the proposed method maintains a robust F1-score of 0.68. Furthermore, it operates with substantially lower computational overhead and no training requirement, making it highly suitable for low-latency industrial monitoring in EAF installations.
