After channel release, the bottom Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.8</sub> Ge<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.2</sub> pFET with seamless Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.6</sub> Ge<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.4</sub> S/D is compressively strained, while the top Si nFET with seamed Si:P (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 10^{{21}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) S/D is also slightly compressively strained due to inward bending of Si:P S/D regrowth. Seamless Si:P/Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.95</sub> C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.05</sub> and seamed Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.8</sub> Ge<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.2</sub> S/D can provide the desired tensile strain<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\textit {xx}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> to top nFETs to boost mobility. The seamless Si:P and seamless Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.6</sub> Ge<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.4</sub> S/D of nFET and pFET, respectively, can provide strain-enhanced mobility and uniform inter-NS strain regardless of the n on p or p on n placement.

title: Memory Non-Sharing and Context Fragmentation in Multi-Agent Systems: Problems and Solutions
authors: Zhengwen He, Mingyong Yin, Dewen Kong, Yubing Duan, Guoxin Zhong, Yulong Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems show great potential in collaborative complex tasks. However, limited by fixed context windows and flawed memory mechanisms, they often suffer from issues such as memory non-sharing, context fragmentation, and hallucination cascades, which severely undermine collaboration effectiveness. Existing studies have alleviated some problems through shared memory, knowledge graphs, and reflection mechanisms. Nevertheless, reflection mostly focuses on correcting current errors, lacking explicit modeling and long-term memory of error causes and failure patterns, leading systems to potentially repeat mistakes in similar scenarios. Building on the framework of shared structured graph memory and reflective update, this paper proposes a multi-agent reflection mechanism incorporating failure pattern memory. The core idea is: during the reflection phase, not only identify and correct erroneous facts and inconsistent reasoning paths, but also extract error types, root causes, and typical context patterns. These are encoded as nodes (e.g., ErrorEvent, RootCause, MitigationRule, ContextPattern) and relational edges in the graph, forming a retrievable failure pattern subgraph. In subsequent task planning, tool calling, and multi-agent collaboration, the system can proactively retrieve these failure pattern memories and inject corresponding preventive rules and risk prompts into the current context, thereby achieving a shift from "post-hoc error correction" to "pre-emptive prevention". We present the complete system architecture and collaboration process, discuss potential advantages compared to traditional multi-agent memory and reflection mechanisms, and design corresponding baseline comparison schemes and evaluation metrics. This framework is expected to yield significant gains in reducing hallucination repetition rates, minimizing cross-agent conflicts, improving task success rates, and enhancing long-term adaptive capabilities.

title: Cooperative Optimization Method for Intersection Signals Based on Transformer-BEV Representation and QMIX Value Decomposition Reinforcement Learning
authors: Shun Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: As urban traffic networks become increasingly complex, achieving intelligent coordinated optimization of intersection signals has become a key issue in intelligent transportation systems. This paper proposes an intersection signal coordination optimization method based on Transformer-BEV representation and QMIX value decomposition reinforcement learning. Building on the existing BEV representation, the method introduces a Transformer encoder-decoder structure to effectively extract global spatiotemporal features among multiple intersections. The extracted features are then input into the QMIX multi-agent reinforcement learning framework, treating each intersection as an agent to achieve distributed signal control. To address the limitations of traditional QMIX in modeling complex inter-agent dependencies, this paper designs an Adaptive Attention QMIX (AAQMIX) module, which adaptively adjusts value decomposition weights through a dynamic traffic interaction graph learning mechanism, thereby enhancing the model's robustness and generalization capability in dynamic traffic scenarios. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms existing mainstream methods in terms of average waiting time, traffic throughput, and inter-intersection coordination.

title: A Cloud-Edge Collaborative Task Offloading Strategy for Cost Optimization in Remote Field Scenarios
authors: Jinhuai Yang, Zhiyuan He, Jing Chen, Xiaojuan Yang, Jianchen Lin, Fenzeng Chen, Qianling Zheng, Qifan Yang, Qianwen Wu, Yuzhong Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Cloud-edge collaborative computing is increasingly applied in complex scenarios like field observation. However, large-scale heterogeneous tasks from terminal devices pose significant challenges for resource scheduling. To address this, a composite wireless channel model is constructed to accurately characterize field environments, incorporating path loss, shadowing, and Rician fading. Based on this, a system cost optimization model is established, considering task latency, storage, and bandwidth constraints. Subsequently, we propose an Adaptive Cooperative Genetic Algorithm-Particle Swarm Optimization (AC-GAPSO) to minimize the overall system cost. AC-GAPSO synergizes the crossover and mutation mechanisms of Genetic Algorithms (GA) with the rapid convergence characteristics of Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). Specifically, a topology-aware scheduling strategy is employed to strictly satisfy task dependency, while an adaptive cooperative mechanism is introduced to enhance global search performance, incorporating a penalty function-based fitness evaluation and feasibility-priority ranking to ensure solution feasibility. Experimental results show that AC-GAPSO outperforms PSO, GA, and Greedy algorithms, achieving an average total cost reduction of 42.02%, validating its effectiveness in field collaborative computing.

title: Design of Robotic Arm Control System Based on Fuzzy PID Algorithm
authors: Tianyu Wu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Addressing the nonlinear and strongly coupled characteristics of robotic manipulators, along with the poor robustness and limited adaptability of traditional PID control under complex operating conditions, this paper presents the designed of a robotic arm control system based on a fuzzy PID algorithm. Initially, the hardware of the robotic arm was divided into four core modules, clarifying the structure and collaborative logic of each component. Subsequently, a fuzzy PID controller is constructed, employing a six-step core process to achieve dynamic tuning of PID parameters. Finally, a simulation model of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) with load disturbance rejection is built using Matlab/Simulink to compare the control performance of traditional PID and fuzzy PID. The simulation results demonstrate that the fuzzy PID algorithm can reduce the three-phase current imbalance of the motor from 8.3% to 3.1%, significantly suppress torque and speed overshoot, substantially shorten disturbance recovery time, and limit steadystate fluctuations of the system to only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 \%} \boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> of those observed with traditional PID. This algorithm effectively enhances the dynamic response speed and disturbance rejection capability of the robotic arm drive system, overcoming the limitations of traditional PID in nonlinear systems and providing a viable solution for optimizing control algorithms for robotic arms in high-end intelligent equipment.

title: A Wideband Q/V Band Cascode Darlington Power Amplifier Using Transmission Line Transformer and Current-Reuse Techniques
authors: Chin-Wei Kuo, Hsin-Chieh Lin, Chien-Nan Kuo, Hwann-Kaeo Chiou
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced current-reuse cascode Darlington broadband power amplifier using the cost effective tsmc <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{\text {TM }} 90-\text{nm}$</tex> CMOS process. It employs a modified Darlington architecture and a transmission-line transformer for ultra-broadband performance. Measurements show a 16.4 dB small-signal gain with a 3-dB bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27 \text{GHz}(33-60 \text{GHz})$</tex>, achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 8 \%}$</tex> fractional bandwidth. The amplifier delivers a wideband flat saturated output power (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{P}_{\text {sat }}$</tex>) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.9 \pm 0.6 \text{dBm}$</tex> from 35 to 60 GHz. The total chip area is only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.82 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex>.

title: Vision Transformer Combined with Force Feedback for Flexible Object Grasping and Recognition in Robotic Arms
authors: Junqi Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In response to the bottleneck of accurately recognizing dynamic deformation and interaction states when a single visual modality is used to grasp flexible objects by a robotic arm, this paper proposes a Vision Tactile Fusion Network (VTFN) based on force guided visual attention. The research aims to address the problem of insufficient deep dynamic correlation mining between visual global deformation and instantaneous physical signals of force perception using existing multimodal methods. The method adopts a dual stream encoding architecture: the visual stream extracts spatial feature maps and global representations containing global deformation context based on the MobileViT-v2 (Mobile Vision Transformer) backbone network; Force flow uses temporal convolutional networks to encode the physical dynamic characteristics of force/torque sequences. This article proposes a directional cross modal fusion module that uses encoded force sensing features as queries to actively retrieve the most relevant regions to the current physical interaction in visual spatial features. Through attention mechanisms, precise adaptive modal alignment and feature fusion are achieved. On the self built “FlexiGrass Dynamic” dataset, VTFN achieved an accuracy of 92.7% and an average F1 score of 0.915. The ablation experiment confirmed the effectiveness of each module, and the noise robustness and cross object generalization tests further showed that the method maintained high recognition accuracy under noise interference, with an average accuracy of 84.0% on unseen objects, demonstrating superior practicality and generalization ability.

title: SoilWalk: An IoT-Enabled Smart Slipper System for Real-Time Soil Health Mapping
authors: Mary Shamala L, Tracita H
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Soil health plays a big role in deciding how well crops grow and how much they yield. Many farmers still face issues because they do not have easy or affordable ways to check their soil condition regularly. SoilWalk is a smart slipper system designed to make this process simple and handy. The slipper has built-in sensors that can measure soil moisture, temperature, and TDS levels while walking on the field. The data is sent wirelessly to a cloud platform using IoT and is shown on a dashboard in real time. Based on the readings, the system helps to identify if the soil is in good, moderate, or poor condition. This idea mainly focuses on helping farmers understand their soil better without depending on lab tests or expensive equipment. It is low-cost, portable, and easy to use, making it a good fit for small and local farmers to improve their crop management and yield.

title: Multi-Parameter Fusion Evaluation and Dynamic Binning Scheduling for Energy Storage Systems
authors: Chenrui Tang, Sipeng Hao, Yurui Li, Yue Yuan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: The inherent inconsistency among individual battery cells leads to the degradation of the overall performance of battery energy storage systems (BESS). This paper proposes a multi-parameter fusion and dynamic binning scheduling strategy to improve battery consistency and optimize system performance. Firstly, a multi-parameter battery state coefficient is constructed based on the State of Charge (SOC), State of Health (SOH), internal resistance, and temperature, which enhances the accuracy and comprehensiveness of battery performance evaluation. Performance calculation formulas for individual cells in both charging and discharging states are also derived. Secondly, cells are categorized into different bins based on their performance; to account for performance variations during operation, a dynamic adjustment mechanism combining event-triggered and timetriggered controls is implemented. Finally, battery power is allocated across different bins according to power response requirements, with limiting and derating constraints integrated to ensure scheduling stability. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces SOC dispersion and promotes the convergence of battery state coefficients. The strategy maintains the stability of the binning structure and power allocation across multiple scenarios, achieving continuous improvement in battery cluster consistency and providing technical support for the efficient operation of BESS.

title: SPPFuzz: A State-Aware Process Pool Based Black-Box Fuzzing Framework for Network Protocols
authors: Yining Liuli, Yan Cao, Xuan Qi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The throughput of stateful network protocol fuzzing is fundamentally constrained by initialization overhead, full-state resets, and kernel protocol stack latency. This paper presents SPPFuzz, a black-box framework that addresses these limitations through three core mechanisms. First, the State-Aware Process Pool employs Dirty-Page-Aware Snapshot Recovery (DPASR) to minimize reset costs by tracking soft-dirty pages. Second, Protocol Semantic Mapping reconstructs network interface semantics in user space to achieve zero-copy communication. Third, Memory Behavior Feedback (MBF) infers internal protocol states by exploiting dirty-page distribution characteristics. Experiments on ProFuzzBench demonstrate that SPPFuzz achieves 17.37× to 38.20× throughput improvement over AFLNet. Furthermore, the framework outperforms the baseline by triggering 4.3× more unique crashes in total and discovering over 3× more unique states.

title: Precision Docking Adaptive Clamp Claw Design Based on Edge Calculation
authors: Boming Li, Wangzhun Chu, Helong Li, Sheng Hua, Guangze Zhu, Gong Chen, Jie Huang, Shunming Teng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Due to the advantages of small size, less footprint and high reliability, GIS equipment has gradually replaced the original open switching equipment (AIS) equipment, which is widely used in substations and converter stations of various voltage levels, and has become the most important form of electrical equipment. For the installation of GIS equipment, there is an urgent need for a mechanized equipment to achieve high-precision docking, replacing the backward is the construction technology. This paper proposes an adaptive clamclaw structure based on edge calculation, which can overcome the problem of large weight and high precision clamping and install it on the multi-degree of freedom robotic arm to complete GIS installation.

title: A Multimodal Embedded System for Real-Time UAV Detection using Vision and Acoustic Signals
authors: Rohith S, Hemanth Kumar D, L. Jegan Antony Marcilin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increase of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) usage has created difficulties in surveillance, security monitoring and airspace safety. It is necessary to detect unauthorized UAV(s) in real-time in order to protect sensitive areas including critical infrastructure, restricted zones and public events. This paper presents a low-power, embedded; UAV Detection System with vision-based object detection and Acoustic signal detection for real-time UAV activity monitoring and detection. Using the YOLOv8 deep learning model to detect UAVs in video images from a surveillance camera as well as a MAX4466 microphone sensor for the detection of UAV-specific rotor noise, a complete UAV detection system can be accomplished. Through the use of data fusion of both visual and acoustic UAV detection data, our system improves reliability in the performance of UAV detection as well as reduces false alarms. Acoustic signal acquisition and preprocessing will occur using an STM32 microcontroller while an ESP32 module will handle communication and alert transmission. Alert notifications will occur in real-time when a UAV is detected through the use of LED, buzzer, GSM-based SMS alerts, and alerts via Telegram. Experimental results show that the UAV Detection System provides reliable UAV detections with the performance of real-time detection capabilities through the fusion of deep learning-based using sound to detect vision is a specialized approach for embedded UAV surveillance and is effective for UAV surveillance systems.

title: FPGA-Based Real-Time Embedded Vision System with Gigabit Ethernet Communication
authors: Yang Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Real-time image processing and low-latency data transmission are essential requirements for embedded vision systems in edge computing scenarios. This paper presents an FPGA-based real-time embedded vision system implemented on the Anlogic EG4S20 platform. The proposed system builds a full-hardware accelerated pipeline covering image acquisition, real-time image processing, and Gigabit Ethernet data transmission. Multiple visual tasks are realized in parallel hardware architecture, including Sobel edge detection, color-based object detection, shape classification, and motion trajectory tracking with fade-out effects. To support stable video streaming, an asynchronous FIFO cross-clock domain buffering scheme and a lightweight UDP packetization mechanism are designed for real-time Gigabit Ethernet transmission. Furthermore, the system supports direct peer-to-peer Ethernet communication between two FPGA boards, providing a feasible prototype for distributed embedded vision applications. Experimental results show that the system achieves a stable frame rate of 12-13 fps at 640x480 resolution, with an end-to-end latency below 100 ms and optimized resource utilization. While the observed frame rate is limited by host-side rendering overhead, the internal FPGA hardware pipeline achieves a deterministic processing latency of less than 3 ms, demonstrating high intrinsic throughput. The proposed design validates the efficiency of FPGA-based parallel acceleration for real-time embedded vision and shows good potential for industrial edge vision applications.

title: Performance Evaluation of Approximate Multipliers for Energy-Efficient Computing Systems
authors: Bhuvana B P, Pown M, V Monishkumar, P Sairam, Bharathi Raju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The paper introduces the deployment and performance analysis of approximate multiplier architectures and compares them to the traditional Array, Wallace and Vedic multipliers 4x4 and 8x8 on FPGA. The comparison will be done regarding hardware utilization and propagation delay. It has been shown through experimental results that the approximate multiplier incurs a smaller number of slices and LUTs than the architectures in both designs. In addition, the proposed design has a lower critical path delay, making it compute time-saving. The results of the observed improvements confirm that the controlled approximation can be effectively used to decrease the complexity of hardware, but the computational performance is not worse. The paper confirms that approximate multipliers are quite appropriate in a situation where small calculation errors can be afforded with great value in return of huge gains in area efficiency and speed, and is thus a good contender to both low-power and high-performance VLSI systems.

title: SAR Image Change Detection Based on Saliency Guidance and Edge Enhancement
authors: Shuanghui Wang, Zhihui Xin, Jianxiang Zeng, Runze Song
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: SAR image change detection is a key remote sensing technology, but it suffers from weak change target indistinctness, edge blurriness and poor robustness in complex noise environments. To address these issues, this paper proposes a SAR image change detection method based on saliency guidance and structure enhancement, following the "localization first, refinement later" idea. Firstly, a high-quality difference map is generated via multi-scale difference construction and structural response enhancement to strengthen the response of weak change targets. Secondly, a saliency detection mechanism is introduced to adaptively screen candidate change regions, globally suppressing background noise and non-structural interference. Finally, gradient and edge structure information are integrated to compensate details and enhance contours of salient regions, restoring the boundary information of change regions. Experimental results show that the method effectively resolves edge blurriness and target breakage in traditional methods, improves the separability between changed and unchanged regions, and enhances robustness in complex scenarios, providing an efficient solution for SAR image change detection.

title: Blockchain based Electronic Health Record (EHR) Management
authors: Shankar Gugulothu, D. Sandhya Rani, L. Swathi, K. Mrudul, Y. Kavya, K. Uday Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing use of digital healthcare has raised the demand for a secured and efficient way of handling the data in the health records. The Electronic Health Record (EHR) Management System based on the blockchain technology represents an innovative approach that has been developed for addressing the issues like possibility of ruptures, lack of transparency, and the absence of patients’ control in the patients’ hands currently present in the prevailing EHR Management Systems. For the proposed project, an EHR Management System will be developed that will be decentralized and capable enough to ensure the secured storage of health records of the patients, along with their efficient sharing within their authorized health care organizations through the use of the blockchain technology. The use of blockchain technology will ensure the EHRs possess the strongest characteristics like immutability, increased level of transparency, and the highest level of smart contract-based management regarding the right of health records’ sharing. The storage of the health files will be done through the storage utilization of the blockchain-based hashes.

title: Realtime Wildlife Detection and Alert System using IoT Enabled Drone with Machine Learning for Anti-Poaching
authors: Prem Anand T P, Jayaganesh M, Akash T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Wildlife poaching and illegal entry into forest areas are still major concerns in protecting biodiversity and endangered species. Monitoring forest areas is difficult due to a lack of manpower and inadequate communication facilities. In this paper, a real-time wildlife detection and alert system is proposed that incorporates machine learning and IoT technologies to increase forest area monitoring. In this proposed system, a Raspberry Pi 5 is utilized as an onboard computer, an Arducam high-resolution camera is utilized to capture real-time video, and a SIM7600 4G LTE module is utilized to transmit and communicate in real-time. The main aim of this paper is to develop a YOLOv12 object detection model that can analyze and detect wildlife, intruders, and poachers in forest areas using a live video feed. A dataset is prepared and utilized to train the model to increase its ability to detect objects in an aerial environment. From the experimental results, it is observed that the proposed optimized YOLOv12 object detection model is able to detect objects with an accuracy of over 65%. Once a suspicious object is detected in a live video feed, a notification is sent to the authorities with information regarding the incident.

title: A Robust Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Lung Tumor Classification using Taguchi Optimization and Fuzzy Inference
authors: Indrish Pranav C K, Ajanthaa Lakkshmanan, Sri Nidhi A, Jesline. D, Ram Saravanan Kumaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Accurate early lung-tumor detection from computed tomography (CT) scans remains difficult due to noise, intensity variations, and borderline cases where tumor boundaries are visually ambiguous. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework named Adversarially Robust Taguchi-Optimized Fuzzy Convolutional Neural Network (ATF-CNN) for automated lung tumor classification. The pipeline begins with CT preprocessing for denoising and region enhancement, followed by CNN-based hierarchical feature extraction. To reduce trial-and-error tuning and improve training stability, Taguchi design of experiments is used to select robust hyperparameters efficiently. A fuzzy inference layer is then introduced to model uncertainty in deep features and to soften decision boundaries for difficult cases. In addition, adversarial training is incorporated to strengthen robustness against small input perturbations. The proposed model performs binary classification (tumor vs. non-tumor) and demonstrates strong performance in terms of accuracy, sensitivity, and AUC, indicating its suitability for computer-aided diagnostic support.

title: CNN-Alex Net Framework for Gallbladder Disease Detection using Ultrasound Imaging
authors: S.Prem Kumar, Yagatheepan S, Sathish Kumar P S, Pratheep P, Vijaya Kishore G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Gallbladder diseases range from benign conditions such as gallstones and inflammatory disorders to highly aggressive malignancies, where delayed diagnosis often results in poor prognosis. Ultrasonography is the primary imaging modality for gallbladder evaluation due to its non-invasive nature, low cost, and wide availability; however, its interpretation is highly operator-dependent and prone to inter-observer variability. To address these challenges, we propose a CNN–AlexNet framework for automated gallbladder disease detection using ultrasound images. The proposed system employs ultrasound-specific preprocessing techniques, including noise reduction, normalization, and data augmentation, followed by transfer learning with a fine-tuned AlexNet architecture optimized using the Adam optimizer. The model performs multi-class classification to identify five gallbladder conditions: normal, abnormal, benign tumor, malignant tumor, and stone. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves high classification accuracy with improved robustness against noise and imaging variability. The integration of a user-friendly graphical interface further enhances clinical applicability by enabling real-time analysis and interpretability. The results indicate that the proposed approach can serve as an effective computer-aided diagnostic tool to support clinicians in early and accurate gallbladder disease detection.

title: AI-Powered Skill Assessment: Personalized Feedback Through Video and Emotion Analysis
authors: A.Priyanka, A.Chiranjeevi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The traditional skill assessment techniques that involve written tests and manual skill assessment analysis are not effective enough to capture the real-time learning behavior and emotional engagement of the learners, leading to incomplete analysis of skill performance. The primary aim of this research work is to develop an intelligent and adaptive skill assessment system that not only analyzes the proficiency of knowledge but also captures the emotional and behavioral aspects of the learners during the skill assessment process. This research work aims to develop an AI-powered skill assessment system that combines dynamic question generation, video emotion analysis and personalized feedback delivery through an interactive user interface. The proposed system uses a language model-driven approach to develop adaptive assessment questions, and a deep learning-based convolutional neural network approach to analyze live video feeds and identify facial emotions such as confidence, stress, focus and hesitation. The user responses, emotional conditions, and performance measures are constantly tracked and recorded for secure storage in a structured database. The result of the experimental assessment proves that the emotion recognition model has an accuracy of 92.4%, whereas the skill evaluation and performance classification module has an accuracy of 94.1%.

title: Research on Genetic Algorithm Optimization for Job-shop Scheduling Problem
authors: Jie Lv, Zewen Lai, Yuanjia Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The research on job-shop scheduling problems is crucial in the field of manufacturing production optimization. The focus of such research is to achieve specific production goals by reasonably arranging task processing sequences and machine allocation. Based on traditional genetic algorithm, this paper optimizes the traditional algorithm through recursive neural network simulation of fitness function and simulated annealing hybrid algorithm. Through benchmark studies, it is found that the hybrid algorithm yields better results.

title: Shielded Med-AI: Privacy-Centric Federated Deep Learning for Healthcare Imaging
authors: Thripthi P Balakrishnan, Gangaraju Vamil, Bommadi Vanitha, Nara Vasantha, Somarpu Sowmya Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Federated learning is now an attractive method of privacy protection in the analysis of medical images. It allows collaborative model training without the requirement to reveal personal patient data. Nevertheless, the balance between diagnostics quality and patient data confidentiality is still a considerable issue, and especially in large healthcare facilities. This paper describes Shielded Med-AI, a privacy-conscious federated deep learning model of multi-disease medical image classification in multiple healthcare organizations. The system uses convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to perform local training on the patient data. The encrypted model updates are sent to the central aggregation server which uses the Federated Averaging algorithm. Noise injection and gradient clipping are applied in order to enhance privacy with the help of differential privacy. There are reduced risks of information leakage through this strategy. Besides, the framework employs weighted aggregation and data augmentation to manage non-IID data distributions. In addition, explainability through Grad-Cam is added to improve clinical transparency and trust by providing the visual understanding of model predictions. This method according to the experimental results preserves a small accuracy loss to enhance privacy with an accuracy of 93.3 in centrally managed environments, 92.1 in federated learning, and 90.8 with different privacy. This is a safe and easy-to-use method and suitable in real healthcare facilities.

title: Battery Management System with IoT based Monitoring and Data Logging
authors: Vaishnavi R, Divyashree B P, Lakith G, Sharmila N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increase adaption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) requires need of intelligent and reliable battery management solutions to ensure user safety and system longevity. This study proposes an IoT based Battery Management System (BMS) for EV which continuously monitors the important parameters like voltage, current, temperature and smoke detection with the help of an ESP32 microcontroller. The measured data is continuously saved in google sheets using cloud based communication. The proposed system uses sensors to monitor normal and abnormal data, and there is a cut-off relay which disconnects the battery if fault occurs to prevent thermal runaway. It includes the integration of Blynk IoT platform for remote monitoring and notification for alerts. In addition, it contains Liquid Crystal Display (LCD) module integrated for local visualization of the battery parameters. The developed BMS system has undergone tests repetitively to minimize the false alerts, also by increasing its performance and reliability, which increases the battery safety, extended operational lifespan and enhancing energy efficiency in the EV.

title: Approximating local properties by tensor network states with constant bond dimension
authors: Yichen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Classical simulation of quantum many-body systems is a fundamental challenge due to their exponentially large Hilbert spaces. Tensor network states are a powerful ansatz to efficiently represent many physically relevant quantum states. A key question is the bond dimension—which determines the number of parameters in the ansatz—required to approximate all local properties to accuracy δ. In one dimension, we prove that an area law for the Rényi entanglement entropy Rα with index α < 1 implies a matrix product state representation with bond dimension poly(1/δ). For (at most constant-fold degenerate) ground states of one-dimensional gapped Hamiltonians, a bond dimension almost linear in 1/δ suffices. In two dimensions, an area law for Rα(α < 1) implies a projected entangled pair state representation with bond dimension eO(1/δ). In both one and two dimensions, analogous results are obtained for states with logarithmic corrections to the area law. These findings rigorously justify the common practice of using a system-size-independent bond dimension in tensor network simulations.

title: High Selectivity Superconducting Dual-Band Bandpass Filter Using Degenerate Modes of Capacitively Loaded Double Ring Resonators
authors: Xinlei Liu, Zhewang Ma, Baoping Ren, Bingqing Miao
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this letter, a capacitively loaded double ring resonator (CL-DRR) with a pair of degenerate modes is proposed. Compared with conventional dual-mode ring resonators, its size is reduced by 86.5%, and its two modes can be controlled independently even though they are closely neighbored. When the CL-DRRs are coupled in different orientations, distinctive coupling characteristics are obtained, and multiple transmission zeros (TZs) are produced. By using dual-tapped feedlines with a compensation tuning technique, the independent control of the external quality factors of the dual passbands is achieved. A compact dual-band bandpass filter (BPF) with a small center-frequency ratio is designed, realizing independent control of both its center frequencies and bandwidths, together with sharp roll-off passbands due to its multiple TZs. The measured response of the fabricated filter using high-temperature superconducting (HTS) films satisfies the design specifications well and shows very low loss.

title: Physics-based self-supervised learning of a deep network for single-shot in-line hologram reconstruction
authors: Dylan Brault, Félix Riedel, Corinne Fournier, Thomas Olivier, Loïc Denis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Digital in-line holographic microscopy is a computational imaging method useful for characterizing the refractive properties of a sample, i.e. the phase shift and absorption. This indirect measurement technique captures a diffraction pattern and uses reconstruction algorithms to retrieve the optical properties of the sample. Since only the intensity of the diffracted wave is recorded on the sensor, this inversion is not trivial, and simple backward propagation leads to artifacts known in optics as the “twin-image”. With advances in deep learning, various algorithms have been developed for the reconstruction of in-line holograms, providing computationally efficient alternatives to iterative algorithms. These algorithms rely either on supervised learning, which requires ground truth knowledge, or physics-based self-supervised algorithms that require additional information, like phase diversity, but require multiple holograms for inference. This paper introduces a new self-supervised physics-based deep learning strategy that leverages phase diversity during training and then reconstructs sample's transmission function from a single in-line hologram during inference. We introduce five datasets of simulated and experimental in-line holograms of beads and bacteria. The proposed method produces accurate quantitative reconstructions similar or even more accurate than those obtained by regularized inversion while reducing the computational time by a factor of 1000.

title: LineRides: Line-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Bicycle Robot Stunts_supp1-3693936.mp4
authors: Seungeun Rho
publish: N/A
abstract: Designing reward functions for agile robotic maneuvers in reinforcement learning remains difficult, and demonstration-based approaches often require reference motions that are unavailable for novel platforms or extreme stunts. We present lineride, a line-guided learning framework that enables a custom bicycle robot to acquire diverse, commandable stunt behaviors from a user-provided spatial guideline and sparse key-orientations, without demonstrations or explicit timing. lineride handles physically infeasible guidelines using a tracking margin that permits controlled deviation, resolves temporal ambiguity by measuring progress via traveled distance along the guideline, and disambiguates motion details through position- and sequence-based key-orientations. We evaluate lineride on the Ultra Mobility Vehicle UMV and show that the policy trained with our methods supports seamless transitions between normal driving and stunt execution, enabling five distinct stunts on command: MiniHop, LargeHop, ThreePointTurn, Backflip, and DriftTurn.

title: Data-Driven Probabilistic Evaluation of Admissible Heat-Source Fluctuation Ranges in District Heating Networks Using Measured Grid-Frequency Data
authors: Zhou Chengjian, Wang Zhonghao, Qin Huan, Gao Sijie
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study develops a data-driven probabilistic framework for evaluating admissible heat-source fluctuation ranges in district-heating networks. Measured grid-frequency-deviation data are first audited, conservatively cleaned, and standardized day by day to build a reliable empirical base process. A standardized disturbance library is then generated by moving block bootstrap so that the main non-Gaussian and temporal-correlation features are retained. The source-side heat-flow disturbance is represented in a unified (μ, σ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) parameter space, and admissibility is evaluated by Monte Carlo simulation using empirical feasibility probability instead of single-path judgment. Temperature-scan results from −20 °C to 0 °C show that both admissible envelopes expand as the weather becomes milder, but the data-driven envelope contracts earlier on the high-μ side, yielding a narrower feasible mean span and a lower variance tolerance in the upper operating range. The framework therefore supports a consistent comparison between ideal Gaussian disturbances and data-driven disturbances and provides a more realistic basis for assessing the stochastic operating capability of district-heating systems.

title: Boundary Constraint and Dynamic Graph Feature Decoupling for Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation
authors: Zhoufeng Liu, Bingrui Li, Chunlei Li, Shumin Ding, Miao Yu, Di Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: The weakly supervised semantic segmentation tasks have progressed through pseudo-labels derived from class activation maps. Most methods perform poorly in complex scenes, capturing local foreground features and relying on simple context. To address these challenges, we propose the boundary constraint and dynamic graph feature decoupling(BC-GFD) approach, which is designed to enhance foreground features representations through three key components: the Moment-Constrained Fusion strategy (MCFS), the Dynamic Graph Relational Mapping(DGRM) strategy, and the semantic difference measurement(SDM) strategy. Specifically, MCFS is designed to emphasize and constrain non-discriminative regions, while DGRM manages long-range dependencies between global contextual semantics to extract global fine-grained features. Meanwhile, SDM focuses on quantifying foreground-background disparity, enhancing the ability to decouple local fine-grained features. BC-GFD effectively focuses on constraining boundary features and integrating global-local complementary foreground features to enhance adaptability to complex contexts. Extensive experiments on the PASCAL VOC 2012 and MS COCO 2014 demonstrate the effectiveness of our method and achieve the state-of-the-art performance.

title: Hierarchical Event-Triggered Cooperative Control of Heterogeneous Vehicle Platoons Under Switching Topologies
authors: Zhiping Shen, Huarui Guo, Yilin Wu, Weikang Yang, Yonggui Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper studies the cooperative control problem for heterogeneous connected vehicle platoons (HCVPs) under switching topologies, which do not require each subgraph to be necessarily connected. First, to effectively save communication costs, a hierarchical control framework composed of an upper-level event-triggered observer (ETO) layer and a lower-level local tracking controller layer is proposed. Within the proposed framework, an ETO for each follower vehicle is designed to observe the leader, and a local tracking controller is designed using the proposed observer to ensure that each follower has the tracking capability of their own observer. Second, to improve the robustness of the system, a completely distributed control protocol is designed for jointly connected undirected topologies, which does not require global network information and is independent of switching rules. Meanwhile, to overcome the challenge of constructing Lyapunov function, a new convergence analysis method based on the contraction argument is employed for jointly connected directed topologies to solve the cooperative control problem of the vehicle platoon (VP). Third, the absence of Zeno-behavior in any finite time can be verified by showing that the event interval between any two consecutive triggering instants is strictly greater than zero. Finally, simulations are used to verify the effectiveness of the proposed protocols.

title: Agentic RTE: A Framework for Synchronizing Multimodal Software Artifacts
authors: Tales Mello Paiva, Ivens da Silva Portugal, Paulo Alencar, Donald Cowan
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: Alzheimer's Disease Detection Using CNN-Based MRI Analysis and Cognitive Assessment
authors: K. Selvasheela, K. Sai Siddhartha, D. Teja Narasimha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The early onset of the Alzheimer's Disease (AD), differences in cognitive reactions, and lack of a reconciled diagnostic systems complicate AD diagnosis. This system fills these gaps by combining multimodal information, namely, user verified demographic information, cognitive assessment results, and structural MRI images to create a powerful prediction pipeline. The system has been built on the principles of high-end CNN-based MRI feature extraction, machine learning classifiers and temporal result tracking to increase the accuracy of the diagnoses and effectively assess the development of the disease. The development of automated report generation amalgamates prediction metrics, progression indicators and interpretations by the system. Moreover, the site offers evidence-based suggestions and prevention measures to user-based risk profiles. Findings show that there is better consistency of detection, monitoring reliability across user sessions and workflow alignment that is appropriate to the clinical setting to identify early Alzheimer's Disease (AD). Tests indicate that system provides more relevant output by achieving an accuracy of 94.7%.

title: Evaluate Cybersecurity Risks in Electric Vehicle Ecosystems: Identify Attack Points in Communication, Infotainment, and Battery Management Modules
authors: Muruganantham Angamuthu, Umamaheswari Vadivel, A. Prasina
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The number of people choosing to drive electric vehicles (EVs) is growing rapidly over the world. The introduction of this ground-breaking technology has the potential to greatly improve digital network bandwidth, power consumption efficiency, and air purity. New cybersecurity vulnerabilities have been created as a result of this change's execution; these vulnerabilities could compromise user trust, operational dependability, privacy, and safety. Infotainment systems, battery management modules, and vehicle-toexternet (V2X) communication networks are the primary targets of this investigation into possible cybersecurity risks in the electric car ecosystem. The current status of electric vehicle design, functional relationships, attack spots, vulnerabilities, and transmission systems are all taken into account in this article in order to develop a comprehensive threat model. The electric vehicle's internal components are linked to external resources including charging stations, traffic infrastructure, and cloud services through the use of wired and wireless protocols in the vehicle's communication subsystem. Inadequate cellular, over-the-air, Ethernet, Bluetooth, or CAN interfaces can lead to data leakage, message modification, or unauthorized access. Because of all the devices and apps it interfaces to, the infotainment system is susceptible to remote software vulnerabilities, lateral propagation into critical safety areas, and privacy intrusions. Tampering with the BMS, which controls thermal behavior, SOC, and cell-level performance, might harm the battery or change charge/discharge policies. This paper finds common flaws using threat analysis, attack surface mapping, and adversarial simulation. Weak intrusion detection, outdated protocols, vulnerable third-party libraries, insecure authentication, and lack of encryption are concerns.

title: Hierarchical MPC with Improved Inter-Layer Coordination for Autonomous Vehicle Path Tracking
authors: Dan Li, Yongfeng Shen, Yansai Wei, Huawei Wan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Conventional single-layer model predictive control (MPC) for autonomous vehicle path tracking often suffers from high optimization dimensionality, strong constraint coupling, and oscillatory control inputs. To address these issues, this paper proposes a hierarchical MPC path-tracking strategy with an improved inter-layer coordination mechanism. A path-tracking error model is first established in the Frenet coordinate system, and a two-layer MPC framework operating on different time scales is constructed. The upper layer generates a reference steering angle using a kinematic error model with integral compensation and curvature feedforward, while the lower layer employs a vehicle dynamic model to track the reference command and introduces a steering tracking term to enhance inter-layer consistency. Simulation results under circular and double lane change (DLC) conditions at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> show that the proposed method outperforms single-layer MPC. The steady-state lateral error is reduced to 0.04 m and 0.12 m, and the peak steering rate is significantly reduced, demonstrating improved tracking accuracy and control smoothness.

title: Clock Tree Power Optimization Method Based on Optimal Pre-merging Greedy Search
authors: Xinnan Xu, Xubin Ping, Kang Li, Zhenglei Huang, Hongliang Zhan, Xueyuan Ma, Ran Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: As semiconductor technology pushes ever deeper into the nanometer regime, worldwide demand for computing performance is surging dramatically. The trend has driven ever-higher integration densities within chips. As a result, the power consumption of the chip keeps growing when it is in active mode. Among the various sources of power consumption, clock tree power represents a significant share, making its design a vital element in the physical design flow. This paper addresses two issues in the clock tree synthesis stage of traditional physical design flow, including the excessive redundancy of clock gating cells, and the insufficient merging of clock gating that share the same input clock. To resolve these issues, this paper presents a clock gating merging method based on a greedy strategy. The goal of this method is to reduce power consumption by optimizing the clock tree structure. Experimental results show that the proposed method reduce the number of clock gating cells and shorten the clock tree wire length, which simplify the clock tree structure. In addition, this method lowers switching power by 12.17%, internal power by 0.53%, and leakage power by 1.34% without affecting timing constraints. Compared with the traditional physical design flow and the optimization algorithms embedded in tool, the proposed method achieves better power optimization performance.

title: Fault-Tolerant Design of a Microwave and Millimeter-Wave Satellite Communication System
authors: Baojun Zhao, Kaitao Zhang, Xinyi Chen, Guanghui Miao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Satellite communication is essential for providing connectivity in areas beyond terrestrial network coverage. Due to the long on-orbit lifetime, limited maintainability, and constrained backup resources of spacecraft, fault tolerance is a critical requirement for onboard communication systems. This paper investigates the fault-tolerance design of a microwave and millimeter-wave onboard communication system for a geostationary spacecraft. The design objective is to maintain service continuity under single-fault conditions and ensure spacecraft safety under double-fault conditions. Mission analysis, fault tree analysis (FTA), failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA), and safety-threshold analysis are conducted. Based on these analyses, a fault-tolerance framework is established, including redundancy allocation, system trade-off design, safe-mode operation, telemetry and command support, and radiation and single-event effects (SEE) protection. The results indicate that the proposed design satisfies the fault-tolerance requirement of service continuity under a single fault and spacecraft safety under double-fault conditions.

title: Towards Universal Shared Control in Teleoperation Without Haptic Feedback*
authors: Max Grobbel, Tristan Schneider, Sören Hohmann
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Teleoperation with non-haptic VR controllers deprives human operators of critical motion feedback. We address this by embedding a multi-objective optimization problem that converts user input into collision-free UR5e joint trajectories while actively suppressing liquid sloshing in a glass. The controller maintains 13 ms average planning latency, confirming real-time performance and motivating the augmentation of this teleoperation approach to further objectives.

title: A Hierarchical Multi-Domain Attention Network for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition
authors: Manzhen Sun, Qi You
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: EEG-based emotion recognition faces significant challenges due to the non-stationary nature of EEG signals, inter-subject variability, and the distributed representation of emotional information across spatial, spectral, and temporal domains. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Multi-Domain Attention Network (HMDA-Net). The framework constructs spatial–frequency representations using differential entropy features and employs hierarchical multi-scale convolution to enhance spatial–spectral modeling. A parallel spatial–channel attention mechanism is introduced to refine discriminative features, while a Transformer-based temporal module captures global dynamic dependencies. Extensive experiments conducted on the DEAP and SEED datasets demonstrate that HMDA-Net achieves 97.61% and 97.12% accuracy for Valence and Arousal classification on DEAP, and 96.17% accuracy on SEED, outperforming representative baseline models. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of the multi-scale and multi-domain attention design. The proposed method provides an effective solution for robust EEG-based emotion recognition.

title: Closing the Loop: AI-Based Learning Diagnosis and Adaptive Teaching Intervention
authors: Xumei Wang, Meiyi Chen, Jing Zhao, Caiwei Tan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the deepening digital transformation of education, precise and personalized teaching intervention has become central to improving teaching quality. Taking the course Digital Image Processing as the practical context, this study selected three classes of the Artificial Intelligence major from the 2023 cohort to conduct a one-year comparative research, and constructed a teaching closed-loop mechanism of "multi-source data collection - AI-based learning status analysis - differentiated teaching intervention", namely "stratification - diagnosis - intervention". Multi-dimensional learning behavior data were collected through the Chaoxing Learning Platform, classroom interaction tools and other channels. AI methods such as cluster analysis and time series modeling were applied to construct group and individual learning profiles of the classes. Based on these profiles, intervention measures such as stratified resource recommendation and dynamic adjustment of teaching strategies were implemented. Verified through two rounds of teaching practice, this mechanism has significantly improved students' learning effectiveness and classroom participation. It provides an operable and replicable practical path for AI-empwered instructional decision-making, promotes the implementation of the student-centered teaching philosophy, and also offers a data-driven intelligent solution for the teaching reform of emerging engineering courses.

title: Bridging the PBL Gap: A Cloud–Edge–Device Conversational AI Platform for STEM Inquiry and Critical Thinking Development
authors: He Huang, Qianyi Wu, Zehui Zhan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Project-based learning is vital for developing students’ core disciplinary and interdisciplinary skills. Traditional PBL models face challenges such as limited resources, delayed formative assessment, and lack of personalized support. To address these, we developed a Conversational AI-Enabled STEM Platform based on a Cloud–Edge–Device architecture. The cloud layer enables regional resource sharing; the edge layer provides low-latency LLM services and real-time AI inference, ensuring privacy; and the device layer integrates tablets and IoT sensors for immersive labs. Core features include AIGC-based content creation, intelligent micro-lectures, multi-source AI assessment, and an incentive scoring system. An interdisciplinary pilot found that students’ technological self-efficacy improved, especially regarding AI’s usefulness. Epistemic Network Analysis was applied to analyze academic achievement data and student-AI dialogue data. The results revealed that both high- and low-achieving students exhibited insufficient openness in their critical thinking dispositions, mainly due to knowledge gaps and an outcome-driven evaluation system. Compared to low achievers, High achievers were better at applying critical thinking in practice. The findings call for an iterative, concept-driven pedagogy, which using sub-problem scenarios, student-AI interaction modeling, and structured questioning, to foster critical thinking, especially in AI-supported learning environments, which offers key insights for improving the STEM platform, especially in supporting open inquiry, adaptive feedback, and guided reasoning.

title: AI-Driven Accounting: The Contribution of IEEE 7007 and IEEE 7010
authors: Nadia Jouini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Since the advent of artificial intelligence (AI), financial accounting has undergone a profound transformation. International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), primarily centered on economic performance, are increasingly insufficient to address the ethical, responsible, and sustainability challenges of the digital economy. Existing research highlights rapid growth in AI applications in accounting, but also reveals fragmentation and a strong emphasis on efficiency and fraud detection, often at the expense of governance and sustainability. Although recent normative efforts call for integrating AI-generated data, a comprehensive conceptual accounting framework is still lacking.We argue that financial accounting must move beyond bookkeeping and reporting to become a digital governance system.This paper introduces AI-Driven Accounting as a governance oriented framework inspired by emerging ethical and well-being standards. It is structured around three levels: assisted, where AI automates routine tasks while ensuring data privacy; augmented, where AI supports decision-making under ethical and sustainability constraints; and autonomous, where AI contributes to governance through compliance management and responsibility allocation.The framework has implications across four dimensions: normative innovation, institutional experimentation, professional training, and international collaboration. Key challenges include measuring well-being, ensuring effective oversight of algorithmic outputs, and preserving equity.

title: The Ethical Architecture of Assessment: Negotiating Teacher Value-Judgments in Generative AI Contexts
authors: Grace T. Flores, Kheven D. Guyo
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid integration of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) into higher education has disrupted traditional assessment practices, raising concerns regarding authorship, originality, fairness, and academic integrity. While existing discourse largely centers on detection technologies and regulation, limited research examines how teachers construct and negotiate ethical judgment in AI-mediated assessment contexts. Addressing this gap, this study develops a grounded theoretical framework, the Ethical Architecture of Assessment, based on semi-structured interviews with thirteen higher education educators across disciplines. Through inductive coding and reflexive thematic analysis, five interconnected processes emerged: (1) suspicion as ethical vigilance, (2) guidance as ethical mediation, (3) adaptation versus resistance as ethical negotiation, (4) fairness and integrity as anchoring principles, and (5) shifting assessment modalities as ethical innovation. Together, these processes form a relational system through which teachers balance authenticity, integrity, and pedagogical responsibility. Interpreted through procedural, interactional, and educational fairness dimensions, the findings demonstrate that professional judgment in AI-integrated classrooms is ethically structured rather than technologically reactive. Although grounded in qualitative depth within a single institutional context, broader empirical validation across disciplines and institutions is needed. This study contributes an original, teacher-centered model reframing AI integration as an ongoing ethical negotiation embedded in professional practice.

title: Enhancing Banking Security through Predictive Machine Learning Models for Payment Fraud Detection
authors: R Satheesh Kumar, C S Aksha, Nikitha P, Sanjeev V S, V Nandana Krishna
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: With digital banking and online payment systems becoming an integral part of our daily activities, fraud has been on the increase along with these systems, causing serious threats to banks as well as users. In an effort to identify fraud activities through rule-based systems used in detecting fraud, fraudsters are always on the move to update their strategies to mimic legitimate user behavior. As such, these systems are becoming slow and inefficient in detecting such activities. This work is aimed at providing an efficient fraud detection mechanism in bank payment systems using machine learning techniques. The work is able to take in transaction behavior and identify unusual patterns that may be deemed to be fraudulent with minimal impact on legitimate transactions. Special emphasis is placed on addressing the problem of dealing with imbalanced datasets to ensure that rare fraud activities are being detected. With its ability to operate in real time and learn continuously, this model is able to adapt to new fraudulent strategies being implemented by fraudsters. This paper is an attempt to drive an intelligent and efficient approach to ensuring security in digital payment systems.

title: Emotion Detection and Classroom Monitoring System
authors: Yuvaraj D. R, Vishal E, Sriram K P
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The Emotion Detection and Classroom Monitoring System which this paper presents uses facial emotion recognition technology to assess student engagement during real-time classroom evaluations. The system captures classroom video and uses deep learning methods to identify four different emotional states which include attentiveness and happiness and confusion and distraction. The system combines personal emotional information to create a measure of total classroom engagement. The system generates automatic alerts to notify instructors about negative emotional patterns which need immediate educational intervention. The proposed framework operates at any scale while maintaining a non-intrusive design which functions effectively in smart classroom settings. The system demonstrates its capability to monitor classroom behavior and deliver data-backed insights which enhance teaching methods and boost student engagement according to experimental findings.

title: Collaborative Problem Based Learning Model in Applied Optics Course
authors: Yankun Zhen, Aping Yang, Yan Bai, Wei Fan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the related course system of optoelectronics, the Applied Optics Course (AOC) focuses on improving learners' performance in practical optical design. Based on the cognitive load theory, many conventional instructional models are less effective. As one of the instructional strategies, Problem Based Learning (PBL) model is often used to help learners enhance interactions and promote higher thinking, thus the PBL model integrated with collaboration skills was applied in the learning activity of the course. The results showed that Collaborative Problem Based Learning (CPBL) model in Applied Optics course is more effective in triggering the learning motivation, improving the efficiency of learning efficiency, strengthening students' design practical operation ability, and fostering team communication and collaboration skills.

title: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: GArBiL-KE: A New Fair, Robust and Efficient Dynamic Hybrid Ensemble Model for Arabic Social Sentiment Analysis
authors: Mohamed Khaldi, Abir Smiti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The increasing demand for accurate, robust, fair, and interpretable sentiment analysis systems for the Arabic language presents significant challenges owing to its linguistic richness, morphological complexity, dialectal diversity, and the scarcity of large-scale annotated corpora. In this study, we propose a novel Dynamic Hybrid Ensemble framework, referred to as GArBiL-KE, designed to address these limitations through the integration of dynamic model selection, sequential representation learning, and deep contextual embeddings specifically tailored for Arabic sentiment classification.The proposed architecture dynamically combines AraBERT base v02 (aubmindlab), a transformer-based language model pretrained on large Arabic corpora, with a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network augmented by a multi-head attention mechanism, and a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) model. These three complementary components are orchestrated using the K-Nearest Oracle Eliminate (KNORAE) strategy, a Dynamic Ensemble Selection (DES) method that adaptively selects the most competent classifiers on a per-instance basis at inference time.The framework was rigorously evaluated on eight benchmark Arabic sentiment analysis datasets spanning multiple domains, linguistic registers, dialectal varieties, and data sources. Experimental results demonstrate outstanding performance, achieving up to 99.11% accuracy and F1-score on the HARD dataset, while consistently outperforming each individual base learner by margins ranging from 3% to 13%. Beyond raw predictive performance, the proposed approach is validated across three additional dimensions: fairness (demographic parity), robustness under adversarial text perturbations, and computational efficiency. The proposed GArBiL-KE framework contributes to advancing Arabic Natural Language Understanding by introducing a scalable, dynamically adaptive, and equitable ensemble architecture suitable for linguistically complex and low-resource environments.

title: GenAI in Pre-Task Planning: A Comparative Study of Oral Fluency, Accuracy, and Complexity in Business English Speaking
authors: Xiuzhen Xiang, Yi Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The importance of pre-task planning(PTP) on students' output has been acknowledged, so is the positive effect of AI in language learning. However seldom scholars study the different effects between AI and conventional strategy in PTP. To build the gap this study investigates the impacts of AI-assisted and conventional PTP on business English speaking. The research involved 50 sophomore business English major students at a university in China. The experimental group use AI-assisted material while the control class followed traditional textbook-based method during PTP. The findings reveal significant better performance in the experimental group's speaking fluency and accuracy than the controlled group. But there is no difference in complexity.

title: Semantic Communication for The Internet of Vehicles: Concepts, Enabling Technologies, and Open Research Directions
authors: Chayma Ayari, Haythem Hayouni, Leila Nasraoui
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Semantic communication is emerging as an effective approach to address the increasing information demands of the Internet of Vehicles (IoV). In dynamic vehicular environments, exchanged information must be timely, task-relevant, and capable of supporting perception, prediction, and decision-making. Unlike traditional systems that transmit raw sensor data without considering its operational value, semantic communication focuses on conveying only the meaningful elements necessary for driving tasks. This survey explores the integration of semantic principles into the IoV and identifies key enabling technologies, including knowledge-based reasoning, learning-driven semantic encoders, context-aware models, and multimodal fusion techniques. Applications such as cooperative perception, traffic management, hazard detection, and maneuver coordination demonstrate the potential to reduce network load while enhancing relevance, consistency, and reliability. Key challenges, such as heterogeneous sensing, environmental uncertainty, and the lack of unified semantic representations across vehicles, are also discussed. Furthermore, the survey highlights future research directions such as uncertainty-aware semantic models, improved cross-vehicle semantic alignment, and shared vehicular ontologies.

title: Research on an AI-Empowered Interdisciplinary Practical Curriculum Model for Developing Engineering Thinking in Junior High School Students
authors: Yunyun Liao, Liping Jiang, Genwei Yi, Juan Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Engineering thinking, as a key competency for solving complex real-world problems, is a vital dimension in cultivating innovative talent. However, current interdisciplinary engineering practice courses often remain trapped in a "science-centric" mindset, hindering the systematic development of students' interdisciplinary literacy and engineering thinking. To address this, this study employs action research methodology. Through three progressively deepening rounds of classroom practice, it completes two full course iterations. The introduction of AI agents enables continuous diagnosis of student engineering performance and provides strategic support. Findings indicate that constructing scenarios around real-world problem-solving, implementing activities through structured design scaffolding, and leveraging AI for data-driven evaluation and feedback loops significantly advance students' engineering thinking—shifting from knowledge application toward systems design, trade-off decision-making, and continuous optimization. Building upon this, the study establishes a trinity-based course implementation model integrating "scenario-scaffolding-evaluation," providing an actionable framework and empirical reference for K–12 science education to deliver interdisciplinary practices focused on engineering thinking.

title: Emerging Generative AI and Evolving Educator Roles: From TPACK to AI-TPACK in Music Education
authors: Yinshu Yan, Yuanyang Yue, Yunqi Jing, Tiantian Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is reshaping the paradigms of educational technology application and driving the evolution of the Technological Pedagogical Content Knowledge (TPACK) framework in the smart era. This study traces the development and expansion of the TPACK framework through a literature review, outlining its complete evolutionary path from initial theoretical construction and measurement tool development to adaptation across disciplines and subsequent extension toward AI. It specifically examines the application and advancement of TPACK in music education. The research reveals a clear trend of integrating generative AI into current music education: shifting from the use of general technological tools to AI-driven instructional innovation, and expanding from a single knowledge dimension to a comprehensive application system encompassing AI literacy, human-machine collaboration, and ethical knowledge. However, the application of TPACK in music education still lags behind its use in other fields, lacking standards for AI-related competencies, supplementary empirical research in the AI era, and validation of cross cultural adaptability. This study proposes a GenAI-M-TPACK competency framework to assist music teachers' professional development in the AI era.

title: Project IMahenalyze : A Structured Intervention to Decode Figurative Meaning in Texts
authors: Jenelin S. Enero, Sunday Joy Sophia P. Duaban, Januer Shane Mantua
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Grade 7 learners often struggle with reading comprehension, particularly in decoding figurative language, implied meaning, symbolism, and imagery. These challenges limit their ability to engage with literary texts and develop higher-order thinking skills, which are crucial at the start of Junior High School under the DepEd MATATAG Curriculum. To address these gaps, the researchers designed Project IMahenalyze, an Electronic Strategic Intervention Material (E-SIM) that integrates multimedia lessons, guided practice, gamified activities, and independent learning tasks. The E-SIM was further enhanced by incorporating the VARK model (Visual, Auditory, Reading & Writing, and Kinesthetic), to provide multimodal learning experiences. Anchored on Constructivist Learning Theory, Cognitive Theory of Multimedia Learning, and Vygotsky’s Zone of Proximal Development, the intervention offered structured scaffolding and active engagement. The study involved Grade 7 students from Alviola Integrated High School–Main Campus in Butuan City, purposively selected based on diagnostic assessments showing difficulties in figurative language comprehension. Unlike traditional interventions, the E-SIM uniquely integrated VARK strategies into digital modules, making learning more engaging and accessible in a resource-constrained public-school setting. Learners showed clear progress after the intervention, moving from the lowest performance level to a more proficient stage. They became more confident in interpreting figurative language and more engaged in reading tasks. This demonstrates that multimodal, technology-enhanced interventions can effectively strengthen comprehension and interpretation skills in Filipino classes. The intervention was implemented over only one week and limited to a single class, restricting generalizability and long-term impact assessment. Further studies should extend implementation time, include larger samples, and explore deeper integration of VARK strategies to maximize learner engagement and mastery of figurative language.

title: Refined Identification of miRNA-Disease Associations Based on Knowledge-awareness Propagation
authors: Yuliang Cai, Zhen Tang, Wei Qiao, Guiyuan Jiang, Fei Gao, Qiang He, Jiawei Zhang, Wei Qian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: MicroRNAs (miRNAs) are small non-coding RNAs orchestrating regulatory networks through sequence-specific target recognition. Understanding miRNA-disease correlations is crucial as high-throughput sequencing data growth outpaces experimental validation, necessitating computational approaches for association discovery. Existing frameworks model miRNA-disease interactions as uniform binary relationships, overlooking semantic diversity in different association mechanisms. We propose BKAMDA (<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>iRNA-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>isease <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>ssociations prediction <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</u>ased on <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>nowledge-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>wareness), a novel knowledge-aware model for predicting miRNA-disease associations.
