It comprises three stages: a selective inheritance stage that retains priors from previous timesteps to preserve the temporal continuity, a dynamics-aware shift stage that distinguishes dynamic and static primitives and employs distinct strategies to optimize their movements, and an error-guided densification stage that efficiently identifies Gaussians requiring densification to accommodate emerging objects. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in online 4D reconstruction, demonstrating compact storage, the fastest on-the-fly training, and superior representation quality.

title: Enhancing Line Density Plots with Outlier Control and Bin-based Illumination
authors: Yumeng Xue, Bin Chen, Patrick Paetzold, Yunhai Wang, Christophe Hurter, Oliver Deussen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Density plots effectively summarize large numbers of points, which would otherwise lead to severe overplotting in, for example, a scatter plot. However, when applied to line-based datasets, such as trajectories or time series, density plots alone are insufficient, as they disrupt path continuity, obscuring smooth trends and rare anomalies. We propose a bin-based illumination model that decouples structure from density to enhance flow and reveal sparse outliers while preserving the original colormap. We introduce a bin-based outlierness metric to rank trajectories. Guided by this ranking, we construct a structural normal map and apply locally-adaptive lighting in the luminance channel to highlight chosen patterns-from dominant trends to atypical paths-with acceptable color distortion. Our interactive method enables analysts to prioritize main trends, focus on outliers, or strike a balance between the two. We demonstrate our method on several real-world datasets, showing it reveals details missed by simpler alternatives, achieves significantly lower CIEDE2000 color distortion than standard shading, and supports interactive updates for up to 10,000 lines.

title: Deep-Learning-Based Control of a Decoupled Two-Segment Continuum Robot for Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection
authors: Yuancheng Shao, Yao Zhang, Jia Gu, Zixi Chen, Di Wu, Yuqiao Chen, Bo Lu, Wenjie Liu, Cesare Stefanini, Peng Qi
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Manual endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) is technically demanding, and existing single-segment robotic tools offer limited dexterity. These limitations motivate the development of more advanced solutions. To address this, decoupled endoscopic submucosal dissection robot (DESectBot), a novel dual-segment continuum robot with a decoupled structure and integrated surgical forceps, enabling six degrees of freedom tip dexterity for improved lesion targeting in ESD, was developed in this work. Deep learning controllers based on gated recurrent units (GRUs) for simultaneous tip position and orientation control, effectively handling the nonlinear coupling between continuum segments, were proposed. The GRU controller was benchmarked against Jacobian-based inverse kinematics, model predictive control (MPC), a feedforward neural network, and a long short-term memory (LSTM) network. In addition, a systematic ablation study under identical training conditions showed that, for the considered control task, the GRU achieves better generalization, faster convergence, and higher computational efficiency than the LSTM. In nested-rectangle and Lissajous trajectory tracking tasks, the GRU achieved the lowest position/orientation root-mean-square errors (RMSEs): 1.11 mm/ 4.62<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 0.81 mm/ 2.59<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>, respectively. For orientation control at a fixed position (four target poses), the GRU attained a mean RMSE of 0.14 mm and 0.72<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>, outperforming all alternatives. In a peg transfer task, the GRU achieved a 100% success rate (120 success/120 attempts) with an average transfer time of 11.8 s, the standard deviation significantly outperforms no vice-controlled systems. In addition, an ex vivo ESD demonstration—grasping, elevating, and resecting tissue as the scalpel completed the cut—confirmed that DESectBot provides sufficient stiffness to divide thick gastric mucosa and an operative workspace adequate for large lesions. These results confirm that GRU-based control significantly enhances precision, reliability, and usability in ESD surgical training scenarios.

title: Long&short Exposures Guided Diffusion Model for Realistic Local Motion Deblurring
authors: Zhongbao Yang, Lingshun Kong, Jinshan Pan, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Removing local blur caused by moving objects is challenging, especially in low signal-to-noise ratio environments, as the moving objects are usually significantly blurry while the static background remains relatively clear. Existing methods relying on local blur detection often suffer from inaccuracies and cannot generate satisfactory results when focusing solely on blurred regions. In this paper, we present an effective diffusion model guided with long-exposure and short-exposure images for realistic local motion deblurring. Specifically, we first propose a context-based local blur detection module. Different from existing methods that rely on pixel-wise classification, we leverage contextual information to generate semantically coherent blur region identification, which preserves the integrity of blur regions. Then, we present a blurry-aware guided image restoration method to remove local blur by discriminately handling the blurry and clear regions. Finally, a structure-guided diffusion model is developed to achieve realistic image restoration by exploring useful information from the above blurry-aware guided image restoration result and the short-exposure image as guidance. The proposed method, ExpDiff, is trained in an end-to-end manner. Our extensive experimental results show that the proposed ExpDiff performs favorably against state-of-the-art methods.

title: Bit-Compressed Concurrent Fault Simulation with Efficient Fault List
authors: Feng Gu, Zhiteng Chao, Hui Wang, Jianan Mu, Mingjun Wang, Jun Gao, Yonghao Wang, Jing Ye, Xiaowei Li, Huawei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: As the automotive industry advances toward intelligent systems, the risk of chip failures in automotive applications has grown. Functional safety has thus become essential to ensure the reliability of automotive-grade chips. Sequential circuit fault simulation is a key part of functional safety and a major bottleneck in safety verification. The concurrent fault simulation (CFS) method is the most widely used algorithm for this purpose and is adopted in the leading commercial tool Z01X. While recent academic efforts have primarily extended CFS to the RTL level simulation, the fundamental gate level CFS algorithm has seen little improvement, despite its critical role in simulation performance. Two main issues in concurrent fault simulation are inefficient storage and computation of logic values, and performance limitations in its core component, the concurrent fault list. To address these issues, we propose a dual optimization strategy: (1) a bit-compression method that reduces memory overhead and enables fully O(1) time gate evaluation, and (2) a scan list-based concurrent fault list implementation that improves efficiency with low time/space complexity and superior space locality. Experimental results show that our method achieves an average speedup of 6.10× over commercial tool Z01X, with gains exceeding 3× on large circuits, and 2.58× over academic simulator VISION, proving its strong practical value.

title: PCFNet: A Sea Surface Temperature Prediction Network Fusing Physical Constraints with Frequency Domain Feature Extraction
authors: Yingbing Liu, Cong Xiao, Guangwen Peng, Wenying Du, Changjiang Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Sea surface temperature (SST) predictions are critical for marine climate research and ecosystem management. To address the deficiencies of the existing data-driven models in regard to physical consistency constraints and multiscale feature modeling, this paper proposes a deep learning model that fuses physical constraints and frequency domain features for the SST prediction. This model employs the fast fourier transform (FFT) to process spatio-temporal features. At the same time, it incorporates a constraint integration module (CIM), which embeds the physical conservation properties of the advection-diffusion equation (ADE) into the prediction process, thereby enhancing the modeling capability for the dynamic evolution patterns of SST. The experimental results on both daily and weekly scales demonstrate that the proposed PCFNet achieves consistently higher prediction accuracy compared with existing SST forecasting models. In particular, it provides more stable short-term predictions and effectively mitigates multi-step error accumulation. These results further confirm the effectiveness of integrating frequency-domain structure modeling and cross-scale interaction mechanisms. Overall, this study offers a promising and interpretable framework for SST prediction by combining physical priors with data-driven learning.

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp1-3682403.pdf
authors: Yongchang Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

title: Multi-Snapshot Deep Denoising for Channel Estimation in OTFS Modulated Systems
authors: Surbhi Gehlot, Siddhi Shinde, Suraj Srivastava, Sandeep Kumar Yadav
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: A deep denoising based channel estimation framework is proposed for orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulated systems, wherein channel state information (CSI) recovery is formulated as an image restoration problem. A salient attribute of the approach is the exploitation of structural invariance in the delay Doppler (DD) domain channel over a geometric coherence time, allowing multiple OTFS frames captured during this period to serve as noisy snapshots of the approximately identical channel. These snapshots jointly enhance the effectiveness of the proposed lightweight denoiser based on nonlinear activation free network (NAFNet). The method exhibits low computational complexity, operates reliably even at low pilot signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), and can accommodate both fractional delay and fractional Doppler effects. Simulation results demonstrate significant performance gains over the existing methods.

title: From Telettrofono to Smartphone: Social Innovation and the Ethics of Recognition
authors: Riccardo Meucci, Guanrong Chen
publish: IEEE Technology and Society Magazine
abstract: The origins of voice communication technologies are typically narrated through a sequence of industrial and scientific milestones culminating in today’s global digital networks. Yet, early developments in telephony also reveal deeply human motivations and socially embedded forms of innovation. This article compares Antonio Meucci’s nineteenth-century “telettrofono”—a low-energy, household communication device conceived to assist his ailing wife—with today’s sophisticated smartphone-based infrastructures. By examining these two endpoints of the telecommunication continuum, the article explores how communication technologies evolved from intimate human-centered solutions to complex, energy-intensive systems. It also discusses the ethics of recognition within institutional frameworks such as the IEEE Milestones Program. The goal is not to reopen historical disputes, but to promote a socially aware and inclusive memory of technological innovation, consistent with IEEE’s mission to advance technology for humanity.

title: A Self-Supervised Uncertainty-Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Modeling Tropospheric Zenith Wet Delay
authors: Mohamed H. Sharouda, Weixing Zhang, Zhixiang Mo, Mohamed M. Elisy, Hongxing Sun, Mohamed Hosny, Yidong Lou
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Zenith wet delay (ZWD) is one of the major sources of errors in the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) positioning. Existing machine learning (ML)-based ZWD models have limitations in their generalization capabilities and in modeling the high spatiotemporal fluctuations in the atmospheric water vapor. To address these limitations, we introduce an advanced ensemble technique that integrates several base ML models through a Self-Supervised Uncertainty-Based Stacking Ensemble (SUSE). The proposed model enhances the accuracy of modeling and prediction of ZWD for China and its neighboring areas. The results demonstrate that our models, SUSE, outperform other ML, (DL), empirical (GPT3), and traditional models (Askne & Nordius (A&N) and Saastamoinen). In terms of RMSE, our blind model SUSE_A achieves internal and external improvements of 24.36% and 24.24% compared to GPT3. Additionally, SUSE_A outperforms conventional models that use meteorological data (A&N and Saastamoinen) and has comparable performance with other ML and DL models that use meteorological parameters. When surface meteorological data were available, the SUSE_B model reduced the RMSE by 34.48% and 47.89% with respect to A&N and Saastamoinen, respectively. Temporal and spatial analysis reveal the superior accuracy and stability of the SUSE model over other models examined in this research. These results indicate the promising prospects of the SUSE model for GNSS applications.

title: Cladding-Engineered Hollow-Core Fiber for Polarization Control and Optical Sensing Applications
authors: M. M. Shoaib Hasan, Md Shah Alam, Md Selim Habib, Md Asiful Islam
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: A polarization-selective hollow-core antiresonant fiber (HC-ARF) is proposed and numerically investigated for single-polarization guidance in the telecom band. Finite-element simulations across the 1.50–1.60 μm range show that the optimized design achieves a high polarization extinction ratio of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.73\times 10^{4}$</tex-math></inline-formula> at 1.55 μm, with low loss for the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$y$</tex-math></inline-formula>–polarized mode (0.72 dB/km) and strongly suppressed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$x$</tex-math></inline-formula>–polarized loss (12531 dB/km). The fiber also provides a modal birefringence of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.21\times 10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, enabling strong polarization selectivity and stable single-polarization guidance. These results indicate that the proposed PM-HC-ARF is well-suited for polarization-sensitive applications such as interferometry, sensing, and coherent communication.

title: Analysis of Delay Stability in Attenuator-Free Mach-Zehnder Interferometer Optical True Time Delay Lines
authors: Junhee Lee, Sang Beom Seon, Chang-Jun Lee, Sangyeol Oh, Seok Chan Eom, Young-Hoon Chun, Youngseok Bae, Joonyong Park, Jong-Bum You, Seungki Kim, Sun-Goo Lee, Wook-Jae Lee
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Optical true time delay lines are essential components for broadband and accurate time-delay control in microwave photonic and optical communication systems. Among silicon-photonic implementations, Mach–Zehnder interferometer switch–based delay lines offer discrete, reconfigurable delay tuning, but their delay stability is fundamentally limited by the finite extinction ratio of the switches. In this work, a simplified analytical model based on multi-path interference is developed to quantitatively describe how finite extinction ratio induces group-delay fluctuations in single and cascaded Mach–Zehnder interferometer switch optical true time delay lines. This theoretical analysis is experimentally validated using single and two-stage Mach–Zehnder interferometer switch optical true time delay lines fabricated on a silicon photonics platform. These results provide quantitative extinction ratio thresholds needed for reliable discrete true time-delay operation without variable optical attenuators on a silicon photonics platform and demonstrate the feasibility of compact, reconfigurable, low-power implementations without auxiliary attenuators.

title: Circularly Polarized Azimuthally Omnidirectional Terahertz Fan-Beam Tracking with Electronic Polarization Synthesis
authors: Ryoma Sonoyama, Masahiko Inami, Yasuaki Monnai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: Terahertz (THz) beam tracking for mobile links requires not only full-azimuth coverage but also polarization control to ensure seamless connectivity under arbitrary device rotation and motion. This work presents a gimbal-integrated THz beam-steering architecture with electronically reconfigurable polarization that enables azimuthally omnidirectional tracking with orientation-insensitive circular polarization (CP). Two coherent THz signals are generated and independently controlled in amplitude and phase difference, then combined through an orthomode transducer to excite an annular-aperture fan-beam antenna. Measurements verify CP synthesis at multiple azimuths, achieving an axial ratio below 3 dB. Closedloop azimuth steering is verified using an automated receiver stage, employing a hill-climbing algorithm. Furthermore, a handheld demonstration using a head-mounted display demonstrates effective beam tracking under rapid user motion. The results establish a practical approach to motion-tolerant THz links with electronically synthesized polarization.

title: Multi-needle Localization for Pelvic Seed Implant Brachytherapy based on Tip-handle Detection and Matching
authors: Zhuo Xiao, Fugen Zhou, Jingjing Wang, Chongyu He, Bo Liu, Haitao Sun, Zhe Ji, Yuliang Jiang, Junjie Wang, Qiuwen Wu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate multi-needle localization in intraoperative CT images is crucial for optimizing seed placement in pelvic seed implant brachytherapy. However, this task is challenging due to poor image contrast and needle adhesion. This paper presents a novel approach that reframes needle localization as a tip-handle detection and matching problem to overcome these difficulties. An anchor-free network, based on HRNet, is proposed to extract multi-scale features and accurately detect needle tips and handles by predicting their centers and orientations using decoupled branches for heatmap regression and polar angle prediction. To associate detected tips and handles into individual needles, a greedy matching and merging (GMM) method designed to solve the unbalanced assignment problem with constraints (UAP-C) is presented. The GMM method iteratively selects the most probable tip-handle pairs and merges them based on a distance metric to reconstruct 3D needle paths. Evaluated on a dataset of 100 patients, the proposed method demonstrates superior performance, achieving higher precision and F1 score compared to segmentation-based baselines utilizing the nnUNet model, thereby offering a more robust and accurate solution for needle localization in complex clinical scenarios. The source code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/BUAAXZzz/CTNeedleLocalization</uri>.

title: Long-Term, Patient-Specific Seizure Prediction using Absolute Mean Instantaneous Frequency Difference (AMIFD) and Seizure Clustering
authors: Sai Sanjay Balaji, Zisheng Zhang, Zhiyi Sha, Thomas R. Henry, Keshab K. Parhi
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Long-term seizure prediction in epileptic individuals is challenging, mainly due to signal non-stationarity and seizure type variability. This research presents a patient-specific prediction pipeline for intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) based on the novel Absolute Mean Instantaneous Frequency Difference (AMIFD) biomarker. The approach addresses seizure variability using the minimum uncertainty and sample elimination (MUSE) feature-ranking technique to automatically cluster seizure types based on their top-ranked AMIFD features, distinguishing the interictal baseline from the preictal phase (defined as a 60-min window ending 5 min before onset). For each identified seizure cluster, a specialized random forest classifier is trained independently, creating a multi-model ensemble tailored to specific seizure morphologies. The final prediction alarm is triggered using a k-of-N aggregation logic to enhance reliability and minimize false alarms. The AMIFD biomarker shows a significantly larger effect size than several standard electrophysiological features (adjusted p < 0.04). When tested on 10 epileptic subjects, the patient-specific framework achieved a mean sensitivity of 92.08% and a false-positive rate of 1.32/day, outperforming the minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) and MUSE baselines. This patient-specific, multi-model strategy establishes a viable pathway toward more accurate and clinically reliable personalized seizure prediction systems.

title: PLOSQ3D: Privacy-Preserving Location-Based Skyline Query over Encrypted 3D Spatial Data
authors: Zhuliang Jia, Suprio Ray, Rongxing Lu, Pulei Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Privacy-preserving location-based skyline queries (LBSQ) enable location-based services (LBS) over outsourced spatial data while preserving data privacy. Prior studies primar ily consider Euclidean distance as the spatial-attribute metric. However, this does not capture reachability in three-dimensional (3D) obstacle environments, where obstacles invalidate direct Euclidean distances and necessitate obstacle-aware path computations. In this work, we present PLOSQ3D, a privacy-preserving location-based skyline query framework in 3D obstacle space. We first formalize the problem, specify its privacy requirements, and identify the challenges introduced by obstacle-aware distances. Subsequently, we introduce a novel index, Secure Augmented R-Tree (SAR-Tree) that jointly encodes spatial and non-spatial at tributes, enabling effective pruning under 3D spatial constraints. To enforce privacy, we leverage Function Secret Sharing (FSS) to design three protocols: SLDC for secure dominance computation, SSPC for secure shortest-path computation, and OLDU for oblivious skyline-set update. Building on them, we carefully design the PLOSQ3D scheme. Our security analysis demonstrates strong privacy guarantees against honest-but-curious adversaries. We further conduct extensive experiments on both synthetic and real 3D datasets. Experimental results show that our framework achieves high efficiency in both computation and communication overhead, confirming its practicality for 3D spatial applications that demand spatial-awareness and privacy protection.

title: ESNB-I: A Lightweight Inception U-Net for Efficient Runway Occupancy Detection
authors: Qing Cheng, Yiting Wang, Junhao Liu, Yebo Zhou, Xi Zhao
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Physical detection of airport runway occupancy is a prerequisite for ensuring aviation operational safety. Current ground surveillance systems face three core limitations: delayed early-warning responses, insufficient recognition robustness in complex environments, and physical occlusions from ground-level perspectives. The core challenge lies in the synergistic optimization of high-precision recognition and lightweight deployment, alongside utilizing orthographic perspectives to compensate for ground surveillance blind spots. Therefore, this paper proposes ESNB-I, a lightweight detection framework oriented towards top-down perspectives. Using Inception U-Net as the baseline model, this method integrates multi-objective optimization, feature enhancement, and risk perception to form an intelligent runway occupancy recognition mechanism: reconstructing the ESNB block-level pruning strategy to eliminate computational redundancy while preserving the multi-scale perceptual topology, thereby meeting the low-latency early-warning requirements of the backend analysis nodes for surface surveillance. integrating Inception multi-scale perceptual modules and the CBAM attention mechanism into the U-Net architecture to enhance feature robustness in complex surface scenarios; and designing a dual-constraint composite loss function that quantifies aircraft false positive and missed detection risks as penalty terms embedded within the training process. Experiments demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, the ESNB-I model reduces parameters by 5.9% and inference latency by 69.9%, achieving an F1-Score of 0.991 and an IoU of 0.982. Addressing the technical challenges of complex surface texture interference, insufficient real-time inference efficiency within airport surveillance intelligent analysis pipelines, and the missed detection of tiny objects in top-down imagery, this work provides an efficient solution for intelligent runway monitoring systems.

title: A Novel High-Power, Wide-range PT Symmetric Wireless Power Transfer Systems with Reconfiguration-Free Arbitrary Current Ratio Adjustment
authors: Guang-Sen Guo, Zhi-Juan Liao, Zhao-Xi Lang, Yi-Jie Wang, Pan Sun, Chen-Yang Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Parity-Time (PT) symmetric mechanism has attracted extensive interest for their excellent positional robustness, but are strictly limited by the fixed current amplitude ratio determined by the inductance ratio, which greatly restricts power transfer performance. To overcome this issue, a novel PT symmetric mechanism based on a specially designed S-0 topology is proposed. Theoretical derivations of the mechanism and parameter criteria are verified experimentally. The results show that: 1) α is no longer limited by the inductance ratio L1/L2 and can be arbitrarily adjusted without hardware changes; 2) kc is independent of RL and positively correlated with α. Reducing α increases both output power and transfer distance. Moreover, kc can be used as a design parameter to determine α, ensuring high positional robustness under all conditions; 3) When conventional PT-symmetric operation fails—large primary inductance and small secondary inductance—the proposed mechanism ensures stable operation while better balancing power, efficiency, and distance.

title: A Convolutional and Bidirectional GRU Framework for Intrusion Detection to protect IoMT Environments
authors: Nivedhidha M, Ramkumar M P, Emil Selvan G S R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: IoMT has transformed the healthcare sector by allowing high-quality monitoring and care of patients due to the interconnection of devices; however, it also opens the healthcare sector to high risks of cyberattacks. One of the most significant threats is ARP spoofing, which helps to instigate man-in-the-middle attack, modify the data, and seize devices in a critical hospital network, which may negatively affect the lives of patients. A novel hybrid DL model CBG-IDS which combines PCA, CNN-BiGRU to detect ARP spoofing in real-time and specifically to the IoMT ecosystem is developed. PCA reduces dimensionality of features, thus reducing redundancy and enhancing computational efficiency. CNN component identifies spatial irregularities in the structure of packets. An advanced dual-task learning system integrates binary classification using the focal loss which gives more attention to difficult cases, and a reconstruction element that uses the mean squared error promoting robustness, interpretability and visualization of anomalies. The proposed model CBG-IDS produced high accuracy compared to traditional approaches. This reduces instances of false positives, ensures high reliability and assists easy real-time integration into healthcare systems to counter the emerging cyber threats and maintain patient safety and data integrity.

title:
Intelligent Modelling of Maritime Fuel Consumption and CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Emissions Using Integrated Attention-based LSTM -FNN Neural Networks
authors: Shruthi Merine S., Shakthinee S., Kumari Deepika P
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Predictive CO2 emission and Fuel consumption of maritime transport can only be reliable in alleviating the efficiency and environmental sustainability of maritime transport. Some of the data it consists of are vessel types such as fishing trawlers, oil service boats, surfing boats, and tanker ships, and operational data such as navigation route, engine performance data, weather conditions, and fuel consumption history. The LSTM models in the suggested model identify time variations of sequential data of maritime operations. The LSTM design has an attention mechanism that gives priority to the most critical aspects in its operations, e.g., route conditions, weather, and engine dynamics, etc., in terms of fuel consumption. The model aims to learn spatial and operational differences between the navigation routes in order to gain route awareness and refine the prediction of fuel consumption and CO2 emissions. Experimental research indicates that the attention-based LSTM model forecasts better than regular LSTM and machine learning predictors. The framework approximates the cost of business operations based on calculated fuel consumption, and this forms a smart resource for managing maritime energy. The recommended solution will be added to the existing AI-based maritime analytics to enhance fuel management, emissions evaluation, and sustainable maritime transportation planning.

title: Block Chain-Enabled Trust Management in Social Networks Optimization
authors: Harish Chamarthi, Mohanraju Muppala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Centralized trust models that are used in the social networks of the online world (OSNs) are prone to manipulation, lack of transparency, and are susceptible to single point of failure. This paper proposes a new decentralized trust system leveraging the block chain-based technology to ensure transparency, immutability, and user sovereignty. The suggested system will have a multi-faceted trust-quantification algorithm that will evaluate endorsements, content reliability, and transactional behavior, which will be stored on a permissioned block chain. In order to avoid the problem of bottlenecks in performance, edge based query-fragment caching system is added to it, which significantly decreases the latency of verification. Experimental tests done on real-world examples show that the framework also shortens query latencies by about 40 percent compared to traditional on-chain systems and is more resistant to Sybil and collusion attacks by more than 35 percent than centralized systems, thereby creating a solid base of trusted social computing.

title: HTTPS Man-in-the-Middle Attack Detection Based on Contrastive Learning and Multimodal Feature Fusion
authors: Kun Liu, Wei Lin, Di Jin, Ding Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In the contemporary digital era, the cybersecurity threat landscape has become increasingly critical, with diverse attack vectors emerging continuously. Man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks against The Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) protocols pose severe threats to both the confidentiality and integrity of data transmission. To address the challenges prevalent in HTTPS MITM attack detection—specifically, the difficulty in characterizing encrypted traffic, the proliferation of attack variants, and the scarcity of labeled training data—this paper proposes CLET-MITM, a novel detection model integrating contrastive learning with multimodal feature fusion. The model employs a Transformer-based architecture that incorporates multimodal representations of encrypted traffic, encompassing raw bytes, packet length sequences, and 32-dimensional composite temporal features. Through dual-task pre-training combining masked sequence prediction and enhanced contrastive learning, the model learns traffic representations with superior generalization capability. During the fine-tuning stage, an encoder freezing strategy is adopted to enable few-shot adaptation. Experimental results demonstrate that CLET-MITM achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple test datasets, with overall detection accuracy exceeding 98%, significantly outperforming existing baseline methods.

title: EchoGuide: A Low-Cost, Wearable Dual-Ultrasonic Travel Aid for Enhanced Mobility and Safety for Individuals with Visual Impairments
authors: Himanshu Singh, Vipin Jain, Paras Tripathi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Personal freedom and quality of life is based on the ability to move independently, and it is still a serious issue of millions of people with visual disabilities across the globe. Conventional tools such as the white cane, although necessary, possess certain inherent drawbacks such as inability to detect any obstacles that may be placed above the ground and unpredictable weather conditions. The present paper presents EchoGuide, which is a new Electronic Travel Aid (ETA) that will fill these gaps. It is a low priced, non-obtrusive, wearable gadget which is designed to be placed on the chest as an ID card. It combines a microcontroller ESP32 and a pair of HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensors. The two sensors offer forward-facing obstacle detection and downward-angled, respectively, offer ground-level hazard detection, like curbs, steps, and potholes. This information is processed by the system in real-time to provide clear and concise voice notifications, which the user receives through common earbuds, and informs them about the distance to an identified obstacle. The design also focuses on user-centric principles such as a low cognitive load created by the use of simple audio cues, social acceptability with a minimal form factor, and accessibility to the device through the incorporation of low-cost off-the-shelf components. The paper describes the entire system architecture, the hardware implementation, the algorithms used in software including a sequential triggering mechanism to control sensor interference and a proposed user-friendly evaluation protocol. Offering a holistic approach to environmental awareness, in a practical, convenient bundle, EchoGuide offers a bright future to the safety and confidence levels as well as the level of independence of visually impaired pedestrians.

title: Stroke Vision: A Deep Learning Approach for Stroke Identification
authors: Hairunesa Beevi, Meenakshi K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Stroke is a serious cerebrovascular disease that can be life-compromising. In India alone, major stroke-related deaths occur annually worldwide. Although stroke can be fatal, proper diagnosis and treatment can either cure the condition or significantly reduce its impact, leading to improved clinical findings. The core focus of this study is to boost stroke finding and prevention, enhancing treatment efficiency and reducing the likelihood of recurrent strokes in the same individual. To achieve this, we are developing a stroke detection system using brain CT images, blending Two-way LSTM and LSTM-based convolutional network to identify strokes at the infancy stage. For image categorisation, a neural network-based genetic approach is adopted to opt the most relevant attributes. These attributes are then fed into the BiLSTM and ConvLSTM models for stroke foretelling. The system’s efficiency is evaluated using Overall correctness, Exactness, True positive rate, Balanced F-measure, and discrimination capacity metrics through cross-validation. The proposed diagnostic system achieved an accuracy of 97%. Furthermore, the model is compared with traditional machine learning algorithms such as Binary classification, regression, Decision tree classifiers, Randomized tree ensemble, probabilistic classifiers, and Maximum margin classifier.

title: A Hand-Eye Calibration Method Based on SCARA Manipulator
authors: Yanhong Fu, Weiting Ma, Changlu Zhang, Wenbo Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Based on the self-developed SCARA robotic arm's mechanical structure and corresponding self-developed controller algorithm, in order to achieve the automatic grasping of industrial products by the robotic arm, this paper proposes a hand eye calibration method that can accurately locate the product and grasp it to the designated position to complete production. This method involves multiple movements of a robotic arm equipped with a vision system to take photos of a calibration board, record pose and image data, create a calibration model, input camera internal and external parameters, input image and pose data, calculate the calibration result (transformation matrix) based on the calibration model, and apply the result to the grasping algorithm, ultimately achieving high-precision industrial automation production.

title: Evaluating Communication and Architectural Overheads in NTT Accelerators for ML-KEM
authors: Stefano Di Matteo, Emanuele Valea
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The standardization of post-quantum cryptography (PQC) has led to the adoption of ML-KEM, a lattice-based key encapsulation mechanism derived from CRYSTALS-Kyber. ML-KEM heavily relies on polynomial multiplication over the Module Learning With Errors (MLWE) problem, efficiently implemented using the Number Theoretic Transform (NTT). While the NTT significantly reduces computational complexity, its irregular memory access patterns and high arithmetic intensity pose challenges for efficient software execution, especially in constrained environments. Consequently, hardware acceleration has become essential for enabling practical ML-KEM deployment in embedded and edge systems. Recent research has proposed several hardware accelerators for the NTT, but often without demonstrating their effectiveness in accelerating full matrix-vector products within ML-KEM. Designing such accelerators requires careful architectural trade-offs involving memory organization, data movement, parallelism, and system integration. The main contribution of this paper is a comprehensive assessment of communication, memory arrangement, and architectural overheads in NTT accelerators. We analyze state-of-the-art NTT acceleration techniques for ML-KEM and we propose a hardware architecture optimized for efficient matrixvector multiplication. Our NTT-based accelerator is integrated into a RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC), and we evaluate the impact of key architectural choices (e.g., parallelism on the butterfly units, latency of communication and memory arrangement) on area and performance.

title: A Compact Modified Multi-Resonant Printed Antenna for High-Frequency Wireless Applications
authors: C. Kumar, G. Yaswanth, C. Uday Kiran, T. N. Monika
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This paper proposes a compact printed antenna for high-frequency wireless communication. An existing reference design was modified to create the antenna configuration by adding more meandering paths, improved coupling sections, and interdigitated capacitor elements. These enhancements improve impedance matching and enable the activation of numerous resonant modes within the required operating frequency range. The antenna structure was designed and analyzed using the CST Studio Suite. Simulation results show significant improvement in the reflection coefficient performance, achieving multiple resonant frequencies with return loss values of less than −10 dB. The antenna exhibits stable radiation characteristics and acceptable efficiency. The proposed antenna is ideal for incorporation into modern wireless communication devices because of its small size, simple design, and enhanced electrical performance.

title: A Human-Centred Adaptive Trust Calibration Framework for Autonomous Vehicles Integrating Explainable AI, Behavioural Feedback, and Cybersecurity Resilience
authors: Shivam Saxena, Supratim Ghosh, Srijan Bandi, Sayan Halder, Ramesh Sekaran, Jeeva S
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: When an autonomous vehicle makes a decision, the stakes are real-and yet most of today’s systems offer the person behind the wheel nothing more than a single number on a screen, with no explanation of what it means or where it came from. This creates a frustrating dilemma: operators either trust the system blindly, or they second -guess it constantly. Both responses have proven deadly.One more rare topic: how a cyberattack might silently alter the internal "thought processes" of a given autonomous system in ways the current systems cannot even detect, let alone react to. The ACTCF addresses both failures directly and head-on: the Adaptive Computational Trust Calibration Framework. At its centre is the concept that trust is a living, dynamic thing that grows, shrinks, and adapts dynamically to real-world observations rather than a set value. It achieves this with three integrated components. First, the Calibration Engine: Instead of accepting a pre-determined amount of trust from the operator, the Calibration Engine continuously observes and learns it. Across many dozens of sessions, this engine closely watches the human operator's hesitations, taking back control, minute facial tics, and more, and constantly refining its understanding. It doesn't make assumptions about how much the human trusts it; it works off observable data and builds cautious, evidence-based reasoning, which sharpens over time. A class of AI called the Transparency Engine: The Transparency Engine's silent decisions are precisely what make it dangerous. It aims to eliminate the silence. Rather than a "leap of faith" to a data point, the Transparency Engine works inside the system, extracting logically consistent explanations of what the system is doing and why. It translates machine reasoning into something a human can actually "look at and evaluate" upon a query. Second, the Integrity Engine: Cybersecurity threats aren't usually loud. They slip into a system via communications channels or sensor feeds, making minute changes which are nearly undetectable before the system has been damaged. This is precisely what the Integrity Engine continuously and silently monitors so that by the time a threat could have been significant, it has already been apprehended and confined. Every engine provides a unique solution to a real problem. However, the reason they function as a system is that, under pressure, trust, transparency, and security collapse into one another rather than being distinct concerns. Relying on a system that is secure but opaque is not safe. When a system changes without providing an explanation, it creates the very kind of complacency that it is meant to prevent. These three engines work together to create a closed loop in which the system listens to those who rely on it, displays its thoughts, and subtly upholds its own integrity. Not three elements combined, but a single, cohesive concept: trust must be consistently earned in order to be genuine.

title: A SAT-Hard Compound Logic Locking Scheme with Empirical Resistance to Known Structural Attacks
authors: Sonali Shukla, Govind Rajhans Jadhav, Durgesh Sardan, Suryakant Toraskar, Jaynarayan T Tudu, Makoto Ikeda, Masahiro Fujita, Virendra Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Logic Locking aims to hide the original functionality of the design using a secret key. It protects hardware intellectual properties (IPs) against IP piracy or IC overproduction. However, an attacker analyzes the structural traces and/or uses Boolean satisfiability based technique called SAT attack to break such logic locking schemes. This motivates us to find a logic locking technique that can work against both SAT and structural analysis attacks. Therefore, this paper introduces a novel multiplier-based logic locking scheme. Leveraging the inherent complexity of multiplier circuits, the proposed scheme exponentially increases the time required for each iteration of a SAT attack. Moreover, a heuristic is also proposed to identify appropriate locations for inserting multiplier instances to increase the number of iterations. The multiplier-based logic locking scheme is further combined with the Anti-SAT scheme to create a robust and effective defense mechanism against SAT attacks and the various other attacks exploiting structural traces. The proposed technique is resilient to the state-of-the-art attack dedicated to the existing compound logic locking schemes. Moreover, the proposed compound logic locking scheme, requires half the number of key inputs than the state-of-the-art logic locking scheme while providing the similar level of security.

title: A Study on AC V2G EV-Pile Communication
authors: Xinglong Ling, Fan Wu, Kaixuan Li, Sicheng Duan, Xiangliang Fang, Yulu Zhong
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of the electric vehicle (EV) population, vehicle-to-grid (V2G), especially AC V2G, is considered a key enabling technology for constructing a new electricity system due to its immense potential for large-scale application. However, the development of AC V2G is facing a significant challenge, which is the lack of national standards unifying the digital communication specifications for bidirectional charge-discharge AC interfaces. Based on the latest guidelines released by the China Electricity Council and a group standard by the Guangzhou Power Industry Association, this study systematically reviews existing technical solutions for AC V2G EVpile communication. It first analyzes the superiorities of AC V2G compared with DC V2G in terms of resource scale, user experience, and cost-effectiveness. Then, it identifies two categories of EV-pile communication pathways. One is the asynchronous serial communication scheme, which relies on Control Pilot (CP) lines and requires no hardware modifications to existing systems. The other is the “hardware upgrade” scheme, which expects to achieve high-reliability communication by incorporating dedicated communication modules (e.g., CAN or LIN transceivers). This paper elaborates on the communication principles, workflows, advantages, and limitations of both solutions, as well as their standardization needs and future challenges, in an attempt to provide a theoretical reference for the standardization and industrial development of AC V2G.

title: Generalization in Vision-Language-Action Models: A Systematic Analysis of Architecture, Reasoning, Data Alignment, and Training Strategies
authors: Xiaoqian Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have substantially advanced robotic autonomous learning and expanded their practical application prospects, yet their generalization capability remains a critical bottleneck, manifesting in poor performance on unseen complex tasks, low adaptability in dynamic/cross-domain scenes, and sensitivity to out-of-training-set data distribution shifts. This paper systematically analyzes four representative methods for enhancing VLA generalization across four core dimensions: model architecture, decision-making logic, data and scene adaptation, and training strategy. It elaborates on the CogACT framework for cognitive-action synergy via closed-loop feedback, the Chain-of-Affordance method for interpretable visual-text reasoning chain-based task decomposition, the ReBot strategy for sim-to-real gap reduction through bidirectional real-to-sim-to-real pipelines and synthetic data generation, and the MAPS framework for modular fine-tuning with differentiated module regularization to allocate generalization capability on demand. These four methods cover the whole process of VLA model design, training and deployment, with notable synergies between different technical paths. The analysis clarifies the core technical routes of current VLA generalization research, and sorts out the key directions for optimizing VLA generalization from architecture innovation, reasoning improvement, data-scene alignment and training strategy refinement, laying a foundation for the subsequent development of generalizable VLA models and the construction of general robotic systems.

title: Construction and Practice of Blended Online and Offline Teaching Mode in Engineering Drawing
authors: Yanjiang Zhao, Jingshu Hu, Yue Meng, Xin Tong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Based on the characteristics and objectives of the engineering drawing course, this study has developed a blended learning model that combines online and offline teaching methods. Through systematic resource construction, data-driven classroom interaction, and continuous project practice, a closed-loop mechanism of teaching, evaluation, feedback and optimization has been formed. Teaching practice has shown that this model effectively improves students’ academic performance, learning engagement, and achievement of drawing competencies.

title: Design and Optimization of a High-Pressure Flare Gas Recovery System in Deepwater Gas Fields Based on Cascade PID Control
authors: Xu Sun, Han Shi, Yong Lai, Yong Yang, Fuming Xiang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Recovery of high-pressure flare gas in deepwater gas fields is a key measure for energy saving and emission reduction. However, conventional gas-phase compression schemes suffer from large footprint and high energy consumption. A “liquid-phase pressurization coupled with flash vaporization” recovery process based on cascade PID control is proposed to achieve efficient and stable recovery of high-pressure flash gas. A dynamic model of the controlled object-separator pressure and liquid level-is established. A cascade PID control structure is adopted, with the master loop controlling pressure and the slave loop controlling liquid level, incorporating feedforward compensation and anti-windup mechanisms. Critical system parameters are obtained via the relay feedback method, PID parameters are tuned using Ziegler–Nichols rules, and further optimized with actual operating data. Field application shows that the system achieves pressure fluctuation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 \%$</tex>, liquid level control accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 \%$</tex>, a recovery rate of high-pressure flare gas of 98.7%, and an annual gas recovery of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 2} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{4}} \mathbf{m}^{\mathbf{3}}$</tex>. In conclusion, the cascade PID control strategy effectively resolves the strong pressure-liquid level coupling issue, providing a high-precision control solution for high-pressure gas recovery under space-constrained conditions on offshore platforms.

title: A Binary Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer Algorithm for Feature Selection and Its Applications to Meteorological Radar Data
authors: Douglas Rodrigues, Leandro A. Passos, Danilo S. Jodas, Kelton A. P. Costa, Clayton R. Pereira, João P. Papa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Feature selection is a fundamental step in high-dimensional learning problems, aiming to reduce dimensionality while preserving predictive performance and improving model interpretability. Metaheuristic optimization methods have been widely adopted for this task due to their ability to explore large combinatorial search spaces. In this work, we propose a binary adaptation of the Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer for wrapper-based feature selection. The continuous dynamics of the original optimizer are mapped to binary selection masks through an S-shaped transfer function, and candidate subsets are evaluated using a Naïve Bayes classifier. The proposed method is evaluated on six benchmark datasets from different domains and on four high-dimensional meteorological radar datasets derived from NEXRAD Level II volumes for hail detection. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves competitive predictive performance compared with well-established binary metaheuristic algorithms, including Binary Bat, Binary Black Hole, Binary Equilibrium Optimizer, Binary Firefly, and Binary Multi-Verse Optimizer. Statistical tests indicate that performance differences are often dataset-dependent, whereas the proposed method exhibits stable behavior across multiple scenarios. Beyond predictive metrics, we introduce a frequency-based analysis of feature selection behavior across multiple runs and optimization methods. This analysis reveals which features are consistently selected, which are robust across metaheuristics, and which exhibit method-dependent selection patterns. In meteorological datasets, the progressive inclusion of gradient, texture, and convective descriptors yields consistent performance gains and clearer identification of robust radar features. The results demonstrate that the proposed Binary Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer is an effective and interpretable tool for feature selection in both benchmark and real-world high-dimensional applications.

title: Extended Reality (XR) for Social Skills Development in Learners with Autism Spectrum Disorders
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Extended Reality (XR)—encompassing virtual, augmented, and mixed reality—offers a powerful new medium for delivering social skills interventions to learners with Autism Spectrum Disorders (ASD). Autistic children and young adults often experience challenges in eye contact, emotion recognition, conversational reciprocity, and navigating complex social rules, which can limit participation in school, community, and employment contexts. Traditional social skills training, though valuable, is frequently constrained by low ecological validity, high staffing demands, and inconsistent generalisation to real-world environments. This paper proposes and evaluates a comprehensive XR-based Social Skills Training (XR-SST) framework designed for autistic learners aged 8–30 years. The framework integrates immersive social scenarios, multi-sensory cues, real-time adaptive feedback, and multi-modal analytics (gaze, speech, and behavioural metrics) within a neurodiversity-affirming design. A multi-site mixed-methods protocol is outlined, comparing XR-SST plus usual supports against an active control of conventional group-based social skills training over a 12-week period, with follow-ups at 3 and 6 months. Quantitative outcomes focus on standardised social skills scales, emotion recognition, conversational reciprocity, and adaptive behaviour, while qualitative data capture user experience, acceptability, and implementation barriers from autistic participants, families, teachers, and therapists. Illustrative findings suggest that XR-SST can yield meaningful improvements in social communication and confidence, with high levels of engagement and perceived safety. The paper concludes by discussing implementation pathways in schools and clinics, ethical and accessibility considerations, and future research directions needed to transform XR from promising innovation into a scalable, inclusive component of social skills education for autistic learners.

title: Immersive Teleoperation of Beyond-Human-Scale Robotic Manipulators: Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Mahdi Hejrati, Jouni Mattila
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Teleoperation of beyond-human-scale robotic manipulators (BHSRMs) presents unique challenges that differ fundamentally from conventional human-scale systems. As these platforms gain relevance in industrial domains such as construction, mining, and disaster response, immersive interfaces must be rethought to support scalable, safe, and effective human–robot collaboration. This paper investigates the control, cognitive, and interface-level challenges of immersive teleoperation in BHSRMs, with a focus on ensuring operator safety, minimizing sensorimotor mismatch, and enhancing the sense of embodiment. We analyze design trade-offs in haptic and visual feedback systems, supported by early experimental comparisons of exoskeleton-and joystick-based control setups. Finally, we outline key research directions for developing new evaluation tools, scaling strategies, and human-centered safety models tailored to large-scale robotic telepresence.

title: ICNWC 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)

title: Comments on “Lightweight Multi-User Public-Key Authenticated Encryption With Keyword Search”
authors: Keita Emura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Xu et al. (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security 2025) proposed a lightweight multi-user public-key authenticated encryption with keyword search (LM-PAEKS) scheme. In this short note, we demonstrate that keyword information can be leaked from ciphertexts in the scheme. We further note that there are shortcomings in the security proof as well.

title: Integrating Video Captioning into ESP Teaching to Enhance Learners' English Pronunciation
authors: Zhao Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Since audiovisual materials became the valuable and widely used resources in language education, it has been particularly important to develop various ways to use them in class. This study investigates the effectiveness of captioning instructional videos to enhance ESP (English for Special Purposes) learners' English pronunciation. Fifty-six students and three teachers at a college in western China participated in the study. The pretest, posttest, and delayed posttest were administered to the students in the experimental and control groups, with the former to caption videos with VisualSubSync, while the latter to learn the same materials with conventional listening-based tasks. All the tests were graded by three qualified CET Spoken English Test (CET-SET) raters. Results revealed the experimental group outscored the control group after the treatment. The questionnaire and interview followed indicated participants generally had a high degree of satisfaction in the captioning-based viewing and expressed the usefulness of it in fostering English pronunciation learning. These findings provide the critical pedagogical implications that video captioning is an effective way not only to integrate pronunciation practice in ESP teaching, but also to direct autonomous learning of English pronunciation.

title: Towards an Agentic and Explainable Runtime Architecture for PV Forecast Delivery
authors: Leila Sakli, Seifeddine Ben Elghali
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Most Photovoltaic (PV) forecasting studies optimize prediction accuracy, but production systems also need safeguards and explanations that operators can inspect. Rather than proposing a new forecasting model, this paper introduces an agentic wrapper for day-ahead PV forecast delivery. The wrapper orchestrates a baseline predictor, applies runtime integrity checks, and corrects or flags outputs when basic physical and operational constraints are violated (e.g., nighttime consistency and capacity bounds). Each forecast is accompanied by structured explanation artifacts, such as triggered checks and reason codes, designed for auditability and integration with operational pipelines. Experiments on real PV plant data over a 24-hour horizon show that the proposed architecture matches the baseline accuracy while improving physical consistency and traceable behavior at runtime.

title: Efficient Edge Deployment of Mixture-of-Experts Models with Hybrid Expert Quantization
authors: Tao Liu, Qipeng Xie, Zhaoyang Han, Weizheng Wang, Shengli Pan, Xiang Zhang, Chunhua Su, Kaishun Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Deploying large language models (LLMs) directly at the edge offers significant benefits in reducing inference latency and enhancing privacy protection. However, this approach presents critical technical challenges due to the limited hardware resources available at the edge. In this paper, we explore the deployment of the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model, a popular LLM architecture, using weight quantization in a typical edge environment with multiple edge servers. Existing methods that uniformly quantize MoE models do not account for the varying importance of different experts or the dynamic nature of edge requests. To address these issues, we propose a hybrid expert quantization strategy, where each edge server quantizes experts to different bit-widths based on the preferences of received requests. Furthermore, we implement a comprehensive system optimization that jointly considers expert quantization and request assignment, aiming to achieve high-precision and low-latency inference services. To manage the complex, coupled optimization challenges, we design a reinforcement learning (RL)-based algorithm. To overcome the slow convergence typical of traditional RL, we pre-train the policy network using a system simulator that generates heuristic solutions, thus accelerating the learning process. Extensive experimental results on real datasets demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms existing methods.

title: 2D Ultrasound Elasticity Imaging of Abdominal Aortic Aneurysms Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Utsav Ratna Tuladhar, Richard Simon, Doran Mix, Michael Richards
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAA) pose a significant clinical risk due to their potential for rupture, which is often asymptomatic but can be fatal. Although maximum diameter is commonly used for risk assessment, diameter alone is insufficient as it does not capture the properties of the underlying material of the vessel wall, which play a critical role in determining the risk of rupture. To overcome this limitation, we propose a deep learning-based framework for elasticity imaging of AAAs with 2D ultrasound. Leveraging finite element simulations, we generate a diverse dataset of displacement fields with their corresponding modulus distributions. We train a model with U-Net architecture and normalized mean squared error (NMSE) to infer the spatial modulus distribution from the axial and lateral components of the displacement fields. This model is evaluated across three experimental domains: digital phantom data from 3D COMSOL simulations, physical phantom experiments using biomechanically distinct vessel models, and clinical ultrasound exams from AAA patients. Our simulated results demonstrate that the proposed deep learning model is able to reconstruct modulus distributions, achieving an NMSE score of 1.6%. Similarly, in phantom data, the predicted modular ratio closely matches the expected values, affirming the model's ability to generalize to phantom data. We compare our approach with an iterative method which shows comparable performance but higher computation time per reconstruction. In contrast, once trained offline, the deep learning method can provide quick and accurate estimates of tissue stiffness from ultrasound images, so as to provide pseudo-realtime predictions of AAA growth.

title: Joint Spatio-Temporal Federated Learning for Cellular Traffic Prediction
authors: Chenhan Zhai, Hui Liu, Ertong Shang, Lizhe Zhang, Zeyu Qiu, Junzhao Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Cellular traffic prediction is essential for the energy-efficient and intelligent management of base stations (BSs). In this context, Federated Learning allows BSs to collaboratively train a shared model without transmitting raw data, thus significantly reducing communication overhead and enhancing privacy protection. Despite these advantages, most existing methods struggle to alleviate the coupled spatio-temporal heterogeneity of cellular traffic data across BSs, resulting in suboptimal global prediction performance. The main reason is that they decouple spatio-temporal relationships by modeling temporal and spatial properties independently, with limited consideration of their dependencies in cellular traffic. To overcome these limitations, we propose FedJST, a joint spatio-temporal federated cellular traffic prediction method that effectively integrates the temporal and spatial properties of cellular traffic from different BSs in two key aspects. Firstly, to capture spatio-temporal dependencies, we propose a multi-scale feature guidance strategy. This strategy leverages global multi-scale feature to incorporate shared spatio-temporal information, encouraging local models to capture complex spatio-temporal interactions. Secondly, we propose a spatio-temporal property-aware BS selection strategy that leverages a lightweight augmented dataset to prioritize representative BSs for training, enabling effective learning of diverse spatio-temporal patterns. We conduct extensive experiments across six cases on two real-world datasets, demonstrating that FedJST achieves average improvements of 5.95%, 4.43%, and 2.99% in RMSE, MAE, and R2 metrics, respectively.

title: TinyArtifactNet: A Resource-Efficient Deep Learning Model for On-Device Motion Artifact Detection in PPG_supp1-3692462.pdf
authors: Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: N/A
abstract: Photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals are widely used in wearable devices for continuous monitoring of cardiovascular vital signs. However, PPG signals are highly susceptible to motion artifacts, which severely degrade signal quality and lead to inaccurate physiological measurements. Consequently, any vital signs derived from contaminated PPG lead to wrong clinical or health-related decisions. To address this challenge, this study proposes TinyArtifactNet, a lightweight hybrid multi-scale temporal deep learning model for efficient and accurate motion artifact detection in PPG signals. The proposed architecture integrates ghost modules, multi-scale dilated convolutions, and squeeze-and-excitation mechanisms to achieve a favorable trade-off between performance and computational efficiency. The effectiveness of TinyArtifactNet was evaluated using three publicly available datasets and one self-curated private dataset. This work presents three key contributions: the design of a lightweight architecture, its deployment on an embedded platform, and real-time validation, all combining within a unified framework. On the PPG-DaLiA dataset, the proposed model achieved state-of-the-art performance among embedded-deployable lightweight models with an accuracy of 88.17% and a Dice score of 88.77%, while requiring only 21.3K parameters and 0.16 MB of memory. Furthermore, cross-dataset evaluation on two completely unseen datasets, TROIKA and WESAD, yielded accuracies of 81.84% and 90.34%, respectively, and Dice scores of 82.93% and 90.05%, respectively, demonstrating strong generalization. In addition, real-time evaluation using raw PPG signals acquired from a low-cost MAX30102 sensor and deployed on an STM32 microcontroller achieved an accuracy of 87.2%. These results confirm the suitability of TinyArtifactNet for real-time motion artifact detection on low-cost, resource-constrained platforms, making it a promising solution for wearable healthcare systems.

title: Gauss-Legendre Accelerated Double Iteration Method for Asteroid Landing Trajectory Planning
authors: Xiaotong Wu, Zibin Sun, Di Wu, Lin Cheng, Shengping Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Asteroid landing trajectory planning remains computationally challenging owing to the irregular gravitational field, the associated complex dynamical environment, and strict terminal accuracy requirements. Although indirect optimal control methods offer high-precision continuous-time solutions, their costate shooting processes typically suffer from high sensitivity, slow convergence, and numerical instability. To address these issues, this study proposes a Gauss-Legendre accelerated double iteration method that unifies a semi-analytical initial costate formulation, efficient multifold Gauss-Legendre integral evaluation, and a Jacobian guided homotopy continuation strategy within one indirect solution framework. The main contributions are threefold: (i) a semi analytical expression for the initial costate is derived, enabling effi cient iterative updates within the indirect framework; (ii) multifold Gauss-Legendre integration is incorporated into the initial costate update process to efficiently evaluate the high-order integral terms arising in each iteration step; and (iii) a Jacobian-based convergence criterion with adaptive homotopy step size adjustment is developed to ensure stable and theoretically predictable convergence. Simulation experiments on asteroid 433 Eros demonstrate that the proposed method reduces computation time by approximately 67.5% compared to traditional shooting methods while achieving robust trajectory re-planning capabilities under significant task mismatches. These features make the method potentially suitable for future onboard applications.

title: A Decision-Analytics Framework Combining DEA, SFA and ML with Mixture Optimization for Industrial Photovoltaics
authors: Gustavo S. Leal, Victor E. M. Valério, Pedro P. Balestrassi, Lupercio F. Bessegato, Farid Melgani
publish: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy
abstract: The rapid rollout of industrial photovoltaic (PV) generation demands robust tools for benchmarking performance and guiding investment. Traditional efficiency techniques – non-parametric data envelopment analysis (DEA) and parametric stochastic-frontier analysis (SFA) – offer complementary strengths, yet each has recognized limitations when applied in isolation. This study proposes a hybrid framework that (i) estimates multiple DEA and SFA frontiers; (ii) learns their predictive patterns with Random Forest, Gradient Boosting and XGBoost models; and (iii) integrates the resulting efficiency scores through an optimized weighting scheme derived from a simplex-lattice mixture design of experiments. The approach is demonstrated on a statewide dataset covering 70 immediate geographic regions of Minas Gerais, Brazil, comprising installed capacity, number of PV units, solar-radiation estimates and realized energy output. Optimization yields a parsimonious composite based primarily on the DEA and SFA models, reducing the principal-component loss metric by 18 % relative to equal weighting. Spatial analysis reveals a clear north–south efficiency gradient correlated with solar insolation; nonetheless, centrally located industrial hubs also achieve high scores, underscoring the moderating role of infrastructure. The findings highlight regions where policy support or technology upgrades could deliver the greatest marginal gains and illustrate how mixture-design optimization can reconcile diverse frontier estimates, offering a generalizable decision-analytics toolkit for renewable-energy assessment.

title: Lattice-based Blind Ring Signature with Applications to Anonymous Voting Systems
authors: Xue Chen, Shiyuan Xu, Yu Guo, Siu-Ming Yiu, Xiaohua Jia, Bin Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the growing adoption of electronic voting in digital societies, ensuring voter anonymity and ballot integrity is essential for secure and trustworthy elections. Blind ring signature is a promising cryptographic primitive that simultaneously provides blindness (concealing the link between signer and message) and ring anonymity (hiding the actual signer within a group), thus enabling anonymous yet verifiable voting. However, most existing schemes either rely on discrete logarithm assumptions, rendering them vulnerable to quantum adversaries, or fail to achieve both blindness and ring anonymity within a single construction. In the post quantum setting, only a handful of lattice-based blind ring signature schemes have been proposed, and all suffer from prohibitively large signature sizes, limiting their practicality for large-scale deployments. Consequently, there is a pressing need for lattice-based blind ring signatures that achieve post-quantum security, simultaneous blindness and ring anonymity, and practical efficiency for large-scale elections. In this work, we present LBRS, the first lattice-based blind ring signature scheme that transforms the two-round SnowBlind protocol [Crypto'23] from the discrete logarithm setting to the lattice setting while seamlessly integrating ring signature functionality. Unlike existing solutions, our design achieves post-quantum security while preserving blindness and introducing anonymity, making it robust against quantum attackers and capable of simultaneously providing blindness and ring anonymity. We implement LBRS within a complete post-quantum anonymous voting architecture, ensuring end-to-end security and full voter anonymity, while maintaining ballot integrity, enabling transparent and verifiable tallying, and supporting practical deployment. We formally prove that LBRS satisfies correctness, anonymity, blindness, and one-more unforgeability under standard lattice assumptions. Experiments show that LBRS reduces blind signature sizes to 58.37% and real signature sizes to 2.75%-16.52% of those in prior arts, while significantly decreasing registration time and maintaining comparable costs in other phases. These advantages scale with ring size, making LBRS a strong candidate for large-scale, post-quantum secure elections.

title: STATE: Safe and Threatening Adversarial Trajectory Engine
authors: Tonmoay Deb, Natalia Denisenko, Chongyang Gao, Valerio La Gatta, Sven de Laaf, Afke Neeleman, Lirika Sola, V.S. Subrahmanian
publish: IEEE Intelligent Systems
abstract: As terrorists ramp up drone use, the need to protect cities, regions, and utilities from drone-based terror attacks has become an urgent priority. We present STATE, a novel GAN-based framework to automatically generate a set of safe and threatening drone trajectories over a region. Using STATE, security officials can generate synthetic trajectories for regions over which no such trajectories were previously recorded, enabling them to better test planned defenses. Jointly with security officials from The Hague, we show that STATE beats six recent baselines, achieving up to 75.8% improvement in trajectory plausibility and 35.8% in threat alignment, as evaluated by police experts.

title: HarmonicLIME: Frequency-Aware Local Explanations for Deep Music Genre Classification
authors: Sridharan Sankaran
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Deep learning models for music genre classification achieve high accuracy but remain opaque, producing decisions that domain experts cannot interpret or audit. Existing post-hoc explainability methods such as LIME and SHAP operate on raw mel-spectrogram bins, yielding saliency maps perceptually meaningless to musicians. We propose HarmonicLIME, a music-aware local explanation framework that perturbs audio in the harmonic-percussive-residual (HPR) decomposed space, producing component-level saliency maps aligned to tonal and rhythmic musical structure. Applied to a VGGish classifier fine-tuned on GTZAN (83.0% accuracy), HarmonicLIME achieves a mean Deletion AUC of 0.1990 (excluding the Classical anomaly), outperforming Mel-LIME (0.2127) and SHAP (0.2582), with statistically significant superiority on Country and Reggae (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p < 0.001$</tex-math></inline-formula>, Wilcoxon signed-rank).
