The proposed Harmonic Localization Ratio reveals genre-consistent saliency structure: Hip-Hop concentrates 81.5% of saliency in the percussive component while Country assigns 76.9% to the harmonic component. Zero-shot transfer to FMA-small confirms cross-dataset generalization, independently replicating Clever Hans diagnostic findings on a structurally distinct genre taxonomy. Code is publicly available to facilitate reproducibility.

title: A Zero-Shot Network for Low-Light Image Enhancement with Brightness-Aware Representation and Semantic-Consistency Guidance
authors: Huihong Huang, Zhida Ren, Xiaowen Shi, Gaowei Wu, Yongqiang Tang, Wensheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Low-light image enhancement aims to restore visibility, contrast, and semantic fidelity to images captured under poor illumination, which often suffer from uneven lighting, detail loss, and noise. Most existing deep learning methods usually rely on paired datasets, which limits their generalization ability. To overcome these limitations, a new zero-shot low-light enhancement network, termed BRSGNet, is introduced to improve brightness-aware representation and enhance semantic consistency. Specifically, a Brightness-Aware Module (BAM) with multi-scale dilated convolution and spatial attention mechanisms is designed to improve illumination estimation while preserving global-local details. Then, a Semantic-Aware Network (SANet) incorporating a Semantic Feature Consistency Loss (SFC-Loss) is introduced to enforce semantic alignment between the enhanced and original images without requiring ground-truth references. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves competitive performance in terms of both visual quality and semantic consistency, showcasing strong generalization across diverse low-light conditions.

title: Improved Predictive Speed Control of Brushless Dual Rotor Motors Considering Speed Measurement Delay and Virtual Inertia Identification
authors: Hui Wu, Shuangxia Niu, Zekai Lyu, Zhenyao Sun, Mingyuan Jiang, Chunhua Liu, Wei Liu, K. T. Chau
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The brushless dual rotor motor (BLDRM), with its multiple electromechanical ports and high integration, holds significant potential for various applications such as electric continuously variable transmission (ECVT) systems and contra-rotating propellers. However, the high pole-pair ratio typically adopted between the rotors and modulation windings in magnetic field modulation design imposes stringent requirements on the dynamic speed response performance, making precise and rapid control challenging. To address these issues, an improved predictive speed control (IPSC) with a discrete third-order extended state observer (ESO) and virtual inertia identification is proposed to strengthen the transient speed performance of BLDRM. With the discrete third-order ESO, the measured speed is precisely compensated, thereby enhancing speed prediction accuracy for IPSC and further improving speed response performance. Besides, accurate parameter tuning and performance analysis are obtained within the discrete domain of the proposed IPSC control strategy. With the virtual moment of inertia as well as adaptive estimation of key parameters, the robustness against model parameter uncertainties is enhanced. Finally, experimental validation on a BLDRM drive platform confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Adaptive Optimal PI Control for Grid-Forming Inverters With Provable Multitime-Scale Stability via Lyapunov-Constrained Learning
authors: Hamad Alduaij, Chenxu Chao, Yang Weng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Modern inverter-based power systems require controllers that adapt to changing grid conditions while maintaining strict stability across nested control loops. Existing approaches face a fundamental tradeoff: classical proportional–integral (PI) controllers offer provable stability but are static, while learning-based methods can adapt but often violate the structural stability guarantees needed for safe operation, especially in systems with tightly coupled, multitime-scale dynamics. This work proposes a distributed adaptive control framework for grid-forming inverters that combines neural gain scheduling with provable Lyapunov stability across multiple time scales. Our approach reformulates nested Lyapunov stability conditions as explicit gain-ratio inequality constraints and embeds them into both: 1) an offline augmented-Lagrangian constrained optimization for base gain synthesis and 2) online neural schedulers that adapt controller parameters to changing operating conditions while preserving stability certificates. A key contribution is the introduction of per-converter dynamic adaptation states governed by Lyapunov-passivity constraints, enabling state-dependent gain scheduling within certified stability envelopes. Implemented within a physics-informed, differentiable simulation environment, controllers tuned with our method preserve stability under severe transients, including large load steps and setpoint changes, while improving closed-loop regulation. This framework retains the familiar PI control architecture while rigorously enforcing stability margins, offering a practical stability-certified learning paradigm for emerging power-electronic systems. The adaptive control performs algorithmic gain synthesis and scheduling as operating conditions change, implemented through learned neural schedulers operating at multiple timescales.

title: Measuring Greenland Ice Sheet Melt based on GNSS-R and Random Forest Using FengYun-3E GNOS-R
authors: Liangyu Chen, Jiachun An, Zemin Wang, Baojun Zhang, Jingxin Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: As surface melting of the Greenland Ice Sheet (GrIS) is an important driver of sea-level rise against the backdrop of accelerating global climate system warming, high-precision monitoring of its state is crucial for understanding the polar-atmosphere coupling mechanism. The melting detection is mainly based on microwave radiometer with spatial resolution of only tens of kilometers, while GNSS-R can have higher spatial resolution of kilometers. GNSS-R ice sheet melting detection mainly uses the reflectivity threshold method, which has low accuracy and lack of discussion of physical mechanisms. This study utilizes Delay-Doppler Map (DDM) data from the GNOS-R sensor aboard China's FY-3E polar-orbiting satellite to construct an ice-sheet melting detection model based on the random forest algorithm with multiple parameters innovatively, achieving spatiotemporal dynamic inversion of the surface freeze-thaw processes over GrIS. Through comparative and combinatorial experiments with four designed feature subsets (waveform-related parameters, power-related parameters, geometric condition constraints, and spatial location constraints), it is found that increasing feature dimensions and complementarity of physical meanings significantly enhance model performance, ensuring stable and accurate freeze-thaw detection under different scattering mechanisms. The optimal model demonstrates excellent performance in multi-metric evaluations, with many evaluation metrics exceeding 96%. The spatial resolution is greatly improved while maintaining similar accuracy to the microwave radiometer. Through combined experiments on different parameter types, this study further reveals the regulatory effect of the incident angle on the scattering mechanism and characteristic response during the surface melting process of the ice sheet. Its physical essence lies in the differences in dielectric loss and dielectric uniformity of the GNSS signal between frozen and melt state of snow at different incident angles. In summary, this study provides a solution combining physical interpretability and computational efficiency for complex scene classification of multisource remote sensing data, holding significant scientific implications for improving input parameters of climate system models and enhancing the prediction accuracy of sea-level rise.

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Suicide Risk Assessment Using Social Media Data
authors: Tsholofelo Mokheleli, Tebogo Makaba, Patrick Ndayizigamiye, Nompumelelo Ndlovu, Hossana Twinomurinzi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate and interpretable suicide risk assessment is critical for timely intervention in mental health contexts. This study presents an explainable machine learning approach for suicide risk assessment using social media data, evaluating both multi-class and binary classification settings alongside a multi-level explainability strategy. Results show that while multi-class classification enables fine-grained risk stratification, it introduces substantial predictive complexity, with models achieving moderate performance (balanced accuracy ≈ 0.53 and ROC-AUC ≈ 0.79). In contrast, binary classification improves stability and discrimination, supporting its suitability for real-world risk prioritisation in digital mental health settings. To enhance interpretability, the study integrates SHAP and LIME to provide complementary global and local explanations. SHAP identifies key linguistic indicators such as temporal references, intent-related terms, and self-referential expressions as primary drivers of elevated risk, while LIME provides instance-level justification for individual predictions. Building on this, a counterfactual explanation method, Counterfactual Explanations with Optimised Contextual Flow (CE-OCF), is introduced. CE-OCF generates minimal, contextually coherent textual modifications that shift model predictions, enabling the simulation of both escalation and de-escalation pathways. The study advances explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) for suicide risk assessment by combining predictive modelling with actionable interpretability. These findings highlight the importance of aligning model performance with clinically relevant explanations, supporting the development of transparent and decision-supportive AI systems for early detection and intervention.

title: MVDR-Integrated TFM for Defect Detection in Geometrically Shadowed Regions
authors: Wei Li, Zheng Hu, Da Teng, Yanxun Xiang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Detecting defects in geometrically shadowed regions remains an unavoidable challenge for ultrasonic array imaging using the total focusing method (TFM). To address this limitation, the MVDR-TFM method is proposed in this work, in which minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) beamforming is integrated into the TFM framework. By minimizing the output power under a distortionless constraint, interference from strong near-field scatterers is suppressed and sidelobe leakage is reduced, thereby enhancing defect visibility in shadowed areas. In an L-shaped specimen, slot-type defects under the structural barrier are successfully identified by the proposed method, whereas conventional TFM cannot. In terms of contrast ratio and contrast-to-noise ratio, the proposed method achieves improvements of 40.44% and 80.34% over the conventional method, respectively. For super-resolution imaging of two side‑drilled holes of 1 mm diameter with a 1 mm spacing below the Rayleigh limit, the peak-to-valley amplitude difference of the resulting lateral cross-sectional profile is increased by 10.53 dB with the proposed method compared to TFM. These results demonstrate improved defect detectability and resolvability of the MVDR-TFM method, enabling more reliable ultrasonic array inspections.

title: Conformal Single-port Microwave Biosensor for Contactless Dielectric Liquid Characterization
authors: Benyamin Harkinezhad, Tomislav Markovic, Robin Evans, Kamran Ghorbani, Efstratios Skafidas, Dominique Schreurs
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a flexible, one-port microwave biosensor for contactless permittivity characterization of biological and chemical samples based on waveguide mode excitation. Unlike conventional resonator-based or multi-port microwave sensors, which rely on resonance tracking or transmission measurements, the proposed sensor operates using a single-port reflection measurement and exploits the excitation and re-coupling of a waveguide mode within the material under test (MUT). The proposed sensor employs a cylindrical coplanar waveguide (CCPW) wrapped around standard laboratory glass test tubes to excite a waveguide mode within the material under test without direct contact. The underlying coupling mechanism is analyzed using field overlap considerations, and its manifestation in the measured reflection coefficient is examined. The excitation and re-coupling of the waveguide mode give rise to characteristic loop features in the S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub> response, which are shown to be strongly dependent on the real and imaginary parts of the sample permittivity. A dedicated deembedding procedure is introduced to isolate the intrinsic sensor response using a single reference measurement. The sensor performance is experimentally validated through measurements on aqueous solutions of ethanol, NaCl, glucose, and yeast. Sensitivity metrics derived from the extracted loop parameters demonstrate minimum sensitivities of 14.1MHz per unit ϵ ′ <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</sub> and 0.21 per unit tanδ. In addition, the sensor exhibits good repeatability, with maximum standard deviations of 0.0054 in magnitude and 7.52° in phase across repeated measurements up to 5 GHz. The results highlight a sensing approach that combines contactless operation, simplified measurement, and high sensitivity, offering a compelling alternative to existing microwave biosensing techniques.

title: Optical Performance Improvement of Thin Crystalline Silicon Solar Cells through ZnS Nanoparticle Integration
authors: A. Sandeep Kumar Reddy, Arijit Bardhan Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: As the next-generation photovoltaic devices, thin and flexible crystalline silicon(c-Si) solar cells have generated a lot of attention because of their lightweight, bendable design and potential for incorporation into wearable and portable applications. Never the less, their smaller thickness restricts optical absorption, which results in a reduction in efficiency. In order to overcome this difficulty sophisticated light management techniques are necessary. This work investigates the application of high-refractive-material nanoparticles, such as ZnS, to enhance the hetero-junction solar cell's light management system. While ZnS nanoparticles scattering and plasmonic- like behavior help to increase optical path lengths within the active layer, their significant absorption coefficient and tunable band gap enable better photon harvesting over a wide spectrum range. Almost 7% reduction of reflectance is shown in the proposed light management structure using validated by FEM (finite element method) based simulation tool. The work describes design strategies, material integration methods, and optical simulations that show how nanoparticles can maximize photocurrent generation, reduce reflection losses, and enhance light coupling. The suggested tactics show a possible technique to achieve sophisticated nanostructured light management schemes in c-Si solar cells that are mechanically flexible, lightweight, and efficient.

title: LLM-driven Dark Web Intelligence Analysis Pipeline
authors: Lei Jiang, Meng Wang, Yongqing Sun, Jiayi Wang, Haohao Zhang, Haohua Duan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Dark web platforms contain abundant early-stage cyber threat intelligence, such as discussions of exploits, malware, and underground services, which is valuable for proactive security analysis. However, dark web intelligence is highly noisy, fragmented, and temporally dispersed across heterogeneous sources, making large-scale daily analysis and reporting challenging. Existing approaches based on keyword matching, static rules, or isolated mining tasks struggle to synthesize multi-source intelligence into coherent analytical reports.In this paper, we formulate daily dark web intelligence analysis as a multi-source document synthesis problem, where selectively incorporating relevant historical context is essential for capturing threat evolution. To address this problem, we propose an LLM-driven analysis pipeline with a dual-channel retrieval mechanism that combines semantic retrieval for high recall and entity-guided structured retrieval for high precision. We further design a hierarchical summarization strategy to incrementally aggregate intelligence across time and sources, reducing long-context reasoning burden and mitigating hallucinations. Experiments on real-world dark web intelligence demonstrate that our approach generates more coherent, comprehensive, and temporally consistent reports than baseline methods, while remaining efficient for daily operational use.

title: Development of a GPS based Smart Fire Detection using UAV-UGV Coordinated Control
authors: Yogesh Varshney, Harshit Sharma, Loveleen Sharma, P.M Tiwari, Mehtab Fatima, Kamlesh Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper shows an intelligent firefighting system that involves a UAV (quad-copter) and UGV (rover) which reduces the latency and the human risk in fire calamities. Traditional methods have very high dependency on human involvement and old systems, which can be unsafe and have high latency in hazardous environments. To address this, the systems shown involves a quad-copter with Raspberry Pi, various sensors like flame, IR camera and GPS module to have real time monitoring on the various areas and detect fire very fast. Whenever the fire is detected, the GPS location is provided by the drone wirelessly to the rover through ESP 32 module. The rover which has the brain - Arduino Mega receives the location and moves towards the point with obstacle avoidance and turns ON the pump to extinguish the fire hotspots. Testing shows reliable fire detection and effective fire suppression on the roots of the fire. The system gives a practical approach for improving the human risk and efficient in fire calamities.

title: RBTGA: A Multi-Strategy Collaborative Enhancement BERT-LSTM Model for Hotel Service Review Analysis
authors: Hanxuan Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Traditional Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT)-Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architectures for sentiment classification suffer from limitations including restricted contextual modeling under static masking strategies, limited long-sequence dependency modeling, and potentially increased computational cost. To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhanced architecture based on Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach (RoBERTa)-Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN)-Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)-Attention. RoBERTa is adopted as the representation layer to improve contextual semantic encoding through optimized pre-training strategies. To replace the structurally complex LSTM, a unified TCN-GRU framework is introduced, where TCN captures multi-scale temporal patterns and GRU preserves long-term sequential information. An Attention mechanism is further applied to perform weighted aggregation of sequence features, enabling emphasis on sentiment-critical semantics. Experiments conducted on a hotel service review dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an F1 score of 0.8872 and an accuracy of 0.8462. Compared with multiple benchmark models, it yields accuracy gains ranging from 0.0045 to 0.1583 and F1 gains from 0.0016 to 0.0987. Overall, the proposed architecture provides a robust and competitive solution for complex sentiment classification tasks.

title: Improved RRT-Connect Path Planning Algorithm for Robotic Arm
authors: Yubo Wan, Guowei Zhang, Mingxuan Lu, Zhongcheng Pu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address the problems of blind sampling, low search efficiency, and unsmooth paths in the traditional RRT-Connect algorithm for six-degree-of-freedom manipulator path planning, an improved RRT-Connect method is proposed. In the random sampling stage, Gaussian sampling is introduced, in which the sampling probability is adaptively adjusted according to the distance between the node and the target, thereby enhancing the directionality of the search. An adaptive step-size extension mechanism is designed to dynamically adjust the expansion step according to collision detection results, improving planning efficiency in complex environments. The length and smoothness of the path are optimized through redundant node elimination and Cantmull-Rom spline interpolation.

title: Research on performance optimization of parallel computing system based on big data technology
authors: Qingyang Wang, Jiamei Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Parallel computing systems occupy a central position in big data processing technologies. Among them, Hadoop MapReduce, as a classic distributed computing framework, has been widely adopted for offline batch data processing. However, due to its design prioritizing high throughput, it exhibits significant latency issues when handling "short jobs" with processing times ranging from tens of seconds to several minutes. Therefore, optimizing its execution efficiency holds significant practical importance. This study conducts an in-depth analysis of Hadoop MapReduce's task scheduling and execution processes, identifies two primary performance bottlenecks, and designs an improved version—AHadoop—compatible with standard Hadoop based on these findings. Specifically, we migrate lightweight Setup/Cleanup tasks to the JobTracker for execution, eliminating the 12-second wait caused by the heartbeat mechanism. Simultaneously, we introduce an RPC-based instant communication mechanism to effectively separate emergency events from routine cluster management messages, thereby accelerating task scheduling. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to standard Hadoop, AHadoop achieves an average overall execution efficiency improvement exceeding 25% for short jobs. For the specific WordCount, Grep, and Sort tasks, performance gains reached 35%, 38%, and 34%, respectively.

title: CNTFET based Energy-efficient Approximate Full Adder for Low Power and High Speed Computing
authors: P. Raja, S. Jayanthi, M. Mohamed Sajid, D. Praveen, A. Sarathi Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Full adders are the core of the arithmetic logic units in digital systems and its architecture has a significant effect on the speed, area of silicon and power dissipation. This paper presents a full adder cell based on the Carbon Nanotube Field Effect Transistor (CNTFET) at 32 nm technology node. A dynamic logic structure is adopted to achieve the objectives of minimizing the number of transistors and the internal switching activities, also to make the circuit simpler and the operating frequency higher. Power dissipation is also minimized by chirality vectors optimization of the evaluation-stage transistors that facilitate the minimization of leakage current. The proposed energy-efficient approximate full adder (EAFA) is evaluated through HSPICE simulation using the Stanford CNTFET model at different supply voltages. The simulation results show an average power dissipation of 0.207 μW, worst case propagation delay of 11.52 ps, and power delay product of 2.387 aJ. The proposed structure offers better energy efficiency as compared to conventional CMOS realization as well as other existing adders based on CNTFET is therefore suitable for high-speed image processing applications.

title: Development of IoT-Enabled Building Management System for Advanced Safety, Energy Efficiency and Environmental Sustainability
authors: Pranay Debnath, Anurag Dey, S. Purushothaman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing need for energy efficiency, safety, and sustainability in modern infrastructure has sped up the use of Internet of Things (IoT) smart building solutions. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Smart IoT-Enabled Building Management System that integrates solar energy for smart energy use and improved safety in institutional buildings. The proposed system uses an ESP32 microcontroller as the main control unit, connected to several sensors including temperature and humidity sensors, motion detectors, gas and flame sensors, light sensors, and current and voltage sensors. This setup allows for real-time monitoring of the environment, analysis of energy use, and detection of hazards. Energy optimization occurs through automated control of appliances with relay modules, lighting based on occupancy, daylight-sensitive switching, and user-driven adjustments with potentiometers to reduce unnecessary energy use. A combined solar-grid management scheme employs a photovoltaic panel, charge controller, and battery storage to use renewable energy for important loads. Sensor data and system status are sent to a cloud platform through Wi-Fi and MQTT, which allows for remote monitoring, control, and alert notifications via a web or mobile dashboard. Testing in a college building shows better energy use, quicker hazard responses, and less reliance on grid power. The proposed system offers a scalable, budget-friendly, and sustainable approach to smart building management and helps create energy-efficient and safe smart campuses.

title: An Attention-based Multimodal Feature Fusion Model for Stroke Prediction with Explainable Clinical Decision Support
authors: Navaneetha Krishnan Muthunambu, Sowmiya K, Manu L B, Karthic E, Nithish S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Stroke is a leading cause of mortality and long-term disability worldwide, making early risk prediction an important component of preventive healthcare. However, clinical data used for stroke prediction are heterogeneous and often include demographic, clinical, and lifestyle variables whose relative importance may vary across patients. Traditional machine learning models typically treat these features as a single homogeneous vector, which limits their ability to capture modality-specific relationships and reduces interpretability in clinical decision support systems. To address these challenges, this study proposes an attention-based multimodal feature fusion model for stroke prediction that integrates heterogeneous patient data while maintaining explainability. The framework first encodes demographic, clinical, and lifestyle features using separate modality-specific neural encoders and then dynamically fuses these representations using an attention mechanism that learns the relative importance of each modality. Experimental evaluation on the publicly available Kaggle Stroke Prediction Dataset demonstrates that the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 91% and an AUC of 0.92, outperforming traditional baseline models such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost. Additionally, modality-level attention weights and SHAP-based feature attribution provide interpretable insights, supporting transparent and trustworthy clinical decision making.

title: Auto-Scaling Strategies for Machine Learning Workloads: Performance, Cost, and Model Reliability: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Dhanush Ponna, Janvi Shah, Sushika Reddy Gade, Kinjal A Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Massive and real-time machine learning (ML) applications impose great demands on resource management in cloud and distributed systems because of the dynamics of workloads, dependency on GPUs, and strict latency constraints. In this paper, a systematic review of reactive, predictive, and hybrid-based auto-scaling methods are presented and customized to ML workloads on clouds-based VMs, in containerized (Kubernetes) and serverless environments. In contrast to the previous surveys, this one has a single three-dimensional comparative framework which is based on performance, cost-efficiency, and model reliability. The analyzed key trade-offs, characteristics of a platform, workload uncertainty, and cold-start latency. The paper brings into the spotlight of the new ML-specific capabilities (e.g., scaling by using learning curves) and new directions, including reinforcement learning-based controllers and energy-aware scaling. Result describe that workload sensitive, reliability sensitive approaches are important to effective machine learning deployments.

title: Comparative Investigation of the Performance of Stainless Steel and 3D-Printed PLA Underwater Thruster Propellers Using FSI and Structural Analysis
authors: Md. Mashud Karim, Bhubon Thiotonius Costa, A K Jamil Shajib, Md. Ashifur Rahaman, Md. Shahidul Islam, Md. Shajratul Alam Towhid, Bishawajit Chakraborty
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: The propellers used by Unmanned Surface Vehicles (USV) and Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (UUV) are primarily made from either plastic, metal, or composite materials. Additive manufacturing can facilitate a promising method for rapid prototyping and developing innovative models. This research investigates the considerations of geometric configuration, fabrication, and performance of 3D-printed polylactic acid (PLA) propellers with a diameter of less than 100 mm using the Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) method. The investigation examines how operational deformation affects thrust and torque, comparing PLA propellers with stainless steel alternatives to identify optimal geometries. To achieve this, unidirectional Fluid-Structure Interaction (FSI) simulations using Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) and structural analysis will be carried out. Computer-aided designs of the propeller geometries were developed by MATLAB programs based on the alterable parameters (rake, skew, and thickness) of the propeller’s blades. The results find that certain geometries with moderate increases in rake and skew with greater thickness achieve higher efficiency, improving superior thrust-to-torque ratios, and making them optimal choices. However, a high increase in rake and skew accompanied by a decrease in thickness leads to a significant drop in efficiency, negatively impacting the thrust-to-torque ratio and overall performance. Although PLA propellers deform more than stainless steel, structural analysis establishes that the stresses are still below the yield strength of the material, guaranteeing operational viability. These findings demonstrate the potential of 3D-printed PLA propellers as an alternative to conventional propellers, offering a path toward a more cost-effective and efficient propulsion system for underwater applications.

title: Machine Learning for Sea State Estimation from Wave-Induced Ship Responses
authors: Remi Larnouhet, Raphael E. G. Mounet, Ulrik D. Nielsen, Gaute Storhaug
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Knowledge of ocean waves is crucial for industry and research. This study evaluates Machine Learning (ML) frameworks for predicting sea state parameters using inertial measurements of linear and angular accelerations from a container vessel during transoceanic voyages. The tested models include LightGBM, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression, and Artificial Neural Networks, with LightGBM emerging as the best-performing and fastest framework. Input discrete frequency features were derived from the power and cross-power spectral densities of heave, roll, and pitch accelerations processed via the Welch algorithm. Sensitivity analysis revealed that a cut-off frequency of $0.3-1.0 \mathrm{~Hz}$, combined with optimal smoothing in the Welch algorithm, effectively balances frequency resolution and computational efficiency, improving model performance while reducing input size

title: Reformulate, Retrieve, Localize: Agents for Repository-Level Bug Localization
authors: Genevieve Caumartin, Glaucia Melo
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: Beyond constant attention: Why VR’s relaxation-dominant patterns better support inclusive learning ecosystems
authors: Kai-Chun Hou, Kuan-Ying Chen, Ping-Han Cheng, Ping-Lin Fan, Kang-Ming Chang, Jui-Yao Tsai, Po-Wei Huang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As educational technology evolves, Virtual Reality (VR) demonstrates significant potential in advancing inclusive education by surmounting physical barriers and providing equitable experiences. However, the impact of immersive media on the core cognitive states of learners requires empirical validation. This study evaluates the effects of different display media on participants' Attention and Relaxation brainwave averages to assess their suitability for future inclusive learning environments. A within-subjects experimental design was employed with 36 participants, who performed virtual exploration tasks under three conditions: Resting (R), 2D Monitor (M), and VR Headset (V). Statistical results revealed a significant interaction between media condition and brainwave type (F(2, 70) = 4.908, p = .010 ). Crucially, the VR condition exhibited a unique "low-pressure immersion" pattern: it was the only condition where the Relaxation average (52.08) exceeded the Attention average (43.81), contrasting with the Attention-dominant patterns observed in the Resting (64.11) and Monitor (55.86) conditions. This physiological reversal suggests that VR immersion effectively filters environmental distractions and induces a "flow" state with reduced cognitive anxiety, providing an empirical foundation for future inclusive digital courseware development.

title: Lateral and Longitudinal Position Estimation and Control for Dynamic WPT Using Receiver Current and Vehicle Dynamics Information_supp1-3694019.mp4
authors: Osamu Shimizu
publish: N/A
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) promises to extend the driving range of electric vehicles. However, efficiency is severely reduced by coil misalignment. Especially when decelerating and stopping in urban driving, both longitudinal and lateral positions become critical. Achieving precise alignment without costly dedicated WPT sensors remains challenging because roadembedded coils are invisible. To address this, this article proposes an integrated position estimation and control framework with a simple current sensor and other vehicle motion sensors. An Unscented Kalman Filter simultaneously estimates both lateral and longitudinal positions by fusing receiver side DC current and vehicle dynamics with a pre-measured mutual inductance map, eliminating the need for dedicated WPT sensors. To ensure accurate alignment, a feedback controller is developed utilizing the estimated states. Experiments using a full-scale electric vehicle, which achieves a maximum received electric power of approximately 1.5 kW, demonstrate that the proposed method achieved a stopping accuracy of 31.4mm and 13.9mm in Root Mean Square Error in the longitudinal and lateral directions, respectively. This precise alignment improves energy efficiency by approximately 6.7% compared to a lateral misalignment of 60mm. Furthermore, through benchmarking with standard Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) functions, specifically Lane Keeping Assist and Adaptive Cruise Control, the results verify that the proposed framework possesses the potential to effectively augment the ADAS system to achieve the precise alignment essential for efficient DWPT.

title: Decoupled Neural Audio Steganography for Adaptive Sender-Side Model Updates
authors: Yanan Li, Qiyang Xiao, Mingxi Zhang, Yanzhen Ren, Lina Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Neural network–based steganography has garnered considerable attention for its strong security. However, existing approaches often suffer from excessive coupling between the embedding and extraction networks: the sender and receiver must employ paired models and maintain strict synchronization. Such synchronization not only complicates deployment but also introduces more severe potential risks of information leakage. To overcome this limitation, we propose a synchronization-free steganographic framework based on decoupled neural embedding networks, following the destruction–restoration principle. In our design, message embedding is realized through a destruction operation, while recovery is achieved using a neural network from the audio restoration domain. This decoupled architecture allows the sender to upgrade, replace, or randomize the embedding network—thus enabling dynamic model changes—without impairing the receiver’s ability to correctly extract the hidden message. As a result, synchronization-related vulnerabilities are fundamentally eliminated. Experimental results demonstrate that even under dynamic changes in the embedding network, the hidden information can still be reliably extracted, confirming both the effectiveness and enhanced security of the proposed approach.

title: Detection of Filled Marks of Answer Sheets by using Deep Learning for Automated Scoring
authors: Li Dandan, Zhang Xinyu, Zhang Yingjie
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Aiming at alleviating teachers' daily burden of grading exams, an object detection method is proposed by using deep learning for the automatic recognition of filled marks on answer sheet images, which are captured by mobile cameras, such as smart phones, cameras, and CCD embedded on laptop etc.). Considering that detection of filled marks on answer sheets is key to achieving automated scoring, in this paper, a Faster R-CNN architecture-based neutral network model is established. To solve the difficulty in the collection of the training dataset, a dataset generating program is developed to simulate the marks filled by human beings, and image augmentation is also used to avoid overfitting. Further, 3000 images are generated for training and validating the neural network model. To validate the pre-trained model, the model evaluation is conducted to verify its properties and the indicators, including recognition accuracy of 97.16%, average IoU of 95.19%, weighted IoU of 97.91%, and average BF-Score of 99.93%. Finally, several answer sheets written by varying tools, including pencil, pen, sign pen, and brush pen, and captured under varying light conditions, are used for testing. The results demonstrate the reliability and robustness of the proposed method. t.

title: Transient Nonlinear-Loss Responses of Ferrite Microstrip Structures to High-Power Microwave Pulses Under Perpendicular-Pumping Configuration
authors: Mingyu Yang, Haiyang Wang, Fadhel M. Ghannouchi, Cheng Zhang, Huan Yan, Jianing Zhao, Hao Li, Qiwei Li, Lianghao Guo, Tianming Li, Zhenyu Zhou, Sidi Liu, Yiyun Wang, Shiyong Ji, Biao Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A theoretical and experimental investigation of the threshold and loss responses of nonlinear (NL) loss effects in ferrite microstrip structures excited by high-power microwave (HPM) pulses under a perpendicular-pumping configuration is studied. Based on the coupling dynamics between the uniform precession mode and parametrically excited half-frequency spin-wave pairs, a pulse duration <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-dependent critical threshold model <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$h_{c}^{\bot }(\tau)$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is developed. In addition, an equivalent simulation method for NL power loss under above-threshold excitation is developed in polycrystalline yttrium iron garnet (YIG) substrate. This method enables the power dissipation along the transmission path and the evolution of the radio frequency (RF) magnetic field to be evaluated in commercial full-wave electromagnetic simulators. Experimental and simulation results at 9.6GHz indicate that, when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau $</tex-math> </inline-formula> is shorter than 100ns, the threshold power exhibits an approximate inverse dependence on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau $</tex-math> </inline-formula>, consistent with the short-pulse energy-threshold behavior expected from classical transient parametric-excitation theory. For a given incident power, a higher threshold leads to weaker achievable NL loss. Increasing the YIG substrate thickness reduces the internal RF magnetic-field intensity, thereby significantly increasing the threshold power and weakening the loss capability. Specifically, for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau = 200$</tex-math> </inline-formula>ns, the threshold power of a 0.5-mm-thick sample is about 14.5times larger than that of a 0.3-mm-thick sample, while the NL loss at an incident power of approximately 600W is reduced by about 5dB. The good agreement between simulations and measurements demonstrates that the proposed method can quantitatively characterize transient NL loss behavior of ferrites under a perpendicular-pumping configuration.

title: MAPLE: Self-Supervised Learning-Enhanced Nonlinear Dimensionality Reduction for Visual Analysis
authors: Zeyang Huang, Takanori Fujiwara, Angelos Chatzimparmpas, Wandrille Duchemin, Andreas Kerren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We present a new nonlinear dimensionality reduction method, MAPLE, that enhances UMAP by improving manifold modeling. MAPLE employs a self-supervised learning approach to more efficiently encode low-dimensional manifold geometry. Central to this approach are maximum manifold capacity representations (MMCRs), which help untangle complex manifolds by compressing variances among locally similar data points while amplifying variance among dissimilar data points. This design is particularly effective for high-dimensional data with substantial intra-cluster variance and curved manifold structures, such as biological or image data. Our qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate that MAPLE can produce clearer visual cluster separations and finer subcluster resolution than UMAP while maintaining a tractable computational cost.

title: Energy-Efficient Cable-Driven Robot Actuator With an Eccentric Multisingular Mechanism
authors: Seungbum Lim, Seungcheol Oh, Jungwook Suh
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Energy efficiency has become increasingly important in robotic systems due to rising energy consumption and environmental concerns. To address this issue, this study presents a low-power tug-of-war cable-driven actuator that reduces drive motor torque demand by providing assistive torque through spring pretension. The proposed actuator adopts a hybrid architecture in which a drive motor generates joint motion, while an assist motor regulates spring pretension. To eliminate the continuous power normally required to maintain high pretension, an eccentric multisingular mechanism is introduced at the assist motor. Owing to its geometric characteristics, the eccentric spool creates specific rotational configurations where the effective moment arm approaches zero, enabling zero-power holding under cable tension. For experimental validation, the proposed actuator was implemented on a three-degree-of-freedom cable-driven manipulator and evaluated through assistive-force characterization, zero-power holding verification, repetitive trajectory tracking, and break-even analysis under different external load conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed actuator reduces drive motor current and total energy consumption by more than 50% compared with a nonassisted configuration, while the switching cost of pretension control is recovered within a small number of cycles. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed actuator for energy-efficient cable-driven robotic systems.

title: Pinched Antenna Systems for Sustainable Net-Zero 6G Near-Field Wireless Communications
authors: Arnav Mukhopadhyay, Keshav Singh, Fan-Shuo Tseng, Kapal Dev, Hyundong Shin
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Current sixth-generation (6G) antenna schemes risk falling short of net-zero emission goals, as they often require large numbers of active radio-frequency (RF) elements and substantial material resources in order to deliver both high-capacity connectivity and precise near-field sensing. There remains a critical gap: architectures that can meet next-generation connectivity demands while sharply reducing energy consumption, carbon emissions, and environmental impact. This article addresses this gap by placing pinched antenna systems (PASS) at the center of a human-centric, energy- and carbon-aware 6G vision. PASS employs movable radiators on low-loss dielectric guides, creating scalable and reconfigurable apertures controlled via digital precoding, per-guide analog weighting, and dynamic pinching. This tri-hybrid approach enables energy-proportional operation, sharp near-field focusing, and significant reductions in active RF hardware and spillover, directly supporting IMT-2030 and sustainable development goals (SDG)-aligned goals. System-level studies show that right-sizing the PASS aperture yields efficiency gains that plateau, guiding optimal resource allocation and minimizing material use. By clustering pinching elements near active users, PASS further reduces interference and unnecessary power expenditure. The architecture enables integrated sensing, wireless power transfer, and physical-layer security, while its modular design supports circularity, repair, and carbon-aware control. Open challenges include scalable calibration, full-duplex operation, and integration with other 6G antenna paradigms. Collectively, PASS emerges as a practical and sustainable foundation for future wireless networks.

title: Distributed Multi-Agent Relative Localization via Reference Frame Coordination
authors: Lingwei Xu, Xiao Shen, Chenglong Tian, Yuan Shen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Accurate and robust relative position information is fundamental for enabling self-organized cooperation in multi-agent systems, particularly for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms in the emerging low-altitude economy. However, achieving high-performance relative localization remains challenging, primarily due to the critical issues of reference frame coordination which establishes and maintains a reference frame consensus across the network. In this paper, we establish an optimization framework that bridges performance bounds and algorithm designs, enabling the derivation of optimization structures for centralized relative localization, which facilitates an alternating direction optimization algorithm for subnetwork-oriented scenarios. We then propose a distributed relative localization scheme that integrates an orientation-aware and dynamics-aware state merging mechanism for coordination across different inertial reference frames. Furthermore, we develop a spatial-temporal cooperation approach through real-time adaptive frame alignment and multi-source information fusion, effectively suppressing error accumulation. Simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithms achieve superior accuracy and robustness, providing an effective solution for relative localization and enabling key applications in the low-altitude economy.

title: Rolling Optimization of a Multi-Period Two-Dimensional Cutting Stock Problem Considering the Future Value of the Remnant Materials
authors: Qiong Xia, Yuzun Wang, Siyuan Yue, Ao Li, Shihao Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: A predictive rolling 2D cutting method is put forward to consider extra future value when orders are placed at different times. Each window solves a bi-objective subproblem that minimizes the present procurement cost and maximizes the end-of-period surplus value, where future value includes both static reusability and dynamic reuse probability (Beta-binomial with forgetting), adjusted by a reset index; decoding is done using a priority index to weigh current savings against waiting value. Scenario layering + multiple seeds experiments show <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \text{1 3. 7 {\%}}$</tex> net cost cut, more reuse, less expiring, and 2 nd level runtime per period, showing its solid and can be engineered.

title: ICONIC 2026 TOC
authors: N/A

title: SE-HybridNet: Squeeze-and-Excitation Enhanced Hybrid Filter Based on EfficientNet for Haze Scene Classification
authors: Soorya K, Cynthia J
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Visual recognition systems undergoing outdoor scene classification in the presence of atmospheric impurities like haze and smoke from wildfires face huge challenges. The imposition of real-world conditions such as contrast degradation, low visibility, and inconsistent illumination has led to traditional CNN-based models not being able to perform very well in terms of accuracy. This study puts forth SE-HybridNet as an image classification framework that is both efficient and robust and which combines the methods of hybrid image filtering, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention, and an EfficientNet backbone. The hybrid preprocessing pipeline acts sequentially integrating those four processes: bilateral smoothing, Gaussian blur, median filtering, and sharpening that so to achieve improving visibility, texture, and structural details. The SE attention block dynamically readjusts channel responses making it possible for the model to indicate and give the importance of haze-relevant features prior to classification. The features after processing are captured by the EfficientNet backbone and sorted into categories like Thick Haze, Thin Haze, Night Haze, and Wildfire. The results of the experiments provide evidence of the claim that SE-HybridNet with 96.4% validation accuracy is superior to the conventional CNN and attention-based methods in terms of both precision and robustness. The methodology put forward indicates that hybrid enhancement along with channel-aware learning can have a significant positive impact on environmental scene understanding.

title: Research on the Electromagnetic Interference Coupling Mechanism and Quantitative Suppression of Hall Sensors in DC Brushless Motors
authors: Ke Mei, Zhifeng Li, Jian Yang, Xiaofei Li, Guofeng Zhang, Xiang Li, Chao Gu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The Hall position sensor serves as the core component for electronic commutation in brushless DC motors (BLDCMs). However, its operation is susceptible to interference from the stator winding electromagnetic field, leading to position measurement errors, disrupted commutation timing, and ultimately causing phase current surges and overcurrent shutdowns. Existing anti-interference solutions primarily rely on passive adjustments, lacking quantitative design principles.Based on an in-depth analysis of interference mechanisms, this paper conducts systematic comparative tests between Hall sensor mounting positions and winding phase currents. It comprehensively acquires interference data under various operating conditions, establishes a quantitative interference assessment system, and innovatively proposes a dual-dimensional active anti-interference technology combining “mounting phase optimization” and “phase current waveform regulation. ”Experimental validation demonstrates that this technology achieves a maximum suppression rate exceeding 80% for Hall sensor phase angle deviation, while enhancing motor load capacity by over 40%. It effectively resolves motor operational stability issues caused by electromagnetic field interference.

title: Distributed Computing for Real-Time Traffic Analysis - A Kafka-Spark Integration
authors: B.L. Velammal, Samyuctaa Sriram, Sahana S, Meenakshy A
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Modern urban transportation systems produce huge amounts of real-time data that need processing power beyond what traditional centralized computing can offer. This paper introduces a distributed computing setup that uses Apache Kafka and Apache Spark to process and analyze streaming traffic data across several cluster nodes. The system features a three- tier setup, including data ingestion, distributed processing, and analytical layers, built on a master-worker configuration with one master node and two worker nodes. Apache Kafka acts as the messaging backbone, providing reliable data streaming. Apache Spark delivers scalable, parallel processing for real-time traffic analysis. The system includes smart algorithms for congestion detection and dynamic load balancing, which improve traffic distribution across different routes based on current conditions. The evaluation shows that the implementation achieves high processing speed with low delay, successfully managing continuous data streams that update every five seconds. The distributed architecture offers fault tolerance and horizontal scalability, enabling the system to keep running during node failures and to increase capacity by adding more worker nodes. This work confirms the effectiveness of open-source distributed computing tools for smart transportation uses, laying a strong groundwork for scalable traffic management solutions in smart cities.

title: Enhanced XGBoost Model for Predicting Credit Risk in Micro Finance Lending
authors: CH. Rushikesh, D. Sri Sai, D. Naveen Reddy, R. Karthikeyan, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Credit risk assessment is very essential in maintaining environmentally friendly lending practices in consumer credit and microfinance, where default rates are naturally high. The paper introduces an AI-based credit risk prediction system that utilizes real data from applications and credit repayment to categorize borrowers into safe and risky groups. Firstly, the presented method combines the applicant's demographic and financial information with their past repayment behavior to create a reliable single binary default indicator. They calculated features to create more descriptive variables like age and length of employment and then transformed the categorical variables and cleaned the data. In order to solve the problem of severe class imbalance in default events, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is used. A classifier based on Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) is created using the balanced dataset in order to capture complicated non-linear relationships between borrower characteristics and default risk. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 95.1%, precision of 93.6%, and recall of 93.5% on the test dataset. Additional evaluation metrics such as F1-score and ROC-AUC are used to further assess model performance under imbalanced conditions. Multiple machine learning models such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, and XGBoost were implemented and compared to evaluate.

title: Multimodal Fusion of Speech and Handwriting Biomarkers for Parkinson’s Disease Diagnosis
authors: Mohana Priya S, Shamyukthaa P, Mary Joseph
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disease that progresses by affecting both motor and non-motor capabilities such as speech, writing, and exhibiting one's feelings. Early detection enables timely intervention, but subjective single-modality assessments dominate most of the current diagnostic approaches. In this paper, we present a novel multimodal framework that combines the power of paralinguistic voice with handwriting data to improve the accuracy and reliability of PD diagnosis. On the voice modality, emotional speech instability can be captured with features extracted using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), prosody features (pitch, jitter, shimmer), and spectral characteristics from speech signal, that are further processed with CNN on spectrograms, GRU on temporal sequences, and XGBoost on classical acoustic features. Fine motor impairments could be captured by handwriting modality through spiral drawing data, processed either by CNN or MLP model. A late fusion layer combines predictions from the voice and handwriting stream to estimate the final probability of Parkinson's. Moreover, Grad-CAM and SHAP/LIME support explainable AI and give insights into the features most influential for the detection. Our experiments on publicly available datasets, namely the Parkinson Speech Dataset (UCI), NeuroVoz emotional speech corpus, and Spiral Drawing Dataset, reveal that the proposed multimodal and emotion-aware approach significantly outperformed state-of-the-art single-modality solutions, resulting in a robust, reproducible, and interpretable solution for the early-stage diagnosis of PD.

title: Early Feature Identification of Thermal Runaway in New Energy Vehicle Battery Packs Based on Lightweight YOLOv9
authors: Boran Ren
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper addresses the delayed response of electrical signal-based warning systems for thermal runaway in new energy vehicle batteries. It proposes an early warning method using infrared thermal imaging and a lightweight YOLOv9 model for visual feature recognition. An efficient network structure is designed to meet real-time onboard processing requirements. A multimodal warning model fuses time-series temperature distribution and spatial hotspot features to capture subtle pre-runaway temperature anomalies. Experiments on a real-vehicle thermal dataset show the optimized model achieves 97.5% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{m A P} {@} 0.5$</tex>, while reducing parameters by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80.6 \%$</tex> and computational complexity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\sim 8 5. 2 \%}$</tex>. It provides an average early warning time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3 7. 5}$</tex> minutes, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim\text{2 2. 3}$</tex> minutes earlier than the original model, significantly outperforming traditional electrical monitoring methods. This offers an efficient and reliable technical path for battery safety management.

title: Nodule Detection in Breast Ultrasound Using Deep Learning-Based Instance Segmentation
authors: R. Anusree, R. Abhilash Hareendranathan, C.P. Mohammed Suhail
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: This study assesses the effectiveness of the instance segmentation algorithm using YOLO26 and its derivatives in instance segmentation within Ultrasound imaging. It aims to evaluate their ability to accurately detect breast cancer and segment three classes, such as benign, malignant, and normal. Performance metrics such as precision, recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95 will be utilized to compare different YOLO26 variants. Additionally, the research will investigate the computational efficiency of these algorithms to gauge their suitability for potential application in breast cancer detection using ultrasound. Among the evaluated YOLO26 variants for breast ultrasound instance segmentation, YOLO26l achieved the best performance with an mAP50 of 0.643 and mAP50-95 of 0.473, demonstrating stable accuracy across multiple IoU thresholds. The YOLO26x variant showed comparable but slightly lower performance despite increased model complexity. YOLO26I also exhibited favorable inference speed, indicating a strong balance between accuracy and computational efficiency for real-time clinical ultrasound applications.

title: Research on Composite Fault Diagnosis Strategy for Axial Field Flux-Switching Permanent Magnet Motor
authors: Ziyi Tai, Haiquan Huang, Wei Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To improve the fault diagnosis efficiency and accuracy of Axial Flux Flux-Switching Permanent Magnet Motors (AFFSPMMs), a composite fault diagnosis strategy for opencircuit faults and position sensor faults is proposed. First, to address the difficulty of threshold determination in conventional open-circuit fault diagnosis methods, an improved phase-current mean-based diagnostic method is presented. Then, the rotor position and speed are estimated by using an improved slidingmode observer, and the estimated speed is compared with the sensor-based speed feedback to enable rapid diagnosis of position sensor faults. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed composite fault diagnosis strategy in an axial-flux fluxswitching permanent magnet motor are experimentally validated. The results demonstrate that the proposed method is capable of rapidly and accurately identifying the fault type and fault location.

title: Smartguard: AI-driven drowsiness and health monitoring for drivers
authors: Sharmikha Sree R, Vennila M S, Keerthika A, Lalitha K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid drowsy driver detection and health monitoring system designed to improve traffic safety through real-time fatigue assessment. The proposed system combines infrared camera-based face analysis using Haar cascades and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with wearable physiological sensing to monitor heart rate and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> levels. An onboard controller combines visual and biometric data to accurately detect driver drowsiness and activate a three-tiered warning mechanism, including audible warnings, IoT-based emergency alerts and automated vehicle braking if the driver is unresponsive. A web-based interface provides real-time visualization of driver health metrics and notification status for remote monitoring. The multimodal fusion approach improves detection reliability and reduces false alarms compared to single-sensor methods, providing a cost-effective and practical solution for proactive crash prevention in long-distance and commercial driving scenarios.

title: An Innovative Application for E-Waste Collection and Recycling Marketplace
authors: Sarah Susan Abraham, Gowtham Padmanabhan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Electronic waste (e-waste) constitutes the most rapidly expanding category of hazardous waste on a global scale. In developing nations such as India, over 90% of e-waste is managed by the informal sector utilizing unregulated practices like open burning, thereby presenting significant environmental and public health hazards. To mitigate this socio-environmental dilemma, we propose the EcoCycle Marketplace, an innovative web-based platform constructed on the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js). In contrast to conventional disposal platforms, Eco-Cycle incorporates Client-Side Edge AI (TensorFlow.js) for instantaneous e-waste identification and automated heuristic weight assessment. The system effectively connects consumers with formal recyclers through a dual-dashboard architecture that features a secure logistics escrow mechanism. To promote user engagement within the system, the platform employs a closed-loop token economy ("Green Points") along with real-time micro-impact metrics that incentivize users to responsibly dispose of items both financially and psychologically. This formalization of the recycling lifecycle, in conjunction with the deployment of a locally executed Natural Language Processing (NLP) Eco-Bot and the establishment of immutable digital traceability, aligns this initiative closely with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production).

title: UART Design and Verification of Compatible Baud Rate Communication
authors: Esakki Rajavel S, Kashwin M S, Pravin A, Vinoth Rajkumar G, Viniston Suthahar J, D J Joel Devadass Daniel
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter commonly know as UART is serial, asynchronous, full-duplex communication protocol which is widely used in the embedded systems and microcontrollers. This paper uses Verilog HDL to design the advanced UART architecture to enhances the auto baud rate detection using adaptive control logic. The design modules contain baud rate generator, transmitter, receiver, Asynchronous FIFO and adaptive control logic and the majority of conventional UART designs has only 8 -bit data transfer in this design we extend the data size to 32-bit and Most of the existing UART has fixed baud rate communication here we proposed the dynamic baud rate detection which the baud rate will be changed upon the data size, if the data size is < 8 it will select 9600 and if it is >14 it will select 115200 for the reliable transmission and receives and verifies the functionality using system verilog and functional verification and constraint random verification, to verify the functionality of the design. All the codes written in Verilog HDL and System verilog for verification using Xillinx vivado and EDA playground.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis of a Hybrid Microgrid-Powered Reverse Osmosis Desalination System Using HOMER Software: A Case Study for Thanjavur, India
authors: Ramkiran.B, Ruma Arora Soni, Ahmad Fitri Yusop, Jayaseelan Veera Sundaram, Shanmuga Priya, Sudhakar Kumarasamy
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The twin challenges of energy shortages and freshwater scarcity represent some of the most pressing concerns facing modern society, particularly in rapidly developing regions of South Asia. Because energy is an essential prerequisite for water purification, these two crises are inherently interlinked. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of a hybrid microgrid-powered reverse osmosis (RO) desalination system, simulated in HOMER Pro, for a real-world case study in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. Leveraging the abundant solar irradiation in the region, various hybrid renewable energy configurations, including photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines, and battery storage, are evaluated and compared based on net present cost (NPC) and greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reduction. Two principal system architectures are investigated: configurations with battery energy storage and those without. A systematic sizing and selection methodology for microgrid components is proposed to optimally match the energy demand of the RO plant. Results demonstrate that appropriately designed hybrid renewable energy systems can deliver cost-competitive freshwater production while substantially curtailing carbon emissions relative to diesel-only baselines. The findings provide actionable guidance for sustainable water infrastructure planning across similar water-stressed, solar-rich regions in India and beyond

title: Embedded Tutorial: AI Vs. Verification Engineer: Fight or Synergy?
authors: Marcela Zachariasova
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The paper investigates the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms in two critical areas of simulationbased verification: (1) the development of verification testbenches (2) debugging activities within simulation environments. The primary objective is to assess how AI can enhance the productivity of verification engineers and improve the overall efficiency and quality of verification workflows in industrial practice. The paper presents the role of functional verification in the chip development flow, reviews widely adopted verification methodologies, AI techniques already used for automation and analysis, as well as the recent state-of-the-art research activities targeting emerging verification challenges. A key contribution is the identification of a significant gap in publicly available, high-quality open-source datasets tailored specifically for training AI models in hardware verification tasks. To ground the discussion in industrial reality, the paper also presents results from a survey conducted among 50 verification engineers, providing insight into which parts of a testbench could most benefit from AI-driven code generation or AI assistance. By combining a comprehensive state-of-the-art review with empirical insights from industry practitioners, this paper provides a structured analysis of current limitations and outlines concrete future research directions aligned with realworld verification needs.

title: Consistent Conjugated Variables Choice for TDPA Passivated Teleoperation Architectures
authors: Camilla Celli, Francesco Porcini, Andrea Bini, Alessandro Filippeschi, Antonio Frisoli
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: In bilateral teleoperation, the operator acts on a local robot to command the motion of a remote follower robot and receives back a force feedback generated by the interaction of the follower robot with the environment. The delay of the communication channel as well as the architecture are sources of instability of the teleoperation loop. Passivation of the active blocks of this loop is a commonly used solution with the Time Domain Passivity Approach (TDPA), and, among the methods that use this solution, many (e.g., Time Domain Power Network - TDPN) exploit electro-mechanical equivalence to calculate the energies that may cause instability and that need to be dissipated. These methods rely on the Maxwell’s equivalence, which provides an overall consistent representation of the system’s dynamics. However, this work highlights that this consistency does not occur for the definition of energies when the low-level controllers include feedforward terms. In these cases, Maxwell’s method is ambiguous and may lead to the definition of conjugate variables that fail to represent energies consistently, thus jeopardizing passivity and, eventually, stability. This work shows that Firestone’s method for electro-mechanical equivalence solves the ambiguity and provides a physically consistent estimation of energies, thus always preserving passivity. After the formal identification of this issue for low-level controllers that include feedforward terms such as compensations, this paper proposes a simulation to highlight the issue. The simulation includes a one Degree of Freedom (DoF) leader and a one DoF follower, and considers TDPN architecture and energy reflection TDPA. The simulation results show the stability obtained with the proposed approach and the difference in the computed energies for both architectures when using Maxwell’s and Firestone’s methods.

title: Design and Implementation of an ISO-Compliant Life Cycle Assessment Framework for Environmental Footprint Evaluation of Corrugated Packaging
authors: Prem Lohia, Aryan Somanna, S Gagan, Venkatesh K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Corrugated packaging is currently common in contemporary supply chains due to its recyclability, low cost, and versatility. However, despite its environmentally friendly nature, the production of corrugated packaging is characterized by a number of labor-intensive processes, energy-intensive processes, and water that have negative impacts on the environment. Therefore, it is important to have an accurate and transparent quantification of these impacts for sustainability reporting, regulatory compliance, and eco-design decision-making. This paper describes the design and development of a prototype calculator for the environmental footprint of corrugated packaging based on the Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) approach. The calculator is ISO 14040/14044-compliant and follows the cradle-to-gate approach with industry benchmark data from FEFCO 2023. A reverse mass balance method is used to simulate material flows, and mathematical expressions are used to calculate the carbon footprint, primary energy demand, and water scarcity. The prototype calculator is shown to be capable of identifying hotspots stage-by-stage and to be effective in the proposed methodology.

title:
Interface-regulated ultra-Sensitive, highly moisture-resistant, low-temperature H

                    <sub>2</sub>

                    <sub>2</sub>
