authors: Zhenghan Zhong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This work successfully synthesized SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> heterojunction nanosheet materials via a two-step hydrothermal method and investigated their gas-sensing performance toward H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S. Experimental results indicate that at 100 ℃, the S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> sensor exhibits a response value of 239 to 10 ppm H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S, representing a 12.6-fold enhancement compared to pristine In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf>/R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf>=19). Concurrently, the incorporation of SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> significantly improves material selectivity and lowers the detection limit to 10 ppb. Furthermore, the sensor maintains 60% response at 80% relative humidity, demonstrating excellent moisture resistance. The performance enhancement primarily stems from three mechanisms: the n-n heterojunction formed at the SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> interface promoting electron migration; the strong adsorption of H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S by Sn<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4+</sup> acidic sites; and the increased oxygen vacancy density facilitating surface reactions. This study provides an effective approach for developing high-sensitivity, high-selectivity, low-temperature, and moisture-resistant H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S gas sensors.

title: Research on the Operation and Maintenance Decision Support System for Power Transmission Tower Foundations Based on State Perception
authors: Yang Zhang, Yu Zhang, Haichao Peng, Qi Xu, Yuhang Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To improve the intelligent identification and early warning capabilities of hidden defects in power transmission tower foundations, this study proposes a state perception-based operation and maintenance decision support system. The method constructs a three-layer collaborative perception system, integrating multi-source data from ground-based InSAR, nearground sensors, and drone inspections. A time-series-physical correlation feature construction method is then designed to extract deep-level indicators, such as the “rainfall response coefficient,” which can sensitively indicate defects. Finally, an innovative gradient boosting decision tree evaluation model is established with physical information constraints, embedding industry safety rules as soft constraints into the machine learning process, achieving highly reliable and interpretable state evaluations. In an 18 -month real-world scenario covering 86 towers, the system achieved a recognition precision of 88.2 % and a recall rate of 85.7 % for “severe defects,” with ablation experiments showing that the introduction of physical constraints reduced false alarms by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 4 \%}$</tex>. Feature attribution analysis revealed that the constructed “rainfall response coefficient” and “simulated differential settlement” were key decision factors in over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex> of correct early warning cases. A complete case review showed that the system successfully issued accurate early warnings 21 days before routine inspections. The results indicate that the proposed system effectively closes the loop from multi-source perception to intelligent decision-making, providing reliable technological tools for predictive maintenance of transmission line towers.

title: Lightweight Cryptographic Framework for IoT-Enabled Healthcare Environments
authors: V. Ceronmani Sharmila, K. Vedhasakthi, S. Suthaviji, D. Velmurugan, D. Priyadharshini, Lavanya Settipalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Modern healthcare applications started using the expanded version of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies and significantly enhanced continuous patient monitoring, emergency response, and remote clinical decision-making. But, interconnected medical devices are deployed in large-scale which introduces serious challenges related to data confidentiality, integrity, latency, and resilience against evolving cyber threats. These challenges are addressed through the proposed SecHealth-Lite, a lightweight and secure framework tailored for IoT-enabled healthcare environments. The important elements of this framework are: (i) a lightweight advanced ECC-based encryption protocol, LECCEP with replay protection, which ensures low-latency and entropy. augmented secure data exchange among resource constrained medical devices; and (ii) a robust Hybrid Anomaly Detection and Trust Management System by combining LSTM, VAE, and SVM models with adaptive trust aggregation to detect both known and unknown attacks in real-time which can be dealt with under this proposed framework. Python-based simulation was used for testing under scenarios of various attacks and with different numbers of malicious nodes. Performance is assessed using key metrics including detection accuracy, false positive rate, latency, and throughput. Experimental results demonstrate that SecHealth-Lite achieves 97% detection accuracy, 2% FPR, 80-95 ms latency, and 150-180 Kbps throughput, consistently outperforming two baseline models by 5-8% in accuracy and 25-40% in operational efficiency. These results confirm that SecHealth-Lite provides an effective, scalable, and practical security solution for real-world healthcare IoT deployments.

title: Toward Immersive Teleoperation with Assistive Force Cues to Guide Users with Obstacle and Target Information
authors: Ajithkumar Narayanan Manaparampil, Annika Schmidt, Aaron Pereira, Miguel Neves, Xuwei Wu, Benedikt Pleintinger, Harsimran Singh, Michael Panzirsch, Thomas Hulin, Neal Y. Lii
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: With advances in recent years, we now have several different possibilities for robot operations in complex environments. Scaling from direct teleoperation to fully autonomous planning and execution, the user may choose their level of involvement as called for by the task at hand. Direct teleoperation requires continuous user engagement, which can result in significant physical and mental workload. On the other hand, autonomous systems are frequently unreliable in unstructured or dynamic environments. Supervised autonomy bridges the gap between the two methods by automating some tasks while keeping the human in the loop and allowing them to intervene between autonomous stages. However, there is still a lack of access for intervention and course correction during each autonomous execution. The presented research introduces an assistive teleoperation approach on top of an autonomous system, where a human teleoperator can intervene, with a haptic user interface, in the robot’s motion during autonomous task execution. Our proposed approach is divided into two stages. The first stage plans the entire trajectory using a motion planner. The second stage generates assistive force cues for the planned trajectory toward the target position while avoiding known obstacles. The robot is in full control of the task execution until the operator introduces conflicting inputs to the haptic device. This inherently allows the system to function autonomously till the operator notices an error and intervenes by providing haptic inputs.

title: Smart Hydrogen Microgrids for Powering Polar Vehicles and Drones Using IoT and Satellite-Assisted Intelligent Networks in Extreme Environments
authors: Mekha K Babu, R.Mamat, Noreffendy Tamaldin, Adrian Valentin Boicea, S. Shanmuga Priya, K. Sudhakar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Extreme environments such as Antarctica create major challenges for generating, storing, and transporting energy. Persistent sub-zero temperatures, remote locations, and limited infrastructure make reliable power supply difficult. Because of this, many polar stations still depend on diesel generators. While these systems are familiar and relatively dependable, they are not environmentally sustainable and require complicated fuel logistics, including long-distance transport and seasonal storage. To address these issues, this study introduces a Smart Hydrogen Microgrid designed to support energy needs for polar ground vehicles and autonomous aerial drones. The concept combines renewable energy, hydrogen-based storage, and intelligent communication networks into a single integrated system. In this framework, hybrid solar and wind resources generate electricity that is used for hydrogen production through water electrolysis. This approach provides a clean method for storing energy over long periods, which is particularly valuable in remote regions with variable renewable supply. The hydrogen produced is later converted back into electricity using fuel cells. This electricity powers electric vehicles and unmanned aerial systems used for logistics, surveillance, and environmental monitoring. At the same time, an Internet of Things (IoT) sensing layer continuously tracks renewable power output, hydrogen storage levels, vehicle operation, and key environmental conditions. Since communication in polar regions is often unreliable, the system also incorporates satellite-assisted connectivity combined with edge computing. This setup allows stable data exchange and supports near real-time decision-making even when network access becomes intermittent. Within the overall architecture, intelligent control algorithms manage energy distribution, optimize vehicle routing, and maintain system performance under changing environmental and operational conditions. Overall, the proposed framework strengthens energy resilience, improves operational reliability, and reduces dependence on fossil fuel supply chains. It also aligns with current developments in smart microgrids, renewable energy integration, and intelligent energy management systems. Although designed for polar research stations, the approach can be scaled and adapted for other remote or infrastructure-limited environments.

title: From Pain to Poetics: Suffering as a Generative Force in AI-Assisted Award-Winning Cinematic Creation
authors: Faten Ridene, Imen Samet
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The rapid development of generative artificial intelligence has introduced new aesthetic and narrative possibilities in contemporary animation cinema, particularly within experimental short films that emulate handcrafted techniques. While existing research has extensively examined the technical efficiency, originality, and production workflows of AI-generated animation, limited attention has been given to its capacity to mediate personal suffering and psychological transformation. This paper addresses this gap by analysing two AI-generated animated short films (A Breath Apart and Lily) by Zoubeir Jlassi, whose work has received major international recognition, including the Google Gemini One Billion AI Film Award. Drawing on interdisciplinary frameworks from generative AI studies, animation theory, and psychology, the study explores how lived experiences of illness, vulnerability, and parenthood are transmuted into symbolic visual narratives through AI-assisted cinematic creation. Grounded in theories of post-traumatic growth, resilience, and shattered assumptions, the analysis demonstrates that AI functions not merely as a production technology, but as an aesthetic and psychological mediator that enables the sublimation of trauma into meaning. The stop-motion, like visual language which is characterized by restrained movement, tactile textures, and contemplative pacing, emerges as a deliberate expressive choice aligned with themes of fragility, healing, and emotional suspension. The findings suggest that AI-generated animation can support deeply human, autobiographical, and emotionally resonant storytelling when guided by strong authorial intent. By situating AI cinema within a framework of creative resilience, this research contributes to ongoing debates on originality, authorship, and the human role in intelligent creative systems, particularly within underrepresented Arab and African contexts.

title: Unified Energy Efficiency Index (UEEI): A Holistic Framework for Energy-Aware Object Detection
authors: Ala Eddine Hsairi, Asma Ben Letaifa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Modern object detection models achieve remarkable accuracy but often incur substantial computational and energy costs, posing challenges for deployment on edge devices and other resource-constrained platforms. While prior works report isolated metrics such as FLOPs, latency, or mAP, there is a lack of a unified framework to evaluate models holistically across energy, performance, and efficiency dimensions. In this paper, we introduce the Unified Energy Efficiency Index (UEEI), a novel metric that integrates energy per inference, latency, computational efficiency, and detection performance into a single scalar index. UEEI enables analytical comparison of models, guides architectural design choices (e.g., convolution vs. attention layers), and provides a standardized evaluation framework for energy-aware object detection. Using representative models including YOLOv5, YOLOv7, and YOLOv13, we demonstrate that UEEI captures non-intuitive trade-offs: models with slightly lower accuracy but substantially lower energy and latency can achieve higher overall efficiency. The proposed framework offers both theoretical insights and practical guidance for designing and selecting energy-conscious object detection architectures, paving the way toward more sustainable and efficient AI systems.

title: A Dual-Driven Model for Cultivating Innovative Control Postgraduates: Integrating Interdisciplinary Knowledge with Generative AI
authors: Junjie Liu, Lipeng Zhang, Yuehui Ji, Qiang Gao
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of the intelligent era presents significant challenges to postgraduate education in control disciplines, notably rigid disciplinary boundaries and a lag in incorporating cutting-edge technologies. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel training model designed to enhance the innovation capability of postgraduate students. This model is propelled by a dual-drive mechanism: "Interdisciplinary Integration" and "Generative Artificial Intelligence." It achieves its objectives through a two-pronged approach: first, by deeply embedding generative AI tools into the core curriculum to broaden students' knowledge base and reinforce systems thinking; and second, by systematically integrating these tools into the entire scientific research training workflow to improve research efficiency and deepen innovative outcomes. The ultimate goal is to cultivate a new generation of control specialists who are proficient in leveraging intelligent technologies to solve complex, interdisciplinary problems at the forefront of the field. This work aims to provide a replicable and scalable paradigm for reforming postgraduate education in control and related discipline.

title: LeafVascSense-AI: A Dual-Spectrum Embedded Imaging System for Early Detection of Vascular Obstruction and Phenological Stage Identification in Hydroponic Crops
authors: Nasreddine Somaali, Mohamed Hayouni, Hatem Zgallai, Fethi Choubani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Timely detection of physiological stress is critical for optimizing growth and yield in hydroponic crops. This paper introduces LeafVascSense-AI, a dual-spectrum embedded sensing and AI system for early vascular stress detection and phenological classification. The framework integrates thermal and RGB imaging with convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to enable multimodal analysis, supporting robust and autonomous monitoring under low-power and low-bandwidth constraints.In this preliminary study, validation is performed using publicly available thermal datasets to assess the feasibility of stress detection. Results show that physiologically relevant patterns can be effectively captured, allowing localization of stressed leaf and canopy regions. Full dual-spectrum integration and proprietary dataset acquisition are currently underway.The system architecture combines an ESP32-based embedded node, LoRa communication, and fault-tolerant local storage, providing a scalable and energy-efficient solution for continuous monitoring. While end-to-end deployment and embedded inference remain in development, the design establishes a solid foundation for real-world implementation.LeafVascSense-AI advances plant phenotyping by coupling multimodal sensing with edge-oriented AI and embedded system design, enabling early stress detection and phenological analysis, and paving the way for future work on multimodal fusion, real-time decision support, and pilot-scale hydroponic validation.

title: Towards Transparent and Secure Biometric Authentication: Leveraging Deep Learning and XAI
authors: Mude Giri Naik, Minuku Vijay Sai, Naik Tarunsai Kishor, Mopidevi Yashwanth, S. Dilli Babu
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Biometric authentication has become an essential safeguard for sensitive digital interactions, yet conventional systems remain vulnerable to sophisticated spoofing attacks, including high-resolution images, video replays, and realistic 3D masks. Traditional security mechanisms, reliant on static features or rigid rules, fail to adapt to modern adversarial attempts. This research introduces a novel Enhanced Deep Learning and Explainable AI Framework (EDLX), architected as an adaptive feature-fusion ensemble rather than a simple combination of standalone networks. By integrating advanced transfer learning (leveraging InceptionV3 parameters) with interpretability-driven model evaluation, the EDLX framework fortifies liveness face detection. The model is fine-tuned on the Spoof in the Wild with Multiple Attacks Version 2 (SiWMv2) dataset, encompassing 14 diverse spoofing techniques. The framework uniquely utilizes Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) to provide actionable transparency in decision-making by visually highlighting the discriminative facial regions utilized for classification. Experimental comparisons demonstrate superior detection performance, with the EDLX ensemble achieving an intradataset accuracy of 99.8%, an Attack Presentation Classification Error Rate (APCER) of 0.2%, and a Bona Fide Presentation Classification Error Rate (BPCER) of 0.2%. Furthermore, rigorous cross-dataset validation against unseen attacks verifies the framework’s resistance to overfitting, sustaining a robust accuracy above 94%. By combining high-performance deep learning models with interpretable XAI techniques, the EDLX framework offers a scalable, transparent, and resilient solution to modern biometric authentication challenges. This dual focus on standard biometric metrics (ACER) and interpretability establishes a clear path for secure, real-world deployment in high-stakes security environments.

title: Construction of Dynamic Pathway for Graduation Project Topics Selection Under the "Industry-Science Education"
authors: Jin-gang Jiang, Rui Zhang, Tao Shen, Yu-dong Bao, Jiang-long Xiong, Jian-peng Sun
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As a crucial component of undergraduate education, the graduation project plays a key role in cultivating students' comprehensive abilities. The quality of its topic selection directly impacts the development of students' holistic competencies and their future professional adaptability. To address issues such as delayed updates to topics databases, insufficient personalized recommendations, and subjective evaluation criteria in the process of selecting graduation project topics, this study proposes the construction of a dynamic pathway and evaluation system for graduation project topics selection. This system is driven by a dual mechanism integrating "industry-education collaboration" and "scientific research-education synergy." By integrating enterprise demands, scientific research projects, and competition outcomes, a multi-dimensional topics sourcing mechanism is established. Furthermore, a multi-layer intelligent framework based on a knowledge graph—incorporating BiLSTM-CRF, TransE, and GAT—is introduced to achieve dynamic and personalized topic recommendations. Meanwhile, an evaluation index system encompassing four dimensions—innovativeness, practical value, feasibility, and comprehensive educational effectiveness—was developed. The analytic hierarchy process (AHP) was employed to determine the weights of each indicator, forming a scientific and quantifiable quality evaluation model. Empirical results demonstrate that this system significantly enhances topic-matching satisfaction, industry relevance, and students’ comprehensive capabilities, thereby providing an effective pathway for optimizing graduation project topic selection in higher education institutions.

title: Wearable Soft Haptic Interface for Tactile Perception of Virtual Rain and Other Environmental Particles
authors: Davide Costabile, Alberto Villani, Domenico Prattichizzo
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Rendering weather phenomena within virtual environments plays a crucial role in enhancing the realism and immersiveness of user experiences. Despite the advancements in audiovisual simulation of such phenomena, their haptic representation remains relatively underexplored. The lack of tactile feedback may result in a perceptual disconnect. To address this sensory imbalance, we have developed and validated a novel wearable haptic interface. This technology is specifically designed to deliver localised, subtle, and transient tactile stimuli, emulating the ephemeral nature of suspended or falling particles as raindrops, snowflakes and sand grains, through the use of soft, compliant materials. Moreover, we exploited a couple of devised interfaces to conduct a user study that assessed whether the haptic rendering of atmospheric particles in rainy VR scenarios improves the user experience, increasing the sense of presence.

title: Empowering English Writing Assessment and Feedback through Large Language Models: A Hybrid Framework Integrating Local Fine-Tuning and Prompt Engineering
authors: Huijun Ma, Charuni Samat, Mingjie Li, Jiajun Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study investigates the application potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) in English writing assessment and feedback. Experiments were conducted to compare a fine-tuned local small-scale LLM with the web-based DeepSeek-R1 model, optimized via prompt engineering. Results indicate that for composition scoring, the fine-tuned local model achieved moderate consistency with human grading, validating its feasibility. Conversely, regarding instructional feedback, while the local model underperformed, the DeepSeek-R1 model combined with optimized prompt engineering yielded significant improvements, demonstrating that high-quality feedback is attainable through prompt engineering. Consequently, provided privacy risks are controlled, teachers and students can leverage such LLMs to effectively enhance English writing instruction and learning outcomes.

title: CAE-YOLO: An Improved Detection Algorithm for Steel Surface Defects
authors: Nini Xiao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional YOLOv5s in steel surface defect detection, including inadequate positional information capture, imbalanced global-local feature representation, and suboptimal small object detection, this paper proposes an improved algorithm with three key optimizations: (1) integration of a Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism into the backbone network to enhance sensitivity to defect orientation and location; (2) introduction of an Explicit Visual Center (EVC) module for preserving local features and corner information while maintaining global context; and (3) adoption of WIoU loss to specifically optimize small object detection. Experimental results on the NEU-DET dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a mAP@0.5 of 77.8%, representing a 13.25% relative improvement over the baseline YOLOv5s while maintaining real-time performance and fulfilling the requirements for industrial surface defect inspection.

title: Trustworthy and Transparent AI for Mitigating Insider Threats in Critical Infrastructure Security (CIS)
authors: Syed Muhammad Hasan Danish
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Insider threats pose a persistent challenge to critical infrastructure security due to their stealthy nature, privileged access, and the difficulty of distinguishing malicious actions from legitimate user behavior. Traditional rule-based monitoring systems often fail to capture subtle patterns of insider compromise, while complex artificial intelligence (AI) solutions risk becoming opaque “black boxes” that limit operator trust and accountability. This paper proposes a theoretical framework for trustworthy and transparent AI-based insider threat detection that integrates principles of explainable AI (XAI), fairness, and robustness into cybersecurity operations for critical infrastructure. Using a lightweight machine learning pipeline executed on Google Colab, simulation of insider activities with synthetic log data demonstrates how classifiers (e.g., logistic regression, random forest) combined with SHAP-based interpretability can highlight anomalous access patterns while maintaining human-interpretable insights. Results indicate that transparent models achieve competitive detection performance (AUC $\gt0.85$) while enabling accountability and operator trust, an essential requirement in mission-critical sectors such as energy, healthcare, and transportation. The proposed framework advances the global discourse on responsible and secure AI deployment, addressing both technical challenges (adversarial robustness, bias mitigation) and governance requirements (transparency, accountability) in critical infrastructure cybersecurity.

title: Research on Macro Placement Optimization Algorithm for Integrated Circuits Based on Improved Q-Learning
authors: Hongyu Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the continuous increase in integrated circuit scale, macro placement has become an extremely challenging NP-hard problem in physical design. Traditional placement algorithms struggle to simultaneously minimize wirelength, satisfy density constraints, and control congestion. This paper proposes DQPlace, an improved Q-Learning-based macro placement optimization algorithm. The placement problem is modeled as a Markov Decision Process. Double DQN is adopted to eliminate Q-value overestimation, ResNet-18 extracts canvas image features, a 4-layer 8-head GAT captures netlist hypergraph structural features, and a macro priority sorting mechanism is introduced. The reward function comprehensively considers half-perimeter wirelength increment, density violation, and congestion penalty to achieve multi-objective immediate optimization. Experiments on the ISPD-2005 benchmark suite show that, compared with the PPO method, DQPlace reduces HPWL by 8.2% on average, peak density by 6.6%, congestion rate by 17.1%, and runtime by 19%, while demonstrating strong generalization on unseen circuits. Ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of the Double DQN, GAT, and priority sorting modules. This work provides a new perspective for the application of deep reinforcement learning in the EDA field.

title: Integrating Haptic Vest to the Virtual Reality
authors: Gillian Culberson, Xiaoli Zhang, Qiaojie Zheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Human sensory integration is the bridge between technology and humans. Through sensations like tactile sensory input using technological means, a human can experience a more robust virtual simulation while optimizing performance levels. This study investigates how haptic vibrations can effectively impact performance, resulting in a higher hit rate and lower average miss margin than without vibrations, within a virtual baseball simulation. During the baseball simulation a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}$</tex>-ounce virtual baseball was thrown at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0}$</tex> miles per hour with a. 01 angular drag. In the baseball simulation, a Haptics vest was used to portray different vibrations during the duration of the ball spawning to when the ball reached the subject. The vibrations were separated into two different trials, with each vibration tested 15 times in random order. The first trial tested various vibrations of left-toright, single continuous, bottom-to-top, slant, and no-vibrations while the baseball followed a consistent trajectory. The second trial tested three different vest vibration locations (left, right, and middle), which corresponded with the baseball trajectory. Both trials resulted in a higher total hit rate and lower average miss margin for each set of vibrations compared to non-vibration. Through observing the total hit rate and average miss margin using a virtual reality baseball simulation, there was a greater performance and hit accuracy with the incorporation of a haptic stimulus.

title: Study on the Evolution of Physicochemical and Electrical Properties of Low-Proportion Mineral Oil Blended with Natural Ester Insulating Oil
authors: Feng Tang, Zhenfa Gao, Hongmin Zou, Chunlei Guo, Zhenyang Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: In the green transformation and retrofilling projects of transformers, a small amount of mineral oil will inevitably remain in natural ester insulating oil, and the law of its performance evolution urgently requires systematic evaluation. This paper takes FR3 natural ester insulating oil and Karamay 25# mineral oil as the research objects, prepares blended insulating oil samples with different mineral oil volume fractions $(1 \% \sim 11 \%)$, and systematically investigates the variation characteristics of their physicochemical properties, electrical properties and combustion properties. The results show that the incorporation of low-proportion mineral oil can reduce the acid value, density and pour point of the blended insulating oil, while increasing the breakdown voltage and volume resistivity and decreasing the dielectric loss factor. Meanwhile, the flash point, fire point and limiting oxygen index (LOI) decrease with the increase of mineral oil proportion, but still maintain a high fire safety margin under the condition of low-proportion incorporation. The research results can provide experimental basis for the retrofilling of mineral oil transformers with natural ester insulating oil.

title: Equipment Selection Optimization of Oilfield Distribution Network by Improved Interval Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Anping Liu, Kun Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the continuous development of oilfield area exploitation, the periodic start-stops of intermittent wells and the random derating of faulty wells lead to continuous load disturbances. This makes it difficult for traditional static equipment selection to match real-time well conditions and continuously achieve the goal of energy saving and efficiency improvement. In addition, there may be sudden changes in well conditions and uncertainty information in different oil wells under actual working conditions. To this end, this paper proposes the DNSGA-III-CL algorithm with central point and autoregressive prediction functions. Firstly, this paper constructs a dynamic interval multi-objective optimization model with the goals of minimizing power loss and maximizing system efficiency. Secondly, a first-order autoregressive dynamic prediction model is constructed using the central point sequence of the previous P generations to predict the center of the feasible region at the next moment, thereby quickly locating the new search direction after sudden changes in well conditions. Individuals are sorted according to non-domination and elitism, and the top 80% of high-quality individuals replace the worst 20% of the parent generation to achieve a dynamic balance between rapid convergence and continuous exploration. Experimental results show that the DNSGA-III-CL algorithm significantly enhances the energy-saving effect and adaptability of the oilfield distribution network and has good prospects for on-site promotion.

title: Multi-Similarity Aware Progressive Gap Minimization for Unsupervised Person Re-Identification
authors: Jun Zhang, Shuli Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Currently, most unsupervised person re-identification (ReID) techniques, despite achieving significant progress, still face several issues. These methods often focus excessively on positive samples, neglecting the benefits of negative samples. They also fail to leverage the potential complementary information among samples, centroids, and between centroids and anchors. This shortcoming prevents them from fully capturing the complex and dynamic relationships between samples and pseudo-labels, leading to suboptimal model optimization. To address this, we propose a Multi-Similarity Aware Progressive Gap Minimization (MAPGM) strategy. MAPGM leverages multi-level similarity to design a novel contrastive learning framework that softens pseudo-labels and smooths probability distributions, alleviating overconffdence in single-sample predictions. Furthermore, it simulates positive augmentation within multi-level similarities by selecting appropriate positives and negatives, and adaptively adjusting weights to manage sample diversity and complexity. Experiments on Market-1501 and DukeMTMC demonstrate that our method signiffcantly outperforms most unsupervised ReID approaches.

title: Mechanism and Circuit Design of Intelligent Explosive Disposal Robot
authors: Bailong Li, Dan Zhou, Qian Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: In recent years, modularity, high mobility and fast task adaptation have become important research directions of special robots (such as explosive ordnance disposal robots). According to the working environment and requirements of modern intelligent explosive disposal robot, this paper designs and optimizes the motion control system of explosive disposal robot, and carries out the related circuit design and simulation. Firstly, the mechanical arm, chassis, motor and other parts of the explosive disposal robot are designed and calculated, and the strength is checked. Then, SolidWorks is used for modeling and design, and the three-dimensional modeling of the intelligent explosion-proof car is completed. The three-dimensional modeling and motion simulation are carried out by SolidWorks, which verifies the mobility of the chassis in complex terrain and the stability of the manipulator operation. Finally, the required functional circuits are designed and simulated. The test shows that the explosive disposal robot can achieve the driving speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> on the level road, the maximum climbing angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 30^{\circ}$</tex>, the climbing speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, and the acceleration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$a=1 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$</tex>. These performance indicators enable the explosive disposal robot to meet the needs of most scene applications. Through the mechanical structure design and simulation, this design provides a scheme that can turn quickly and replace the structure design independently for the intelligent explosive disposal vehicle, and provides reference value for the transformation design of intelligent robot.

title: RadTempSense: Physics-Informed Degradation Modeling with Adaptive Compensation and Multi-Modal Diagnosis for Radiation-Hardened Fiber Optic Temperature Sensors
authors: Wenlong Zhang, Wenhua Yang, Liang Zhang, Qianqian Wang, Yaohan Hu, Xiaofeng Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Fiber optic temperature sensors deployed in nuclear reactors and high-energy physics facilities face severe degradation under ionizing radiation, compromising measurement integrity and operational safety. Current approaches suffer from three critical limitations: (1) lack of physics-informed models capturing radiation-induced attenuation (RIA) mechanisms and their coupling with thermal dynamics, (2) absence of dose-aware compensation strategies that adapt to progressive sensor degradation, and (3) inability to discriminate between multiple failure modes in radiation-damaged Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG) sensors. We propose RadTempSense, a comprehensive framework integrating radiation physics modeling, adaptive compensation, and multi-modal failure diagnosis. Our approach introduces three key innovations: (1) a coupled RIA-temperature error model incorporating color center formation, densification, and thermal annealing effects, (2) a dose-aware compensation mechanism with online calibration that reduces measurement errors by up to 78% at high radiation doses, and (3) a Dynamic Diagnostic Similarity Matrix (DDSM) for tracking failure mode evolution across radiation exposure levels. Extensive experiments on simulated nuclear-grade sensor data demonstrate that RadTempSense maintains temperature measurement accuracy within ±2°C up to 35 kGy cumulative dose, while achieving 94.3% diagnostic accuracy for five distinct failure modes.

title: Research on Coordinated Optimization Strategies for Electro-Gas Coupled Microgrids
authors: Youzhong Wu, Jinzhong Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: With rising renewable energy penetration, the stochastic nature of distributed generation has intensified wind and solar curtailment, causing economic penalties. To address this, this paper proposes a coordinated optimization strategy for an integrated electricity-gas microgrid incorporating Power-toGas (P2G) technology. A microgrid model comprising microturbines, renewables, storage, and P2G is established. Subsequently, a dispatch model minimizing comprehensive operating costs-including specific curtailment penalties-is formulated and solved using the Genetic Algorithm (GA). Case studies demonstrate that P2G effectively absorbs surplus renewable energy, significantly reducing penalty costs and enhancing both the system’s economic efficiency and energy utilization rate.

title: Design and Analysis of External Rotor High Speed Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor for Flywheel Energy Storage
authors: Deyun Pan, Fengge Zhang, Yanwen Zheng, Bin Rui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: This study takes a flywheel energy storage system as the application background and focuses on the stringent requirements it imposes on the drive motor in terms of high power density, high efficiency, and high reliability. Accordingly, a highspeed outer-rotor permanent magnet synchronous motor with a rated power of 100 kW and a rated speed of $20,000 \mathrm{r} / \mathrm{min}$ is designed. Under high-speed operating conditions, the eddy current loss in the permanent magnets increases significantly, which not only reduces the motor efficiency but also leads to magnet overheating and an elevated risk of demagnetization. To address this issue, the work investigates the influence of permanent magnet segmentation on eddy current loss. By varying the number of magnet segments and comparing the results with those of a conventional motor employing integral magnets, the variation of eddy current loss in the magnets under different segmentation schemes is systematically analyzed, and its impact on the overall loss distribution and efficiency is examined, aiming to achieve an optimal trade-off between eddy current reduction and structural complexity. A two-dimensional finite element model of the motor is established in Ansys Maxwell, and electromagnetic field as well as eddy current loss simulations are carried out for different segmentation numbers. The internal loss density distribution of the magnets and the quantitative relationship between total loss and segmentation number are obtained, from which the optimal segmentation scheme suitable for the targeted operating conditions is determined.

title: Dual-Beam Sweeping and Tracking for mmWave Massive MIMO ISAC Using Hierarchical Codebooks
authors: Hang Yu, Jianxiao Xie, Zejun Zhang, Ziyu Yan, QingLong Fei
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Efficient beam alignment for millimeter-wave (mmWave) massive MIMO integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) must jointly address communication–sensing resource coupling and user/target mobility. This letter proposes a weighted hierarchical dual-beam sweeping and tracking framework, where candidate communication/sensing beam pairs are evaluated by a coupled score with a power-split factor ρ, enabling a controllable signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) tradeoff. To improve coarse-layer reliability, aperture-preserving hierarchical codebooks are adopted to synthesize wide beams by coherently combining full-aperture narrow beams with an offline phase taper. To enhance temporal robustness, tracker-assisted refinement based on cubature/extended Kalman filtering or spline extrapolation is further combined with a rollback mechanism to suppress abnormal estimation jumps. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves higher angular accuracy, a smoother communication–sensing tradeoff, and lower error-spike and estimate-jump rates than representative benchmark methods, demonstrating better robustness and stability.

title: HSANet: Hierarchical Semantic Alignment Network via Local-Global Adaptive Injection for Cropland Extraction
authors: Bo Zhang, Guoyou Li, Qiang Zhang, Yaxiong Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Delineation of farmland parcels is essential for precision agriculture and environmental monitoring, yet remains challenging due to spectral variability, boundary ambiguity, and complex background interference. We present HSANet, a high-precision semantic segmentation network built on three synergistic innovations. First, the Local-Global Adaptive Injection (LGAI) Module fuses local and global features via dual paths: a local branch with the MogaMambaEnhancer (MME) captures fine-grained textures through gated bottleneck and bidirectional Mamba scanning, while a global branch with the GlobalMambaKernel (GMK) models long-range dependencies via visual state-space modeling and decomposed large-kernel convolution. Second, the Global Pyramid Enhancer (GPE) Head replaces conventional refinement convolutions in the feature pyramid with a global convolution interaction mechanism, jointly enhancing multi-scale representations with global context and local detail. Third, the Anisotropic Error Diffusion and Structural Consolidation Loss (AEDSLoss) identifies structurally significant errors through iterative diffusion and applies modulated focal and geometric penalties to enforce pixel-level accuracy and topological coherence. Extensive experiments on the FGFD and FTW datasets demonstrate that HSANet consistently surpasses state-of-the-art baselines, achieving mIoU/F1 of 87.25%/93.19% and 72.26%/83.90%, respectively, highlighting its potential for high-resolution agricultural mapping.

title:
Overcoming BEOL Thermal Constraints in Heterogenous Integration of α-Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    UV-C Photodetectors Using Low-Temperature Thermal Compression Bonding
authors: Ji Yun Lee, Dong Yun Sung, Sun Jae Kim, Jong Kyung Park, Sarah Eunkyung Kim, Dae-Woo Jeon, Wan SiK Hwang, Seul Ki Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: This work demonstrates a low-temperature heterogeneous integration strategy for α-Ga₂O₃-based functional devices that complies with the stringent thermal budget constraints (<400 °C) of silicon CMOS back-end-of-line (BEOL) processing. Although α-Ga₂O₃ exhibits excellent solar-blind UV-C photoresponse, its high epitaxial growth temperature (>450 °C) has hindered direct monolithic integration with CMOS circuitry. To address this incompatibility, α-Ga₂O₃ was synthesized at 470 °C and subsequently integrated at 350 °C using a Cu–Cu thermal compression bonding (TCB) platform. A dual-functional Ti interlayer architecture, consisting of a 10 nm passivation layer and a 50 nm adhesion layer, facilitates low-temperature bonding while maintaining interfacial stability between metal and oxide layers. Cu–Cu TCB performed at 350 °C under an applied pressure of 10 MPa resulted in a continuous and void-free metallurgical junction. Atomic-scale interfacial analyses indicate that the 50 nm Ti adhesion layer plays a key role in limiting Cu diffusion and mitigating thermo-mechanical stress, contributing to the preservation of the α-Ga₂O₃ active layer during bonding. The heterogeneously integrated UV-C photodetector exhibits photo-to-dark current ratios comparable to those of devices fabricated under high-temperature growth conditions, even under ultra-low-intensity UV-C illumination. These results demonstrate that low-temperature Cu–Cu TCB can be extended beyond conventional BEOL-compatible packaging to enable the integration of high-temperature functional oxides within BEOL thermal constraints. This work highlights a packaging-level heterogeneous integration strategy rather than device-level performance optimization.

title: Torque-based Electromechanical Coupling Analysis for Railway Vibration Energy Harvesting System
authors: Siyuan Wu, Bin Zhang, Xingwei Wei, Jie Luo, Pavel M. Trivailo, Jun Gao, Shengxi Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Vibration energy generated during train operations can be harvested to provide a stable power supply for distributed wireless sensor nodes. Existing research has primarily focused on optimizing energy harvesting structures and interface circuits, whereas mechanical damage under prolonged vibration exposure remains underexplored. This study invests the dynamics of the energy harvester to clarify how excitation forces influence structural stability. The irregular nature of track vibrations makes generator torque the primary means of controlling excitation force variations. Therefore, a torque prediction model was established by integrating the operating principles of buck converters. This model accurately forecasts dynamic torque changes based on load variations, thereby enabling the assessment of structural damage induced by load fluctuations. Experimental results show that at 100 r/min and load resistances from 3Ω to 4kΩ, model prediction errors remain below 5%. Additionally, comparative analysis with two types of DC-DC converter validates the model dynamic behavior. Reducing current and adopting high-efficiency converters both contribute to torque optimization. A low-torque current-limiting (LTCL) circuit is designed to mitigate high-torque durations and further validate the accuracy of the proposed model.

title: MuSCAT:An End-to-end Multi-scale Confidence-Aware Deep Framework for Understanding Text from Images in Unconstrained Environments
authors: Aditya Patil, Tauseef Khan, Ayatullah Faruk Mollah
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Understanding texts from imagery is pertinent for diverse applications ranging from traditional text-embedded image reading to perceiving text-labeled environments. However, text reading in the wild is quite challenging due to environmental heterogeneity, degradation and deformation of acquired images, multi-lingual scenarios, diverse text appearance, etc. Though recent deep learning approaches are demonstrating reasonably good performance, they often struggle in complex and wild imagery. In this paper, a novel multi-scale end-to-end deep framework is presented for effective text understanding from complex and multilingual scene images. It involves (i) crafting of fuzzy textness map with VGG-16 driven class-confidence aggregation of multi-scale text proposals, (ii) designing of a soft-to-hard tuneable tightness-aware text detection technique using the fuzzy textness map, and (iii) recognition of the detected text regions followed by translation into native language. Rigorous experiments over a number of related benchmark datasets viz. ICDAR 2013 focused scene text, AU-MSTR and Total-Text and obtained findings demonstrate high accuracy in text extraction, recognition and translation in uncontrollable environments, outperforming other contemporary methods. It has produced an overall f-measure score of 85.39% on the AU-MSTR, 86.03% on the ICDAR 2013 and 81.69% on the Total-Text, indicating its effectiveness and robustness. The source codes and documentation have been publicly released at https://github.com/adinp1213/MuSCATText-Reader.

title: Efficient Decentralized Federated Generative Adversarial Network Learning over Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data_supp1-3693874.pdf
authors: Alia Asheralieva
publish: N/A
abstract: We propose DHetGAN, a decentralized federated generative adversarial network (GAN) learning framework for multi-source heterogeneous data. Unlike standard decentralized federated learning for non-GAN models, DHetGAN requires each node to solve a local minimax GAN game via adversarial training of its discriminator and generator, which are then averaged in a decentralized manner to reach consensus. To address data heterogeneity across nodes, we develop a GAN-intrinsic aggregation strategy that assigns averaging weights using a privacy-preserving divergence proxy quantifying inter-node distributional divergence. The proxy is estimated locally by each node – without revealing sensitive data – from coupled discriminator-genera tor values calibrated by the neighborhood-level maximal discrimina tor confidence, specific to the minimax GAN game. To reduce communication overhead, we present a compression method tailored to DHet GAN. We theoretically analyze convergence guarantees of our frame work, demonstrating strong inter-node coupling, contracting gradient magnitudes, and uniformly bounded consensus dynamics for both discriminators and generators. We also establish a sufficient condition for full mode coverage in DHetGAN and identify practical training set tings under which this condition holds. Extensive experiments and comparisons with benchmarks show that DHetGAN achieves superior generative performance, strong stability, diversity of mode coverage, and fast convergence, while substantially reducing communication overhead, even under extreme non-i.i.d. data heterogeneity.

title: Semantic-Oriented Robust Sentence Level Text Watermark for Large Language Models
authors: Bo Li, Kun Zhang, Cheng Song, Xi Chen, Hailin Zhou, Richang Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Text watermarking focuses on embedding identifiable information into the generated content, which has become increasingly important with the rapid development of large language models (LLMs). Existing watermarking works either divide the vocabulary of LLMs into “green” and “red” tokens for the watermark generation (i.e., token-level watermark), or use the distance of generated sentence embeddings to distinguish the “green” and “red” partitions (i.e., sentence-level watermark). Despite the achieved progress, existing methods remain vulnerable to various attacks or exhibit limited generalization to out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios. To this end, we focus on sentence-level watermarking and propose a novel semantic-oriented robust text watermark for LLMs (SoTW). Specifically, we first employ a pre-trained embedding model to extract representations of generated sentences. Then, different from existing sentence-level works, we design a novel semantic quantization autoencoder (SQAE) to generate discrete sentence partition representations. Moreover, a semantic loss and a consistency loss are designed to ensure the generalization and robustness of generated watermarks. Furthermore, we develop an efficient detection method for our proposed SoTW. Extensive experiments on three LLMs across three publicly available datasets demonstrate the robustness of SoTW against various attacks in OOD settings. We also release the code to facilitate the community<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/libolb/SoTW</uri>.

title: ARCHON: An Agentic AI Approach for Self-Optimized and Adaptive Intent Management in Distributed Computation Over 6G
authors: Petros Maratos, Eleni Fotopoulou, Anastasios Zafeiropoulos, Constantinos Vassilakis, Grigorios Kakkavas, Symeon Papavassiliou
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: A paradigm shift is underway in 6G from the design of reactive systems to autonomous and adaptive solutions that take advantage of agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI). Agentic AI solutions emerge to inject distributed intelligence into 6G management and orchestration, where multiple agents can plan steps and collaborate to achieve common goals. In the current article, we detail an Agentic AI approach for managing the intent for distributed applications deployed over 6G infrastructure. Distributed applications are provided on computing resources that span from the radio access to the edge to the core part of a 6G-enabled computing continuum. The proposed approach leverages Knowledge Graph and Generative AI technologies, enabling agents to reason over explicit relationships and dynamically generate explainable and reliable plans. A prototype implementation is detailed, accompanied by evaluation results that highlight the efficiency of the proposed approach to improve automation and efficiency on the intent lifecycle management.

title: Towards a co-design framework for bipedal robots
authors: Jacobo Torres-Figueroa, Eduardo Vega-Alvarado, Maria B. Calva-Yañez, Noemi Hernandez-Oliva, Edgar A. Portilla-Flores
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, a co-design framework for developing bipedal robots capable of steady energy-efficient dynamic locomotion is proposed. In contrast to conventional design methodologies, where the mechanical structure, the walking pattern generator, and the control parameters are sequentially developed, the proposed approach integrates all these components in a unified optimization process. The design problem is formulated as a multiobjective dynamic problem to simultaneously optimize the structural parameters of the robot, the walking pattern generator, and the gains of the low-level controllers. The general objective is to maximize the walked distance while minimizing the energy consumption during locomotion. A modified multiobjective version of the Differential Evolution algorithm was employed to solve the optimization problem, in combination with a mechanism for constraint handling based on Pareto Dominance. The framework generates a solution set with feasible designs, each including the optimal dimensions of the robotic links, the corresponding walking patterns and the controller gains. As a case study, a bipedal robot with anthropomorphic dimensions was synthesized and evaluated using multi-body simulation. The obtained solutions were analyzed in terms of Cost of Transport and compared to available bipedal commercial robots. These results showed that the proposed framework can generate feasible solutions, that were in average 10% more efficient than those of the commercial robots, highlighting the advantages of co-design strategies for the development of bipedal robotic systems.

title: IEEE Dataport
authors: N/A

title: Small Traffic Object Detection on Drone Views: A Lightweight Network Based on Neural Architecture Search and Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement
authors: Wei Sun, Xuancheng Liu, Xiaorui Zhang, Tiantian Peng, Quanbo Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: In the field of intelligent transportation, the integration of drones with object detection algorithms enables the flexible collection of traffic information. To address the challenges posed by limited computational resources on drone platforms and the small object sizes observed from aerial views, this study proposes a lightweight detection network for small traffic objects. Specifically, a new neural architecture search (NAS) method based on the principle of maximum entropy is introduced. By considering actual latency as a constraint, we employ a coarse-to-fine evolutionary search strategy to develop a lightweight and high-performance backbone network. Meanwhile, the model’s accuracy and real-time performance are further enhanced by incorporating a dual-branch anchor-free decoupled head that effectively separates the tasks of classification and localization. To tackle the issue of detecting small objects in aerial images, a cascade receptive field aggregation module (CRAM) is proposed, which embeds receptive field paths of different sizes into the feature pyramid, thereby enhancing the model’s multi-scale feature learning capability. Furthermore, a new Wise-IoU loss with distance attention is employed to more accurately regress ground truth boxes of small traffic objects and improves detection accuracy. Extensive experiments on the VisDrone2019-DET dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The proposed method shows great potential for enabling the implementation of traffic object detection algorithms on resource-constrained devices such as drones.

title: Decision Transformers for RIS-Assisted Systems with Diffusion Model-Based Channel Acquisition
authors: Jie Zhang, Yiyang Ni, Jun Li, Guangji Chen, Zhe Wang, Long Shi, Shi Jin, Wen Chen, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) have been recognized as a revolutionary technology for future wireless networks. However, RIS-assisted communications have to continuously tune phase-shifts relying on accurate channel state information (CSI) that is generally difficult to obtain due to the large number of RIS channels. The joint design of CSI acquisition and subsection RIS phase-shifts remains a significant challenge in dynamic environments. In this paper, we propose a diffusion-enhanced decision Transformer (DEDT) framework consisting of a diffusion model (DM) designed for efficient CSI acquisition and a decision Transformer (DT) utilized for phase-shift optimizations. Specifically, we first propose a novel DM mechanism, i.e., conditional imputation based on denoising diffusion probabilistic model, for rapidly acquiring real-time full CSI by exploiting the spatial correlations inherent in wireless channels. Then, we optimize beamforming schemes based on the DT architecture, which pre-trains on historical environments to establish a robust policy model. Next, we incorporate a fine-tuning mechanism to ensure rapid beamforming adaptation to new environments, eliminating the retraining process that is imperative in conventional reinforcement learning (RL) methods. Simulation results demonstrate that DEDT can enhance efficiency and adaptability of RIS-aided communications with fluctuating channel conditions compared to state-of-the-art RL methods.

title: MITLIR: An Imitation-based Reward Shaping Method in Autonomous Driving Scenarios through Map in the Loop
authors: Shan He, Shen Ying, Shi Chen, Yang Zhang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) possesses substantial decision-making potential, yet its advantages over imitation learning (IL) in autonomous driving are not immediately evident, primarily due to the absence of human prior knowledge, which often leads to cold-start problems. To address this, the human-in-the-loop (HITL) approach has emerged, incorporating human experts into the agent training process to facilitate policy convergence. However, traditional HITL methods rely heavily on real-time human supervision and ambiguity in human-designed reward functions poses a significant limitation to the broader application of RL. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a novel map-in-the-loop imitation-based reward shaping method (MITLIR). In this approach, a high-definition (HD) map-based navigation algorithm serves as the expert policy, thereby reducing human labor costs. Drawing inspiration from imitation learning, the expert actions are used to define a loss between the expert and agent actions, which in turn is adopted as the reward signal in reinforcement learning. The main contributions are as follows: (1) This form of reward shaping only requires explicit low-level action outputs from the expert, without relying on semantic interpretations derived from human experience, thus enabling real-time, gradient-rich feedback for the agent. (2) It encourages the agent to mimic the expert policy and effectively mitigates the RL cold-start problem through HITL action interventions. (3) we examine its adaptability to exploratory policies and its integration with sparse rewards, as well as its compatibility with fine-tuning in continued training, (4) We demonstrate its robustness compared to interactive imitation learning algorithms using the same policy network and loss function under noisy conditions.

title: On Maximizing Quality-of-Service of Mixed-Criticality Systems with Energy Constraints
authors: Yi-Wen Zhang, Lei Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Energy consumption plays a crucial role in battery-powered mixed-criticality systems (MCS). However, recent research has not considered energy consumption as a strict constraint. In this paper, we focus on the processor speed allocation issue of MCS with energy constraints, with the goal of maximizing the Quality-of-Service of low-criticality (LO) tasks in the high-criticality (HI) mode. First, this problem is formulated as a nonlinear optimization problem. Second, we propose a simple heuristic algorithm called ASHA to solve this problem on unicore platforms. The ASHA algorithm first calculates the maximum utilization of LO tasks in HI mode and then uses the greedy algorithm to assign the compensating factor of LO tasks. Third, we extend this solution to multicore platforms. Finally, experimental results indicate that our ASHA algorithm performs more effectively on a unicore platform, while Criticality-Unaware Worst Fit Decreasing algorithm shows better performance on multicore platforms.

title: ByFeDis: Toward Byzantine-Robust Heterogeneous Federated Learning via Knowledge Distillation in Railway Intelligent Control Systems
authors: Shijia Guo, Rong Sun, Jingwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Railway intelligent train control system must accommodate a wide range of scenarios due to the extensive variation in its operational environment. Federated learning based on knowledge distillation offers an efficient training paradigm for such systems by supporting model heterogeneity and reducing communication overhead. However, conventional federated knowledge distillation approaches often exhibit limited robustness against untrusted clients and do not facilitate continuous learning. In this paper, we design a Byzantine-robust federated knowledge distillation scheme, named ByFeDis, which mitigates the impact of malicious clients by removing outliers and low-value learning data, thereby improving learning efficiency. Additionally, we enhance the scalability of federated knowledge distillation in an improved scheme ByFeDis*, which is built on ByFeDis and enables continuous training for newly joined clients. Specifically, ByFeDis measures the soft labels uploaded by clients using Euclidean distance to identify poisoned soft labels that disrupt the training process. These abnormal soft labels include not only malicious perturbed samples that significantly deviate from the distribution of the true labels, but also speculative soft labels that are overly close to the true labels. To further enhance privacy protection, we employ fully homomorphic encryption to enable secure aggregation, thereby safeguarding sensitive client information. Theoretical analysis confirms that our scheme effectively preserves privacy while reducing communication complexity compared with existing approaches. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets demonstrate that ByFeDis achieves, on average, a 2% improvement in accuracy over other Byzantine-robust schemes, while ByFeDis* further enhances model accuracy by 3% and reduces premature convergence by up to 10 training rounds in subsequent learning.

title: Information-Theoretic Subspace Optimization for Robust Electromagnetic Inverse Scattering with Correntropy Criterion
authors: Tingsen Zhang, Xifeng Li, Mingwu Tu, Dongjie Bi, Yongle Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Electromagnetic inverse scattering problems (EISPs) are highly sensitive to noise due to their nonlinearity and illposedness. In practical measurements, electromagnetic signals are typically weak and are corrupted by heterogeneous noise, which further complicates stable inversion. Nevertheless, most iterative methods rely on the mean-squared error (MSE) objective and thus exhibit limited robustness to non-Gaussian perturbations, including speckle, impulse, and heavy-tailed noise that are prevalent in real-world scenarios. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a maximum correntropy criterion–based subspace optimization method (MCC-SOM) from an information-theoretic perspective, which employs the maximum correntropy criterion (MCC) as an alternative to MSE. The resulting non-convex optimization problem is solved using a half-quadratic framework, which recasts the original problem into a sequence of iteratively reweighted least-squares subproblems. This enables the adaptive suppression of outliers via a Gaussian-kernel weighting mechanism. Moreover, we provide a rigorous theoretical analysis of the algorithm’s convergence and robustness. Comprehensive experiments on diverse targets under a wide range of noise conditions demonstrate that MCC-SOM consistently and significantly outperforms the classic SOM, with particularly pronounced performance under impulse noise. This study not only introduces a high-performance algorithm for robust EISPs but also offers a validated and effective information-theoretic framework for solving challenging inverse problems in complex noise conditions.

title: Decouple then Converge: Handling Unknown Unlabeled Distributions in Long-Tailed Semi-Supervised Learning
authors: Kai Gan, Tong Wei, Min-Ling Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: While long-tailed semi-supervised learning (LTSSL) has attracted growing attention in many real-world classification tasks, existing LTSSL algorithms typically assume that labeled and unlabeled data share nearly identical class distributions. When this assumption is violated, these methods can perform poorly because they rely on biased model-generated pseudo-labels. To address this issue, we propose a simple yet effective approach called <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> for LTSSL with unknown unlabeled class distributions. Specifically, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> decouples learning into two specialized branches: a standard branch that focuses on head classes and a balanced branch that focuses on tail classes. During training, the two branches interact and gradually converge, allowing them to complement each other and ultimately achieve strong performance across all classes. Despite its simplicity, we show that <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of standard LTSSL benchmarks, e.g., an averaged 2.7% absolute increase in test accuracy against existing algorithms when the class distributions of labeled and unlabeled data are mismatched. Even when the class distributions are identical, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> consistently outperforms many sophisticated LTSSL algorithms. Furthermore, we conduct extensive ablation analyses to tease apart the factors that are the most important to the success of <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small>. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Gank0078/DeCon</uri>.

title: Neural Motion Path: a Path-based Authoring System for Pre-rendered Character Animation_supp1-3693253.mp4
authors: Yoonsang Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Authoring high-quality character animation is essential in multimedia production, especially for pre-rendered formats such as films and TV series. These workflows are often iterative, requiring frequent adjustments and immediate visual feedback, and typically involve editing existing motion, such as mocap data. We present Neural Motion Path (NMP), a deep learning-based system designed to support this authoring process by enabling full-body motion editing through joint level motion path manipulation. While joint rotations are essential for expressive human motion, explicitly specifying them imposes a significant burden on users; NMP addresses this challenge by enabling intu itive position-only motion path editing while implicitly inferring plausible rotation trajectories. To address the inherent tension between motion plausibility and precise constraint satisfaction, NMP explicitly decouples motion synthesis from constraint enforcement within an autoregressive framework. NMP generates realistic, context-aware motion without fine tuning every path detail, making it accessible to novices while supporting the demands of detailed animation authoring. It combines a motion generator with a novel RotationNet for inferring joint rotation, and a constraint imposer that enforces end-effector constraints via an Explicit Weight Sparse Expert Model (EW-SEM). The system supports terrain adaptation and authoring operations like Concatenate, Insert, and Mix. Implemented as a Blender add-on, NMP supports real-time playback and interactive workflows. A user study with novice users shows that NMP improves satisfaction, efficiency, and perceived motion quality compared to the conventional layered keyframing approach, highlighting its potential as an accessible and effective authoring tool.

title: ACHILLES: A Machine Learning Framework for Explainable and Generalized Automotive Intrusion Detection System
authors: Nishat I. Mowla, Kyi Thar, Sarder Fakhrul Abedin, Aamir Mahmood, Zhu Han, Mikael Gidlund, Kabir Fahria, Konstantinos Giapantzis, Antonios Lalas, Joakim Rosell, Mahshid Helali Moghadam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper addresses the need for an explainable and generalized intrusion detection system (IDS) for the in-vehicle networks (IVNs). While machine learning (ML)-based IDS solutions show promising performance, there are still some challenges, such as the lack of trustworthiness and scarcity of attack representing data, hindering their adoption in the automotive cybersecurity. To address these issues, this paper proposes a centralized ML model training and decentralized execution-based framework, namely ACHILLES, that facilitates an explainable and generalizable automotive IDS. Under ACHILLES, different ML models can be trained centrally to enhance decentralized and onboard intrusion detection performance with multiple automotive datasets. In addition, we generate standard feature formats to assess the ML model’s generalization efficacy, where the quality of generalization and explainability is evaluated with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) by identifying the importance of the feature. We also propose a meta-learning scheme to construct suitable ML models trained by the proposed standard feature formats. The proposed feature format exhibits significant performance gain during ML model training and testing with four state-of-the-art controller area network (CAN)-bus datasets containing real, advanced attacks. The experimental results indicate that developing ML models using the generated generalized features and the meta learning-based model building process leads to enhanced performance. In particular, under the dataset cross train-test setting, the proposed feature format enhances the average accuracy by 40.1% for the baseline model, 32.4% for the meta-learned DNN, and 23.6% for the meta-learned Random Forest, compared with the baseline feature format.

title: Kalman Filter-based Mobile User Channel Estimation and User Localization with Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Zhuoxuan Ju, Miloš Doroslovački
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to minimize the Mean Squared Error (MSE) in channel estimation between mobile users and rectangular Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) within wireless communication systems. Meanwhile, the user localization is realized based on the estimated Channel State Information (CSI). Here, we assume a non-linear, user’s position-dependent system model, for a user with high mobility, an RIS with multiple elements, and a base station (BS) with multiple antennas. After that, we apply the Kalman Filtering (KF) like algorithms to reduce MSE in estimating parameters of this time-variant channel model. Additionally, we propose a Non-Circular Noise Kalman Filter (NCNKF) to address scenarios with non-circular complex state-space noise. Furthermore, we apply the Discrete Space Fourier Transform (DSFT) method, combined with an interpolation technique to decrease the Normalized Root Mean Squared Error (NRMSE) for the user localization based on the estimated CSI. The Cramer-Rao Lower Bounds (CRLBs) for both the estimation of channel state and user localization are derived to check the algorithms performance in the case of fixed users, while minimum mean squared error (MMSE) bounds are employed in the case of moving users. Numerical results show that the proposed algorithms outperform the other approaches and can be very close to the CRLBs.
